country_code.description: Country code of the B2C tenant. The `country_code` should be valid for the specified `data_residency_location`. See [official docs](https://aka.ms/B2CDataResidency) for valid country codes. Required when creating a new resource. Changing this forces a new AAD B2C Directory to be created.

data_residency_location.description: Location in which the B2C tenant is hosted and data resides. The `data_residency_location` should be valid for the specified `country_code`. See [official docs](https://aka.ms/B2CDataResidenc) for more information. Changing this forces a new AAD B2C Directory to be created. Possible values are `Asia Pacific`, `Australia`, `Europe`, `Global` and `United States`.

display_name.description: The initial display name of the B2C tenant. Required when creating a new resource. Changing this forces a new AAD B2C Directory to be created.

domain_name.description: Domain name of the B2C tenant, including the `.onmicrosoft.com` suffix. Changing this forces a new AAD B2C Directory to be created.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the AAD B2C Directory should exist. Changing this forces a new AAD B2C Directory to be created.

sku_name.description: Billing SKU for the B2C tenant. Must be one of: `PremiumP1` or `PremiumP2` (`Standard` is not supported). See [official docs](https://aka.ms/b2cBilling) for more information.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the AAD B2C Directory.

domain_configuration_type.description: The configuration type of this Active Directory Domain. Possible values are `FullySynced` and `ResourceTrusting`

domain_name.description: The Active Directory domain to use. See [official documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-domain-services/tutorial-create-instance#create-a-managed-domain) for constraints and recommendations

filtered_sync_enabled.description: Whether to enable group-based filtered sync (also called scoped synchronisation). Defaults to `false`.

initial_replica_set.description: An `initial_replica_set` block as defined below. The initial replica set inherits the same location as the Domain Service resource.

initial_replica_set.subnet_id.description: The ID of the subnet in which to place the initial replica set

location.description: The Azure location where the Domain Service exists

name.description: The display name for your managed Active Directory Domain Service resource

notifications.additional_recipients.description: A list of additional email addresses to notify when there are alerts in the managed domain.

notifications.notify_dc_admins.description: Whether to notify members of the _AAD DC Administrators_ group when there are alerts in the managed domain.

notifications.notify_global_admins.description: Whether to notify all Global Administrators when there are alerts in the managed domain.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group in which the Domain Service should exist

secure_ldap.enabled.description: Whether to enable secure LDAP for the managed domain. For more information, please see [official documentation on enabling LDAPS](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-domain-services/tutorial-configure-ldaps), paying particular attention to the section on network security to avoid unnecessarily exposing your service to Internet-borne bruteforce attacks.

secure_ldap.external_access_enabled.description: Whether to enable external access to LDAPS over the Internet. Defaults to `false`.

secure_ldap.pfx_certificate.description: The certificate/private key to use for LDAPS, as a base64-encoded TripleDES-SHA1 encrypted PKCS#12 bundle (PFX file).

secure_ldap.pfx_certificate_password.description: The password to use for decrypting the PKCS#12 bundle (PFX file).

security.kerberos_armoring_enabled.description: Whether to enable Kerberos Armoring. Defaults to `false`.

security.kerberos_rc4_encryption_enabled.description: Whether to enable Kerberos RC4 Encryption. Defaults to `false`.

security.ntlm_v1_enabled.description: Whether to enable legacy NTLM v1 support. Defaults to `false`.

security.sync_kerberos_passwords.description: Whether to synchronize Kerberos password hashes to the managed domain. Defaults to `false`.

security.sync_ntlm_passwords.description: Whether to synchronize NTLM password hashes to the managed domain. Defaults to `false`.

security.sync_on_prem_passwords.description: Whether to synchronize on-premises password hashes to the managed domain. Defaults to `false`.

security.tls_v1_enabled.description: Whether to enable legacy TLS v1 support. Defaults to `false`.

sku.description: The SKU to use when provisioning the Domain Service resource. One of `Standard`, `Enterprise` or `Premium`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags assigned to the resource.

domain_service_id.description: The ID of the Domain Service for which to create this Replica Set

location.description: The Azure location where this Replica Set should exist

subnet_id.description: The ID of the subnet in which to place this Replica Set

domain_service_id.description: The ID of the Active Directory Domain Service. Changing this forces a new Active Directory Domain Service Trust to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Active Directory Domain Service Trust. Changing this forces a new Active Directory Domain Service Trust to be created.

password.description: The password of the inbound trust set in the on-premise Active Directory Domain Service.

trusted_domain_dns_ips.description: Specifies a list of DNS IPs that are used to resolve the on-premise Active Directory Domain Service.

trusted_domain_fqdn.description: The FQDN of the on-premise Active Directory Domain Service.

enabled.description: Should Advanced Threat Protection be enabled on this resource?

target_resource_id.description: The ID of the Azure Resource which to enable Advanced Threat Protection on

name.description: The Name which should be used for this Advisor suppression. Changing this forces a new Advisor suppression to be created.

recommendation_id.description: The ID of the Advisor recommendation to suppress. Changing this forces a new Advisor suppression to be created.

resource_id.description: The ID of the Resource to suppress the Advisor recommendation for. Changing this forces a new Advisor suppression to be created.

ttl.description: A optional time to live value. If omitted, the suppression will not expire. Changing this forces a new Advisor suppression to be created.

application_insights_id.description: The Application Insights ID that should be used by this AI Foundry Hub.

container_registry_id.description: The Container Registry ID that should be used by this AI Foundry Hub.

description.description: The description of this AI Foundry Hub.

encryption.description: An `encryption` block as defined below. Changing this forces a new AI Foundry Hub to be created.

encryption.key_id.description: The Key Vault URI to access the encryption key.

encryption.key_vault_id.description: The Key Vault ID where the customer owned encryption key exists.

encryption.user_assigned_identity_id.description: The user assigned identity ID that has access to the encryption key.

friendly_name.description: The display name of this AI Foundry Hub.

high_business_impact_enabled.description: Whether High Business Impact (HBI) should be enabled or not. Enabling this setting will reduce diagnostic data collected by the service. Changing this forces a new AI Foundry Hub to be created. Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this AI Foundry Hub.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this AI Foundry Hub. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

key_vault_id.description: The Key Vault ID that should be used by this AI Foundry Hub. Changing this forces a new AI Foundry Hub to be created.

location.description: The Azure Region where the AI Foundry Hub should exist. Changing this forces a new AI Foundry Hub to be created.

managed_network.isolation_mode.description: The isolation mode of the AI Foundry Hub. Possible values are `Disabled`, `AllowOnlyApprovedOutbound`, and `AllowInternetOutbound`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this AI Foundry Hub. Changing this forces a new AI Foundry Hub to be created.

primary_user_assigned_identity.description: The user assigned identity ID that represents the AI Foundry Hub identity. This must be set when enabling encryption with a user assigned identity.

public_network_access.description: Whether public network access for this AI Service Hub should be enabled. Possible values include `Enabled` and `Disabled`. Defaults to `Enabled`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the AI Foundry Hub should exist. Changing this forces a new AI Foundry Hub to be created.

storage_account_id.description: The Storage Account ID that should be used by this AI Foundry Hub. Changing this forces a new AI Foundry Hub to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the AI Foundry Hub.

ai_services_hub_id.description: The AI Foundry ID under which this Project should be created. Changing this forces a new AI Foundry Project to be created.

description.description: The description of this AI Foundry Project.

friendly_name.description: The display name of this AI Foundry Project.

high_business_impact_enabled.description: Whether High Business Impact (HBI) should be enabled or not. Enabling this setting will reduce diagnostic data collected by the service. Changing this forces a new AI Foundry Project to be created. Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this AI Foundry Project.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this AI Foundry Project. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

location.description: The Azure Region where the AI Foundry Project should exist. Changing this forces a new AI Foundry Project to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this AI Foundry Project. Changing this forces a new AI Foundry Project to be created.

primary_user_assigned_identity.description: The user assigned identity ID that represents the AI Foundry Hub identity. This must be set when enabling encryption with a user assigned identity.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the AI Foundry Project.

custom_subdomain_name.description: The subdomain name used for token-based authentication. This property is required when `network_acls` is specified

customer_managed_key.identity_client_id.description: The Client ID of the User Assigned Identity that has access to the key. This property only needs to be specified when there are multiple identities attached to the Azure AI Service.

customer_managed_key.key_vault_key_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault Key which should be used to encrypt the data in this AI Services Account. Exactly one of `key_vault_key_id`, `managed_hsm_key_id` must be specified.

customer_managed_key.managed_hsm_key_id.description: The ID of the managed HSM Key which should be used to encrypt the data in this AI Services Account. Exactly one of `key_vault_key_id`, `managed_hsm_key_id` must be specified.

fqdns.description: List of FQDNs allowed for the AI Services Account.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this AI Services Account.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this AI Services Account. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned`

local_authentication_enabled.description: Whether local authentication is enabled for the AI Services Account. Defaults to `true`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

name.description: Specifies the name of the AI Services Account

network_acls.description: A `network_acls` block as defined below. When this property is specified, `custom_subdomain_name` is also required to be set.

network_acls.bypass.description: Whether to allow trusted Azure Services to access the service. Possible values are `None` and `AzureServices`. Defaults to `AzureServices`.

network_acls.default_action.description: The Default Action to use when no rules match from `ip_rules` / `virtual_network_rules`. Possible values are `Allow` and `Deny`.

network_acls.ip_rules.description: One or more IP Addresses, or CIDR Blocks which should be able to access the AI Services Account.

outbound_network_access_restricted.description: Whether outbound network access is restricted for the AI Services Account. Defaults to `false`.

public_network_access.description: Whether public network access is allowed for the AI Services Account. Possible values are `Enabled` and `Disabled`. Defaults to `Enabled`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which the AI Services Account is created

sku_name.description: Specifies the SKU Name for this AI Services Account. Possible values are `F0`, `F1`, `S0`, `S`, `S1`, `S2`, `S3`, `S4`, `S5`, `S6`, `P0`, `P1`, `P2`, `E0` and `DC0`.

storage.identity_client_id.description: The client ID of the Managed Identity associated with the Storage Account.

storage.storage_account_id.description: The ID of the Storage Account.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

virtual_network_rules.ignore_missing_vne.description: Whether to ignore a missing Virtual Network Service Endpoint or not. Default to `false`.

virtual_network_rules.subnet_id.description: The ID of the subnet which should be able to access this AI Services Account.

admin_users.description: List of email addresses of admin users.

backup_blob_container_uri.description: URI and SAS token for a blob container to store backups.

ipv4_firewall_rule.description: One or more `ipv4_firewall_rule` block(s) as defined below.

ipv4_firewall_rule.name.description: Specifies the name of the firewall rule.

ipv4_firewall_rule.range_end.description: End of the firewall rule range as IPv4 address.

ipv4_firewall_rule.range_start.description: Start of the firewall rule range as IPv4 address.

location.description: The Azure location where the Analysis Services Server exists

name.description: The name of the Analysis Services Server. Only lowercase Alphanumeric characters allowed, starting with a letter

power_bi_service_enabled.description: Indicates if the Power BI service is allowed to access or not.

querypool_connection_mode.description: Controls how the read-write server is used in the query pool. If this value is set to `All` then read-write servers are also used for queries. Otherwise with `ReadOnly` these servers do not participate in query operations. Defaults to `All`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group in which the Analysis Services Server should be exist

sku.description: SKU for the Analysis Services Server. Possible values are: `D1`, `B1`, `B2`, `S0`, `S1`, `S2`, `S4`, `S8`, `S9`, `S8v2` and `S9v2`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

display_name.description: A display name for this API Connection.

managed_api_id.description: The ID of the Managed API which this API Connection is linked to. Changing this forces a new API Connection to be created.

name.description: The Name which should be used for this API Connection. Changing this forces a new API Connection to be created.

parameter_values.description: A map of parameter values associated with this API Connection.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where this API Connection should exist. Changing this forces a new API Connection to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the API Connection.

additional_location.description: One or more `additional_location` blocks as defined below.

additional_location.capacity.description: The number of compute units in this region. Defaults to the capacity of the main region.

additional_location.gateway_disabled.description: Only valid for an Api Management service deployed in multiple locations. This can be used to disable the gateway in this additional location.

additional_location.location.description: The name of the Azure Region in which the API Management Service should be expanded to.

additional_location.public_ip_address_id.description: ID of a standard SKU IPv4 Public IP.

additional_location.virtual_network_conf.description: A `virtual_network_configuration` block as defined below. Required when `virtual_network_type` is `External` or `Internal`.

certificate.description: One or more `certificate` blocks (up to 10) as defined below.

certificate.encoded_certificate.description: The Base64 Encoded PFX or Base64 Encoded X.509 Certificate.

certificate.store_name.description: The name of the Certificate Store where this certificate should be stored. Possible values are `CertificateAuthority` and `Root`.

client_certificate_enabled.description: Enforce a client certificate to be presented on each request to the gateway? This is only supported when SKU type is `Consumption`.

delegation.subscriptions_enabled.description: Should subscription requests be delegated to an external url? Defaults to `false`.

delegation.user_registration_enabled.description: Should user registration requests be delegated to an external url? Defaults to `false`.

delegation.validation_key.description: A base64-encoded validation key to validate, that a request is coming from Azure API Management.

gateway_disabled.description: Disable the gateway in main region? This is only supported when `additional_location` is set.

hostname_configuration.developer_portal.description: One or more `developer_portal` blocks as documented below.

hostname_configuration.management.description: One or more `management` blocks as documented below.

hostname_configuration.portal.description: One or more `portal` blocks as documented below.

hostname_configuration.proxy.description: One or more `proxy` blocks as documented below.

hostname_configuration.scm.description: One or more `scm` blocks as documented below.

identity.certificate_password.description: The password associated with the certificate provided above.

identity.host_name.description: The Hostname to use for the Management API.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this API Management Service.

identity.key_vault_certificate_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault Secret containing the SSL Certificate, which must be of the type `application/x-pkcs12`.

identity.negotiate_client_certificate.description: Should Client Certificate Negotiation be enabled for this Hostname? Defaults to `false`.

identity.ssl_keyvault_identity_client_id.description: System or User Assigned Managed identity clientId as generated by Azure AD, which has `GET` access to the keyVault containing the SSL certificate.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this API Management Service. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

location.description: The Azure location where the API Management Service exists

min_api_version.description: The version which the control plane API calls to API Management service are limited with version equal to or newer than.

name.description: The name of the API Management Service

notification_sender_email.description: Email address from which the notification will be sent.

protocols.http2_enabled.description: Should HTTP/2 be supported by the API Management Service? Defaults to `false`.

proxy.certificate_password.description: The password associated with the certificate provided above.

proxy.default_ssl_binding.description: Is the certificate associated with this Hostname the Default SSL Certificate? This is used when an SNI header isn't specified by a client. Defaults to `false`.

proxy.host_name.description: The Hostname to use for the Management API.

proxy.key_vault_certificate_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault Secret containing the SSL Certificate, which must be of the type `application/x-pkcs12`.

proxy.negotiate_client_certificate.description: Should Client Certificate Negotiation be enabled for this Hostname? Defaults to `false`.

proxy.ssl_keyvault_identity_client_id.description: The Managed Identity Client ID to use to access the Key Vault. This Identity must be specified in the `identity` block to be used.

public_ip_address_id.description: ID of a standard SKU IPv4 Public IP.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Is public access to the service allowed? Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group in which the API Management Service should exist

security.backend_ssl30_enabled.description: Should SSL 3.0 be enabled on the backend of the gateway? Defaults to `false`.

security.backend_tls10_enabled.description: Should TLS 1.0 be enabled on the backend of the gateway? Defaults to `false`.

security.backend_tls11_enabled.description: Should TLS 1.1 be enabled on the backend of the gateway? Defaults to `false`.

security.frontend_ssl30_enabled.description: Should SSL 3.0 be enabled on the frontend of the gateway? Defaults to `false`.

security.frontend_tls10_enabled.description: Should TLS 1.0 be enabled on the frontend of the gateway? Defaults to `false`.

security.frontend_tls11_enabled.description: Should TLS 1.1 be enabled on the frontend of the gateway? Defaults to `false`.

security.tls_ecdhe_ecdsa_with_aes128_cbc.description: Should the `TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA` cipher be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

security.tls_ecdhe_ecdsa_with_aes256_cbc.description: Should the `TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA` cipher be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

security.tls_ecdhe_rsa_with_aes128_cbc_s.description: Should the `TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA` cipher be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

security.tls_ecdhe_rsa_with_aes256_cbc_s.description: Should the `TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA` cipher be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

security.tls_rsa_with_aes128_cbc_sha256_.description: Should the `TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256` cipher be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

security.tls_rsa_with_aes128_cbc_sha_cip.description: Should the `TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA` cipher be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

security.tls_rsa_with_aes128_gcm_sha256_.description: Should the `TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256` cipher be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

security.tls_rsa_with_aes256_cbc_sha256_.description: Should the `TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA256` cipher be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

security.tls_rsa_with_aes256_cbc_sha_cip.description: Should the `TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA` cipher be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

security.tls_rsa_with_aes256_gcm_sha384_.description: Should the `TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384` cipher be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

security.triple_des_ciphers_enabled.description: Should the `TLS_RSA_WITH_3DES_EDE_CBC_SHA` cipher be enabled for alL TLS versions (1.0, 1.1 and 1.2)?

sign_in.enabled.description: Should anonymous users be redirected to the sign in page?

sign_up.enabled.description: Can users sign up on the development portal?

sku_name.description: `sku_name` is a string consisting of two parts separated by an underscore(\_). The first part is the `name`, valid values include: `Consumption`, `Developer`, `Basic`, `BasicV2`, `Standard`, `StandardV2`, `Premium` and `PremiumV2`. The second part is the `capacity` (e.g. the number of deployed units of the `sku`), which must be a positive `integer` (e.g. `Developer_1`).

tags.description: A mapping of tags assigned to the resource.

tenant_access.enabled.description: Should the access to the management API be enabled?

terms_of_service.consent_required.description: Should the user be asked for consent during sign up?

terms_of_service.enabled.description: Should Terms of Service be displayed during sign up?.

terms_of_service.text.description: The Terms of Service which users are required to agree to in order to sign up.

virtual_network_configuration.description: A `virtual_network_configuration` block as defined below. Required when `virtual_network_type` is `External` or `Internal`.

virtual_network_configuration.subnet_id.description: The id of the subnet that will be used for the API Management.

virtual_network_type.description: The type of virtual network you want to use, valid values include: `None`, `External`, `Internal`. Defaults to `None`.

zones.description: Specifies a list of Availability Zones in which this API Management service should be located.

api_management_name.description: The Name of the API Management Service where this API should be created

api_type.description: Type of API. Possible values are `graphql`, `http`, `soap`, and `websocket`. Defaults to `http`.

contact.email.description: The email address of the contact person/organization.

contact.name.description: The name of the contact person/organization.

contact.url.description: Absolute URL of the contact information.

description.description: A description of the API Management API, which may include HTML formatting tags.

display_name.description: The display name of the API.

import.content_format.description: The format of the content from which the API Definition should be imported. Possible values are: `openapi`, `openapi+json`, `openapi+json-link`, `openapi-link`, `swagger-json`, `swagger-link-json`, `wadl-link-json`, `wadl-xml`, `wsdl` and `wsdl-link`.

import.content_value.description: The Content from which the API Definition should be imported. When a `content_format` of `*-link-*` is specified this must be a URL, otherwise this must be defined inline. The URL must be accessible and return a valid document; otherwise, deployment may fail.

import.wsdl_selector.description: A `wsdl_selector` block as defined below, which allows you to limit the import of a WSDL to only a subset of the document. This can only be specified when `content_format` is `wsdl` or `wsdl-link`.

name.description: The name of the API Management API

oauth2_authorization.authorization_serve.description: OAuth authorization server identifier. The name of an [OAuth2 Authorization Server](https://www.terraform.io/docs/providers/azurerm/r/api_management_authorization_server.html).

openid_authentication.bearer_token_sendi.description: How to send token to the server. A list of zero or more methods. Valid values are `authorizationHeader` and `query`.

openid_authentication.openid_provider_na.description: OpenID Connect provider identifier. The name of an [OpenID Connect Provider](https://www.terraform.io/docs/providers/azurerm/r/api_management_openid_connect_provider.html).

path.description: The Path for this API Management API, which is a relative URL which uniquely identifies this API and all of its resource paths within the API Management Service.

protocols.description: A list of protocols the operations in this API can be invoked. Possible values are `http`, `https`, `ws`, and `wss`.

resource_group_name.description: The Name of the Resource Group where the API Management API exists

revision.description: The Revision which used for this API

revision_description.description: The description of the API Revision of the API Management API.

service_url.description: Absolute URL of the backend service implementing this API.

source_api_id.description: The API id of the source API, which could be in format `azurerm_api_management_api.example.id` or in format `azurerm_api_management_api.example.id;rev=1`

subscription_key_parameter_names.header.description: The name of the HTTP Header which should be used for the Subscription Key.

subscription_key_parameter_names.query.description: The name of the QueryString parameter which should be used for the Subscription Key.

subscription_required.description: Should this API require a subscription key? Defaults to `true`.

terms_of_service_url.description: Absolute URL of the Terms of Service for the API.

version.description: The Version number of this API, if this API is versioned.

version_description.description: The description of the API Version of the API Management API.

version_set_id.description: The ID of the Version Set which this API is associated with.

wsdl_selector.endpoint_name.description: The name of endpoint (port) to import from WSDL.

wsdl_selector.service_name.description: The name of service to import from WSDL.

always_log_errors.description: Always log errors. Send telemetry if there is an erroneous condition, regardless of sampling settings.

api_management_logger_id.description: The ID (name) of the Diagnostics Logger.

api_management_name.description: The name of the API Management Service instance. Changing this forces a new API Management Service API Diagnostics Logs to be created.

api_name.description: The name of the API on which to configure the Diagnostics Logs. Changing this forces a new API Management Service API Diagnostics Logs to be created.

body_bytes.description: Number of payload bytes to log (up to 8192).

data_masking.mode.description: The data masking mode. Possible values are `Mask` and `Hide` for `query_params`. The only possible value is `Mask` for `headers`.

data_masking.value.description: The name of the header or the query parameter to mask.

headers_to_log.description: Specifies a list of headers to log.

http_correlation_protocol.description: The HTTP Correlation Protocol to use. Possible values are `None`, `Legacy` or `W3C`.

identifier.description: Identifier of the Diagnostics Logs. Possible values are `applicationinsights` and `azuremonitor`. Changing this forces a new API Management Service API Diagnostics Logs to be created.

operation_name_format.description: The format of the Operation Name for Application Insights telemetries. Possible values are `Name`, and `Url`. Defaults to `Name`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the API Management Service API Diagnostics Logs should exist. Changing this forces a new API Management Service API Diagnostics Logs to be created.

sampling_percentage.description: Sampling (%). For high traffic APIs, please read this [documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/api-management/api-management-howto-app-insights#performance-implications-and-log-sampling) to understand performance implications and log sampling. Valid values are between `0.0` and `100.0`.

verbosity.description: Logging verbosity. Possible values are `verbose`, `information` or `error`.

api_management_name.description: The Name of the API Management Service where the API exists

api_name.description: The name of the API within the API Management Service where this API Operation should be created

description.description: A description for this API Operation, which may include HTML formatting tags.

display_name.description: The Display Name for this API Management Operation.

example.description.description: A long description for this example.

example.external_value.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

example.summary.description: A short description for this example.

form_parameter.default_value.description: The default value for this Form Parameter.

form_parameter.description.description: A description of this Form Parameter.

form_parameter.example.description: One or more `example` blocks as defined above.

form_parameter.name.description: The Name of this Form Parameter.

circuit_breaker_rule.accept_retry_after_.description: Specifies whether the circuit breaker should honor `Retry-After` requests. Defaults to `false`.

circuit_breaker_rule.name.description: The name of the circuit breaker rule.

circuit_breaker_rule.trip_duration.description: Specifies the duration for which the circuit remains open before retrying, in ISO 8601 format.

credentials.certificate.description: A list of client certificate thumbprints to present to the backend host. The certificates must exist within the API Management Service.

credentials.header.description: A mapping of header parameters to pass to the backend host. The keys are the header names and the values are a comma separated string of header values. This is converted to a list before being passed to the API.

credentials.query.description: A mapping of query parameters to pass to the backend host. The keys are the query names and the values are a comma separated string of query values. This is converted to a list before being passed to the API.

description.description: The description of the backend.

failure_condition.count.description: Specifies the number of failures within the specified interval that will trigger the circuit breaker. Possible values are between `1` and `10000`.

failure_condition.error_reasons.description: Specifies a list of error reasons to consider as failures.

failure_condition.interval_duration.description: Specifies the time window over which failures are counted, in ISO 8601 format.

failure_condition.percentage.description: Specifies the percentage of failures within the specified interval that will trigger the circuit breaker. Possible values are between `1` and `100`.

failure_condition.status_code_range.description: One or more `status_code_range` blocks as defined below.

name.description: The name of the API Management backend

protocol.description: The protocol used by the backend host. Possible values are `http` or `soap`.

proxy.password.description: The password to connect to the proxy server.

proxy.username.description: The username to connect to the proxy server.

resource_id.description: The management URI of the backend host in an external system. This URI can be the ARM Resource ID of Logic Apps, Function Apps or API Apps, or the management endpoint of a Service Fabric cluster.

The client certificate thumbprint for the management endpoint.

url.description: The backend host URL should be specified in the format `"https://backend.com/api"`, avoiding trailing slashes (/) to minimize misconfiguration risks. Azure API Management instance will append the backend resource name to this URL. This URL typically serves as the `base-url` in the [`set-backend-service`](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/api-management/set-backend-service-policy) policy, enabling seamless transitions from frontend to backend.

users.description: A list of users to target for this feature.

key.description: The name of the App Configuration Key to create

label.description: The label of the App Configuration Key

locked.description: Should this App Configuration Key be Locked to prevent changes?

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

type.description: The type of the App Configuration Key. It can either be `kv` (simple [key/value](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-app-configuration/concept-key-value)) or `vault` (where the value is a reference to a [Key Vault Secret](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/services/key-vault/). Defaults to `kv`.

value.description: The value of the App Configuration Key. This should only be set when type is set to `kv`.

vault_key_reference.description: The ID of the vault secret this App Configuration Key refers to. This should only be set when `type` is set to `vault`.

active_directory.allowed_audiences.description: Allowed audience values to consider when validating JWTs issued by Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_id.description: The Client ID of this relying party application. Enables OpenIDConnection authentication with Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_secret.description: The Client Secret of this relying party application. If no secret is provided, implicit flow will be used.

app_service_plan_id.description: The ID of the App Service Plan within which to create this App Service.

app_settings.description: A key-value pair of App Settings.

application_logs.file_system_level.description: Log level for filesystem based logging. Supported values are `Error`, `Information`, `Verbose`, `Warning` and `Off`. Defaults to `Off`.

auth_settings.additional_login_params.description: Login parameters to send to the OpenID Connect authorization endpoint when a user logs in. Each parameter must be in the form "key=value".

auth_settings.allowed_external_redirect_.description: External URLs that can be redirected to as part of logging in or logging out of the app.

auth_settings.default_provider.description: The default provider to use when multiple providers have been set up. Possible values are `AzureActiveDirectory`, `Facebook`, `Google`, `MicrosoftAccount` and `Twitter`.

auth_settings.issuer.description: Issuer URI. When using Azure Active Directory, this value is the URI of the directory tenant, e.g. <https://sts.windows.net/{tenant-guid}/>.

auth_settings.runtime_version.description: The runtime version of the Authentication/Authorization module.

auth_settings.token_refresh_extension_ho.description: The number of hours after session token expiration that a session token can be used to call the token refresh API. Defaults to `72`.

auth_settings.token_store_enabled.description: If enabled the module will durably store platform-specific security tokens that are obtained during login flows. Defaults to `false`.

auth_settings.unauthenticated_client_act.description: The action to take when an unauthenticated client attempts to access the app. Possible values are `AllowAnonymous` and `RedirectToLoginPage`.

azure_blob_storage.level.description: The level at which to log. Possible values include `Error`, `Warning`, `Information`, `Verbose` and `Off`. **NOTE:** this field is not available for `http_logs`

azure_blob_storage.retention_in_days.description: The number of days to retain logs for.

azure_blob_storage.sas_url.description: The URL to the storage container with a shared access signature token appended.

backup.enabled.description: Is this Backup enabled? Defaults to `true`.

backup.name.description: Specifies the name for this Backup.

backup.storage_account_url.description: The SAS URL to a Storage Container where Backups should be saved.

client_affinity_enabled.description: Should the App Service send session affinity cookies, which route client requests in the same session to the same instance?

client_cert_enabled.description: Does the App Service require client certificates for incoming requests? Defaults to `false`.

client_cert_mode.description: Mode of client certificates for this App Service. Possible values are `Required`, `Optional` and `OptionalInteractiveUser`. If this parameter is set, `client_cert_enabled` must be set to `true`, otherwise this parameter is ignored.

connection_string.description: One or more `connection_string` blocks as defined below.

connection_string.name.description: The name of the Connection String.

connection_string.type.description: The type of the Connection String. Possible values are `APIHub`, `Custom`, `DocDb`, `EventHub`, `MySQL`, `NotificationHub`, `PostgreSQL`, `RedisCache`, `ServiceBus`, `SQLAzure` and `SQLServer`.

connection_string.value.description: The value for the Connection String.

cors.allowed_origins.description: A list of origins which should be able to make cross-origin calls. `*` can be used to allow all calls.

enabled.description: Is the App Service Enabled? Defaults to `true`.

facebook.app_id.description: The App ID of the Facebook app used for login

facebook.app_secret.description: The App Secret of the Facebook app used for Facebook login.

facebook.oauth_scopes.description: The OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Facebook login authentication. <https://developers.facebook.com/docs/facebook-login>

file_system.retention_in_days.description: The number of days to retain logs for.

file_system.retention_in_mb.description: The maximum size in megabytes that HTTP log files can use before being removed.

google.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Google web application.

google.client_secret.description: The client secret associated with the Google web application.

google.oauth_scopes.description: The OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication. <https://developers.google.com/identity/sign-in/web/>

headers.x_azure_fdid.description: A list of allowed Azure FrontDoor IDs in UUID notation with a maximum of 8.

headers.x_fd_health_probe.description: A list to allow the Azure FrontDoor health probe header. Only allowed value is "1".

headers.x_forwarded_for.description: A list of allowed 'X-Forwarded-For' IPs in CIDR notation with a maximum of 8

headers.x_forwarded_host.description: A list of allowed 'X-Forwarded-Host' domains with a maximum of 8.

https_only.description: Can the App Service only be accessed via HTTPS? Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of user managed identity ids to be assigned. Required if `type` is `UserAssigned`.

identity.type.description: Specifies the identity type of the App Service. Possible values are `SystemAssigned` (where Azure will generate a Service Principal for you), `UserAssigned` where you can specify the Service Principal IDs in the `identity_ids` field, and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` which assigns both a system managed identity as well as the specified user assigned identities.

ip_restriction.action.description: Does this restriction `Allow` or `Deny` access for this IP range. Defaults to `Allow`.

ip_restriction.headers.description: The `headers` block for this specific `ip_restriction` as defined below.

ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The IP Address used for this IP Restriction in CIDR notation.

ip_restriction.name.description: The name for this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority for this IP Restriction. Restrictions are enforced in priority order. By default, priority is set to 65000 if not specified.

ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.virtual_network_subnet_id.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

key_vault_reference_identity_id.description: The User Assigned Identity Id used for looking up KeyVault secrets. The identity must be assigned to the application. [For more information see - Access vaults with a user-assigned identity](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/app-service-key-vault-references#access-vaults-with-a-user-assigned-identity)

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

logs.detailed_error_messages_enabled.description: Should `Detailed error messages` be enabled on this App Service? Defaults to `false`.

logs.failed_request_tracing_enabled.description: Should `Failed request tracing` be enabled on this App Service? Defaults to `false`.

microsoft.client_id.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft.client_secret.description: The OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft.oauth_scopes.description: The OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Microsoft Account authentication. <https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dn631845.aspx>

name.description: Specifies the name of the App Service

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the App Service

schedule.frequency_interval.description: Sets how often the backup should be executed.

schedule.frequency_unit.description: Sets the unit of time for how often the backup should be executed. Possible values are `Day` or `Hour`.

schedule.keep_at_least_one_backup.description: Should at least one backup always be kept in the Storage Account by the Retention Policy, regardless of how old it is?

schedule.retention_period_in_days.description: Specifies the number of days after which Backups should be deleted. Defaults to `30`.

schedule.start_time.description: Sets when the schedule should start working.

scm_ip_restriction.action.description: Allow or Deny access for this IP range. Defaults to `Allow`.

scm_ip_restriction.headers.description: The `headers` block for this specific `scm_ip_restriction` as defined below.

scm_ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The IP Address used for this IP Restriction in CIDR notation.

scm_ip_restriction.name.description: The name for this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority for this IP Restriction. Restrictions are enforced in priority order. By default, priority is set to 65000 if not specified.

scm_ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.virtual_network_subne.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

site_config.acr_use_managed_identity_cre.description: Are Managed Identity Credentials used for Azure Container Registry pull

site_config.acr_user_managed_identity_cl.description: If using User Managed Identity, the User Managed Identity Client Id

site_config.always_on.description: Should the app be loaded at all times? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.app_command_line.description: App command line to launch, e.g. `/sbin/myserver -b 0.0.0.0`.

site_config.auto_swap_slot_name.description: The name of the slot to automatically swap to during deployment

site_config.default_documents.description: The ordering of default documents to load, if an address isn't specified.

site_config.dotnet_framework_version.description: The version of the .NET framework's CLR used in this App Service. Possible values are `v2.0` (which will use the latest version of the .NET framework for the .NET CLR v2 - currently `.net 3.5`), `v4.0` (which corresponds to the latest version of the .NET CLR v4 - which at the time of writing is `.net 4.7.1`), `v5.0` and `v6.0`. [For more information on which .NET CLR version to use based on the .NET framework you're targeting - please see this table](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/.NET_Framework_version_history#Overview). Defaults to `v4.0`.

site_config.ftps_state.description: State of FTP / FTPS service for this App Service. Possible values include: `AllAllowed`, `FtpsOnly` and `Disabled`.

site_config.health_check_path.description: The health check path to be pinged by App Service. [For more information - please see App Service health check announcement](https://azure.github.io/AppService/2020/08/24/healthcheck-on-app-service.html).

site_config.http2_enabled.description: Is HTTP2 Enabled on this App Service? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.ip_restriction.description: A list of `ip_restriction` objects representing IP restrictions as defined below.

site_config.java_container.description: The Java Container to use. If specified `java_version` and `java_container_version` must also be specified. Possible values are `JAVA`, `JETTY`, and `TOMCAT`.

site_config.java_container_version.description: The version of the Java Container to use. If specified `java_version` and `java_container` must also be specified.

site_config.java_version.description: The version of Java to use. If specified `java_container` and `java_container_version` must also be specified. Possible values are `1.7`, `1.8` and `11` and their specific versions - except for Java 11 (e.g. `1.7.0_80`, `1.8.0_181`, `11`)

site_config.linux_fx_version.description: Linux App Framework and version for the App Service. Possible options are a Docker container (`DOCKER|<user/image:tag>`), a base-64 encoded Docker Compose file (`COMPOSE|${filebase64("compose.yml")}`) or a base-64 encoded Kubernetes Manifest (`KUBE|${filebase64("kubernetes.yml")}`).

site_config.local_mysql_enabled.description: Is "MySQL In App" Enabled? This runs a local MySQL instance with your app and shares resources from the App Service plan.

site_config.managed_pipeline_mode.description: The Managed Pipeline Mode. Possible values are `Integrated` and `Classic`. Defaults to `Integrated`.

site_config.min_tls_version.description: The minimum supported TLS version for the app service. Possible values are `1.0`, `1.1`, and `1.2`. Defaults to `1.2` for new app services.

site_config.number_of_workers.description: The scaled number of workers (for per site scaling) of this App Service. Requires that `per_site_scaling` is enabled on the `azurerm_app_service_plan`. [For more information - please see Microsoft documentation on high-density hosting](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/manage-scale-per-app).

site_config.php_version.description: The version of PHP to use in this App Service. Possible values are `5.5`, `5.6`, `7.0`, `7.1`, `7.2`, `7.3` and `7.4`.

site_config.python_version.description: The version of Python to use in this App Service. Possible values are `2.7` and `3.4`.

site_config.remote_debugging_enabled.description: Is Remote Debugging Enabled? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.remote_debugging_version.description: Which version of Visual Studio should the Remote Debugger be compatible with? Currently only `VS2022` is supported.

site_config.scm_ip_restriction.description: A list of `scm_ip_restriction` objects representing IP restrictions as defined below.

site_config.scm_type.description: The type of Source Control enabled for this App Service. Defaults to `None`. Possible values are: `BitbucketGit`, `BitbucketHg`, `CodePlexGit`, `CodePlexHg`, `Dropbox`, `ExternalGit`, `ExternalHg`, `GitHub`, `LocalGit`, `None`, `OneDrive`, `Tfs`, `VSO`, and `VSTSRM`

site_config.scm_use_main_ip_restriction.description: IP security restrictions for scm to use main. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.use_32_bit_worker_process.description: Should the App Service run in 32 bit mode, rather than 64 bit mode?

site_config.vnet_route_all_enabled.description: Should all outbound traffic to have Virtual Network Security Groups and User Defined Routes applied? Defaults to `false`.

source_control.branch.description: The branch of the remote repository to use. Defaults to 'master'.

source_control.rollback_enabled.description: Enable roll-back for the repository. Defaults to `false` if not specified.

source_control.use_mercurial.description: Use Mercurial if `true`, otherwise uses Git.

storage_account.type.description: The type of storage. Possible values are `AzureBlob` and `AzureFiles`.

pfx_blob.description: The base64-encoded contents of the certificate

hostname_binding_id.description: The ID of the Custom Domain/Hostname Binding. Changing this forces a new App Service Certificate Binding to be created.

ssl_state.description: The type of certificate binding. Allowed values are `IpBasedEnabled` or `SniEnabled`. Changing this forces a new App Service Certificate Binding to be created.

auto_renew.description: true if the certificate should be automatically renewed when it expires; otherwise, false. Defaults to `true`.

csr.description: Last CSR that was created for this order.

distinguished_name.description: The Distinguished Name for the App Service Certificate Order.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

product_type.description: Certificate product type, such as `Standard` or `WildCard`. Defaults to `Standard`.

validity_in_years.description: Duration in years (must be between `1` and `3`). Defaults to `1`.

authentication.certificate.description: Service principal certificate for `servicePrincipal` auth. Should be specified when `type` is set to `servicePrincipalCertificate`.

authentication.client_id.description: Client ID for `userAssignedIdentity` or `servicePrincipal` auth. Should be specified when `type` is set to `servicePrincipalSecret` or `servicePrincipalCertificate`. When `type` is set to `userAssignedIdentity`, `client_id` and `subscription_id` should be either both specified or both not specified.

authentication.client_type.description: The application client type. Possible values are `none`, `dotnet`, `java`, `python`, `go`, `php`, `ruby`, `django`, `nodejs` and `springBoot`. Defaults to `none`.

authentication.secret.description: Password or account key for secret auth. `secret` and `name` should be either both specified or both not specified when `type` is set to `secret`.

authentication.vnet_solution.description: The type of the VNet solution. Possible values are `serviceEndpoint`, `privateLink`.

target_resource_id.description: The ID of the target resource

hostname.description: Specifies the Custom Hostname to use for the App Service, example `www.example.com`

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which the App Service exists

ssl_state.description: The SSL type. Possible values are `IpBasedEnabled` and `SniEnabled`

allow_new_private_endpoint_connections.description: Should new Private Endpoint Connections be allowed. Defaults to `true`.

cluster_setting.description: Zero or more `cluster_setting` blocks as defined below.

cluster_setting.name.description: The name of the Cluster Setting.

cluster_setting.value.description: The value for the Cluster Setting.

dedicated_host_count.description: This ASEv3 should use dedicated Hosts. Possible values are `2`

internal_load_balancing_mode.description: Specifies which endpoints to serve internally in the Virtual Network for the App Service Environment. Possible values are `None` (for an External VIP Type), and `"Web, Publishing"` (for an Internal VIP Type). Defaults to `None`

name.description: The name of the App Service Environment

remote_debugging_enabled.description: Whether to enable remote debug. Defaults to `false`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the App Service Environment exists. Defaults to the Resource Group of the Subnet (specified by `subnet_id`)

subnet_id.description: The ID of the Subnet which the App Service Environment should be connected to

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

zone_redundant.description: Set to `true` to deploy the ASEv3 with availability zones supported. Zonal ASEs can be deployed in some regions, you can refer to [Availability Zone support for App Service Environments](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/environment/zone-redundancy). You can only set either `dedicated_host_count` or `zone_redundant` but not both

app_service_name.description: Specifies the name of the App Service

relay_id.description: The ID of the Service Bus Relay

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the App Service

send_key_name.description: The name of the Service Bus key which has Send permissions. Defaults to `RootManageSharedAccessKey`.

custom_hostname_binding_id.description: The ID of the App Service Custom Hostname Binding for the Certificate. Changing this forces a new App Service Managed Certificate to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the App Service Managed Certificate.

app_service_environment_id.description: The ID of the App Service Environment where the App Service Plan should be located. Changing

is_xenon.description: Whether to create a xenon App Service Plan.

kind.description: The kind of the App Service Plan to create. Possible values are `Windows` (also available as `App`), `Linux`, `elastic` (for Premium Consumption), `xenon` and `FunctionApp` (for a Consumption Plan). Defaults to `Windows`

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

maximum_elastic_worker_count.description: The maximum number of total workers allowed for this ElasticScaleEnabled App Service Plan.

name.description: Specifies the name of the App Service Plan component

per_site_scaling.description: Can Apps assigned to this App Service Plan be scaled independently? If set to `false` apps assigned to this plan will scale to all instances of the plan.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the App Service Plan component

sku.description: A `sku` block as documented below.

sku.capacity.description: Specifies the number of workers associated with this App Service Plan.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

zone_redundant.description: Specifies if the App Service Plan should be Zone Redundant

app_service_name.description: The name of the App Service. Changing this forces a new App Service Public Certificate to be created.

blob.description: The base64-encoded contents of the certificate. Changing this forces a new App Service Public Certificate to be created.

certificate_location.description: The location of the certificate. Possible values are `CurrentUserMy`, `LocalMachineMy` and `Unknown`. Changing this forces a new App Service Public Certificate to be created.

certificate_name.description: The name of the public certificate. Changing this forces a new App Service Public Certificate to be created.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the App Service Public Certificate should exist. Changing this forces a new App Service Public Certificate to be created.

active_directory.allowed_audiences.description: Allowed audience values to consider when validating JWTs issued by Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_id.description: The Client ID of this relying party application. Enables OpenIDConnection authentication with Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_secret.description: The Client Secret of this relying party application. If no secret is provided, implicit flow will be used.

app_service_name.description: The name of the App Service within which to create the App Service Slot

app_service_plan_id.description: The ID of the App Service Plan within which to create this App Service Slot

app_settings.description: A key-value pair of App Settings.

application_logs.file_system_level.description: The file system log level. Possible values are `Off`, `Error`, `Warning`, `Information`, and `Verbose`. Defaults to `Off`.

auth_settings.additional_login_params.description: Login parameters to send to the OpenID Connect authorization endpoint when a user logs in. Each parameter must be in the form "key=value".

auth_settings.allowed_external_redirect_.description: External URLs that can be redirected to as part of logging in or logging out of the app.

auth_settings.default_provider.description: The default provider to use when multiple providers have been set up. Possible values are `AzureActiveDirectory`, `Facebook`, `Google`, `MicrosoftAccount` and `Twitter`.

auth_settings.issuer.description: Issuer URI. When using Azure Active Directory, this value is the URI of the directory tenant, e.g. <https://sts.windows.net/{tenant-guid}/>.

auth_settings.runtime_version.description: The runtime version of the Authentication/Authorization module.

auth_settings.token_refresh_extension_ho.description: The number of hours after session token expiration that a session token can be used to call the token refresh API. Defaults to `72`.

auth_settings.token_store_enabled.description: If enabled the module will durably store platform-specific security tokens that are obtained during login flows. Defaults to `false`.

auth_settings.unauthenticated_client_act.description: The action to take when an unauthenticated client attempts to access the app. Possible values are `AllowAnonymous` and `RedirectToLoginPage`.

azure_blob_storage.level.description: The level at which to log. Possible values include `Error`, `Warning`, `Information`, `Verbose` and `Off`. **NOTE:** this field is not available for `http_logs`

azure_blob_storage.retention_in_days.description: The number of days to retain logs for.

azure_blob_storage.sas_url.description: The URL to the storage container, with a Service SAS token appended.

client_affinity_enabled.description: Should the App Service Slot send session affinity cookies, which route client requests in the same session to the same instance?

connection_string.description: An `connection_string` block as defined below.

connection_string.name.description: The name of the Connection String.

connection_string.type.description: The type of the Connection String. Possible values are `APIHub`, `Custom`, `DocDb`, `EventHub`, `MySQL`, `NotificationHub`, `PostgreSQL`, `RedisCache`, `ServiceBus`, `SQLAzure`, and `SQLServer`.

connection_string.value.description: The value for the Connection String.

cors.allowed_origins.description: A list of origins which should be able to make cross-origin calls. `*` can be used to allow all calls.

enabled.description: Is the App Service Slot Enabled? Defaults to `true`.

facebook.app_id.description: The App ID of the Facebook app used for login

facebook.app_secret.description: The App Secret of the Facebook app used for Facebook login.

facebook.oauth_scopes.description: The OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Facebook login authentication. <https://developers.facebook.com/docs/facebook-login>

file_system.retention_in_days.description: The number of days to retain logs for.

file_system.retention_in_mb.description: The maximum size in megabytes that HTTP log files can use before being removed.

google.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Google web application.

google.client_secret.description: The client secret associated with the Google web application.

google.oauth_scopes.description: The OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication. <https://developers.google.com/identity/sign-in/web/>

headers.x_azure_fdid.description: A list of allowed Azure FrontDoor IDs in UUID notation with a maximum of 8.

headers.x_fd_health_probe.description: A list to allow the Azure FrontDoor health probe header. Only allowed value is "1".

headers.x_forwarded_for.description: A list of allowed 'X-Forwarded-For' IPs in CIDR notation with a maximum of 8

headers.x_forwarded_host.description: A list of allowed 'X-Forwarded-Host' domains with a maximum of 8.

https_only.description: Can the App Service Slot only be accessed via HTTPS? Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of user managed identity ids to be assigned. Required if `type` is `UserAssigned`.

identity.type.description: Specifies the identity type of the App Service. Possible values are `SystemAssigned` (where Azure will generate a Service Principal for you), `UserAssigned` where you can specify the Service Principal IDs in the `identity_ids` field, and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` which assigns both a system managed identity as well as the specified user assigned identities.

ip_restriction.action.description: Does this restriction `Allow` or `Deny` access for this IP range. Defaults to `Allow`.

ip_restriction.headers.description: The `headers` block for this specific `ip_restriction` as defined below. The HTTP header filters are evaluated after the rule itself and both conditions must be true for the rule to apply.

ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The IP Address used for this IP Restriction in CIDR notation.

ip_restriction.name.description: The name for this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority for this IP Restriction. Restrictions are enforced in priority order. By default, priority is set to 65000 if not specified.

ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.virtual_network_subnet_id.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

key_vault_reference_identity_id.description: The User Assigned Identity Id used for looking up KeyVault secrets. The identity must be assigned to the application. See [Access vaults with a user-assigned identity](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/app-service-key-vault-references#access-vaults-with-a-user-assigned-identity) for more information.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

logs.detailed_error_messages_enabled.description: Should `Detailed error messages` be enabled on this App Service slot? Defaults to `false`.

logs.failed_request_tracing_enabled.description: Should `Failed request tracing` be enabled on this App Service slot? Defaults to `false`.

microsoft.client_id.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft.client_secret.description: The OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft.oauth_scopes.description: The OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Microsoft Account authentication. <https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dn631845.aspx>

name.description: Specifies the name of the App Service Slot component

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the App Service Slot component

scm_ip_restriction.action.description: Allow or Deny access for this IP range. Defaults to `Allow`.

scm_ip_restriction.headers.description: The `headers` block for this specific `scm_ip_restriction` as defined below.

scm_ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The IP Address used for this IP Restriction in CIDR notation.

scm_ip_restriction.name.description: The name for this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority for this IP Restriction. Restrictions are enforced in priority order. By default, priority is set to 65000 if not specified.

scm_ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.virtual_network_subne.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

site_config.acr_use_managed_identity_cre.description: Are Managed Identity Credentials used for Azure Container Registry pull

site_config.acr_user_managed_identity_cl.description: If using User Managed Identity, the User Managed Identity Client Id

site_config.always_on.description: Should the slot be loaded at all times? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.app_command_line.description: App command line to launch, e.g. `/sbin/myserver -b 0.0.0.0`.

site_config.auto_swap_slot_name.description: The name of the slot to automatically swap to during deployment

site_config.default_documents.description: The ordering of default documents to load, if an address isn't specified.

site_config.dotnet_framework_version.description: The version of the .NET framework's CLR used in this App Service Slot. Possible values are `v2.0` (which will use the latest version of the .NET framework for the .NET CLR v2 - currently `.net 3.5`), `v4.0` (which corresponds to the latest version of the .NET CLR v4 - which at the time of writing is `.net 4.7.1`), `v5.0` and `v6.0`. [For more information on which .NET CLR version to use based on the .NET framework you're targeting - please see this table](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/.NET_Framework_version_history#Overview). Defaults to `v4.0`.

site_config.ftps_state.description: State of FTP / FTPS service for this App Service Slot. Possible values include: `AllAllowed`, `FtpsOnly` and `Disabled`.

site_config.health_check_path.description: The health check path to be pinged by App Service Slot. [For more information - please see App Service health check announcement](https://azure.github.io/AppService/2020/08/24/healthcheck-on-app-service.html).

site_config.http2_enabled.description: Is HTTP2 Enabled on this App Service? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.ip_restriction.description: A list of `ip_restriction` objects representing IP restrictions as defined below.

site_config.java_container.description: The Java Container to use. If specified `java_version` and `java_container_version` must also be specified. Possible values are `JAVA`, `JETTY`, and `TOMCAT`.

site_config.java_container_version.description: The version of the Java Container to use. If specified `java_version` and `java_container` must also be specified.

site_config.java_version.description: The version of Java to use. If specified `java_container` and `java_container_version` must also be specified. Possible values are `1.7`, `1.8`, and `11` and their specific versions - except for Java 11 (e.g. `1.7.0_80`, `1.8.0_181`, `11`)

site_config.linux_fx_version.description: Linux App Framework and version for the App Service Slot. Possible options are a Docker container (`DOCKER|<user/image:tag>`), a base-64 encoded Docker Compose file (`COMPOSE|${filebase64("compose.yml")}`) or a base-64 encoded Kubernetes Manifest (`KUBE|${filebase64("kubernetes.yml")}`).

site_config.local_mysql_enabled.description: Is "MySQL In App" Enabled? This runs a local MySQL instance with your app and shares resources from the App Service plan.

site_config.managed_pipeline_mode.description: The Managed Pipeline Mode. Possible values are `Integrated` and `Classic`. Defaults to `Integrated`.

site_config.min_tls_version.description: The minimum supported TLS version for the app service. Possible values are `1.0`, `1.1`, and `1.2`. Defaults to `1.2` for new app services.

site_config.number_of_workers.description: The scaled number of workers (for per site scaling) of this App Service Slot. Requires that `per_site_scaling` is enabled on the `azurerm_app_service_plan`. [For more information - please see Microsoft documentation on high-density hosting](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/manage-scale-per-app).

site_config.php_version.description: The version of PHP to use in this App Service Slot. Possible values are `5.5`, `5.6`, `7.0`, `7.1`, `7.2`, `7.3`, and `7.4`.

site_config.python_version.description: The version of Python to use in this App Service Slot. Possible values are `2.7` and `3.4`.

site_config.remote_debugging_enabled.description: Is Remote Debugging Enabled? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.remote_debugging_version.description: Which version of Visual Studio should the Remote Debugger be compatible with? Currently only `VS2022` is supported.

site_config.scm_ip_restriction.description: A list of `scm_ip_restriction` objects representing IP restrictions as defined below.

site_config.scm_type.description: The type of Source Control enabled for this App Service Slot. Defaults to `None`. Possible values are: `BitbucketGit`, `BitbucketHg`, `CodePlexGit`, `CodePlexHg`, `Dropbox`, `ExternalGit`, `ExternalHg`, `GitHub`, `LocalGit`, `None`, `OneDrive`, `Tfs`, `VSO`, and `VSTSRM`

site_config.scm_use_main_ip_restriction.description: IP security restrictions for scm to use main. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.use_32_bit_worker_process.description: Should the App Service Slot run in 32 bit mode, rather than 64 bit mode?

site_config.vnet_route_all_enabled.description: Should all outbound traffic to have Virtual Network Security Groups and User Defined Routes applied? Defaults to `false`.

authentication_certificate.description: One or more `authentication_certificate` blocks as defined below.

authentication_certificate.data.description: The contents of the Authentication Certificate which should be used.

authentication_certificate.name.description: The Name of the Authentication Certificate to use.

autoscale_configuration.max_capacity.description: Maximum capacity for autoscaling. Accepted values are in the range `2` to `125`.

autoscale_configuration.min_capacity.description: Minimum capacity for autoscaling. Accepted values are in the range `0` to `100`.

backend_address_pool.description: One or more `backend_address_pool` blocks as defined below.

backend_address_pool.fqdns.description: A list of FQDN's which should be part of the Backend Address Pool.

backend_address_pool.ip_addresses.description: A list of IP Addresses which should be part of the Backend Address Pool.

backend_http_settings.affinity_cookie_na.description: The name of the affinity cookie.

backend_http_settings.authentication_cer.description: One or more `authentication_certificate_backend` blocks as defined below.

backend_http_settings.cookie_based_affin.description: Is Cookie-Based Affinity enabled? Possible values are `Enabled` and `Disabled`.

backend_http_settings.dedicated_backend_.description: Whether to use a dedicated backend connection. Defaults to `false`.

backend_http_settings.host_name.description: Host header to be sent to the backend servers. Cannot be set if `pick_host_name_from_backend_address` is set to `true`.

backend_http_settings.name.description: The name of the Authentication Certificate.

backend_http_settings.path.description: The Path which should be used as a prefix for all HTTP requests.

backend_http_settings.pick_host_name_fro.description: Whether host header should be picked from the host name of the backend server. Defaults to `false`.

backend_http_settings.port.description: The port which should be used for this Backend HTTP Settings Collection.

backend_http_settings.probe_name.description: The name of an associated HTTP Probe.

backend_http_settings.protocol.description: The Protocol which should be used. Possible values are `Http` and `Https`.

backend_http_settings.request_timeout.description: The request timeout in seconds, which must be between 1 and 86400 seconds. Defaults to `30`.

condition.ignore_case.description: Perform a case in-sensitive comparison. Defaults to `false`

condition.negate.description: Negate the result of the condition evaluation. Defaults to `false`

condition.pattern.description: The pattern, either fixed string or regular expression, that evaluates the truthfulness of the condition.

connection_draining.drain_timeout_sec.description: The number of seconds connection draining is active. Acceptable values are from `1` second to `3600` seconds.

connection_draining.enabled.description: If connection draining is enabled or not.

custom_error_configuration.description: One or more `custom_error_configuration` blocks as defined below.

custom_error_configuration.custom_error_.description: Error page URL of the application gateway customer error.

custom_error_configuration.status_code.description: Status code of the application gateway customer error. Possible values are `HttpStatus400`, `HttpStatus403`, `HttpStatus404`, `HttpStatus405`, `HttpStatus408`, `HttpStatus500`, `HttpStatus502`, `HttpStatus503` and `HttpStatus504`

disabled_rule_group.rule_group_name.description: The rule group where specific rules should be disabled. Possible values are `BadBots`, `crs_20_protocol_violations`, `crs_21_protocol_anomalies`, `crs_23_request_limits`, `crs_30_http_policy`, `crs_35_bad_robots`, `crs_40_generic_attacks`, `crs_41_sql_injection_attacks`, `crs_41_xss_attacks`, `crs_42_tight_security`, `crs_45_trojans`, `crs_49_inbound_blocking`, `General`, `GoodBots`, `KnownBadBots`, `Known-CVEs`, `REQUEST-911-METHOD-ENFORCEMENT`, `REQUEST-913-SCANNER-DETECTION`, `REQUEST-920-PROTOCOL-ENFORCEMENT`, `REQUEST-921-PROTOCOL-ATTACK`, `REQUEST-930-APPLICATION-ATTACK-LFI`, `REQUEST-931-APPLICATION-ATTACK-RFI`, `REQUEST-932-APPLICATION-ATTACK-RCE`, `REQUEST-933-APPLICATION-ATTACK-PHP`, `REQUEST-941-APPLICATION-ATTACK-XSS`, `REQUEST-942-APPLICATION-ATTACK-SQLI`, `REQUEST-943-APPLICATION-ATTACK-SESSION-FIXATION`, `REQUEST-944-APPLICATION-ATTACK-JAVA`, `UnknownBots`, `METHOD-ENFORCEMENT`, `PROTOCOL-ENFORCEMENT`, `PROTOCOL-ATTACK`, `LFI`, `RFI`, `RCE`, `PHP`, `NODEJS`, `XSS`, `SQLI`, `FIX`, `JAVA`, `MS-ThreatIntel-WebShells`, `MS-ThreatIntel-AppSec`, `MS-ThreatIntel-SQLI`, `MS-ThreatIntel-CVEs` and `MS-ThreatIntel-XSS`.

disabled_rule_group.rules.description: A list of rules which should be disabled in that group. Disables all rules in the specified group if `rules` is not specified.

exclusion.match_variable.description: Match variable of the exclusion rule to exclude header, cookie or GET arguments. Possible values are `RequestArgKeys`, `RequestArgNames`, `RequestArgValues`, `RequestCookieKeys`, `RequestCookieNames`, `RequestCookieValues`, `RequestHeaderKeys`, `RequestHeaderNames` and `RequestHeaderValues`

exclusion.selector.description: String value which will be used for the filter operation. If empty will exclude all traffic on this `match_variable`

exclusion.selector_match_operator.description: Operator which will be used to search in the variable content. Possible values are `Contains`, `EndsWith`, `Equals`, `EqualsAny` and `StartsWith`. If empty will exclude all traffic on this `match_variable`

fips_enabled.description: Is FIPS enabled on the Application Gateway?

firewall_policy_id.description: The ID of the Web Application Firewall Policy.

force_firewall_policy_association.description: Is the Firewall Policy associated with the Application Gateway?
