storage_account_node_identity.description: Specifies the user assigned identity for the storage account.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

account_name.description: The name of the Batch account

allow_updates.description: A value indicating whether packages within the application may be overwritten using the same version string. Defaults to `true`.

default_version.description: The package to use if a client requests the application but does not specify a version. This property can only be set to the name of an existing package.

display_name.description: The display name for the application.

name.description: The name of the application. This must be unique within the account

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group that contains the Batch account

account_name.description: Specifies the name of the Batch account

certificate.description: The base64-encoded contents of the certificate.

format.description: The format of the certificate. Possible values are `Cer` or `Pfx`.

password.description: The password to access the certificate's private key. This can only be specified when `format` is `Pfx`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Batch account

thumbprint_algorithm.description: The algorithm of the certificate thumbprint. At this time the only supported value is `SHA1`

batch_pool_id.description: The ID of the Batch Pool. Changing this forces a new Batch Job to be created.

common_environment_properties.description: Specifies a map of common environment settings applied to this Batch Job. Changing this forces a new Batch Job to be created.

display_name.description: The display name of this Batch Job. Changing this forces a new Batch Job to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Batch Job. Changing this forces a new Batch Job to be created.

priority.description: The priority of this Batch Job, possible values can range from -1000 (lowest) to 1000 (highest). Defaults to `0`.

task_retry_maximum.description: The number of retries to each Batch Task belongs to this Batch Job. If this is set to `0`, the Batch service does not retry Tasks. If this is set to `-1`, the Batch service retries Batch Tasks without limit.

account_name.description: Specifies the name of the Batch account in which the pool will be created

auto_scale.description: A `auto_scale` block that describes the scale settings when using auto scale as defined below.

auto_scale.evaluation_interval.description: The interval to wait before evaluating if the pool needs to be scaled. Defaults to `PT15M`.

auto_scale.formula.description: The autoscale formula that needs to be used for scaling the Batch pool.

auto_user.elevation_level.description: The elevation level of the user identity under which the start task runs. Possible values are `Admin` or `NonAdmin`. Defaults to `NonAdmin`.

auto_user.scope.description: The scope of the user identity under which the start task runs. Possible values are `Task` or `Pool`. Defaults to `Task`.

azure_blob_file_system.account_key.description: The Azure Storage Account key. This property is mutually exclusive with both `sas_key` and `identity_id`; exactly one must be specified.

azure_blob_file_system.blobfuse_options.description: Additional command line options to pass to the mount command. These are 'net use' options in Windows and 'mount' options in Linux.

azure_blob_file_system.container_name.description: The Azure Blob Storage Container name.

azure_blob_file_system.identity_id.description: The ARM resource id of the user assigned identity. This property is mutually exclusive with both `account_key` and `sas_key`; exactly one must be specified.

azure_blob_file_system.relative_mount_pa.description: The relative path on compute node where the file system will be mounted All file systems are mounted relative to the Batch mounts directory, accessible via the `AZ_BATCH_NODE_MOUNTS_DIR` environment variable.

azure_blob_file_system.sas_key.description: The Azure Storage SAS token. This property is mutually exclusive with both `account_key` and `identity_id`; exactly one must be specified.

azure_file_share.azure_file_url.description: The Azure Files URL. This is of the form 'https://{account}.file.core.windows.net/'.

azure_file_share.mount_options.description: Additional command line options to pass to the mount command. These are 'net use' options in Windows and 'mount' options in Linux.

azure_file_share.relative_mount_path.description: The relative path on compute node where the file system will be mounted All file systems are mounted relative to the Batch mounts directory, accessible via the `AZ_BATCH_NODE_MOUNTS_DIR` environment variable.

cifs_mount.mount_options.description: Additional command line options to pass to the mount command. These are 'net use' options in Windows and 'mount' options in Linux.

cifs_mount.password.description: The password to use for authentication against the CIFS file system.

cifs_mount.relative_mount_path.description: The relative path on compute node where the file system will be mounted All file systems are mounted relative to the Batch mounts directory, accessible via the `AZ_BATCH_NODE_MOUNTS_DIR` environment variable.

cifs_mount.source.description: The URI of the file system to mount.

cifs_mount.user_name.description: The user to use for authentication against the CIFS file system.

container.image_name.description: The image to use to create the container in which the task will run. This is the full image reference, as would be specified to "docker pull". If no tag is provided as part of the image name, the tag ":latest" is used as a default.

container.run_options.description: Additional options to the container create command. These additional options are supplied as arguments to the "docker create" command, in addition to those controlled by the Batch Service.

container.working_directory.description: A flag to indicate where the container task working directory is. Possible values are `TaskWorkingDirectory` and `ContainerImageDefault`.

container_configuration.description: The container configuration used in the pool's VMs. One `container_configuration` block as defined below.

container_configuration.container_image_.description: A list of container image names to use, as would be specified by `docker pull`

container_configuration.container_regist.description: One or more `container_registries` blocks as defined below. Additional container registries from which container images can be pulled by the pool's VMs

container_configuration.type.description: The type of container configuration. Possible value is `DockerCompatible`.

container_registries.password.description: The password to log into the registry server

container_registries.user_assigned_ident.description: The reference to the user assigned identity to use to access an Azure Container Registry instead of username and password

container_registries.user_name.description: The user name to log into the registry server

data_disks.description: A `data_disks` block describes the data disk settings as defined below.

data_disks.caching.description: Values are: "none" - The caching mode for the disk is not enabled. "readOnly" - The caching mode for the disk is read only. "readWrite" - The caching mode for the disk is read and write. For information about the caching options see: <https://blogs.msdn.microsoft.com/windowsazurestorage/2012/06/27/exploring-windows-azure-drives-disks-and-images/>. Possible values are `None`, `ReadOnly` and `ReadWrite`. Defaults to `ReadOnly`.

data_disks.disk_size_gb.description: The initial disk size in GB when creating new data disk.

data_disks.lun.description: The lun is used to uniquely identify each data disk. If attaching multiple disks, each should have a distinct lun. The value must be between 0 and 63, inclusive.

data_disks.storage_account_type.description: The storage account type to be used for the data disk. Values are: Possible values are `Standard_LRS` - The data disk should use standard locally redundant storage. `Premium_LRS` - The data disk should use premium locally redundant storage. Defaults to `Standard_LRS`.

disk_encryption.description: A `disk_encryption` block, as defined below, describes the disk encryption configuration applied on compute nodes in the pool. Disk encryption configuration is not supported on Linux pool created with Virtual Machine Image or Shared Image Gallery Image.

disk_encryption.disk_encryption_target.description: On Linux pool, only \"TemporaryDisk\" is supported; on Windows pool, \"OsDisk\" and \"TemporaryDisk\" must be specified.

display_name.description: Specifies the display name of the Batch pool

endpoint_configuration.backend_port.description: The port number on the compute node. Acceptable values are between `1` and `65535` except for `29876`, `29877` as these are reserved

endpoint_configuration.frontend_port_ran.description: The range of external ports that will be used to provide inbound access to the backendPort on individual compute nodes in the format of `1000-1100`. Acceptable values range between `1` and `65534` except ports from `50000` to `55000` which are reserved by the Batch service. All ranges within a pool must be distinct and cannot overlap. Values must be a range of at least `100` nodes

endpoint_configuration.name.description: The name of the endpoint. The name must be unique within a Batch pool, can contain letters, numbers, underscores, periods, and hyphens. Names must start with a letter or number, must end with a letter, number, or underscore, and cannot exceed 77 characters

endpoint_configuration.network_security_.description: A list of `network_security_group_rules` blocks as defined below that will be applied to the endpoint. The maximum number of rules that can be specified across all the endpoints on a Batch pool is `25`. If no network security group rules are specified, a default rule will be created to allow inbound access to the specified backendPort. Set as documented in the network_security_group_rules block below

endpoint_configuration.protocol.description: The protocol of the endpoint. Acceptable values are `TCP` and `UDP`

extensions.auto_upgrade_minor_version.description: Indicates whether the extension should use a newer minor version if one is available at deployment time. Once deployed, however, the extension will not upgrade minor versions unless redeployed, even with this property set to true.

extensions.automatic_upgrade_enabled.description: Indicates whether the extension should be automatically upgraded by the platform if there is a newer version available. Supported values are `true` and `false`.

extensions.name.description: The name of the virtual machine extension.

extensions.protected_settings.description: JSON formatted protected settings for the extension, the value should be encoded with [`jsonencode`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/functions/jsonencode) function. The extension can contain either `protected_settings` or `provision_after_extensions` or no protected settings at all.

extensions.provision_after_extensions.description: The collection of extension names. Collection of extension names after which this extension needs to be provisioned.

extensions.publisher.description: The name of the extension handler publisher.The name of the extension handler publisher.

extensions.settings_json.description: JSON formatted public settings for the extension, the value should be encoded with [`jsonencode`](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/functions/jsonencode) function.

fixed_scale.description: A `fixed_scale` block that describes the scale settings when using fixed scale as defined below.

fixed_scale.node_deallocation_method.description: It determines what to do with a node and its running task(s) if the pool size is decreasing. Values are `Requeue`, `RetainedData`, `TaskCompletion` and `Terminate`.

fixed_scale.resize_timeout.description: The timeout for resize operations. Defaults to `PT15M`.

fixed_scale.target_dedicated_nodes.description: The number of nodes in the Batch pool. Defaults to `1`.

fixed_scale.target_low_priority_nodes.description: The number of low priority nodes in the Batch pool. Defaults to `0`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Batch Account.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Batch Account. Only possible value is `UserAssigned`.

inter_node_communication.description: Whether the pool permits direct communication between nodes. This imposes restrictions on which nodes can be assigned to the pool. Enabling this value can reduce the chance of the requested number of nodes to be allocated in the pool. Values allowed are `Disabled` and `Enabled`. Defaults to `Enabled`.

license_type.description: The type of on-premises license to be used when deploying the operating system. This only applies to images that contain the Windows operating system, and should only be used when you hold valid on-premises licenses for the nodes which will be deployed. If omitted, no on-premises licensing discount is applied. Values are: "Windows_Server" - The on-premises license is for Windows Server. "Windows_Client" - The on-premises license is for Windows Client.

linux_user_configuration.gid.description: The user ID of the user account. The `uid` and `gid` properties must be specified together or not at all. If not specified the underlying operating system picks the uid.

linux_user_configuration.ssh_private_key.description: The SSH private key for the user account. The private key must not be password protected. The private key is used to automatically configure asymmetric-key based authentication for SSH between nodes in a Linux pool when the pool's enableInterNodeCommunication property is true (it is ignored if enableInterNodeCommunication is false). It does this by placing the key pair into the user's .ssh directory. If not specified, password-less SSH is not configured between nodes (no modification of the user's .ssh directory is done).

linux_user_configuration.uid.description: The group ID for the user account. The `uid` and `gid` properties must be specified together or not at all. If not specified the underlying operating system picks the gid.

max_tasks_per_node.description: Specifies the maximum number of tasks that can run concurrently on a single compute node in the pool. Defaults to `1`

metadata.description: A map of custom batch pool metadata.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Batch pool

network_configuration.description: A `network_configuration` block that describes the network configurations for the Batch pool as defined below

network_configuration.accelerated_networ.description: Whether to enable accelerated networking. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `false`

network_configuration.dynamic_vnet_assig.description: The scope of dynamic vnet assignment. Allowed values: `none`, `job`

network_configuration.endpoint_configura.description: A list of `endpoint_configuration` blocks that can be used to address specific ports on an individual compute node externally as defined below. Set as documented in the inbound_nat_pools block below

network_configuration.public_address_pro.description: Type of public IP address provisioning. Supported values are `BatchManaged`, `UserManaged` and `NoPublicIPAddresses`.

network_configuration.public_ips.description: A list of public IP ids that will be allocated to nodes

network_configuration.subnet_id.description: The ARM resource identifier of the virtual network subnet which the compute nodes of the pool will join

network_security_group_rules.access.description: The action that should be taken for a specified IP address, subnet range or tag. Acceptable values are `Allow` and `Deny`

network_security_group_rules.priority.description: The priority for this rule. The value must be at least `150`

network_security_group_rules.source_addr.description: The source address prefix or tag to match for the rule

network_security_group_rules.source_port.description: The source port ranges to match for the rule. Valid values are `*` (for all ports 0 - 65535) or arrays of ports or port ranges (i.e. `100-200`). The ports should in the range of 0 to 65535 and the port ranges or ports can't overlap. If any other values are provided the request fails with HTTP status code 400. Default value will be `*`

nfs_mount.mount_options.description: Additional command line options to pass to the mount command. These are 'net use' options in Windows and 'mount' options in Linux.

nfs_mount.relative_mount_path.description: The relative path on compute node where the file system will be mounted All file systems are mounted relative to the Batch mounts directory, accessible via the `AZ_BATCH_NODE_MOUNTS_DIR` environment variable.

nfs_mount.source.description: The URI of the file system to mount.

node_agent_sku_id.description: Specifies the SKU of the node agents that will be created in the Batch pool

node_placement.description: A `node_placement` block that describes the placement policy for allocating nodes in the pool as defined below.

node_placement.policy.description: The placement policy for allocating nodes in the pool. Values are: "Regional": All nodes in the pool will be allocated in the same region; "Zonal": Nodes in the pool will be spread across different zones with the best effort balancing. Defaults to `Regional`.

os_disk_placement.description: Specifies the ephemeral disk placement for operating system disk for all VMs in the pool. This property can be used by user in the request to choose which location the operating system should be in. e.g., cache disk space for Ephemeral OS disk provisioning. For more information on Ephemeral OS disk size requirements, please refer to Ephemeral OS disk size requirements for Windows VMs at <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/windows/ephemeral-os-disks#size-requirements> and Linux VMs at <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/linux/ephemeral-os-disks#size-requirements>. The only possible value is `CacheDisk`.

resource_file.auto_storage_container_nam.description: The storage container name in the auto storage account.

resource_file.blob_prefix.description: The blob prefix to use when downloading blobs from an Azure Storage container. Only the blobs whose names begin with the specified prefix will be downloaded. The property is valid only when `auto_storage_container_name` or `storage_container_url` is used. This prefix can be a partial filename or a subdirectory. If a prefix is not specified, all the files in the container will be downloaded.

resource_file.file_mode.description: The file permission mode represented as a string in octal format (e.g. `"0644"`). This property applies only to files being downloaded to Linux compute nodes. It will be ignored if it is specified for a `resource_file` which will be downloaded to a Windows node. If this property is not specified for a Linux node, then a default value of 0770 is applied to the file.

resource_file.file_path.description: The location on the compute node to which to download the file, relative to the task's working directory. If the `http_url` property is specified, the `file_path` is required and describes the path which the file will be downloaded to, including the filename. Otherwise, if the `auto_storage_container_name` or `storage_container_url` property is specified, `file_path` is optional and is the directory to download the files to. In the case where `file_path` is used as a directory, any directory structure already associated with the input data will be retained in full and appended to the specified filePath directory. The specified relative path cannot break out of the task's working directory (for example by using '..').

resource_file.http_url.description: The URL of the file to download. If the URL is Azure Blob Storage, it must be readable using anonymous access; that is, the Batch service does not present any credentials when downloading the blob. There are two ways to get such a URL for a blob in Azure storage: include a Shared Access Signature (SAS) granting read permissions on the blob, or set the ACL for the blob or its container to allow public access.

resource_file.storage_container_url.description: The URL of the blob container within Azure Blob Storage. This URL must be readable and listable using anonymous access; that is, the Batch service does not present any credentials when downloading the blob. There are two ways to get such a URL for a blob in Azure storage: include a Shared Access Signature (SAS) granting read and list permissions on the blob, or set the ACL for the blob or its container to allow public access.

resource_file.user_assigned_identity_id.description: An identity reference from pool's user assigned managed identity list.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Batch pool

security_profile.description: A `security_profile` block that describes the security settings for the Batch pool as defined below

security_profile.host_encryption_enabled.description: Whether to enable host encryption for the Virtual Machine or Virtual Machine Scale Set. This will enable the encryption for all the disks including Resource/Temp disk at host itself. Possible values are `true` and `false`

security_profile.secure_boot_enabled.description: Whether to enable secure boot for the Virtual Machine or Virtual Machine Scale Set. Possible values are `true` and `false`

security_profile.security_type.description: The security type of the Virtual Machine. Possible values are `confidentialVM` and `trustedLaunch`

security_profile.vtpm_enabled.description: Whether to enable virtual trusted platform module (vTPM) for the Virtual Machine or Virtual Machine Scale Set. Possible values are `true` and `false`

start_task.description: A `start_task` block that describes the start task settings for the Batch pool as defined below.

start_task.command_line.description: The command line executed by the start task.

start_task.common_environment_properties.description: A map of strings (key,value) that represents the environment variables to set in the start task.

start_task.container.description: A `container` block is the settings for the container under which the start task runs as defined below. When this is specified, all directories recursively below the `AZ_BATCH_NODE_ROOT_DIR` (the root of Azure Batch directories on the node) are mapped into the container, all task environment variables are mapped into the container, and the task command line is executed in the container.

start_task.resource_file.description: One or more `resource_file` blocks that describe the files to be downloaded to a compute node as defined below.

start_task.task_retry_maximum.description: The number of retry count. If this is set to `0`, the Batch service does not retry Tasks. If this is set to `-1`, the Batch service retries Batch Tasks without limit.

start_task.user_identity.description: A `user_identity` block that describes the user identity under which the start task runs as defined below.

start_task.wait_for_success.description: A flag that indicates if the Batch pool should wait for the start task to be completed. Default to `false`.

stop_pending_resize_operation.description: Whether to stop if there is a pending resize operation on this pool.

storage_image_reference.description: A `storage_image_reference` block for the virtual machines that will compose the Batch pool as defined below

storage_image_reference.id.description: Specifies the ID of the Custom Image which the virtual machines should be created from

storage_image_reference.offer.description: Specifies the offer of the image used to create the virtual machines

storage_image_reference.publisher.description: Specifies the publisher of the image used to create the virtual machines

storage_image_reference.sku.description: Specifies the SKU of the image used to create the virtual machines

storage_image_reference.version.description: Specifies the version of the image used to create the virtual machines

target_node_communication_mode.description: The desired node communication mode for the pool. Possible values are `Classic`, `Default` and `Simplified`.

task_scheduling_policy.description: A `task_scheduling_policy` block that describes how tasks are distributed across compute nodes in a pool as defined below. If not specified, the default is spread as defined below.

task_scheduling_policy.node_fill_type.description: Supported values are "Pack" and "Spread". "Pack" means as many tasks as possible (taskSlotsPerNode) should be assigned to each node in the pool before any tasks are assigned to the next node in the pool. "Spread" means that tasks should be assigned evenly across all nodes in the pool.

user_accounts.description: A `user_accounts` block that describes the list of user accounts to be created on each node in the pool as defined below.

user_accounts.elevation_level.description: The elevation level of the user account. "NonAdmin" - The auto user is a standard user without elevated access. "Admin" - The auto user is a user with elevated access and operates with full Administrator permissions. The default value is nonAdmin.

user_accounts.linux_user_configuration.description: The `linux_user_configuration` block defined below is a linux-specific user configuration for the user account. This property is ignored if specified on a Windows pool. If not specified, the user is created with the default options.

user_accounts.name.description: The name of the user account.

user_accounts.password.description: The password for the user account.

user_accounts.windows_user_configuration.description: The `windows_user_configuration` block defined below is a windows-specific user configuration for the user account. This property can only be specified if the user is on a Windows pool. If not specified and on a Windows pool, the user is created with the default options.

user_identity.auto_user.description: A `auto_user` block that describes the user identity under which the start task runs as defined below.

user_identity.user_name.description: The username to be used by the Batch pool start task.

vm_size.description: Specifies the size of the VM created in the Batch pool

windows.description: A `windows` block that describes the Windows configuration in the pool as defined below.

windows.enable_automatic_updates.description: Whether automatic updates are enabled on the virtual machine. Defaults to `true`.

windows_user_configuration.login_mode.description: Specifies login mode for the user. The default value for VirtualMachineConfiguration pools is interactive mode and for CloudServiceConfiguration pools is batch mode. Values supported are "Batch" and "Interactive".

active.description: Is the cost management export active? Default is `true`.

billing_account_id.description: The id of the billing account on which to create an export

export_data_options.time_frame.description: The time frame for pulling data for the query. If custom, then a specific time period must be provided. Possible values include: `WeekToDate`, `MonthToDate`, `BillingMonthToDate`, `TheLast7Days`, `TheLastMonth`, `TheLastBillingMonth`, `Custom`.

export_data_options.type.description: The type of the query. Possible values are `ActualCost`, `AmortizedCost` and `Usage`.

export_data_storage_location.container_i.description: The Resource Manager ID of the container where exports will be uploaded

export_data_storage_location.root_folder.description: The path of the directory where exports will be uploaded

file_format.description: Format for export. Valid values are `Csv` only. Default is `Csv`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Cost Management Export

recurrence_period_end_date.description: The date the export will stop capturing information.

recurrence_period_start_date.description: The date the export will start capturing information.

recurrence_type.description: How often the requested information will be exported. Valid values include `Annually`, `Daily`, `Monthly`, `Weekly`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Blueprint.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Blueprint. Possible values are `SystemAssigned` and `UserAssigned`.

location.description: The Azure location of the Assignment

lock_exclude_actions.description: a list of up to 200 actions that are permitted to bypass the locks applied by the Blueprint.

lock_exclude_principals.description: a list of up to 5 Principal IDs that are permitted to bypass the locks applied by the Blueprint.

lock_mode.description: The locking mode of the Blueprint Assignment. One of `None` (Default), `AllResourcesReadOnly`, or `AllResourcesDoNotDelete`. Defaults to `None`.

name.description: The name of the Blueprint Assignment

parameter_values.description: a JSON string to supply Blueprint Assignment parameter values.

resource_groups.description: a JSON string to supply the Blueprint Resource Group information.

target_subscription_id.description: The Subscription ID the Blueprint Published Version is to be applied to

version_id.description: The ID of the Published Version of the blueprint to be assigned.

bot_name.description: The name of the Bot Resource this channel will be associated with

location.description: The supported Azure location where the resource exists

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group where the Alexa Channel should be created

skill_id.description: The Alexa skill ID for the Alexa Channel.

bot_name.description: The name of the Bot Resource this channel will be associated with

cognitive_account_id.description: The ID of the Cognitive Account this Bot Channel should be associated with.

cognitive_service_access_key.description: The access key to access the Cognitive Service.

cognitive_service_location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the Cognitive Service resource exists.

custom_speech_model_id.description: The custom speech model id for the Direct Line Speech Channel.

custom_voice_deployment_id.description: The custom voice deployment id for the Direct Line Speech Channel.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group where the Direct Line Speech Channel should be created

bot_name.description: The name of the Bot Resource this channel will be associated with

enabled.description: Enables/Disables this site. Defaults to `true`.

endpoint_parameters_enabled.description: Is the endpoint parameters enabled for this site?

enhanced_authentication_enabled.description: Enables additional security measures for this site, see [Enhanced Directline Authentication Features](https://blog.botframework.com/2018/09/25/enhanced-direct-line-authentication-features). Disabled by default.

location.description: The supported Azure location where the resource exists

name.description: The name of the site

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Bot Channel

site.description: A site represents a client application that you want to connect to your bot. One or more `site` blocks as defined below.

storage_enabled.description: Is the storage site enabled for detailed logging? Defaults to `true`.

trusted_origins.description: This field is required when `is_secure_site_enabled` is enabled. Determines which origins can establish a Directline conversation for this site.

user_upload_enabled.description: Is the user upload enabled for this site? Defaults to `true`.

v1_allowed.description: Enables v1 of the Directline protocol for this site. Defaults to `true`.

v3_allowed.description: Enables v3 of the Directline protocol for this site. Defaults to `true`.

bot_name.description: The name of the Bot Resource this channel will be associated with

email_address.description: The email address that the Bot will authenticate with.

email_password.description: The email password that the Bot will authenticate with.

location.description: The supported Azure location where the resource exists

magic_code.description: The magic code used to set up OAUTH authentication.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Bot Channel

bot_name.description: The name of the Bot Resource this channel will be associated with

facebook_application_id.description: The Facebook Application ID for the Facebook Channel.

facebook_application_secret.description: The Facebook Application Secret for the Facebook Channel.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

page.description: One or more `page` blocks as defined below.

page.access_token.description: The Facebook Page Access Token for the Facebook Channel.

page.id.description: The Facebook Page ID for the Facebook Channel.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group where the Facebook Channel should be created

bot_name.description: The name of the Bot Resource this channel will be associated with

line_channel.description: One or more `line_channel` blocks as defined below.

line_channel.access_token.description: The access token which is used to call the Line Channel API.

line_channel.secret.description: The secret which is used to access the Line Channel.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group where the Line Channel should be created

bot_name.description: The name of the Bot Resource this channel will be associated with

calling_enabled.description: Specifies whether to enable Microsoft Teams channel calls. This defaults to `false`.

calling_web_hook.description: Specifies the webhook for Microsoft Teams channel calls.

capacity_reservation_group_id.description: The ID of the Capacity Reservation Group where the Capacity Reservation exists

name.description: Specifies the name of this Capacity Reservation

sku.description: A `sku` block as defined below.

sku.capacity.description: Specifies the number of instances to be reserved. It must be greater than or equal to `0` and not exceed the quota in the subscription.

sku.name.description: Name of the sku, such as `Standard_F2`

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

zone.description: Specifies the Availability Zone for this Capacity Reservation

location.description: The Azure location where the Capacity Reservation Group exists

name.description: Specifies the name of this Capacity Reservation Group

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the resource group the Capacity Reservation Group is located in

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

zones.description: Specifies a list of Availability Zones for this Capacity Reservation Group

cache_expiration_action.behavior.description: The behavior of the cache. Valid values are `BypassCache`, `Override` and `SetIfMissing`.

cache_expiration_action.duration.description: Duration of the cache. Only allowed when `behavior` is set to `Override` or `SetIfMissing`. Format: `[d.]hh:mm:ss`

cache_key_query_string_action.behavior.description: The behavior of the cache key for query strings. Valid values are `Exclude`, `ExcludeAll`, `Include` and `IncludeAll`.

cache_key_query_string_action.parameters.description: Comma separated list of parameter values.

content_types_to_compress.description: An array of strings that indicates a content types on which compression will be applied. The value for the elements should be MIME types.

cookies_condition.match_values.description: List of values for the cookie. This is required if `operator` is not `Any`.

cookies_condition.transforms.description: A list of transforms. Valid values are `Lowercase` and `Uppercase`.

delivery_rule.description: Rules for the rules engine. An endpoint can contain up until 4 of those rules that consist of conditions and actions. A `delivery_rule` blocks as defined below.

delivery_rule.name.description: The Name which should be used for this Delivery Rule.

delivery_rule.order.description: The order used for this rule. The order values should be sequential and begin at `1`.

geo_filter.description: A set of Geo Filters for this CDN Endpoint. Each `geo_filter` block supports fields documented below.

geo_filter.action.description: The Action of the Geo Filter. Possible values include `Allow` and `Block`.

geo_filter.country_codes.description: A List of two letter country codes (e.g. `US`, `GB`) to be associated with this Geo Filter.

geo_filter.relative_path.description: The relative path applicable to geo filter.

global_delivery_rule.description: Actions that are valid for all resources regardless of any conditions. A `global_delivery_rule` block as defined below.

is_compression_enabled.description: Indicates whether compression is to be enabled.

is_http_allowed.description: Specifies if http allowed. Defaults to `true`.

is_https_allowed.description: Specifies if https allowed. Defaults to `true`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

modify_request_header_action.action.description: Action to be executed on a header value. Valid values are `Append`, `Delete` and `Overwrite`.

modify_request_header_action.value.description: The value of the header. Only needed when `action` is set to `Append` or `overwrite`.

modify_response_header_action.action.description: Action to be executed on a header value. Valid values are `Append`, `Delete` and `Overwrite`.

modify_response_header_action.value.description: The value of the header. Only needed when `action` is set to `Append` or `overwrite`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the CDN Endpoint

optimization_type.description: What types of optimization should this CDN Endpoint optimize for? Possible values include `DynamicSiteAcceleration`, `GeneralMediaStreaming`, `GeneralWebDelivery`, `LargeFileDownload` and `VideoOnDemandMediaStreaming`.

origin.description: The set of origins of the CDN endpoint. When multiple origins exist, the first origin will be used as primary and rest will be used as failover options. Each `origin` block supports fields documented below

origin.host_name.description: A string that determines the hostname/IP address of the origin server. This string can be a domain name, Storage Account endpoint, Web App endpoint, IPv4 address or IPv6 address

origin.http_port.description: The HTTP port of the origin. Defaults to `80`

origin.https_port.description: The HTTPS port of the origin. Defaults to `443`

origin.name.description: The name of the origin. This is an arbitrary value. However, this value needs to be unique under the endpoint

origin_host_header.description: The host header CDN provider will send along with content requests to origins.

origin_path.description: The path used at for origin requests.

post_arg_condition.match_values.description: List of string values. This is required if `operator` is not `Any`.

post_arg_condition.transforms.description: A list of transforms. Valid values are `Lowercase` and `Uppercase`.

probe_path.description: the path to a file hosted on the origin which helps accelerate delivery of the dynamic content and calculate the most optimal routes for the CDN. This is relative to the `origin_path`.

profile_name.description: The CDN Profile to which to attach the CDN Endpoint

query_string_condition.match_values.description: List of string values. This is required if `operator` is not `Any`.

query_string_condition.transforms.description: A list of transforms. Valid values are `Lowercase` and `Uppercase`.

querystring_caching_behaviour.description: Sets query string caching behavior. Allowed values are `IgnoreQueryString`, `BypassCaching` and `UseQueryString`. `NotSet` value can be used for `Premium Verizon` CDN profile. Defaults to `IgnoreQueryString`.

remote_address_condition.match_values.description: List of string values. For `GeoMatch` `operator` this should be a list of country codes (e.g. `US` or `DE`). List of IP address if `operator` equals to `IPMatch`. This is required if `operator` is not `Any`.

request_body_condition.match_values.description: List of string values. This is required if `operator` is not `Any`.

request_body_condition.transforms.description: A list of transforms. Valid values are `Lowercase` and `Uppercase`.

request_header_condition.match_values.description: List of header values. This is required if `operator` is not `Any`.

request_header_condition.transforms.description: A list of transforms. Valid values are `Lowercase` and `Uppercase`.

request_uri_condition.match_values.description: List of string values. This is required if `operator` is not `Any`.

request_uri_condition.transforms.description: A list of transforms. Valid values are `Lowercase` and `Uppercase`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the CDN Endpoint

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

url_file_extension_condition.match_value.description: List of string values. This is required if `operator` is not `Any`.

url_file_extension_condition.transforms.description: A list of transforms. Valid values are `Lowercase` and `Uppercase`.

url_file_name_condition.match_values.description: List of string values. This is required if `operator` is not `Any`.

url_file_name_condition.transforms.description: A list of transforms. Valid values are `Lowercase` and `Uppercase`.

url_path_condition.match_values.description: List of string values. This is required if `operator` is not `Any`.

url_path_condition.transforms.description: A list of transforms. Valid values are `Lowercase` and `Uppercase`.

url_redirect_action.fragment.description: Specifies the fragment part of the URL. This value must not start with a `#`.

url_redirect_action.hostname.description: Specifies the hostname part of the URL.

url_redirect_action.path.description: Specifies the path part of the URL. This value must begin with a `/`.

url_redirect_action.protocol.description: Specifies the protocol part of the URL. Valid values are `MatchRequest`, `Http` and `Https`. Defaults to `MatchRequest`.

url_redirect_action.query_string.description: Specifies the query string part of the URL. This value must not start with a `?` or `&` and must be in `<key>=<value>` format separated by `&`.

url_redirect_action.redirect_type.description: Type of the redirect. Valid values are `Found`, `Moved`, `PermanentRedirect` and `TemporaryRedirect`.

url_rewrite_action.destination.description: This value must start with a `/` and can't be longer than 260 characters.

url_rewrite_action.preserve_unmatched_pa.description: Whether preserve an unmatched path. Defaults to `true`.

url_rewrite_action.source_pattern.description: This value must start with a `/` and can't be longer than 260 characters.

cdn_endpoint_id.description: The ID of the CDN Endpoint. Changing this forces a new CDN Endpoint Custom Domain to be created.

cdn_managed_https.certificate_type.description: The type of HTTPS certificate. Possible values are `Shared` and `Dedicated`.

cdn_managed_https.protocol_type.description: The type of protocol. Possible values are `ServerNameIndication` and `IPBased`.

cdn_managed_https.tls_version.description: The minimum TLS protocol version that is used for HTTPS. Possible values are `TLS10` (representing TLS 1.0/1.1), `TLS12` (representing TLS 1.2) and `None` (representing no minimums). Defaults to `TLS12`.

host_name.description: The host name of the custom domain. Changing this forces a new CDN Endpoint Custom Domain to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this CDN Endpoint Custom Domain. Changing this forces a new CDN Endpoint Custom Domain to be created.

user_managed_https.key_vault_secret_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault Secret that contains the HTTPS certificate.

user_managed_https.tls_version.description: The minimum TLS protocol version that is used for HTTPS. Possible values are `TLS10` (representing TLS 1.0/1.1), `TLS12` (representing TLS 1.2) and `None` (representing no minimums). Defaults to `TLS12`.

cdn_frontdoor_profile_id.description: The ID of the Front Door Profile. Changing this forces a new Front Door Custom Domain to be created.

dns_zone_id.description: The ID of the Azure DNS Zone which should be used for this Front Door Custom Domain. If you are using Azure to host your [DNS domains](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/dns/dns-overview), you must delegate the domain provider's domain name system (DNS) to an Azure DNS Zone. For more information, see [Delegate a domain to Azure DNS](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/dns/dns-delegate-domain-azure-dns). Otherwise, if you're using your own domain provider to handle your DNS, you must validate the Front Door Custom Domain by creating the DNS TXT records manually.

host_name.description: The host name of the domain. The `host_name` field must be the FQDN of your domain(e.g. `contoso.fabrikam.com`). Changing this forces a new Front Door Custom Domain to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Front Door Custom Domain. Possible values must be between 2 and 260 characters in length, must begin with a letter or number, end with a letter or number and contain only letters, numbers and hyphens. Changing this forces a new Front Door Custom Domain to be created.

tls.cdn_frontdoor_secret_id.description: Resource ID of the Front Door Secret.

tls.certificate_type.description: Defines the source of the SSL certificate. Possible values include `CustomerCertificate` and `ManagedCertificate`. Defaults to `ManagedCertificate`.

tls.minimum_tls_version.description: TLS protocol version that will be used for Https. Possible values are `TLS12`. Defaults to `TLS12`.

cdn_frontdoor_custom_domain_id.description: The ID of the Front Door Custom Domain that should be managed by the association resource. Changing this forces a new association resource to be created.

cdn_frontdoor_route_ids.description: One or more IDs of the Front Door Route to which the Front Door Custom Domain is associated with.

cdn_frontdoor_profile_id.description: The ID of the Front Door Profile within which this Front Door Endpoint should exist. Changing this forces a new Front Door Endpoint to be created.

enabled.description: Specifies if this Front Door Endpoint is enabled? Defaults to `true`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Front Door Endpoint. Changing this forces a new Front Door Endpoint to be created.

tags.description: Specifies a mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Front Door Endpoint.

captcha_cookie_expiration_in_minutes.description: Specifies the Captcha cookie lifetime in minutes. Possible values are between `5` and `1440`. Defaults to`30` minutes.

custom_block_response_body.description: If a `custom_rule` block's action type is `block`, this is the response body. The body must be specified in base64 encoding.

custom_block_response_status_code.description: If a `custom_rule` block's action type is `block`, this is the response status code. Possible values are `200`, `403`, `405`, `406`, or `429`.

custom_rule.description: One or more `custom_rule` blocks as defined below.

custom_rule.action.description: The action to perform when the rule is matched. Possible values are `Allow`, `Block`, `Log`, `Redirect`, `JSChallenge`, or `CAPTCHA`.

custom_rule.enabled.description: Is the rule is enabled or disabled? Defaults to `true`.

custom_rule.match_condition.description: One or more `match_condition` block defined below. Can support up to `10` `match_condition` blocks.

custom_rule.name.description: Gets name of the resource that is unique within a policy. This name can be used to access the resource.

custom_rule.priority.description: The priority of the rule. Rules with a lower value will be evaluated before rules with a higher value. Defaults to `1`.

custom_rule.rate_limit_duration_in_minut.description: The rate limit duration in minutes. Defaults to `1`.

custom_rule.rate_limit_threshold.description: The rate limit threshold. Defaults to `10`.

custom_rule.type.description: The type of rule. Possible values are `MatchRule` or `RateLimitRule`.

enabled.description: Is the Front Door Firewall Policy enabled? Defaults to `true`.

exclusion.match_variable.description: The variable type to be excluded. Possible values are `QueryStringArgNames`, `RequestBodyPostArgNames`, `RequestCookieNames`, `RequestHeaderNames`, `RequestBodyJsonArgNames`

exclusion.operator.description: Comparison operator to apply to the selector when specifying which elements in the collection this exclusion applies to. Possible values are: `Equals`, `Contains`, `StartsWith`, `EndsWith`, or `EqualsAny`.

exclusion.selector.description: Selector for the value in the `match_variable` attribute this exclusion applies to.

js_challenge_cookie_expiration_in_minute.description: Specifies the JavaScript challenge cookie lifetime in minutes, after which the user will be revalidated. Possible values are between `5` to `1440` minutes. Defaults to `30` minutes.

log_scrubbing.enabled.description: Is log scrubbing enabled? Possible values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `true`.

log_scrubbing.scrubbing_rule.description: One or more `scrubbing_rule` blocks as defined below.

managed_rule.description: One or more `managed_rule` blocks as defined below.

managed_rule.action.description: The action to perform for all default rule set rules when the managed rule is matched or when the anomaly score is 5 or greater depending on which version of the default rule set you are using. Possible values include `Allow`, `Log`, `Block`, or `Redirect`.

managed_rule.exclusion.description: One or more `exclusion` blocks as defined below.

managed_rule.override.description: One or more `override` blocks as defined below.

managed_rule.type.description: The name of the managed rule to use with this resource. Possible values include `DefaultRuleSet`, `Microsoft_DefaultRuleSet`, `BotProtection`, or `Microsoft_BotManagerRuleSet`.

managed_rule.version.description: The version of the managed rule to use with this resource. Possible values depends on which default rule set type you are using, for the `DefaultRuleSet` type the possible values include `1.0` or `preview-0.1`. For `Microsoft_DefaultRuleSet` the possible values include `1.1`, `2.0`, or `2.1`. For `BotProtection` the value must be `preview-0.1` and for `Microsoft_BotManagerRuleSet` the possible values include `1.0` and `1.1`.

match_condition.match_values.description: Up to `600` possible values to match. Limit is in total across all `match_condition` blocks and `match_values` arguments. String value itself can be up to `256` characters in length.

match_condition.match_variable.description: The request variable to compare with. Possible values are `Cookies`, `PostArgs`, `QueryString`, `RemoteAddr`, `RequestBody`, `RequestHeader`, `RequestMethod`, `RequestUri`, or `SocketAddr`.

match_condition.negation_condition.description: Should the result of the condition be negated.

match_condition.operator.description: Comparison type to use for matching with the variable value. Possible values are `Any`, `BeginsWith`, `Contains`, `EndsWith`, `Equal`, `GeoMatch`, `GreaterThan`, `GreaterThanOrEqual`, `IPMatch`, `LessThan`, `LessThanOrEqual`, or `RegEx`.

match_condition.selector.description: Match against a specific key if the `match_variable` is `QueryString`, `PostArgs`, `RequestHeader`, or `Cookies`.

match_condition.transforms.description: Up to `5` transforms to apply. Possible values are `Lowercase`, `RemoveNulls`, `Trim`, `Uppercase`, `URLDecode`, or `URLEncode`.

mode.description: The Front Door Firewall Policy mode. Possible values are `Detection`, `Prevention`.

name.description: The name of the policy

override.exclusion.description: One or more `exclusion` blocks as defined below.

override.rule.description: One or more `rule` blocks as defined below. If none are specified, all of the rules in the group will be disabled.

override.rule_group_name.description: The managed rule group to override.

redirect_url.description: If action type is redirect, this field represents redirect URL for the client.

request_body_check_enabled.description: Should policy managed rules inspect the request body content? Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group

rule.action.description: The action to be applied when the managed rule matches or when the anomaly score is 5 or greater. Possible values are `Allow`, `CAPTCHA`, `Log`, `Block`, `Redirect`, `AnomalyScoring` and `JSChallenge`.

rule.enabled.description: Is the managed rule override enabled or disabled. Defaults to `false`

rule.exclusion.description: One or more `exclusion` blocks as defined below.

scrubbing_rule.match_variable.description: The variable to be scrubbed from the logs. Possible values include `QueryStringArgNames`, `RequestBodyJsonArgNames`, `RequestBodyPostArgNames`, `RequestCookieNames`, `RequestHeaderNames`, `RequestIPAddress`, or `RequestUri`.

scrubbing_rule.operator.description: When the `match_variable` is a collection, operate on the `selector` to specify which elements in the collection this `scrubbing_rule` applies to. Possible values are `Equals` or `EqualsAny`. Defaults to `Equals`.

scrubbing_rule.selector.description: When the `match_variable` is a collection, the `operator` is used to specify which elements in the collection this `scrubbing_rule` applies to.

sku_name.description: The sku's pricing tier for this Front Door Firewall Policy. Possible values include `Standard_AzureFrontDoor` or `Premium_AzureFrontDoor`

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the Front Door Firewall Policy.

cdn_frontdoor_origin_group_id.description: The ID of the Front Door Origin Group within which this Front Door Origin should exist. Changing this forces a new Front Door Origin to be created.

certificate_name_check_enabled.description: Specifies whether certificate name checks are enabled for this origin.

enabled.description: Should the origin be enabled? Possible values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `true`.

host_name.description: The IPv4 address, IPv6 address or Domain name of the Origin.

http_port.description: The value of the HTTP port. Must be between `1` and `65535`. Defaults to `80`.

https_port.description: The value of the HTTPS port. Must be between `1` and `65535`. Defaults to `443`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Front Door Origin. Changing this forces a new Front Door Origin to be created.

origin_host_header.description: The host header value (an IPv4 address, IPv6 address or Domain name) which is sent to the origin with each request. If unspecified the hostname from the request will be used.

priority.description: Priority of origin in given origin group for load balancing. Higher priorities will not be used for load balancing if any lower priority origin is healthy. Must be between `1` and `5` (inclusive). Defaults to `1`.

private_link.location.description: Specifies the location where the Private Link resource should exist

private_link.private_link_target_id.description: The ID of the Azure Resource to connect to via the Private Link.

private_link.request_message.description: Specifies the request message that will be submitted to the `private_link_target_id` when requesting the private link endpoint connection. Values must be between `1` and `140` characters in length. Defaults to `Access request for CDN FrontDoor Private Link Origin`.

private_link.target_type.description: Specifies the type of target for this Private Link Endpoint. Possible values are `blob`, `blob_secondary`, `Gateway`, `managedEnvironments`, `sites`, `web` and `web_secondary`.

weight.description: The weight of the origin in a given origin group for load balancing. Must be between `1` and `1000`. Defaults to `500`.

cdn_frontdoor_profile_id.description: The ID of the Front Door Profile within which this Front Door Origin Group should exist. Changing this forces a new Front Door Origin Group to be created.

health_probe.interval_in_seconds.description: Specifies the number of seconds between health probes. Possible values are between `1` and `255` seconds (inclusive).

health_probe.path.description: Specifies the path relative to the origin that is used to determine the health of the origin. Defaults to `/`.

health_probe.protocol.description: Specifies the protocol to use for health probe. Possible values are `Http` and `Https`.

health_probe.request_type.description: Specifies the type of health probe request that is made. Possible values are `GET` and `HEAD`. Defaults to `HEAD`.

load_balancing.additional_latency_in_mil.description: Specifies the additional latency in milliseconds for probes to fall into the lowest latency bucket. Possible values are between `0` and `1000` milliseconds (inclusive). Defaults to `50`.

load_balancing.sample_size.description: Specifies the number of samples to consider for load balancing decisions. Possible values are between `0` and `255` (inclusive). Defaults to `4`.

load_balancing.successful_samples_requir.description: Specifies the number of samples within the sample period that must succeed. Possible values are between `0` and `255` (inclusive). Defaults to `3`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Front Door Origin Group. Changing this forces a new Front Door Origin Group to be created.

restore_traffic_time_to_healed_or_new_en.description: Specifies the amount of time which should elapse before shifting traffic to another endpoint when a healthy endpoint becomes unhealthy or a new endpoint is added. Possible values are between `0` and `50` minutes (inclusive). Default is `10` minutes.

session_affinity_enabled.description: Specifies whether session affinity should be enabled on this host. Defaults to `true`.

Possible values include `application/eot`, `application/font`, `application/font-sfnt`, `application/javascript`, `application/json`, `application/opentype`, `application/otf`, `application/pkcs7-mime`, `application/truetype`, `application/ttf`, `application/vnd.ms-fontobject`, `application/xhtml+xml`, `application/xml`, `application/xml+rss`, `application/x-font-opentype`, `application/x-font-truetype`, `application/x-font-ttf`, `application/x-httpd-cgi`, `application/x-mpegurl`, `application/x-opentype`, `application/x-otf`, `application/x-perl`, `application/x-ttf`, `application/x-javascript`, `font/eot`, `font/ttf`, `font/otf`, `font/opentype`, `image/svg+xml`, `text/css`, `text/csv`, `text/html`, `text/javascript`, `text/js`, `text/plain`, `text/richtext`, `text/tab-separated-values`, `text/xml`, `text/x-script`, `text/x-component` or `text/x-java-source`.

route_configuration_override_action.cach.description: `HonorOrigin` the Front Door will always honor origin response header directive. If the origin directive is missing, Front Door will cache contents anywhere from `1` to `3` days. `OverrideAlways` the TTL value returned from your Front Door Origin is overwritten with the value specified in the action. This behavior will only be applied if the response is cacheable. `OverrideIfOriginMissing` if no TTL value gets returned from your Front Door Origin, the rule sets the TTL to the value specified in the action. This behavior will only be applied if the response is cacheable. `Disabled` the Front Door will not cache the response contents, irrespective of Front Door Origin response directives. Possible values include `HonorOrigin`, `OverrideAlways`, `OverrideIfOriginMissing` or `Disabled`.

When Cache behavior is set to `Override` or `SetIfMissing`, this field specifies the cache duration to use. The maximum duration is 366 days specified in the `d.HH:MM:SS` format(e.g. `365.23:59:59`). If the desired maximum cache duration is less than 1 day then the maximum cache duration should be specified in the `HH:MM:SS` format(e.g. `23:59:59`).

route_configuration_override_action.cdn_.description: The Front Door Origin Group resource ID that the request should be routed to. This overrides the configuration specified in the Front Door Endpoint route.

route_configuration_override_action.comp.description: Should the Front Door dynamically compress the content? Possible values include `true` or `false`.

route_configuration_override_action.forw.description: The forwarding protocol the request will be redirected as. This overrides the configuration specified in the route to be associated with. Possible values include `MatchRequest`, `HttpOnly` or `HttpsOnly`.

route_configuration_override_action.quer.description: `IncludeSpecifiedQueryStrings` query strings specified in the `query_string_parameters` field get included when the cache key gets generated. `UseQueryString` cache every unique URL, each unique URL will have its own cache key. `IgnoreSpecifiedQueryStrings` query strings specified in the `query_string_parameters` field get excluded when the cache key gets generated. `IgnoreQueryString` query strings aren't considered when the cache key gets generated. Possible values include `IgnoreQueryString`, `UseQueryString`, `IgnoreSpecifiedQueryStrings` or `IncludeSpecifiedQueryStrings`.
A list of query string parameter names.

server_port_condition.match_values.description: A list of one or more integer values(e.g. "1") representing the value of the client port to match. Possible values include `80` or `443`. If multiple values are specified, they're evaluated using `OR` logic.

server_port_condition.negate_condition.description: If `true` operator becomes the opposite of its value. Possible values `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`. Details can be found in the `Condition Operator List` below.

ssl_protocol_condition.negate_condition.description: If `true` operator becomes the opposite of its value. Possible values `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`. Details can be found in the `Condition Operator List` below.

url_file_extension_condition.negate_cond.description: If `true` operator becomes the opposite of its value. Possible values `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`. Details can be found in the `Condition Operator List` below.

url_path_condition.negate_condition.description: If `true` operator becomes the opposite of its value. Possible values `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`. Details can be found in the `Condition Operator List` below.

url_redirect_action.destination_fragment.description: The fragment to use in the redirect. The value must be a string between `0` and `1024` characters in length, leave blank to preserve the incoming fragment. Defaults to `""`.

url_redirect_action.destination_hostname.description: The host name you want the request to be redirected to. The value must be a string between `0` and `2048` characters in length, leave blank to preserve the incoming host.

url_redirect_action.destination_path.description: The path to use in the redirect. The value must be a string and include the leading `/`, leave blank to preserve the incoming path. Defaults to `""`.

url_redirect_action.query_string.description: The query string used in the redirect URL. The value must be in the <key>=<value> or <key>={`action_server_variable`} format and must not include the leading `?`, leave blank to preserve the incoming query string. Maximum allowed length for this field is `2048` characters. Defaults to `""`.

url_redirect_action.redirect_protocol.description: The protocol the request will be redirected as. Possible values include `MatchRequest`, `Http` or `Https`. Defaults to `MatchRequest`.

url_redirect_action.redirect_type.description: The response type to return to the requestor. Possible values include `Moved`, `Found` , `TemporaryRedirect` or `PermanentRedirect`.

url_rewrite_action.destination.description: The destination path to use in the rewrite. The destination path overwrites the source pattern.

url_rewrite_action.preserve_unmatched_pa.description: Append the remaining path after the source pattern to the new destination path? Possible values `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`.

url_rewrite_action.source_pattern.description: The source pattern in the URL path to replace. This uses prefix-based matching. For example, to match all URL paths use a forward slash `"/"` as the source pattern value.

custom_question_answering_search_service.description: If `kind` is `TextAnalytics` this specifies the ID of the Search service.
If `kind` is `TextAnalytics` this specifies the key of the Search service.

custom_subdomain_name.description: The subdomain name used for Entra ID token-based authentication. This attribute is required when `network_acls` is specified. This attribute is also required when using the OpenAI service with libraries which assume the Azure OpenAI endpoint is a subdomain on `https://openai.azure.com/`, eg. `https://<custom_subdomain_name>.openai.azure.com/`. This can be specified during creation or added later, but once set

customer_managed_key.identity_client_id.description: The Client ID of the User Assigned Identity that has access to the key. This property only needs to be specified when there're multiple identities attached to the Cognitive Account.

customer_managed_key.key_vault_key_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault Key which should be used to Encrypt the data in this Cognitive Account.

dynamic_throttling_enabled.description: Whether to enable the dynamic throttling for this Cognitive Service Account. This attribute cannot be set when the `kind` is `OpenAI` or `AIServices`.

fqdns.description: List of FQDNs allowed for the Cognitive Account.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Cognitive Account.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Cognitive Account. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

kind.description: Specifies the type of Cognitive Service Account that should be created. Possible values are `Academic`, `AIServices`, `AnomalyDetector`, `Bing.Autosuggest`, `Bing.Autosuggest.v7`, `Bing.CustomSearch`, `Bing.Search`, `Bing.Search.v7`, `Bing.Speech`, `Bing.SpellCheck`, `Bing.SpellCheck.v7`, `CognitiveServices`, `ComputerVision`, `ContentModerator`, `ContentSafety`, `CustomSpeech`, `CustomVision.Prediction`, `CustomVision.Training`, `Emotion`, `Face`, `FormRecognizer`, `ImmersiveReader`, `LUIS`, `LUIS.Authoring`, `MetricsAdvisor`, `OpenAI`, `Personalizer`, `QnAMaker`, `Recommendations`, `SpeakerRecognition`, `Speech`, `SpeechServices`, `SpeechTranslation`, `TextAnalytics`, `TextTranslation` and `WebLM`

local_auth_enabled.description: Whether local authentication methods is enabled for the Cognitive Account. Defaults to `true`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

metrics_advisor_aad_client_id.description: The Azure AD Client ID (Application ID). This attribute is only set when kind is `MetricsAdvisor`

metrics_advisor_aad_tenant_id.description: The Azure AD Tenant ID. This attribute is only set when kind is `MetricsAdvisor`

metrics_advisor_super_user_name.description: The super user of Metrics Advisor. This attribute is only set when kind is `MetricsAdvisor`

metrics_advisor_website_name.description: The website name of Metrics Advisor. This attribute is only set when kind is `MetricsAdvisor`

name.description: Specifies the name of the Cognitive Service Account

network_acls.description: A `network_acls` block as defined below. When this property is specified, `custom_subdomain_name` is also required to be set.

network_acls.bypass.description: Whether to allow trusted Azure Services to access the service. Possible values are `None` and `AzureServices`.

network_acls.default_action.description: The Default Action to use when no rules match from `ip_rules` / `virtual_network_rules`. Possible values are `Allow` and `Deny`.

network_acls.ip_rules.description: One or more IP Addresses, or CIDR Blocks which should be able to access the Cognitive Account.

network_injection.description: A `network_injection` block as defined below. Only applicable if the `kind` is set to `AIServices`.

network_injection.scenario.description: Specifies what features network injection applies to. The only possible value is `agent`.

network_injection.subnet_id.description: The ID of the subnet which the Agent Client is injected into.

outbound_network_access_restricted.description: Whether outbound network access is restricted for the Cognitive Account. Defaults to `false`.

project_management_enabled.description: Whether project management is enabled. Can only be set to `true` when `kind` is set to `AIServices`. Once enabled, disabling `project_management_enabled`

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether public network access is allowed for the Cognitive Account. Defaults to `true`.

qna_runtime_endpoint.description: A URL to link a QnAMaker cognitive account to a QnA runtime.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which the Cognitive Service Account is created

sku_name.description: Specifies the SKU Name for this Cognitive Service Account. Possible values are `C2`, `C3`, `C4`, `D3`, `DC0`, `E0`, `F0`, `F1`, `P0`, `P1`, `P2`, `S`, `S0`, `S1`, `S2`, `S3`, `S4`, `S5` and `S6`.

storage.identity_client_id.description: The client ID of the managed identity associated with the storage resource.

storage.storage_account_id.description: Full resource id of a Microsoft.Storage resource.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

virtual_network_rules.ignore_missing_vne.description: Whether ignore missing vnet service endpoint or not. Defaults to `false`.

virtual_network_rules.subnet_id.description: The ID of the subnet which should be able to access this Cognitive Account.

key_vault_key_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault Key which should be used to Encrypt the data in this Cognitive Account.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Cognitive Account Project. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

name.description: The name of the Cognitive Account Project

cognitive_account_id.description: The ID of the Cognitive Services Account. Changing this forces a new Cognitive Account Rai Blocklist to be created.

description.description: A short description for the Cognitive Account Rai Blocklist.

name.description: The name of the Cognitive Account Rai Blocklist. Changing this forces a new Cognitive Account Rai Blocklist to be created.

cognitive_account_id.description: The ID of the Cognitive Service Account to which this RAI Policy should be associated

content_filter.block_enabled.description: Whether the filter should block content. Possible values are `true` or `false`.

content_filter.filter_enabled.description: Whether the filter is enabled. Possible values are `true` or `false`.

content_filter.name.description: The name of the content filter.

content_filter.severity_threshold.description: The severity threshold for the filter. Possible values are `Low`, `Medium` or `High`.

content_filter.source.description: Content source to apply the content filter. Possible values are `Prompt` or `Completion`.

mode.description: The mode of the RAI Policy. Possible values are `Default`, `Deferred`, `Blocking` or `Asynchronous_filter`.

name.description: The name of the Cognitive Services Account Deployment

sku.description: A `sku` block as defined below.

sku.capacity.description: Tokens-per-Minute (TPM). The unit of measure for this field is in the thousands of Tokens-per-Minute. Defaults to `1` which means that the limitation is `1000` tokens per minute. If the resources SKU supports scale in/out then the capacity field should be included in the resources' configuration. If the scale in/out is not supported by the resources SKU then this field can be safely omitted. For more information about TPM please see the [product documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/ai-services/openai/how-to/quota?tabs=rest).

sku.family.description: If the service has different generations of hardware, for the same SKU, then that can be captured here

sku.name.description: The name of the SKU. Possible values include `Standard`, `DataZoneBatch`, `DataZoneStandard`, `DataZoneProvisionedManaged`, `GlobalBatch`, `GlobalProvisionedManaged`, `GlobalStandard`, and `ProvisionedManaged`

sku.size.description: The SKU size. When the name field is the combination of tier and some other value, this would be the standalone code

sku.tier.description: Possible values are `Free`, `Basic`, `Standard`, `Premium`, `Enterprise`. This property is required only when multiple tiers are available with the SKU name

version_upgrade_option.description: Deployment model version upgrade option. Possible values are `OnceNewDefaultVersionAvailable`, `OnceCurrentVersionExpired`, and `NoAutoUpgrade`. Defaults to `OnceNewDefaultVersionAvailable`.

data_location.description: The location where the Communication service stores its data at rest. Possible values are `Africa`, `Asia Pacific`, `Australia`, `Brazil`, `Canada`, `Europe`, `France`, `Germany`, `India`, `Japan`, `Korea`, `Norway`, `Switzerland`, `UAE`, `UK`, `usgov` and `United States`. notification.enabled.description: Should the notification be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

notification.operator.description: The comparison operator for the notification. Must be one of `EqualTo`, `GreaterThan`, or `GreaterThanOrEqualTo`.

notification.threshold.description: Threshold value associated with a notification. Notification is sent when the cost exceeded the threshold. It is always percent and has to be between 0 and 1000.

notification.threshold_type.description: The type of threshold for the notification. This determines whether the notification is triggered by forecasted costs or actual costs. The allowed values are `Actual` and `Forecasted`. Default is `Actual`.

time_period.end_date.description: The end date for the budget. If not set this will be 10 years after the start date.

time_period.start_date.description: The start date for the budget. The start date must be first of the month and should be less than the end date. Budget start date must be on or after June 1, 2017. authentication.secret_name.description: The name of the Container App Secret to use for this Scale Rule Authentication.

authentication.trigger_parameter.description: The Trigger Parameter name to use the supply the value retrieved from the `secret_name`.
