azure_queue_scale_rule.queue_length.description: The value of the length of the queue to trigger scaling actions.

azure_queue_scale_rule.queue_name.description: The name of the Azure Queue

container.args.description: A list of extra arguments to pass to the container.

container.command.description: A command to pass to the container to override the default. This is provided as a list of command line elements without spaces.

container.cpu.description: The amount of vCPU to allocate to the container.

container.env.description: One or more `env` blocks as detailed below.

container.image.description: The image to use to create the container.

container.memory.description: The amount of memory to allocate to the container.

container_app_environment_id.description: The ID of the Container App Environment within which this Container App should exist

cors.allow_credentials_enabled.description: Whether user credentials are allowed in the cross-origin request is enabled. Defaults to `false`.

cors.allowed_headers.description: Specifies the list of request headers that are permitted in the actual request.

cors.allowed_methods.description: Specifies the list of HTTP methods are allowed when accessing the resource in a cross-origin request.

cors.allowed_origins.description: Specifies the list of origins that are allowed to make cross-origin calls.

cors.exposed_headers.description: Specifies the list of headers exposed to the browser in the response to a cross-origin request.

cors.max_age_in_seconds.description: Specifies the number of seconds that the browser can cache the results of a preflight request.

custom_scale_rule.authentication.description: Zero or more `authentication` blocks as defined below.

custom_scale_rule.custom_rule_type.description: The Custom rule type. Possible values include: `activemq`, `artemis-queue`, `kafka`, `pulsar`, `aws-cloudwatch`, `aws-dynamodb`, `aws-dynamodb-streams`, `aws-kinesis-stream`, `aws-sqs-queue`, `azure-app-insights`, `azure-blob`, `azure-data-explorer`, `azure-eventhub`, `azure-log-analytics`, `azure-monitor`, `azure-pipelines`, `azure-servicebus`, `azure-queue`, `cassandra`, `cpu`, `cron`, `datadog`, `elasticsearch`, `external`, `external-push`, `gcp-stackdriver`, `gcp-storage`, `gcp-pubsub`, `graphite`, `http`, `huawei-cloudeye`, `ibmmq`, `influxdb`, `kubernetes-workload`, `liiklus`, `memory`, `metrics-api`, `mongodb`, `mssql`, `mysql`, `nats-jetstream`, `stan`, `tcp`, `new-relic`, `openstack-metric`, `openstack-swift`, `postgresql`, `predictkube`, `prometheus`, `rabbitmq`, `redis`, `redis-cluster`, `redis-sentinel`, `redis-streams`, `redis-cluster-streams`, `redis-sentinel-streams`, `selenium-grid`,`solace-event-queue`, and `github-runner`.

custom_scale_rule.metadata.description: - A map of string key-value pairs to configure the Custom Scale Rule.

custom_scale_rule.name.description: The name of the Scaling Rule

dapr.app_port.description: The port which the application is listening on. This is the same as the `ingress` port.

dapr.app_protocol.description: The protocol for the app. Possible values include `http` and `grpc`. Defaults to `http`.

env.name.description: The name of the environment variable for the container.

env.secret_name.description: The name of the secret that contains the value for this environment variable.

env.value.description: The value for this environment variable.

http_scale_rule.authentication.description: Zero or more `authentication` blocks as defined below.

http_scale_rule.concurrent_requests.description: - The number of concurrent requests to trigger scaling.

http_scale_rule.name.description: The name of the Scaling Rule

identity.identity_ids.description: - A list of one or more Resource IDs for User Assigned Managed identities to assign. Required when `type` is set to `UserAssigned` or `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned`.

identity.type.description: The type of managed identity to assign. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

ingress.allow_insecure_connections.description: Should this ingress allow insecure connections?

ingress.client_certificate_mode.description: The client certificate mode for the Ingress. Possible values are `require`, `accept`, and `ignore`.

ingress.exposed_port.description: The exposed port on the container for the Ingress traffic.

ingress.external_enabled.description: Are connections to this Ingress from outside the Container App Environment enabled? Defaults to `false`.

ingress.ip_security_restriction.description: One or more `ip_security_restriction` blocks for IP-filtering rules as defined below.

ingress.target_port.description: The target port on the container for the Ingress traffic.

ingress.traffic_weight.description: One or more `traffic_weight` blocks as detailed below.

ingress.transport.description: The transport method for the Ingress. Possible values are `auto`, `http`, `http2` and `tcp`. Defaults to `auto`.

init_container.args.description: A list of extra arguments to pass to the container.

init_container.command.description: A command to pass to the container to override the default. This is provided as a list of command line elements without spaces.

init_container.cpu.description: The amount of vCPU to allocate to the container.

init_container.env.description: One or more `env` blocks as detailed below.

init_container.image.description: The image to use to create the container.

init_container.memory.description: The amount of memory to allocate to the container.

ip_security_restriction.description.description: Describe the IP restriction rule that is being sent to the container-app.

ip_security_restriction.ip_address_range.description: The incoming IP address or range of IP addresses (in CIDR notation).

ip_security_restriction.name.description: Name for the IP restriction rule.

liveness_probe.failure_count_threshold.description: The number of consecutive failures required to consider this probe as failed. Possible values are between `1` and `10`. Defaults to `3`.

liveness_probe.host.description: The probe hostname. Defaults to the pod IP address. Setting a value for `Host` in `headers` can be used to override this for `HTTP` and `HTTPS` type probes.

liveness_probe.initial_delay.description: The number of seconds elapsed after the container has started before the probe is initiated. Possible values are between `0` and `60`. Defaults to `1` seconds.

liveness_probe.interval_seconds.description: How often, in seconds, the probe should run. Possible values are in the range `1` - `240`. Defaults to `10`.

liveness_probe.path.description: The URI to use with the `host` for http type probes. Not valid for `TCP` type probes. Defaults to `/`.

liveness_probe.port.description: The port number on which to connect. Possible values are between `1` and `65535`.

liveness_probe.timeout.description: Time in seconds after which the probe times out. Possible values are in the range `1` - `240`. Defaults to `1`.

liveness_probe.transport.description: Type of probe. Possible values are `TCP`, `HTTP`, and `HTTPS`.

max_inactive_revisions.description: The maximum of inactive revisions allowed for this Container App.

name.description: The name for this Container App

readiness_probe.failure_count_threshold.description: The number of consecutive failures required to consider this probe as failed. Possible values are between `1` and `30`. Defaults to `3`.

readiness_probe.host.description: The probe hostname. Defaults to the pod IP address. Setting a value for `Host` in `headers` can be used to override this for `HTTP` and `HTTPS` type probes.

readiness_probe.initial_delay.description: The number of seconds elapsed after the container has started before the probe is initiated. Possible values are between `0` and `60`. Defaults to `0` seconds.

readiness_probe.interval_seconds.description: How often, in seconds, the probe should run. Possible values are between `1` and `240`. Defaults to `10`

readiness_probe.path.description: The URI to use for http type probes. Not valid for `TCP` type probes. Defaults to `/`.

readiness_probe.port.description: The port number on which to connect. Possible values are between `1` and `65535`.

readiness_probe.success_count_threshold.description: The number of consecutive successful responses required to consider this probe as successful. Possible values are between `1` and `10`. Defaults to `3`.

readiness_probe.timeout.description: Time in seconds after which the probe times out. Possible values are in the range `1` - `240`. Defaults to `1`.

readiness_probe.transport.description: Type of probe. Possible values are `TCP`, `HTTP`, and `HTTPS`.

registry.identity.description: Resource ID for the User Assigned Managed identity to use when pulling from the Container Registry.

registry.password_secret_name.description: The name of the Secret Reference containing the password value for this user on the Container Registry, `username` must also be supplied.

registry.server.description: The hostname for the Container Registry.

registry.username.description: The username to use for this Container Registry, `password_secret_name` must also be supplied..

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which the Container App Environment is to be created

revision_mode.description: The revisions operational mode for the Container App. Possible values include `Single` and `Multiple`. In `Single` mode, a single revision is in operation at any given time. In `Multiple` mode, more than one revision can be active at a time and can be configured with load distribution via the `traffic_weight` block in the `ingress` configuration.

secret.description: One or more `secret` block as detailed below.

secret.identity.description: The identity to use for accessing the Key Vault secret reference. This can either be the Resource ID of a User Assigned Identity, or `System` for the System Assigned Identity.

secret.key_vault_secret_id.description: The ID of a Key Vault secret. This can be a versioned or version-less ID.

startup_probe.failure_count_threshold.description: The number of consecutive failures required to consider this probe as failed. Possible values are between `1` and `30`. Defaults to `3`.

startup_probe.host.description: The value for the host header which should be sent with this probe. If unspecified, the IP Address of the Pod is used as the host header. Setting a value for `Host` in `headers` can be used to override this for `HTTP` and `HTTPS` type probes.

startup_probe.initial_delay.description: The number of seconds elapsed after the container has started before the probe is initiated. Possible values are between `0` and `60`. Defaults to `0` seconds.

startup_probe.interval_seconds.description: How often, in seconds, the probe should run. Possible values are between `1` and `240`. Defaults to `10`

startup_probe.path.description: The URI to use with the `host` for http type probes. Not valid for `TCP` type probes. Defaults to `/`.

startup_probe.port.description: The port number on which to connect. Possible values are between `1` and `65535`.

startup_probe.timeout.description: Time in seconds after which the probe times out. Possible values are in the range `1` - `240`. Defaults to `1`.

startup_probe.transport.description: Type of probe. Possible values are `TCP`, `HTTP`, and `HTTPS`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the Container App.

tcp_scale_rule.authentication.description: Zero or more `authentication` blocks as defined below.

tcp_scale_rule.concurrent_requests.description: - The number of concurrent requests to trigger scaling.

tcp_scale_rule.name.description: The name of the Scaling Rule

template.azure_queue_scale_rule.description: One or more `azure_queue_scale_rule` blocks as defined below.

template.container.description: One or more `container` blocks as detailed below.

template.cooldown_period_in_seconds.description: The number of seconds to wait before scaling down the number of instances again. Defaults to `300`.

template.custom_scale_rule.description: One or more `custom_scale_rule` blocks as defined below.

template.http_scale_rule.description: One or more `http_scale_rule` blocks as defined below.

template.init_container.description: The definition of an init container that is part of the group as documented in the `init_container` block below.

template.max_replicas.description: The maximum number of replicas for this container.

template.min_replicas.description: The minimum number of replicas for this container.

template.polling_interval_in_seconds.description: The interval in seconds used for polling KEDA. Defaults to `30`.

template.revision_suffix.description: The suffix for the revision. This value must be unique for the lifetime of the Resource. If omitted the service will use a hash function to create one.

template.tcp_scale_rule.description: One or more `tcp_scale_rule` blocks as defined below.

template.termination_grace_period_second.description: The time in seconds after the container is sent the termination signal before the process if forcibly killed.

traffic_weight.label.description: The label to apply to the revision as a name prefix for routing traffic.

traffic_weight.latest_revision.description: This traffic Weight applies to the latest stable Container Revision. At most only one `traffic_weight` block can have the `latest_revision` set to `true`.

traffic_weight.percentage.description: The percentage of traffic which should be sent this revision.

traffic_weight.revision_suffix.description: The suffix string to which this `traffic_weight` applies.

volume.mount_options.description: Mount options used while mounting the AzureFile. Must be a comma-separated string e.g. `dir_mode=0751,file_mode=0751`.

volume.storage_type.description: The type of storage volume. Possible values are `AzureFile`, `EmptyDir`, `NfsAzureFile` and `Secret`. Defaults to `EmptyDir`.

volume_mounts.name.description: The name of the Volume to be mounted in the container.

volume_mounts.path.description: The path in the container at which to mount this volume.

volume_mounts.sub_path.description: The sub path of the volume to be mounted in the container.

workload_profile_name.description: The name of the Workload Profile in the Container App Environment to place this Container App.

certificate_binding_type.description: The Certificate Binding type. Possible values are `Auto`, `Disabled` and `SniEnabled`. Required with `container_app_environment_certificate_id`

container_app_environment_certificate_id.description: The ID of the Container App Environment Certificate to use

container_app_id.description: The ID of the Container App to which this Custom Domain should be bound

name.description: The fully qualified name of the Custom Domain. Must be the CN or a named SAN in the certificate specified by the `container_app_environment_certificate_id`

dapr_application_insights_connection_str.description: Application Insights connection string used by Dapr to export Service to Service communication telemetry

identity.identity_ids.description: - A list of one or more Resource IDs for User Assigned Managed identities to assign. Required when `type` is set to `UserAssigned` or `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned`.

identity.type.description: The type of managed identity to assign. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

infrastructure_resource_group_name.description: Name of the platform-managed resource group created for the Managed Environment to host infrastructure resources

infrastructure_subnet_id.description: The existing Subnet to use for the Container Apps Control Plane

internal_load_balancer_enabled.description: Should the Container Environment operate in Internal Load Balancing Mode? Defaults to `false`

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the Container App Environment is to exist

log_analytics_workspace_id.description: The ID for the Log Analytics Workspace to link this Container Apps Managed Environment to.

logs_destination.description: Where the application logs will be saved for this Container Apps Managed Environment. Possible values include `log-analytics` and `azure-monitor`. Omitting this value will result in logs being streamed only.

mutual_tls_enabled.description: Should mutual transport layer security (mTLS) be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

name.description: The name of the Container Apps Managed Environment

public_network_access.description: The public network access setting for the Container App Environment. Possible values are `Enabled` and `Disabled`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which the Container App Environment is to be created

workload_profile.workload_profile_type.description: Workload profile type for the workloads to run on. Possible values include `Consumption`, `Consumption-GPU-NC24-A100`, `Consumption-GPU-NC8as-T4`, `D4`, `D8`, `D16`, `D32`, `E4`, `E8`, `E16`, `E32`, `NC24-A100`, `NC48-A100` and `NC96-A100`.

zone_redundancy_enabled.description: Should the Container App Environment be created with Zone Redundancy enabled? Defaults to `false`

certificate_blob_base64.description: The Certificate Private Key as a base64 encoded PFX or PEM

certificate_key_vault.identity.description: The managed identity to authenticate with Azure Key Vault. Possible values are the resource ID of user-assigned identity, and `System` for system-assigned identity. Defaults to `System`

certificate_key_vault.key_vault_secret_i.description: The ID of the Key Vault Secret containing the certificate

container_app_environment_id.description: The Container App Managed Environment ID to configure this Certificate on

name.description: The name of the Container Apps Environment Certificate

readiness_probe.success_count_threshold.description: The number of consecutive successful responses required to consider this probe as successful. Possible values are between `1` and `10`. Defaults to `3`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

volume.mount_options.description: Mount options used while mounting the AzureFile. Must be a comma-separated string e.g. `dir_mode=0751,file_mode=0751`.

volume.storage_name.description: The name of the storage to use for the volume.

volume.storage_type.description: The type of storage to use for the volume. Possible values are `AzureFile`, `EmptyDir`, `NfsAzureFile` and `Secret`. Defaults to `EmptyDir`.

client_token_ids.description: Specifies a list of IDs of Container Registry Tokens, which are meant to be used by the clients to connect to the Connected Registry.

container_registry_id.description: The ID of the Container Registry that this Connected Registry will reside in. Changing this forces a new Container Connected Registry to be created.

log_level.description: The verbosity of the logs. Possible values are `None`, `Debug`, `Information`, `Warning` and `Error`. Defaults to `None`.

mode.description: The mode of the Connected Registry. Possible values are `Mirror`, `ReadOnly`, `ReadWrite` and `Registry`. Changing this forces a new Container Connected Registry to be created. Defaults to `ReadWrite`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Container Connected Registry. Changing this forces a new Container Connected Registry to be created.

notification.description: One or more `notification` blocks as defined below.

notification.action.description: The action of the artifact that wants to be subscribed for the Connected Registry. Possible values are `push`, `delete` and `*` (i.e. any).

notification.digest.description: The digest of the artifact that wants to be subscribed for the Connected Registry.

notification.name.description: The name of the artifact that wants to be subscribed for the Connected Registry.

notification.tag.description: The tag of the artifact that wants to be subscribed for the Connected Registry.

parent_registry_id.description: The ID of the parent registry. This can be either a Container Registry ID or a Connected Registry ID. Changing this forces a new Container Connected Registry to be created.

sync_message_ttl.description: The period of time (in form of ISO8601) for which a message is available to sync before it is expired. Allowed range is from `P1D` to `P90D`. Defaults to `P1D`.

sync_schedule.description: The cron expression indicating the schedule that the Connected Registry will sync with its parent. Defaults to `* * * * *`.

sync_token_id.description: The ID of the Container Registry Token which is used for synchronizing the Connected Registry. Changing this forces a new Container Connected Registry to be created.

sync_window.description: The time window (in form of ISO8601) during which sync is enabled for each schedule occurrence. Allowed range is from `PT3H` to `P7D`.

volume.read_only.description: Specify if the volume is to be mounted as read only or not. The default value is `false`

volume.secret.description: A map of secrets that will be mounted as files in the volume

volume.share_name.description: The Azure storage share that is to be mounted as a volume. This must be created on the storage account specified as above

volume.storage_account_key.description: The access key for the Azure Storage account specified as above

volume.storage_account_name.description: The Azure storage account from which the volume is to be mounted

zones.description: A list of Availability Zones in which this Container Group is located

admin_enabled.description: Specifies whether the admin user is enabled. Defaults to `false`.

anonymous_pull_enabled.description: Whether to allow anonymous (unauthenticated) pull access to this Container Registry. This is only supported on resources with the `Standard` or `Premium` SKU.

data_endpoint_enabled.description: Whether to enable dedicated data endpoints for this Container Registry? This is only supported on resources with the `Premium` SKU.

encryption.identity_client_id.description: The client ID of the managed identity associated with the encryption key.

encryption.key_vault_key_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault Key.

export_policy_enabled.description: Boolean value that indicates whether export policy is enabled. Defaults to `true`. In order to set it to `false`, make sure the `public_network_access_enabled` is also set to `false`.

georeplications.description: One or more `georeplications` blocks as documented below.

georeplications.location.description: A location where the container registry should be geo-replicated.

georeplications.regional_endpoint_enable.description: Whether regional endpoint is enabled for this Container Registry?

georeplications.tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to this replication location.

georeplications.zone_redundancy_enabled.description: Whether zone redundancy is enabled for this replication location? Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Container Registry.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Container Registry. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

ip_rule.action.description: The behaviour for requests matching this rule. At this time the only supported value is `Allow`

ip_rule.ip_range.description: The CIDR block from which requests will match the rule.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

name.description: Specifies the name of the Container Registry. Only Alphanumeric characters allowed

network_rule_bypass_option.description: Whether to allow trusted Azure services to access a network-restricted Container Registry? Possible values are `None` and `AzureServices`. Defaults to `AzureServices`.

network_rule_set.default_action.description: The behaviour for requests matching no rules. Either `Allow` or `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`

network_rule_set.ip_rule.description: One or more `ip_rule` blocks as defined below.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether public network access is allowed for the container registry. Defaults to `true`.

quarantine_policy_enabled.description: Boolean value that indicates whether quarantine policy is enabled.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Container Registry

retention_policy_in_days.description: The number of days to retain and untagged manifest after which it gets purged.

sku.description: The SKU name of the container registry. Possible values are `Basic`, `Standard` and `Premium`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

trust_policy_enabled.description: Boolean value that indicated whether trust policy is enabled. Defaults to `false`.

zone_redundancy_enabled.description: Whether zone redundancy is enabled for this Container Registry?

container_registry_name.description: Name of Azure Container Registry to create an Agent Pool for. Changing this forces a new Azure Container Registry Agent Pool to be created.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Azure Container Registry Agent Pool should exist. Changing this forces a new Azure Container Registry Agent Pool to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Azure Container Registry Agent Pool. Changing this forces a new Azure Container Registry Agent Pool to be created.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Azure Container Registry Agent Pool should exist. Changing this forces a new Azure Container Registry Agent Pool to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Azure Container Registry Agent Pool.

tier.description: Sets the VM your agent pool will run on. Valid values are: `S1` (2 vCPUs, 3 GiB RAM), `S2` (4 vCPUs, 8 GiB RAM), `S3` (8 vCPUs, 16 GiB RAM) or `I6` (64 vCPUs, 216 GiB RAM, Isolated). Defaults to `S1`. Changing this forces a new Azure Container Registry Agent Pool to be created.

virtual_network_subnet_id.description: The ID of the Virtual Network Subnet Resource where the agent machines will be running. Changing this forces a new Azure Container Registry Agent Pool to be created.

container_registry_id.description: The ID of the Container Registry where the Cache Rule should apply

credential_set_id.description: The ARM resource ID of the Credential Store which is associated with the Cache Rule.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Container Registry Cache Rule. Only Alphanumeric characters allowed

source_repo.description: The name of the source repository path

target_repo.description: The name of the new repository path to store artifacts

authentication_credentials.password_secr.description: The URI of the secret containing the password in a Key Vault.

authentication_credentials.username_secr.description: The URI of the secret containing the username in a Key Vault.

container_registry_id.description: The ID of the Container Registry. Changing this forces a new Container Registry Credential Set to be created.

identity.type.description: The type of Managed Service Identity that is configured on for the Container Registry Credential Set. Currently the only possible value is `SystemAssigned`.

login_server.description: The login server for the Credential Set. Changing this forces a new Container Registry Credential Set to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Container Registry Credential Set. Changing this forces a new Container Registry Credential Set to be created.

actions.description: A list of actions to attach to the scope map (e.g. `repo/content/read`, `repo2/content/delete`).

container_registry_name.description: The name of the Container Registry

description.description: The description of the Container Registry.

name.description: Specifies the name of the scope map

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Container Registry token

agent_pool_name.description: The name of the dedicated Container Registry Agent Pool for this Container Registry Task.

agent_setting.cpu.description: The number of cores required for the Container Registry Task. Possible value is `2`.

authentication.expire_in_seconds.description: Time in seconds that the token remains valid.

authentication.refresh_token.description: The refresh token used to refresh the access token.

authentication.scope.description: The scope of the access token.

authentication.token.description: The access token used to access the source control provider.

authentication.token_type.description: The type of the token. Possible values are `PAT` (personal access token) and `OAuth`.

base_image_trigger.enabled.description: Should the trigger be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

base_image_trigger.name.description: The name which should be used for this trigger.

base_image_trigger.type.description: The type of the trigger. Possible values are `All` and `Runtime`.

base_image_trigger.update_trigger_endpoi.description: The endpoint URL for receiving the trigger.

base_image_trigger.update_trigger_payloa.description: Type of payload body for the trigger. Possible values are `Default` and `Token`.

container_registry_id.description: The ID of the Container Registry that this Container Registry Task resides in. Changing this forces a new Container Registry Task to be created.

custom.identity.description: The managed identity assigned to this custom credential. For user assigned identity, the value is the client ID of the identity. For system assigned identity, the value is `[system]`.

custom.login_server.description: The login server of the custom Container Registry.

custom.password.description: The password for logging into the custom Container Registry. It can be either a plain text of password, or a Keyvault Secret ID.

custom.username.description: The username for logging into the custom Container Registry. It can be either a plain text of username, or a Keyvault Secret ID.

docker_step.arguments.description: Specifies a map of arguments to be used when executing this step.

docker_step.cache_enabled.description: Should the image cache be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

docker_step.context_access_token.description: The token (Git PAT or SAS token of storage account blob) associated with the context for this step.

docker_step.context_path.description: The URL (absolute or relative) of the source context for this step. If the context is an url you can reference a specific branch or folder via `#branch:folder`.

docker_step.dockerfile_path.description: The Dockerfile path relative to the source context.

docker_step.image_names.description: Specifies a list of fully qualified image names including the repository and tag.

docker_step.push_enabled.description: Should the image built be pushed to the registry or not? Defaults to `true`.

docker_step.secret_arguments.description: Specifies a map of *secret* arguments to be used when executing this step.

docker_step.target.description: The name of the target build stage for the docker build.

enabled.description: Should this Container Registry Task be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

encoded_step.context_access_token.description: The token (Git PAT or SAS token of storage account blob) associated with the context for this step.

encoded_step.context_path.description: The URL (absolute or relative) of the source context for this step.

encoded_step.secret_values.description: Specifies a map of secret values that can be passed when running a task.

encoded_step.task_content.description: The (optionally base64 encoded) content of the build template.

encoded_step.value_content.description: The (optionally base64 encoded) content of the build parameters.

encoded_step.values.description: Specifies a map of values that can be passed when running a task.

file_step.context_access_token.description: The token (Git PAT or SAS token of storage account blob) associated with the context for this step.

file_step.context_path.description: The URL (absolute or relative) of the source context for this step.

file_step.secret_values.description: Specifies a map of secret values that can be passed when running a task.

file_step.task_file_path.description: The task template file path relative to the source context.

file_step.value_file_path.description: The parameters file path relative to the source context.

file_step.values.description: Specifies a map of values that can be passed when running a task.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Container Registry Task.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Container Registry Task. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

is_system_task.description: Whether this Container Registry Task is a system task. Changing this forces a new Container Registry Task to be created. Defaults to `false`.

log_template.description: The template that describes the run log artifact.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Container Registry Task. Changing this forces a new Container Registry Task to be created.

platform.architecture.description: The OS architecture. Possible values are `amd64`, `x86`, `386`, `arm` and `arm64`.

platform.os.description: The operating system type required for the task. Possible values are `Windows` and `Linux`.

platform.variant.description: The variant of the CPU. Possible values are `v6`, `v7`, `v8`.

registry_credential.custom.description: One or more `custom` blocks as defined above.

source.login_mode.description: The login mode for the source registry. Possible values are `None` and `Default`.

source_trigger.description: One or more `source_trigger` blocks as defined below.

source_trigger.branch.description: The branch name of the source code.

source_trigger.enabled.description: Should the trigger be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

source_trigger.events.description: Specifies a list of source events corresponding to the trigger. Possible values are `commit` and `pullrequest`.

source_trigger.name.description: The name which should be used for this trigger.

source_trigger.repository_url.description: The full URL to the source code repository.

source_trigger.source_type.description: The type of the source control service. Possible values are `Github` and `VisualStudioTeamService`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Container Registry Task.

timeout_in_seconds.description: The timeout of this Container Registry Task in seconds. The valid range lies from 300 to 28800. Defaults to `3600`.

timer_trigger.description: One or more `timer_trigger` blocks as defined below.

timer_trigger.enabled.description: Should the trigger be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

timer_trigger.name.description: The name which should be used for this trigger.

timer_trigger.schedule.description: The CRON expression for the task schedule.

container_registry_task_id.description: The ID of the Container Registry Task that to be scheduled. Changing this forces a new Container Registry Task Schedule to be created.

container_registry_name.description: The name of the Container Registry

enabled.description: Should the Container Registry token be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the token

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Container Registry token

scope_map_id.description: The ID of the Container Registry Scope Map associated with the token.

container_registry_token_id.description: The ID of the Container Registry Token that this Container Registry Token Password resides in. Changing this forces a new Container Registry Token Password to be created.

password.expiry.description: The expiration date of the password in RFC3339 format. If not specified, the password never expires

actions.description: A list of actions that trigger the Webhook to post notifications. At least one action needs to be specified. Valid values are: `push`, `delete`, `quarantine`, `chart_push`, `chart_delete`

custom_headers.description: Custom headers that will be added to the webhook notifications request.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

name.description: Specifies the name of the Container Registry Webhook. Only Alphanumeric characters allowed

registry_name.description: The Name of Container registry this Webhook belongs to

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Container Registry Webhook

scope.description: Specifies the scope of repositories that can trigger an event. For example, `foo:*` means events for all tags under repository `foo`. `foo:bar` means events for 'foo:bar' only. `foo` is equivalent to `foo:latest`. Empty means all events. Defaults to `""`.

service_uri.description: Specifies the service URI for the Webhook to post notifications.

status.description: Specifies if this Webhook triggers notifications or not. Valid values: `enabled` and `disabled`. Default is `enabled`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

access_key_metadata_writes_enabled.description: Is write operations on metadata resources (databases, containers, throughput) via account keys enabled? Defaults to `true`.

analytical_storage.schema_type.description: The schema type of the Analytical Storage for this Cosmos DB account. Possible values are `FullFidelity` and `WellDefined`.

analytical_storage_enabled.description: Enable Analytical Storage option for this Cosmos DB account. Defaults to `false`. Enabling and then disabling analytical storage

automatic_failover_enabled.description: Enable automatic failover for this Cosmos DB account.

backup.interval_in_minutes.description: The interval in minutes between two backups. Possible values are between 60 and 1440. Defaults to `240`.

backup.retention_in_hours.description: The time in hours that each backup is retained. Possible values are between 8 and 720. Defaults to `8`.

backup.storage_redundancy.description: The storage redundancy is used to indicate the type of backup residency. Possible values are `Geo`, `Local` and `Zone`. Defaults to `Geo`.

backup.tier.description: The continuous backup tier. Possible values are `Continuous7Days` and `Continuous30Days`.

backup.type.description: The type of the `backup`. Possible values are `Continuous` and `Periodic`.

burst_capacity_enabled.description: Enable burst capacity for this Cosmos DB account. Defaults to `false`.

capabilities.description: The capabilities which should be enabled for this Cosmos DB account. Value is a `capabilities` block as defined below.

capacity.total_throughput_limit.description: The total throughput limit imposed on this Cosmos DB account (RU/s). Possible values are at least `-1`. `-1` means no limit.

consistency_level.description: The Consistency Level to use for this CosmosDB Account - can be either `BoundedStaleness`, `Eventual`, `Session`, `Strong` or `ConsistentPrefix`.

consistency_policy.description: Specifies one `consistency_policy` block as defined below, used to define the consistency policy for this CosmosDB account.

cors_rule.allowed_headers.description: A list of headers that are allowed to be a part of the cross-origin request.

cors_rule.allowed_methods.description: A list of HTTP headers that are allowed to be executed by the origin. Valid options are `DELETE`, `GET`, `HEAD`, `MERGE`, `POST`, `OPTIONS`, `PUT` or `PATCH`.

cors_rule.allowed_origins.description: A list of origin domains that will be allowed by CORS.

cors_rule.exposed_headers.description: A list of response headers that are exposed to CORS clients.

cors_rule.max_age_in_seconds.description: The number of seconds the client should cache a preflight response. Possible values are between `1` and `2147483647`.

create_mode.description: The creation mode for the CosmosDB Account. Possible values are `Default` and `Restore`

database.collection_names.description: A list of the collection names for the restore request

database.name.description: The database name for the restore request

default_identity_type.description: The default identity for accessing Key Vault. Possible values are `FirstPartyIdentity`, `SystemAssignedIdentity` or `UserAssignedIdentity`. Defaults to `FirstPartyIdentity`.

failover_priority.description: The failover priority of the region. A failover priority of `0` indicates a write region. The maximum value for a failover priority = (total number of regions - 1). Failover priority values must be unique for each of the regions in which the database account exists. Changing this causes the location to be re-provisioned and cannot be changed for the location with failover priority `0`.

free_tier_enabled.description: Enable the Free Tier pricing option for this Cosmos DB account. Defaults to `false`

geo_location.description: Specifies a `geo_location` resource, used to define where data should be replicated with the `failover_priority` 0 specifying the primary location. Value is a `geo_location` block as defined below.

gremlin_database.graph_names.description: A list of the Graph names for the restore request

gremlin_database.name.description: The Gremlin Database name for the restore request

id.description: The ID of the virtual network subnet.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Cosmos Account.

identity.type.description: The Type of Managed Identity assigned to this Cosmos account. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned` and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned`.

ignore_missing_vnet_service_endpoint.description: If set to true, the specified subnet will be added as a virtual network rule even if its CosmosDB service endpoint is not active. Defaults to `false`.

ip_range_filter.description: A set of IP addresses or IP address ranges in CIDR form to be included as the allowed list of client IPs for a given database account. For example `["55.0.1.0/24", "55.0.2.0/24"]`.

is_virtual_network_filter_enabled.description: Enables virtual network filtering for this Cosmos DB account.

key_vault_key_id.description: A versionless Key Vault Key ID for CMK encryption

kind.description: Specifies the Kind of CosmosDB to create - possible values are `GlobalDocumentDB`, `MongoDB` and `Parse`. Defaults to `GlobalDocumentDB`

local_authentication_disabled.description: Disable local authentication and ensure only MSI and AAD can be used exclusively for authentication. Defaults to `false`. Can be set only when using the SQL API.

location.description: The name of the Azure region to host replicated data.

managed_hsm_key_id.description: A versionless Managed HSM Key ID for CMK encryption

max_interval_in_seconds.description: When used with the Bounded Staleness consistency level, this value represents the time amount of staleness (in seconds) tolerated. The accepted range for this value is `5` - `86400` (1 day). Defaults to `5`. Required when `consistency_level` is set to `BoundedStaleness`.

max_staleness_prefix.description: When used with the Bounded Staleness consistency level, this value represents the number of stale requests tolerated. The accepted range for this value is `10` – `2147483647`. Defaults to `100`. Required when `consistency_level` is set to `BoundedStaleness`.

minimal_tls_version.description: Specifies the minimal TLS version for the CosmosDB account. Possible values are: `Tls`, `Tls11`, and `Tls12`. Defaults to `Tls12`.

mongo_server_version.description: The Server Version of a MongoDB account. Possible values are `7.0`, `6.0`, `5.0`, `4.2`, `4.0`, `3.6`, and `3.2`.

multiple_write_locations_enabled.description: Enable multiple write locations for this Cosmos DB account.

name.description: The capability to enable - Possible values are `AllowSelfServeUpgradeToMongo36`, `DeleteAllItemsByPartitionKey`, `DisableRateLimitingResponses`, `EnableAggregationPipeline`, `EnableCassandra`, `EnableGremlin`, `EnableMongo`, `EnableMongo16MBDocumentSupport`, `EnableMongoRetryableWrites`, `EnableMongoRoleBasedAccessControl`, `EnableNoSQLVectorSearch`, `EnableNoSQLFullTextSearch`, `EnablePartialUniqueIndex`, `EnableServerless`, `EnableTable`, `EnableTtlOnCustomPath`, `EnableUniqueCompoundNestedDocs`, `MongoDBv3.4` and `mongoEnableDocLevelTTL`.

network_acl_bypass_for_azure_services.description: If Azure services can bypass ACLs. Defaults to `false`.

network_acl_bypass_ids.description: The list of resource Ids for Network Acl Bypass for this Cosmos DB account.

offer_type.description: Specifies the Offer Type to use for this CosmosDB Account; currently, this can only be set to `Standard`.

partition_merge_enabled.description: Is partition merge on the Cosmos DB account enabled? Defaults to `false`.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether or not public network access is allowed for this CosmosDB account. Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which the CosmosDB Account is created

restore.gremlin_database.description: One or more `gremlin_database` blocks as defined below

restore.restore_timestamp_in_utc.description: The creation time of the database or the collection (Datetime Format `RFC 3339`)

restore.source_cosmosdb_account_id.description: The resource ID of the restorable database account from which the restore has to be initiated. The example is `/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/providers/Microsoft.DocumentDB/locations/{location}/restorableDatabaseAccounts/{restorableDatabaseAccountName}`

restore.tables_to_restore.description: A list of specific tables available for restore

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

virtual_network_rule.description: Specifies a `virtual_network_rule` block as defined below, used to define which subnets are allowed to access this CosmosDB account.

zone_redundant.description: Should zone redundancy be enabled for this region? Defaults to `false`.

authentication_method.description: The authentication method that is used to authenticate clients. Possible values are `None` and `Cassandra`. Defaults to `Cassandra`.

client_certificate_pems.description: A list of TLS certificates that is used to authorize client connecting to the Cassandra Cluster.

default_admin_password.description: The initial admin password for this Cassandra Cluster

delegated_management_subnet_id.description: The ID of the delegated management subnet for this Cassandra Cluster. Changing this forces a new Cassandra Cluster to be created.

external_gossip_certificate_pems.description: A list of TLS certificates that is used to authorize gossip from unmanaged Cassandra Data Center.

external_seed_node_ip_addresses.description: A list of IP Addresses of the seed nodes in unmanaged the Cassandra Data Center which will be added to the seed node lists of all managed nodes.

hours_between_backups.description: The number of hours to wait between taking a backup of the Cassandra Cluster. Defaults to `24`.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Cassandra Cluster. The only possible value is `SystemAssigned`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Cassandra Cluster should exist. Changing this forces a new Cassandra Cluster to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Cassandra Cluster. Changing this forces a new Cassandra Cluster to be created.

repair_enabled.description: Is the automatic repair enabled on the Cassandra Cluster? Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Cassandra Cluster should exist. Changing this forces a new Cassandra Cluster to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags assigned to the resource.

version.description: The version of Cassandra what the Cluster converges to run. Possible values are `3.11`, `4.0`, `4.1` and `5.0`. Defaults to `3.11`. Changing this forces a new Cassandra Cluster to be created.

availability_zones_enabled.description: Determines whether availability zones are enabled. Defaults to `true`.

backup_storage_customer_key_uri.description: The key URI of the customer key to use for the encryption of the backup Storage Account.

base64_encoded_yaml_fragment.description: The fragment of the cassandra.yaml configuration file to be included in the cassandra.yaml for all nodes in this Cassandra Datacenter. The fragment should be Base64 encoded and only a subset of keys is allowed.

cassandra_cluster_id.description: The ID of the Cassandra Cluster. Changing this forces a new Cassandra Datacenter to be created.

delegated_management_subnet_id.description: The ID of the delegated management subnet for this Cassandra Datacenter. Changing this forces a new Cassandra Datacenter to be created.

disk_count.description: Determines the number of p30 disks that are attached to each node.

disk_sku.description: The Disk SKU that is used for this Cassandra Datacenter. Defaults to `P30`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Cassandra Datacenter should exist. Changing this forces a new Cassandra Datacenter to be created.

managed_disk_customer_key_uri.description: The key URI of the customer key to use for the encryption of the Managed Disk.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Cassandra Datacenter. Changing this forces a new Cassandra Datacenter to be created.

node_count.description: The number of nodes the Cassandra Datacenter should have. The number should be equal or greater than `3`. Defaults to `3`.

sku_name.description: Determines the selected sku. Defaults to `Standard_E16s_v5`.

account_name.description: The name of the Cosmos DB Cassandra KeySpace to create the table within

autoscale_settings.description: An `autoscale_settings` block as defined below. This must be set upon database creation otherwise it cannot be updated without a manual terraform destroy-apply.

autoscale_settings.max_throughput.description: The maximum throughput of the Cassandra KeySpace (RU/s). Must be between `1,000` and `1,000,000`. Must be set in increments of `1,000`. Conflicts with `throughput`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Cosmos DB Cassandra KeySpace

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which the Cosmos DB Cassandra KeySpace is created

throughput.description: The throughput of Cassandra KeySpace (RU/s). Must be set in increments of `100`. The minimum value is `400`. This must be set upon database creation otherwise it cannot be updated without a manual terraform destroy-apply.

analytical_storage_ttl.description: Time to live of the Analytical Storage. Possible values are between `-1` and `2147483647` except `0`. `-1` means the Analytical Storage never expires

autoscale_settings.description: An `autoscale_settings` block as defined below. This must be set upon database creation otherwise it cannot be updated without a manual terraform destroy-apply.

autoscale_settings.max_throughput.description: The maximum throughput of the Cassandra Table (RU/s). Must be between `1,000` and `1,000,000`. Must be set in increments of `1,000`. Conflicts with `throughput`.

cassandra_keyspace_id.description: The ID of the Cosmos DB Cassandra Keyspace to create the table within

cluster_key.name.description: Name of the cluster key to be created.

cluster_key.order_by.description: Order of the key. Currently supported values are `Asc` and `Desc`.

column.name.description: Name of the column to be created.

column.type.description: Type of the column to be created.

default_ttl.description: Time to live of the Cosmos DB Cassandra table. Possible values are at least `-1`. `-1` means the Cassandra table never expires.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Cosmos DB Cassandra Table

partition_key.name.description: Name of the column to partition by.

schema.cluster_key.description: One or more `cluster_key` blocks as defined below.

schema.column.description: One or more `column` blocks as defined below.

schema.partition_key.description: One or more `partition_key` blocks as defined below.

throughput.description: The throughput of Cassandra KeySpace (RU/s). Must be set in increments of `100`. The minimum value is `400`. This must be set upon database creation otherwise it cannot be updated without a manual terraform destroy-apply.

account_name.description: The name of the CosmosDB Account to create the Gremlin Database within

autoscale_settings.description: An `autoscale_settings` block as defined below. This must be set upon database creation otherwise it cannot be updated without a manual terraform destroy-apply.

autoscale_settings.max_throughput.description: The maximum throughput of the Gremlin database (RU/s). Must be between `1,000` and `1,000,000`. Must be set in increments of `1,000`. Conflicts with `throughput`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Cosmos DB Gremlin Database

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which the Cosmos DB Gremlin Database is created

throughput.description: The throughput of the Gremlin database (RU/s). Must be set in increments of `100`. The minimum value is `400`. This must be set upon database creation otherwise it cannot be updated without a manual terraform destroy-apply.

account_name.description: The name of the CosmosDB Account to create the Gremlin Graph within

analytical_storage_ttl.description: The time to live of Analytical Storage for this Cosmos DB Gremlin Graph. Possible values are between `-1` to `2147483647` not including `0`. If present and the value is set to `-1`, it means never expire.

autoscale_settings.description: An `autoscale_settings` block as defined below. This must be set upon database creation otherwise it cannot be updated without a manual terraform destroy-apply. Requires `partition_key_path` to be set.

frequency.description: The trigger frequency. Valid values include `Minute`, `Hour`, `Day`, `Week`, `Month`. Defaults to `Minute`.

interval.description: The interval for how often the trigger occurs. This defaults to `1`.

monthly.week.description: The occurrence of the specified day during the month. For example, a `monthly` property with `weekday` and `week` values of `Sunday, -1` means the last Sunday of the month.

monthly.weekday.description: The day of the week on which the trigger runs. For example, a `monthly` property with a `weekday` value of `Sunday` means every Sunday of the month.

schedule.days_of_month.description: Day(s) of the month on which the trigger is scheduled. This value can be specified with a monthly frequency only.

schedule.days_of_week.description: Days of the week on which the trigger is scheduled. This value can be specified only with a weekly frequency.

schedule.hours.description: Hours of the day on which the trigger is scheduled.

schedule.minutes.description: Minutes of the hour on which the trigger is scheduled.

interval.description: Specifies the interval of Tumbling Window. Changing this forces a new resource.

max_concurrency.description: The max number for simultaneous trigger run fired by Tumbling Window. Possible values are between `1` and `50`. Defaults to `50`.

criteria.absolute_criteria.description: Possible values are `AllBackup`, `FirstOfDay`, `FirstOfWeek`, `FirstOfMonth` and `FirstOfYear`. These values mean the first successful backup of the day/week/month/year. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy Blob Storage to be created.

criteria.days_of_month.description: Must be between `0` and `28`. `0` for last day within the month. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy Blob Storage to be created.

criteria.days_of_week.description: Possible values are `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday`, `Saturday` and `Sunday`. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy Blob Storage to be created.

criteria.months_of_year.description: Possible values are `January`, `February`, `March`, `April`, `May`, `June`, `July`, `August`, `September`, `October`, `November` and `December`. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy Blob Storage to be created. When this property is specified, exactly one of the following must also be set: `days_of_month`, `days_of_week`

criteria.scheduled_backup_times.description: Specifies a list of backup times for backup in the `RFC3339` format. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy Blob Storage to be created.

criteria.weeks_of_month.description: Possible values are `First`, `Second`, `Third`, `Fourth` and `Last`. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy Blob Storage to be created. When this property is specified, exactly one of the following must also be set: `days_of_month`, `days_of_week`

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Backup Policy PostgreSQL should exist. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy PostgreSQL to be created.

retention_rule.description: One or more `retention_rule` blocks as defined below. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy PostgreSQL to be created.

retention_rule.criteria.description: A `criteria` block as defined below. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy PostgreSQL to be created.

retention_rule.duration.description: Duration after which the backup is deleted. It should follow `ISO 8601` duration format. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy PostgreSQL to be created.

retention_rule.name.description: The name which should be used for this retention rule. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy PostgreSQL to be created.

retention_rule.priority.description: Specifies the priority of the rule. The priority number must be unique for each rule. The lower the priority number, the higher the priority of the rule. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy PostgreSQL to be created.

time_zone.description: Specifies the Time Zone which should be used by the backup schedule. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy PostgreSQL to be created.

vault_name.description: The name of the Backup Vault where the Backup Policy PostgreSQL should exist. Changing this forces a new Backup Policy PostgreSQL to be created.

backup_repeating_time_intervals.description: Specifies a list of repeating time interval. It supports weekly back. It should follow `ISO 8601` repeating time interval format

criteria.absolute_criteria.description: Possible values are `AllBackup`, `FirstOfDay`, `FirstOfWeek`, `FirstOfMonth` and `FirstOfYear`. These values mean the first successful backup of the day/week/month/year

criteria.scheduled_backup_times.description: Specifies a list of backup times for backup in the `RFC3339` format

life_cycle.data_store_type.description: The type of data store. The only possible value is `VaultStore`

life_cycle.duration.description: The retention duration up to which the backups are to be retained in the data stores. It should follow `ISO 8601` duration format

name.description: Specifies the name of the Backup Policy for the PostgreSQL Flexible Server

retention_rule.description: One or more `retention_rule` blocks as defined below

retention_rule.name.description: Specifies the name of the retention rule

retention_rule.priority.description: Specifies the priority of the rule. The priority number must be unique for each rule. The lower the priority number, the higher the priority of the rule

time_zone.description: Specifies the Time Zone which should be used by the backup schedule

vault_id.description: The ID of the Backup Vault where the Backup Policy PostgreSQL Flexible Server should exist

cross_region_restore_enabled.description: Whether to enable cross-region restore for the Backup Vault.

soft_delete.description: The state of soft delete for this Backup Vault. Possible values are `AlwaysOn`, `Off`, and `On`. Defaults to `On`.

sku_name.description: The `sku_name` is comprised of two segments separated by a hyphen (e.g. `TEA_1Node_UPS_Heater-Standard`). The first segment of the `sku_name` defines the `name` of the SKU, possible values are `Gateway`, `EdgeMR_Mini`, `EdgeP_Base`, `EdgeP_High`, `EdgePR_Base`, `EdgePR_Base_UPS`, `GPU`, `RCA_Large`, `RCA_Small`, `RDC`, `TCA_Large`, `TCA_Small`, `TDC`, `TEA_1Node`, `TEA_1Node_UPS`, `TEA_1Node_Heater`, `TEA_1Node_UPS_Heater`, `TEA_4Node_Heater`, `TEA_4Node_UPS_Heater` or `TMA`. The second segment defines the `tier` of the `sku_name`, possible values are `Standard`. For more information see the [product documentation]("https://docs.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/microsoft.azure.management.databoxedge.models.sku?view=azure-dotnet"). Changing this forces a new Databox Edge Device to be created.

allow_forwarded_traffic.description: Can the forwarded traffic from the VMs in the local virtual network be forwarded to the remote virtual network? Defaults to `false`.

allow_gateway_transit.description: Can the gateway links be used in the remote virtual network to link to the Databricks virtual network? Defaults to `false`.

allow_virtual_network_access.description: Can the VMs in the local virtual network space access the VMs in the remote virtual network space? Defaults to `true`.

custom_parameters.nat_gateway_name.description: Name of the NAT gateway for Secure Cluster Connectivity (No Public IP) workspace subnets (only for workspace with managed virtual network). Defaults to `nat-gateway`

custom_parameters.private_subnet_network.description: The resource ID of the `azurerm_subnet_network_security_group_association` resource which is referred to by the `private_subnet_name` field. This is the same as the ID of the subnet referred to by the `private_subnet_name` field. Required if `virtual_network_id` is set.

custom_parameters.public_ip_name.description: Name of the Public IP for No Public IP workspace with managed virtual network. Defaults to `nat-gw-public-ip`

custom_parameters.public_subnet_name.description: The name of the Public Subnet within the Virtual Network. Required if `virtual_network_id` is set

custom_parameters.public_subnet_network_.description: The resource ID of the `azurerm_subnet_network_security_group_association` resource which is referred to by the `public_subnet_name` field. This is the same as the ID of the subnet referred to by the `public_subnet_name` field. Required if `virtual_network_id` is set.

custom_parameters.storage_account_name.description: Default Databricks File Storage account name. Defaults to a randomized name(e.g. `dbstoragel6mfeghoe5kxu`)

custom_parameters.storage_account_sku_na.description: Storage account SKU name. Possible values include `Standard_LRS`, `Standard_GRS`, `Standard_RAGRS`, `Standard_GZRS`, `Standard_RAGZRS`, `Standard_ZRS`, `Premium_LRS` or `Premium_ZRS`. Defaults to `Standard_GRS`.

custom_parameters.vnet_address_prefix.description: Address prefix for Managed virtual network. Defaults to `10.139`

customer_managed_key_enabled.description: Is the workspace enabled for customer managed key encryption? If `true` this enables the Managed Identity for the managed storage account. Possible values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`. This field is only valid if the Databricks Workspace `sku` is set to `premium`.

default_storage_firewall_enabled.description: Disallow public access to default storage account. Defaults to `false`.

enhanced_security_compliance.description: An `enhanced_security_compliance` block as documented below. This feature is only valid if `sku` is set to `premium`.

enhanced_security_compliance.automatic_c.description: Enables automatic cluster updates for this workspace. Defaults to `false`.

enhanced_security_compliance.compliance_.description: Enables compliance security profile for this workspace. Defaults to `false`.
A list of standards to enforce on this workspace. Possible values include `HIPAA` and `PCI_DSS`.

enhanced_security_compliance.enhanced_se.description: Enables enhanced security monitoring for this workspace. Defaults to `false`.

infrastructure_encryption_enabled.description: Is the Databricks File System root file system enabled with a secondary layer of encryption with platform managed keys? Possible values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`. This field is only valid if the Databricks Workspace `sku` is set to `premium`
