firewall_policy_id.description: The ID of the Firewall Policy where the Firewall Policy Rule Collection Group should exist. Changing this forces a new Firewall Policy Rule Collection Group to be created.

http_headers.name.description: Specifies the name of the header.

http_headers.value.description: Specifies the value of the value.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Firewall Policy Rule Collection Group. Changing this forces a new Firewall Policy Rule Collection Group to be created.

nat_rule_collection.description: One or more `nat_rule_collection` blocks as defined below.

nat_rule_collection.action.description: The action to take for the NAT rules in this collection. Currently, the only possible value is `Dnat`.

nat_rule_collection.description.description: The description which should be used for this rule.

nat_rule_collection.destination_addresse.description: Specifies a list of destination IP addresses (including CIDR, IP range and `*`) or Service Tags.

nat_rule_collection.destination_fqdn_tag.description: Specifies a list of destination FQDN tags.

nat_rule_collection.destination_fqdns.description: Specifies a list of destination FQDNs.

nat_rule_collection.destination_ip_group.description: Specifies a list of destination IP groups.

nat_rule_collection.destination_ports.description: Specifies a list of destination ports. Only one destination port is supported in a NAT rule.

nat_rule_collection.destination_urls.description: Specifies a list of destination URLs for which policy should hold. Needs Premium SKU for Firewall Policy. Conflicts with `destination_fqdns`.

nat_rule_collection.http_headers.description: Specifies a list of HTTP/HTTPS headers to insert. One or more `http_headers` blocks as defined below.

nat_rule_collection.name.description: The name which should be used for this rule.

nat_rule_collection.priority.description: The priority of the NAT rule collection. The range is `100` - `65000`.

nat_rule_collection.protocols.description: Specifies a list of network protocols this rule applies to. Possible values are `TCP`, `UDP`.

nat_rule_collection.source_addresses.description: Specifies a list of source IP addresses (including CIDR, IP range and `*`).

nat_rule_collection.source_ip_groups.description: Specifies a list of source IP groups.

nat_rule_collection.terminate_tls.description: Boolean specifying if TLS shall be terminated (true) or not (false). Must be `true` when using `destination_urls`. Needs Premium SKU for Firewall Policy.

nat_rule_collection.web_categories.description: Specifies a list of web categories to which access is denied or allowed depending on the value of `action` above. Needs Premium SKU for Firewall Policy.

network_rule_collection.description: One or more `network_rule_collection` blocks as defined below.

network_rule_collection.action.description: The action to take for the network rules in this collection. Possible values are `Allow` and `Deny`.

network_rule_collection.name.description: The name which should be used for this network rule collection.

network_rule_collection.priority.description: The priority of the network rule collection. The range is `100` - `65000`.

network_rule_collection.rule.description: One or more `network_rule` blocks as defined below.

priority.description: The priority of the Firewall Policy Rule Collection Group. The range is 100-65000.

protocols.port.description: Port number of the protocol. Range is 0-64000.

protocols.type.description: Protocol type. Possible values are `Http` and `Https`.

customer_managed_key.key_vault_key_id.description: The Key Vault Key Id that will be used to encrypt the Fluid Relay Server.

customer_managed_key.user_assigned_ident.description: The User Assigned Managed Identity ID to be used for accessing the Customer Managed Key for encryption.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Fluid Relay Service.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Fluid Relay Service. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`,`UserAssigned` and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Fluid Relay Server should exist. Changing this forces a new Fluid Relay Server to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Fluid Relay Server. Changing this forces a new Fluid Relay Server to be created.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Fluid Relay Server should exist. Changing this forces a new Fluid Relay Server to be created.

storage_sku.description: Sku of the storage associated with the resource, Possible values are `standard` and `basic`. Changing this forces a new Fluid Relay Server to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Fluid Relay Server.

backend.address.description: Location of the backend (IP address or FQDN)

backend.enabled.description: Specifies if the backend is enabled or not. Valid options are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `true`.

backend.host_header.description: The value to use as the host header sent to the backend.

backend.http_port.description: The HTTP TCP port number. Possible values are between `1` - `65535`.

backend.https_port.description: The HTTPS TCP port number. Possible values are between `1` - `65535`.

backend.priority.description: Priority to use for load balancing. Higher priorities will not be used for load balancing if any lower priority backend is healthy. Defaults to `1`.

backend.weight.description: Weight of this endpoint for load balancing purposes. Defaults to `50`.

backend_pool.health_probe_name.description: Specifies the name of the `backend_pool_health_probe` block within this resource to use for this `Backend Pool`.

backend_pool.load_balancing_name.description: Specifies the name of the `backend_pool_load_balancing` block within this resource to use for this `Backend Pool`.

backend_pool.name.description: Specifies the name of the Backend Pool.

backend_pool_health_probe.enabled.description: Is this health probe enabled? Defaults to `true`.

backend_pool_health_probe.interval_in_se.description: The number of seconds between each Health Probe. Defaults to `120`.

backend_pool_health_probe.name.description: Specifies the name of the Health Probe.

backend_pool_health_probe.path.description: The path to use for the Health Probe. Default is `/`.

backend_pool_health_probe.probe_method.description: Specifies HTTP method the health probe uses when querying the backend pool instances. Possible values include: `GET` and `HEAD`. Defaults to `GET`.

backend_pool_health_probe.protocol.description: Protocol scheme to use for the Health Probe. Possible values are `Http` and `Https`. Defaults to `Http`.

backend_pool_load_balancing.additional_l.description: The additional latency in milliseconds for probes to fall into the lowest latency bucket. Defaults to `0`.

backend_pool_load_balancing.name.description: Specifies the name of the Load Balancer.

backend_pool_load_balancing.sample_size.description: The number of samples to consider for load balancing decisions. Defaults to `4`.

backend_pool_load_balancing.successful_s.description: The number of samples within the sample period that must succeed. Defaults to `2`.

backend_pool_settings.backend_pools_send.description: Specifies the send and receive timeout on forwarding request to the backend. When the timeout is reached, the request fails and returns. Possible values are between `0` - `240`. Defaults to `60`.

backend_pool_settings.enforce_backend_po.description: Enforce certificate name check on `HTTPS` requests to all backend pools, this setting will have no effect on `HTTP` requests. Permitted values are `true` or `false`.

forwarding_configuration.backend_pool_na.description: Specifies the name of the Backend Pool to forward the incoming traffic to.

forwarding_configuration.cache_duration.description: Specify the minimum caching duration (in ISO8601 notation e.g. `P1DT2H` for 1 day and 2 hours). Needs to be greater than 0 and smaller than 365 days. `cache_duration` works only in combination with `cache_enabled` set to `true`.

forwarding_configuration.cache_enabled.description: Specifies whether to Enable caching or not. Valid options are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`.

forwarding_configuration.cache_query_par.description: Defines cache behaviour in relation to query string parameters. Valid options are `StripAll`, `StripAllExcept`, `StripOnly` or `StripNone`. Defaults to `StripAll`.
Specify query parameters (array). Works only in combination with `cache_query_parameter_strip_directive` set to `StripAllExcept` or `StripOnly`.

forwarding_configuration.cache_use_dynam.description: Whether to use dynamic compression when caching. Valid options are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`.

forwarding_configuration.custom_forwardi.description: Path to use when constructing the request to forward to the backend. This functions as a URL Rewrite. Default behaviour preserves the URL path.

forwarding_configuration.forwarding_prot.description: Protocol to use when redirecting. Valid options are `HttpOnly`, `HttpsOnly`, or `MatchRequest`. Defaults to `HttpsOnly`.

friendly_name.description: A friendly name for the Front Door service.

frontend_endpoint.host_name.description: Specifies the host name of the `frontend_endpoint`. Must be a domain name. In order to use a name.azurefd.net domain, the name value must match the Front Door name.

frontend_endpoint.session_affinity_enabl.description: Whether to allow session affinity on this host. Valid options are `true` or `false` Defaults to `false`.

frontend_endpoint.session_affinity_ttl_s.description: The TTL to use in seconds for session affinity, if applicable. Defaults to `0`.

frontend_endpoint.web_application_firewa.description: Defines the Web Application Firewall policy `ID` for each host.

load_balancer_enabled.description: Should the Front Door Load Balancer be Enabled? Defaults to `true`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Front Door service. Must be globally unique

redirect_configuration.custom_fragment.description: The destination fragment in the portion of URL after '#'. Set this to add a fragment to the redirect URL.

redirect_configuration.custom_host.description: Set this to change the URL for the redirection.

redirect_configuration.custom_path.description: The path to retain as per the incoming request, or update in the URL for the redirection.

redirect_configuration.custom_query_stri.description: Replace any existing query string from the incoming request URL.

redirect_configuration.redirect_protocol.description: Protocol to use when redirecting. Valid options are `HttpOnly`, `HttpsOnly`, or `MatchRequest`.

redirect_configuration.redirect_type.description: Status code for the redirect. Valida options are `Moved`, `Found`, `TemporaryRedirect`, `PermanentRedirect`.

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the Resource Group in which the Front Door service should exist

routing_rule.accepted_protocols.description: Protocol schemes to match for the Backend Routing Rule. Possible values are `Http` and `Https`.

routing_rule.enabled.description: `Enable` or `Disable` use of this Backend Routing Rule. Permitted values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `true`.

routing_rule.frontend_endpoints.description: The names of the `frontend_endpoint` blocks within this resource to associate with this `routing_rule`.

routing_rule.name.description: Specifies the name of the Routing Rule.

routing_rule.patterns_to_match.description: The route patterns for the Backend Routing Rule.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

custom_https_configuration.azure_key_vau.description: The name of the Key Vault secret representing the full certificate PFX.
The version of the Key Vault secret representing the full certificate PFX.
The ID of the Key Vault containing the SSL certificate.

custom_https_configuration.certificate_s.description: Certificate source to encrypted `HTTPS` traffic with. Allowed values are `FrontDoor` or `AzureKeyVault`. Defaults to `FrontDoor`.

custom_https_provisioning_enabled.description: Should the HTTPS protocol be enabled for this custom domain associated with the Front Door?

frontend_endpoint_id.description: The ID of the Front Door Frontend Endpoint which this configuration refers to

custom_block_response_body.description: If a `custom_rule` block's action type is `block`, this is the response body. The body must be specified in base64 encoding.

custom_block_response_status_code.description: If a `custom_rule` block's action type is `block`, this is the response status code. Possible values are `200`, `403`, `405`, `406`, or `429`.

custom_rule.description: One or more `custom_rule` blocks as defined below.

custom_rule.action.description: The action to perform when the rule is matched. Possible values are `Allow`, `Block`, `Log`, or `Redirect`.

custom_rule.enabled.description: Is the rule is enabled or disabled? Defaults to `true`.

custom_rule.match_condition.description: One or more `match_condition` block defined below. Can support up to `10` `match_condition` blocks.

custom_rule.name.description: Gets name of the resource that is unique within a policy. This name can be used to access the resource.

custom_rule.priority.description: The priority of the rule. Rules with a lower value will be evaluated before rules with a higher value. Defaults to `1`.

custom_rule.rate_limit_duration_in_minut.description: The rate limit duration in minutes. Defaults to `1`.

custom_rule.rate_limit_threshold.description: The rate limit threshold. Defaults to `10`.

custom_rule.type.description: The type of rule. Possible values are `MatchRule` or `RateLimitRule`.

enabled.description: Is the policy a enabled state or disabled state. Defaults to `true`.

exclusion.match_variable.description: The variable type to be excluded. Possible values are `QueryStringArgNames`, `RequestBodyPostArgNames`, `RequestCookieNames`, `RequestHeaderNames`.

exclusion.operator.description: Comparison operator to apply to the selector when specifying which elements in the collection this exclusion applies to. Possible values are: `Equals`, `Contains`, `StartsWith`, `EndsWith`, `EqualsAny`.

exclusion.selector.description: Selector for the value in the `match_variable` attribute this exclusion applies to.

managed_rule.description: One or more `managed_rule` blocks as defined below.

managed_rule.exclusion.description: One or more `exclusion` blocks as defined below.

managed_rule.override.description: One or more `override` blocks as defined below.

managed_rule.type.description: The name of the managed rule to use with this resource.

managed_rule.version.description: The version on the managed rule to use with this resource.

match_condition.match_values.description: Up to `600` possible values to match. Limit is in total across all `match_condition` blocks and `match_values` arguments. String value itself can be up to `256` characters long.

match_condition.match_variable.description: The request variable to compare with. Possible values are `Cookies`, `PostArgs`, `QueryString`, `RemoteAddr`, `RequestBody`, `RequestHeader`, `RequestMethod`, `RequestUri`, or `SocketAddr`.

match_condition.negation_condition.description: Should the result of the condition be negated.

match_condition.operator.description: Comparison type to use for matching with the variable value. Possible values are `Any`, `BeginsWith`, `Contains`, `EndsWith`, `Equal`, `GeoMatch`, `GreaterThan`, `GreaterThanOrEqual`, `IPMatch`, `LessThan`, `LessThanOrEqual` or `RegEx`.

match_condition.selector.description: Match against a specific key if the `match_variable` is `QueryString`, `PostArgs`, `RequestHeader` or `Cookies`.

match_condition.transforms.description: Up to `5` transforms to apply. Possible values are `Lowercase`, `RemoveNulls`, `Trim`, `Uppercase`, `URLDecode` or`URLEncode`.

mode.description: The firewall policy mode. Possible values are `Detection`, `Prevention`. Defaults to `Prevention`.

name.description: The name of the policy

override.exclusion.description: One or more `exclusion` blocks as defined below.

override.rule.description: One or more `rule` blocks as defined below. If none are specified, all of the rules in the group will be disabled.

override.rule_group_name.description: The managed rule group to override.

redirect_url.description: If action type is redirect, this field represents redirect URL for the client.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group

rule.action.description: The action to be applied when the rule matches. Possible values are `Allow`, `Block`, `Log`, or `Redirect`.

rule.enabled.description: Is the managed rule override enabled or disabled. Defaults to `false`

rule.exclusion.description: One or more `exclusion` blocks as defined below.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the Web Application Firewall Policy.

enabled.description: Whether this Rules engine configuration is enabled? Defaults to `true`.

frontdoor_name.description: The name of the Front Door instance

match_condition.negate_condition.description: can be set to `true` or `false` to negate the given condition. Defaults to `false`.

match_condition.selector.description: match against a specific key when `variable` is set to `PostArgs` or `RequestHeader`. It cannot be used with `QueryString` and `RequestMethod`.

match_condition.transform.description: can be set to one or more values out of `Lowercase`, `RemoveNulls`, `Trim`, `Uppercase`, `UrlDecode` and `UrlEncode`

match_condition.value.description: (array) can contain one or more strings.

name.description: The name of the Rules engine configuration

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group

rule.match_condition.description: One or more `match_condition` block as defined below.

rule.priority.description: Priority of the rule, must be unique per rules engine definition.

active_directory.allowed_audiences.description: Allowed audience values to consider when validating JWTs issued by Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_id.description: The Client ID of this relying party application. Enables OpenIDConnection authentication with Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_secret.description: The Client Secret of this relying party application. If no secret is provided, implicit flow will be used.

app_service_plan_id.description: The ID of the App Service Plan within which to create this Function App.

app_settings.description: A map of key-value pairs for [App Settings](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/functions-app-settings) and custom values.

auth_settings.additional_login_params.description: Login parameters to send to the OpenID Connect authorization endpoint when a user logs in. Each parameter must be in the form "key=value".

auth_settings.allowed_external_redirect_.description: External URLs that can be redirected to as part of logging in or logging out of the app.

auth_settings.default_provider.description: The default provider to use when multiple providers have been set up. Possible values are `AzureActiveDirectory`, `Facebook`, `Google`, `MicrosoftAccount` and `Twitter`.

auth_settings.issuer.description: Issuer URI. When using Azure Active Directory, this value is the URI of the directory tenant, e.g. <https://sts.windows.net/{tenant-guid}/>.

auth_settings.runtime_version.description: The runtime version of the Authentication/Authorization module.

auth_settings.token_refresh_extension_ho.description: The number of hours after session token expiration that a session token can be used to call the token refresh API. Defaults to `72`.

auth_settings.token_store_enabled.description: If enabled the module will durably store platform-specific security tokens that are obtained during login flows. Defaults to `false`.

auth_settings.unauthenticated_client_act.description: The action to take when an unauthenticated client attempts to access the app. Possible values are `AllowAnonymous` and `RedirectToLoginPage`.

client_cert_mode.description: The mode of the Function App's client certificates requirement for incoming requests. Possible values are `Required` and `Optional`.

connection_string.description: An `connection_string` block as defined below.

connection_string.name.description: The name of the Connection String.

connection_string.type.description: The type of the Connection String. Possible values are `APIHub`, `Custom`, `DocDb`, `EventHub`, `MySQL`, `NotificationHub`, `PostgreSQL`, `RedisCache`, `ServiceBus`, `SQLAzure` and `SQLServer`.

connection_string.value.description: The value for the Connection String.

cors.allowed_origins.description: A list of origins which should be able to make cross-origin calls. `*` can be used to allow all calls.

daily_memory_time_quota.description: The amount of memory in gigabyte-seconds that your application is allowed to consume per day. Setting this value only affects function apps under the consumption plan.

enable_builtin_logging.description: Should the built-in logging of this Function App be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

enabled.description: Is the Function App enabled? Defaults to `true`.

facebook.app_id.description: The App ID of the Facebook app used for login

facebook.app_secret.description: The App Secret of the Facebook app used for Facebook login.

facebook.oauth_scopes.description: The OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Facebook login authentication. <https://developers.facebook.com/docs/facebook-login>

google.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Google web application.

google.client_secret.description: The client secret associated with the Google web application.

google.oauth_scopes.description: The OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication. <https://developers.google.com/identity/sign-in/web/>

headers.x_azure_fdid.description: A list of allowed Azure FrontDoor IDs in UUID notation with a maximum of 8.

headers.x_fd_health_probe.description: A list to allow the Azure FrontDoor health probe header. Only allowed value is "1".

headers.x_forwarded_for.description: A list of allowed 'X-Forwarded-For' IPs in CIDR notation with a maximum of 8

headers.x_forwarded_host.description: A list of allowed 'X-Forwarded-Host' domains with a maximum of 8.

https_only.description: Can the Function App only be accessed via HTTPS? Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of user managed identity ids to be assigned. Required if `type` is `UserAssigned`.

identity.type.description: Specifies the identity type of the Function App. Possible values are `SystemAssigned` (where Azure will generate a Service Principal for you), `UserAssigned` where you can specify the Service Principal IDs in the `identity_ids` field, and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` which assigns both a system managed identity as well as the specified user assigned identities.

ip_restriction.action.description: Does this restriction `Allow` or `Deny` access for this IP range. Defaults to `Allow`.

ip_restriction.headers.description: The `headers` block for this specific `ip_restriction` as defined below.

ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The IP Address used for this IP Restriction in CIDR notation.

ip_restriction.name.description: The name for this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority for this IP Restriction. Restrictions are enforced in priority order. By default, the priority is set to 65000 if not specified.

ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.virtual_network_subnet_id.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

key_vault_reference_identity_id.description: The User Assigned Identity Id used for looking up KeyVault secrets. The identity must be assigned to the application. See [Access vaults with a user-assigned identity](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/app-service-key-vault-references#access-vaults-with-a-user-assigned-identity) for more information.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

microsoft.client_id.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft.client_secret.description: The OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft.oauth_scopes.description: The OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Microsoft Account authentication. <https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/dn631845.aspx>

name.description: Specifies the name of the Function App

os_type.description: A string indicating the Operating System type for this function app. Possible values are `linux` and ``(empty string)

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Function App

scm_ip_restriction.action.description: Allow or Deny access for this IP range. Defaults to `Allow`.

scm_ip_restriction.headers.description: The `headers` block for this specific `scm_ip_restriction` as defined below.

scm_ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The IP Address used for this IP Restriction in CIDR notation.

scm_ip_restriction.name.description: The name for this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority for this IP Restriction. Restrictions are enforced in priority order. By default, priority is set to 65000 if not specified.

scm_ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.virtual_network_subne.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

site_config.always_on.description: Should the Function App be loaded at all times? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.app_scale_limit.description: The number of workers this function app can scale out to. Only applicable to apps on the Consumption and Premium plan.

site_config.auto_swap_slot_name.description: The name of the slot to automatically swap to during deployment

site_config.dotnet_framework_version.description: The version of the .NET framework's CLR used in this function app. Possible values are `v4.0` (including .NET Core 2.1 and 3.1), `v5.0` and `v6.0`. [For more information on which .NET Framework version to use based on the runtime version you're targeting - please see this table](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/functions-dotnet-class-library#supported-versions). Defaults to `v4.0`.

site_config.elastic_instance_minimum.description: The number of minimum instances for this function app. Only affects apps on the Premium plan. Possible values are between `1` and `20`.

site_config.ftps_state.description: State of FTP / FTPS service for this function app. Possible values include: `AllAllowed`, `FtpsOnly` and `Disabled`. Defaults to `AllAllowed`.

site_config.health_check_path.description: Path which will be checked for this function app health.

site_config.http2_enabled.description: Specifies whether or not the HTTP2 protocol should be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.ip_restriction.description: A list of `ip_restriction` objects representing IP restrictions as defined below.

site_config.java_version.description: Java version hosted by the function app in Azure. Possible values are `1.8`, `11` & `17` (In-Preview).

site_config.linux_fx_version.description: Linux App Framework and version for the AppService, e.g. `DOCKER|(golang:latest)`.

site_config.min_tls_version.description: The minimum supported TLS version for the function app. Possible values are `1.0`, `1.1`, and `1.2`. Defaults to `1.2` for new function apps.

site_config.pre_warmed_instance_count.description: The number of pre-warmed instances for this function app. Only affects apps on the Premium plan.

site_config.runtime_scale_monitoring_ena.description: Should Runtime Scale Monitoring be enabled?. Only applicable to apps on the Premium plan. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.scm_ip_restriction.description: A list of `scm_ip_restriction` objects representing IP restrictions as defined below.

site_config.scm_type.description: The type of Source Control used by the Function App. Valid values include: `BitBucketGit`, `BitBucketHg`, `CodePlexGit`, `CodePlexHg`, `Dropbox`, `ExternalGit`, `ExternalHg`, `GitHub`, `LocalGit`, `None` (default), `OneDrive`, `Tfs`, `VSO`, and `VSTSRM`.

site_config.scm_use_main_ip_restriction.description: IP security restrictions for scm to use main. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.use_32_bit_worker_process.description: Should the Function App run in 32 bit mode, rather than 64 bit mode? Defaults to `true`.

site_config.vnet_route_all_enabled.description: Should all outbound traffic to have Virtual Network Security Groups and User Defined Routes applied? Defaults to `false`.

source_control.branch.description: The branch of the remote repository to use. Defaults to 'master'.

source_control.manual_integration.description: Limits to manual integration. Defaults to `false` if not specified.

source_control.repo_url.description: The URL of the source code repository.

source_control.rollback_enabled.description: Enable roll-back for the repository. Defaults to `false` if not specified.

source_control.use_mercurial.description: Use Mercurial if `true`, otherwise uses Git.

storage_account_access_key.description: The access key which will be used to access the backend storage account for the Function App.

storage_account_name.description: The backend storage account name which will be used by this Function App (such as the dashboard, logs)

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

twitter.consumer_key.description: The OAuth 1.0a consumer key of the Twitter application used for sign-in.

twitter.consumer_secret.description: The OAuth 1.0a consumer secret of the Twitter application used for sign-in.

version.description: The runtime version associated with the Function App. Defaults to `~1`.

overwrite_network_config.description: The swap action should overwrite the Production slot's network configuration with the configuration from this slot. Defaults to `true`

slot_id.description: The ID of the Slot to swap with `Production`.

authentication.description: The authentication info. An `authentication` block as defined below.

authentication.certificate.description: Service principal certificate for `servicePrincipal` auth. Should be specified when `type` is set to `servicePrincipalCertificate`.

authentication.client_id.description: Client ID for `userAssignedIdentity` or `servicePrincipal` auth. Should be specified when `type` is set to `servicePrincipalSecret` or `servicePrincipalCertificate`. When `type` is set to `userAssignedIdentity`, `client_id` and `subscription_id` should be either both specified or both not specified.

authentication.client_type.description: The application client type. Possible values are `none`, `dotnet`, `java`, `python`, `go`, `php`, `ruby`, `django`, `nodejs` and `springBoot`. Defaults to `none`.

authentication.name.description: Username or account name for secret auth. `name` and `secret` should be either both specified or both not specified when `type` is set to `secret`.

authentication.principal_id.description: Principal ID for `servicePrincipal` auth. Should be specified when `type` is set to `servicePrincipalSecret` or `servicePrincipalCertificate`.

authentication.secret.description: Password or account key for secret auth. `secret` and `name` should be either both specified or both not specified when `type` is set to `secret`.

authentication.secret_store.description: An option to store secret value in secure place. An `secret_store` block as defined below.

authentication.subscription_id.description: Subscription ID for `userAssignedIdentity`. `subscription_id` and `client_id` should be either both specified or both not specified.

authentication.type.description: The authentication type. Possible values are `systemAssignedIdentity`, `userAssignedIdentity`, `servicePrincipalSecret`, `servicePrincipalCertificate`, `secret`

authentication.vnet_solution.description: The type of the VNet solution. Possible values are `serviceEndpoint`, `privateLink`.

function_app_id.description: The ID of the data source function app

name.description: The name of the service connection

secret_store.key_vault_id.description: The key vault id to store secret.

target_resource_id.description: The ID of the target resource

active_directory.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed audience values to consider when validating JWTs issued by Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_secret.description: The Client Secret for the Client ID. Cannot be used with `client_secret_setting_name`.

active_directory.client_secret_setting_n.description: The App Setting name that contains the client secret of the Client. Cannot be used with `client_secret`.

active_directory_v2.allowed_applications.description: The list of allowed Applications for the Default Authorisation Policy.

active_directory_v2.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed audience values to consider when validating JWTs issued by Azure Active Directory.

active_directory_v2.allowed_groups.description: The list of allowed Group Names for the Default Authorisation Policy.

active_directory_v2.allowed_identities.description: The list of allowed Identities for the Default Authorisation Policy.

active_directory_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with Azure Active Directory.

active_directory_v2.client_secret_certif.description: The thumbprint of the certificate used for signing purposes.

active_directory_v2.client_secret_settin.description: The App Setting name that contains the client secret of the Client.

active_directory_v2.jwt_allowed_client_a.description: A list of Allowed Client Applications in the JWT Claim.

active_directory_v2.jwt_allowed_groups.description: A list of Allowed Groups in the JWT Claim.

active_directory_v2.login_parameters.description: A map of key-value pairs to send to the Authorisation Endpoint when a user logs in.

active_directory_v2.tenant_auth_endpoint.description: The Azure Tenant Endpoint for the Authenticating Tenant. e.g. `https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant-guid}/v2.0/`

active_directory_v2.www_authentication_d.description: Should the www-authenticate provider should be omitted from the request? Defaults to `false`.

always_ready.description: One or more `always_ready` blocks as defined below.

always_ready.instance_count.description: The instance count of the `always_ready` of the Function App. The minimum number is `0`. The total number of `instance_count` should not exceed the `maximum_instance_count`.

always_ready.name.description: The name of the `always_ready` of the Function App.

app_service_logs.disk_quota_mb.description: The amount of disk space to use for logs. Valid values are between `25` and `100`. Defaults to `35`.

app_service_logs.retention_period_days.description: The retention period for logs in days. Valid values are between `0` and `99999`.(never delete).

app_settings.description: A map of key-value pairs for [App Settings](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/functions-app-settings) and custom values.

apple_v2.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Apple web application.

apple_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for Apple Login.

auth_settings.additional_login_parameter.description: Specifies a map of login Parameters to send to the OpenID Connect authorization endpoint when a user logs in.

auth_settings.allowed_external_redirect_.description: Specifies a list of External URLs that can be redirected to as part of logging in or logging out of the Linux Web App.

auth_settings.default_provider.description: The default authentication provider to use when multiple providers are configured. Possible values include: `AzureActiveDirectory`, `Facebook`, `Google`, `MicrosoftAccount`, `Twitter`, `Github`

auth_settings.enabled.description: Should the Authentication / Authorization feature be enabled for the Linux Web App?

auth_settings.issuer.description: The OpenID Connect Issuer URI that represents the entity which issues access tokens for this Linux Web App.

auth_settings.runtime_version.description: The RuntimeVersion of the Authentication / Authorization feature in use for the Linux Web App.

auth_settings.token_refresh_extension_ho.description: The number of hours after session token expiration that a session token can be used to call the token refresh API. Defaults to `72` hours.

auth_settings.token_store_enabled.description: Should the Linux Web App durably store platform-specific security tokens that are obtained during login flows? Defaults to `false`.

auth_settings.unauthenticated_client_act.description: The action to take when an unauthenticated client attempts to access the app. Possible values include: `RedirectToLoginPage`, `AllowAnonymous`.

auth_settings_v2.auth_enabled.description: Should the AuthV2 Settings be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

auth_settings_v2.config_file_path.description: The path to the App Auth settings.

auth_settings_v2.custom_oidc_v2.description: Zero or more `custom_oidc_v2` blocks as defined below.

auth_settings_v2.default_provider.description: The Default Authentication Provider to use when the `unauthenticated_action` is set to `RedirectToLoginPage`. Possible values include: `apple`, `azureactivedirectory`, `facebook`, `github`, `google`, `twitter` and the `name` of your `custom_oidc_v2` provider.

auth_settings_v2.excluded_paths.description: The paths which should be excluded from the `unauthenticated_action` when it is set to `RedirectToLoginPage`.

auth_settings_v2.forward_proxy_conventio.description: The convention used to determine the url of the request made. Possible values include `NoProxy`, `Standard`, `Custom`. Defaults to `NoProxy`.

auth_settings_v2.forward_proxy_custom_ho.description: The name of the custom header containing the host of the request.

auth_settings_v2.forward_proxy_custom_sc.description: The name of the custom header containing the scheme of the request.

auth_settings_v2.http_route_api_prefix.description: The prefix that should precede all the authentication and authorisation paths. Defaults to `/.auth`.

auth_settings_v2.require_authentication.description: Should the authentication flow be used for all requests.

auth_settings_v2.require_https.description: Should HTTPS be required on connections? Defaults to `true`.

auth_settings_v2.runtime_version.description: The Runtime Version of the Authentication and Authorisation feature of this App. Defaults to `~1`.

auth_settings_v2.unauthenticated_action.description: The action to take for requests made without authentication. Possible values include `RedirectToLoginPage`, `AllowAnonymous`, `Return401`, and `Return403`. Defaults to `RedirectToLoginPage`.

azure_static_web_app_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with Azure Static Web App Authentication.

client_certificate_enabled.description: Should the function app use Client Certificates.

client_certificate_exclusion_paths.description: Paths to exclude when using client certificates, separated by ;

client_certificate_mode.description: The mode of the Function App's client certificates requirement for incoming requests. Possible values are `Required`, `Optional`, and `OptionalInteractiveUser`. Defaults to `Optional`.

connection_string.description: One or more `connection_string` blocks as defined below.

connection_string.name.description: The name which should be used for this Connection.

connection_string.type.description: Type of database. Possible values include: `MySQL`, `SQLServer`, `SQLAzure`, `Custom`, `NotificationHub`, `ServiceBus`, `EventHub`, `APIHub`, `DocDb`, `RedisCache`, and `PostgreSQL`.

cors.allowed_origins.description: Specifies a list of origins that should be allowed to make cross-origin calls.

cors.support_credentials.description: Are credentials allowed in CORS requests? Defaults to `false`.

custom_oidc_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with the Custom OIDC.

custom_oidc_v2.name.description: The name of the Custom OIDC Authentication Provider.

custom_oidc_v2.name_claim_type.description: The name of the claim that contains the users name.

custom_oidc_v2.openid_configuration_endp.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for the Custom OIDC Login.

custom_oidc_v2.scopes.description: The list of the scopes that should be requested while authenticating.

enabled.description: Is the Function App enabled? Defaults to `true`.

facebook.app_id.description: The App ID of the Facebook app used for login.

facebook.app_secret.description: The App Secret of the Facebook app used for Facebook login. Cannot be specified with `app_secret_setting_name`.

facebook.app_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `app_secret` value used for Facebook login. Cannot be specified with `app_secret`.

facebook.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes to be requested as part of Facebook login authentication.

facebook_v2.app_id.description: The App ID of the Facebook app used for login.

facebook_v2.app_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `app_secret` value used for Facebook Login.

facebook_v2.graph_api_version.description: The version of the Facebook API to be used while logging in.

facebook_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of scopes that should be requested as part of Facebook Login authentication.

github.client_id.description: The ID of the GitHub app used for login.

github.client_secret.description: The Client Secret of the GitHub app used for GitHub login. Cannot be specified with `client_secret_setting_name`.

github.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for GitHub login. Cannot be specified with `client_secret`.

github.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of GitHub login authentication.

github_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the GitHub app used for login..

github_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for GitHub Login.

github_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that should be requested as part of GitHub Login authentication.

google.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Google web application.

google.client_secret.description: The client secret associated with the Google web application. Cannot be specified with `client_secret_setting_name`.

google.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for Google login. Cannot be specified with `client_secret`.

google.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication. If not specified, `openid`, `profile`, and `email` are used as default scopes.

google_v2.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed Audiences that should be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication.

google_v2.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Google web application.

google_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for Google Login.

google_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that should be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication.

headers.x_azure_fdid.description: Specifies a list of Azure Front Door IDs.

headers.x_fd_health_probe.description: Specifies if a Front Door Health Probe should be expected. The only possible value is `1`.

headers.x_forwarded_for.description: Specifies a list of addresses for which matching should be applied. Omitting this value means allow any.

headers.x_forwarded_host.description: Specifies a list of Hosts for which matching should be applied.

http_concurrency.description: The Http concurrency of the instances on which your app runs. The supported value are from `1` to `1000`.

https_only.description: Is Https Connection enforced to the function app. Defaults to `false`

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Linux Function App.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Linux Function App. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

instance_memory_in_mb.description: The memory size of the instances on which your app runs. Reference the Microsoft Documentation for the [currently supported values](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/flex-consumption-plan#instance-memory). Defaults to `2048`.

ip_restriction.action.description: The action to take. Possible values are `Allow` or `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

ip_restriction.description.description: The Description of this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The CIDR notation of the IP or IP Range to match. For example: `10.0.0.0/24` or `192.168.10.1/32`

ip_restriction.name.description: The name which should be used for this `ip_restriction`.

ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority value of this `ip_restriction`. Defaults to `65000`.

ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.virtual_network_subnet_id.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Function App should exist. Changing this forces a new Function App to be created.

login.allowed_external_redirect_urls.description: External URLs that can be redirected to as part of logging in or logging out of the app. This is an advanced setting typically only needed by Windows Store application backends.

login.cookie_expiration_convention.description: The method by which cookies expire. Possible values include: `FixedTime`, and `IdentityProviderDerived`. Defaults to `FixedTime`.

login.cookie_expiration_time.description: The time after the request is made when the session cookie should expire. Defaults to `08:00:00`.

login.logout_endpoint.description: The endpoint to which logout requests should be made.

login.nonce_expiration_time.description: The time after the request is made when the nonce should expire. Defaults to `00:05:00`.

login.preserve_url_fragments_for_logins.description: Should the fragments from the request be preserved after the login request is made. Defaults to `false`.

login.token_refresh_extension_time.description: The number of hours after session token expiration that a session token can be used to call the token refresh API. Defaults to `72` hours.

login.token_store_enabled.description: Should the Token Store configuration Enabled. Defaults to `false`

login.token_store_path.description: The directory path in the App Filesystem in which the tokens will be stored.

login.token_store_sas_setting_name.description: The name of the app setting which contains the SAS URL of the blob storage containing the tokens.

login.validate_nonce.description: Should the nonce be validated while completing the login flow. Defaults to `true`.

maximum_instance_count.description: The number of workers this function app can scale out to. The supported value are from `1` to `1000`.

microsoft.client_id.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft.client_secret.description: The OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication. Cannot be specified with `client_secret_setting_name`.

microsoft.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name containing the OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication. Cannot be specified with `client_secret`.

microsoft.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Microsoft Account authentication. If not specified, `wl.basic` is used as the default scope.

microsoft_v2.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed Audiences that will be requested as part of Microsoft Sign-In authentication.

microsoft_v2.client_id.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name containing the OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of Login scopes that should be requested as part of Microsoft Account authentication.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Function App. Changing this forces a new Function App to be created. Limit the function name to 32 characters to avoid naming collisions. For more information about [Function App naming rule](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/resource-name-rules#microsoftweb) and [Host ID Collisions](https://github.com/Azure/azure-functions-host/wiki/Host-IDs#host-id-collisions)

public_network_access_enabled.description: Should public network access be enabled for the Function App. Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Function App should exist. Changing this forces a new Linux Function App to be created.

runtime_name.description: The Runtime of the Linux Function App. Possible values are `node`, `dotnet-isolated`, `powershell`, `python`, `java` and `custom`.

runtime_version.description: The Runtime version of the Linux Function App. Accepted values varies with the value of `runtime_name`.

schedule.frequency_interval.description: How often the backup should be executed (e.g. for weekly backup, this should be set to `7` and `frequency_unit` should be set to `Day`).

schedule.frequency_unit.description: The unit of time for how often the backup should take place. Possible values include: `Day` and `Hour`.

schedule.keep_at_least_one_backup.description: Should the service keep at least one backup, regardless of age of backup. Defaults to `false`.

schedule.retention_period_days.description: After how many days backups should be deleted. Defaults to `30`.

schedule.start_time.description: When the schedule should start working in RFC-3339 format.

scm_ip_restriction.action.description: The action to take. Possible values are `Allow` or `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

scm_ip_restriction.description.description: The Description of this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The CIDR notation of the IP or IP Range to match. For example: `10.0.0.0/24` or `192.168.10.1/32`

scm_ip_restriction.name.description: The name which should be used for this `ip_restriction`.

scm_ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority value of this `ip_restriction`. Defaults to `65000`.

scm_ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.virtual_network_subne.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

service_plan_id.description: The ID of the App Service Plan within which to create this Function App. Changing this forces a new Linux Function App to be created.

site_config.api_definition_url.description: The URL of the API definition that describes this Linux Function App.

site_config.api_management_api_id.description: The ID of the API Management API for this Linux Function App.

site_config.app_command_line.description: The App command line to launch.

site_config.application_insights_connect.description: The Connection String for linking the Linux Function App to Application Insights.

site_config.application_insights_key.description: The Instrumentation Key for connecting the Linux Function App to Application Insights.

site_config.container_registry_managed_i.description: The Client ID of the Managed Service Identity to use for connections to the Azure Container Registry.

site_config.container_registry_use_manag.description: Should connections for Azure Container Registry use Managed Identity.

site_config.default_documents.description: Specifies a list of Default Documents for the Linux Web App.

site_config.health_check_eviction_time_i.description: The amount of time in minutes that a node can be unhealthy before being removed from the load balancer. Possible values are between `2` and `10`. Only valid in conjunction with `health_check_path`.

site_config.health_check_path.description: The path to be checked for this function app health.

site_config.http2_enabled.description: Specifies if the HTTP2 protocol should be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.ip_restriction.description: One or more `ip_restriction` blocks as defined above.

site_config.ip_restriction_default_actio.description: The Default action for traffic that does not match any `ip_restriction` rule. possible values include `Allow` and `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

site_config.load_balancing_mode.description: The Site load balancing mode. Possible values include: `WeightedRoundRobin`, `LeastRequests`, `LeastResponseTime`, `WeightedTotalTraffic`, `RequestHash`, `PerSiteRoundRobin`. Defaults to `LeastRequests` if omitted.

site_config.managed_pipeline_mode.description: Managed pipeline mode. Possible values include: `Integrated`, `Classic`. Defaults to `Integrated`.

site_config.minimum_tls_version.description: The configures the minimum version of TLS required for SSL requests. Possible values include: `1.0`, `1.1`, `1.2` and `1.3`. Defaults to `1.2`.

site_config.remote_debugging_enabled.description: Should Remote Debugging be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.remote_debugging_version.description: The Remote Debugging Version. Possible values include `VS2017`, `VS2019`, and `VS2022`.

site_config.runtime_scale_monitoring_ena.description: Should Scale Monitoring of the Functions Runtime be enabled?

site_config.scm_ip_restriction.description: One or more `scm_ip_restriction` blocks as defined above.

site_config.scm_ip_restriction_default_a.description: The Default action for traffic that does not match any `scm_ip_restriction` rule. possible values include `Allow` and `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

site_config.scm_minimum_tls_version.description: The minimum version of TLS required for SSL requests to the SCM site. Possible values include `1.0`, `1.1`, `1.2` and `1.3`. Defaults to `1.2`.

site_config.scm_use_main_ip_restriction.description: Should the Linux Function App `ip_restriction` configuration be used for the SCM also.

site_config.use_32_bit_worker.description: Should the Linux Web App Linux Function App use a 32-bit worker. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.vnet_route_all_enabled.description: Should the Linux Function App route all traffic through the virtual network. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.websockets_enabled.description: Should Web Sockets be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.worker_count.description: The number of Workers for this Linux Function App.

sticky_settings.app_setting_names.description: A list of `app_setting` names that the Linux Function App will not swap between Slots when a swap operation is triggered.

sticky_settings.connection_string_names.description: A list of `connection_string` names that the Linux Function App will not swap between Slots when a swap operation is triggered.

storage_access_key.description: The access key which will be used to access the backend storage account for the Function App.

storage_authentication_type.description: The authentication type which will be used to access the backend storage account for the Function App. Possible values are `StorageAccountConnectionString`, `SystemAssignedIdentity`, and `UserAssignedIdentity`.

storage_container_endpoint.description: The backend storage container endpoint which will be used by this Function App.

storage_container_type.description: The storage container type used for the Function App. The current supported type is `blobContainer`.

storage_user_assigned_identity_id.description: The user assigned Managed Identity to access the storage account. Conflicts with `storage_access_key`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Linux Function App.

twitter.consumer_key.description: The OAuth 1.0a consumer key of the Twitter application used for sign-in.

twitter.consumer_secret.description: The OAuth 1.0a consumer secret of the Twitter application used for sign-in. Cannot be specified with `consumer_secret_setting_name`.

twitter.consumer_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the OAuth 1.0a consumer secret of the Twitter application used for sign-in. Cannot be specified with `consumer_secret`.

twitter_v2.consumer_key.description: The OAuth 1.0a consumer key of the Twitter application used for sign-in.

twitter_v2.consumer_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the OAuth 1.0a consumer secret of the Twitter application used for sign-in.

virtual_network_subnet_id.description: The subnet id which will be used by this Function App for [regional virtual network integration](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-vnet-integration#regional-virtual-network-integration).

webdeploy_publish_basic_authentication_e.description: Should the default WebDeploy Basic Authentication publishing credentials enabled. Defaults to `true`.

zip_deploy_file.description: The local path and filename of the Zip packaged application to deploy to this Linux Function App.

config_json.description: The config for this Function in JSON format.

enabled.description: Should this function be enabled. Defaults to `true`.

file.name.description: The filename of the file to be uploaded

function_app_id.description: The ID of the Function App in which this function should reside

language.description: The language the Function is written in. Possible values are `CSharp`, `Custom`, `Java`, `Javascript`, `Python`, `PowerShell`, and `TypeScript`.

name.description: The name of the function

test_data.description: The test data for the function.

function_app_id.description: The ID of the Function App for this Hybrid Connection

port.description: The port to use for the endpoint

relay_id.description: The ID of the Relay Hybrid Connection to use

send_key_name.description: The name of the Relay key with `Send` permission to use. Defaults to `RootManageSharedAccessKey`

active_directory.allowed_audiences.description: Allowed audience values to consider when validating JWTs issued by Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_id.description: The Client ID of this relying party application. Enables OpenIDConnection authentication with Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_secret.description: The Client Secret of this relying party application. If no secret is provided, implicit flow will be used.

app_service_plan_id.description: The ID of the App Service Plan within which to create this Function App Slot

app_settings.description: A key-value pair of App Settings.

auth_settings.additional_login_params.description: login parameters to send to the OpenID Connect authorization endpoint when a user logs in. Each parameter must be in the form "key=value".

auth_settings.allowed_external_redirect_.description: External URLs that can be redirected to as part of logging in or logging out of the app.

auth_settings.default_provider.description: The default provider to use when multiple providers have been set up. Possible values are `AzureActiveDirectory`, `Facebook`, `Google`, `MicrosoftAccount` and `Twitter`.

auth_settings.issuer.description: Issuer URI. When using Azure Active Directory, this value is the URI of the directory tenant, e.g. <https://sts.windows.net/{tenant-guid}/>.

auth_settings.runtime_version.description: The runtime version of the Authentication/Authorization module.

auth_settings.token_refresh_extension_ho.description: The number of hours after session token expiration that a session token can be used to call the token refresh API. Defaults to `72`.

auth_settings.token_store_enabled.description: If enabled the module will durably store platform-specific security tokens that are obtained during login flows. Defaults to `false`.

auth_settings.unauthenticated_client_act.description: The action to take when an unauthenticated client attempts to access the app. Possible values are `AllowAnonymous` and `RedirectToLoginPage`.

connection_string.description: A `connection_string` block as defined below.

connection_string.name.description: The name of the Connection String.

connection_string.type.description: The type of the Connection String. Possible values are `APIHub`, `Custom`, `DocDb`, `EventHub`, `MySQL`, `NotificationHub`, `PostgreSQL`, `RedisCache`, `ServiceBus`, `SQLAzure` and `SQLServer`.

connection_string.value.description: The value for the Connection String.

cors.allowed_origins.description: A list of origins which should be able to make cross-origin calls. `*` can be used to allow all calls.

daily_memory_time_quota.description: The amount of memory in gigabyte-seconds that your application is allowed to consume per day. Setting this value only affects function apps under the consumption plan.

enable_builtin_logging.description: Should the built-in logging of the Function App be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

enabled.description: Is the Function App enabled? Defaults to `true`.

facebook.app_id.description: The App ID of the Facebook app used for login

facebook.app_secret.description: The App Secret of the Facebook app used for Facebook login.

facebook.oauth_scopes.description: The OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Facebook login authentication. <https://developers.facebook.com/docs/facebook-login>

function_app_name.description: The name of the Function App within which to create the Function App Slot

google.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Google web application.

google.client_secret.description: The client secret associated with the Google web application.

google.oauth_scopes.description: The OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication. <https://developers.google.com/identity/sign-in/web/>

headers.x_azure_fdid.description: A list of allowed Azure FrontDoor IDs in UUID notation with a maximum of 8.

headers.x_fd_health_probe.description: A list to allow the Azure FrontDoor health probe header. Only allowed value is "1".

headers.x_forwarded_for.description: A list of allowed 'X-Forwarded-For' IPs in CIDR notation with a maximum of 8

headers.x_forwarded_host.description: A list of allowed 'X-Forwarded-Host' domains with a maximum of 8.

https_only.description: Can the Function App only be accessed via HTTPS? Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of user managed identity ids to be assigned. Required if `type` is `UserAssigned`.

site_config.always_on.description: Should the Function App be loaded at all times? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.app_scale_limit.description: The number of workers this function app can scale out to. Only applicable to apps on the Consumption and Premium plan.

site_config.auto_swap_slot_name.description: The name of the slot to automatically swap to during deployment

site_config.dotnet_framework_version.description: The version of the .NET framework's CLR used in this function app. Possible values are `v4.0` (including .NET Core 2.1 and 3.1), `v5.0` and `v6.0`. [For more information on which .NET Framework version to use based on the runtime version you're targeting - please see this table](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/functions-dotnet-class-library#supported-versions). Defaults to `v4.0`.

site_config.elastic_instance_minimum.description: The number of minimum instances for this function app. Only applicable to apps on the Premium plan.

site_config.ftps_state.description: State of FTP / FTPS service for this function app. Possible values include: `AllAllowed`, `FtpsOnly` and `Disabled`.

site_config.health_check_path.description: Path which will be checked for this function app health.

site_config.http2_enabled.description: Specifies whether or not the HTTP2 protocol should be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.ip_restriction.description: A list of `ip_restriction` objects representing IP restrictions as defined below.

site_config.java_version.description: Java version hosted by the function app in Azure. Possible values are `1.8`, `11` & `17` (In-Preview).

site_config.linux_fx_version.description: Linux App Framework and version for the AppService, e.g. `DOCKER|(golang:latest)`.

site_config.min_tls_version.description: The minimum supported TLS version for the function app. Possible values are `1.0`, `1.1`, and `1.2`. Defaults to `1.2` for new function apps.

site_config.pre_warmed_instance_count.description: The number of pre-warmed instances for this function app. Only affects apps on the Premium plan.

site_config.runtime_scale_monitoring_ena.description: Should Runtime Scale Monitoring be enabled?. Only applicable to apps on the Premium plan. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.scm_ip_restriction.description: A list of `scm_ip_restriction` objects representing IP restrictions as defined below.

site_config.scm_type.description: The type of Source Control used by this function App. Valid values include: `BitBucketGit`, `BitBucketHg`, `CodePlexGit`, `CodePlexHg`, `Dropbox`, `ExternalGit`, `ExternalHg`, `GitHub`, `LocalGit`, `None` (default), `OneDrive`, `Tfs`, `VSO`, and `VSTSRM`.

site_config.scm_use_main_ip_restriction.description: IP security restrictions for scm to use main. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.use_32_bit_worker_process.description: Should the Function App run in 32 bit mode, rather than 64 bit mode? Defaults to `true`.

location.description: Specifies The Azure Region where the resource exists. Changing this force a new resource to be created.

name.description: Specifies The name of the Healthbot Service resource

resource_group_name.description: Specifies The name of the Resource Group in which to create the Healthbot Service

sku_name.description: The name which should be used for the SKU of the service. Possible values are `C0`, `C1`, `F0`, `PES` and `S1`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the service.

cors.allow_credentials.description: Whether to allow credentials in CORS. Defaults to `false`.

cors.allowed_headers.description: A list of allowed headers for CORS.

cors.allowed_methods.description: A list of allowed methods for CORS.

cors.allowed_origins.description: A list of allowed origins for CORS.

cors.max_age_in_seconds.description: The maximum age in seconds for the CORS configuration (must be between 0 and 99998 inclusive).

data_partitions_enabled.description: If data partitions are enabled or not. Defaults to `false`. Changing this forces a new Healthcare DICOM Service to be created.

encryption_key_url.description: The URL of the key to use for encryption as part of the customer-managed key encryption settings. For more details, refer to the [Azure Customer-Managed Keys Overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/common/customer-managed-keys-overview).

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Assigned Identity IDs which should be assigned to this Healthcare DICOM service.

identity.type.description: The type of identity used for the Healthcare DICOM service. Possible values are `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned` and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned`. If `UserAssigned` is set, an `identity_ids` must be set as well.

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the Healthcare DICOM Service should be created. Changing this forces a new Healthcare DICOM Service to be created.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Healthcare DICOM Service. Changing this forces a new Healthcare DICOM Service to be created.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether to enabled public networks when data plane traffic coming from public networks while private endpoint is enabled. Defaults to `true`.

storage.file_system_name.description: The filesystem name of connected storage account. Changing this forces a new Healthcare DICOM Service to be created.

storage.storage_account_id.description: The resource ID of connected storage account. Changing this forces a new Healthcare DICOM Service to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the Healthcare DICOM Service.

workspace_id.description: Specifies the ID of the Healthcare Workspace where the Healthcare DICOM Service should exist. Changing this forces a new Healthcare DICOM Service to be created.

access_policy_object_ids.description: A list of the access policies of the service instance.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of one or more Resource IDs for User Assigned Managed identities to assign. Required when `type` is set to `UserAssigned`.

identity.type.description: The type of managed identity to assign. Possible values are `UserAssigned` and `SystemAssigned`.

access_policy_object_ids.description: A set of Azure object IDs that are allowed to access the Service. If not configured, the default value is the object id of the service principal or user that is running Terraform.

authentication_configuration.smart_proxy.description: (Boolean) Enables the 'SMART on FHIR' option for mobile and web implementations.

cors_configuration.allow_credentials.description: (Boolean) If credentials are allowed via CORS.

cors_configuration.allowed_headers.description: A set of headers to be allowed via CORS.

cors_configuration.allowed_methods.description: The methods to be allowed via CORS. Possible values are `DELETE`, `GET`, `HEAD`, `MERGE`, `POST`, `OPTIONS`, `PATCH` and `PUT`.

cors_configuration.allowed_origins.description: A set of origins to be allowed via CORS.

cors_configuration.max_age_in_seconds.description: The max age to be allowed via CORS.

cosmosdb_key_vault_key_versionless_id.description: A versionless Key Vault Key ID for CMK encryption of the backing database

cosmosdb_throughput.description: The provisioned throughput for the backing database. Range of `400`-`100000`. Defaults to `1000`.

identity.type.description: The type of managed identity to assign. The only possible value is `SystemAssigned`.

kind.description: The type of the service. Values at time of publication are: `fhir`, `fhir-Stu3` and `fhir-R4`. Default value is `fhir`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure Region where the Service should be created

name.description: The name of the service instance. Used for service endpoint, must be unique within the audience

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether public network access is enabled or disabled for this service instance. Defaults to `true`.

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the Healthcare Workspace should be created. Changing this forces a new Healthcare Workspace to be created.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Healthcare Workspace. Changing this forces a new Healthcare Workspace to be created.
