tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the Healthcare Workspace.

access_rule.access.description: The access level for this rule. Possible values are: `rw`, `ro`, `no`.

access_rule.anonymous_gid.description: The anonymous GID used when `root_squash_enabled` is `true`.

access_rule.anonymous_uid.description: The anonymous UID used when `root_squash_enabled` is `true`.

access_rule.filter.description: The filter applied to the `scope` for this rule. The filter's format depends on its scope: `default` scope matches all clients and has no filter value; `network` scope takes a CIDR format; `host` takes an IP address or fully qualified domain name. If a client does not match any filter rule and there is no default rule, access is denied.

access_rule.scope.description: The scope of this rule. The `scope` and (potentially) the `filter` determine which clients match the rule. Possible values are: `default`, `network`, `host`.

access_rule.submount_access_enabled.description: Whether allow access to subdirectories under the root export?

automatically_rotate_key_to_latest_enabl.description: Specifies whether the HPC Cache automatically rotates Encryption Key to the latest version.

bind.dn.description: The Bind Distinguished Name (DN) identity to be used in the secure LDAP connection.

bind.password.description: The Bind password to be used in the secure LDAP connection.

cache_size_in_gb.description: The size of the HPC Cache, in GB. Possible values are `3072`, `6144`, `12288`, `21623`, `24576`, `43246`, `49152` and `86491`

default_access_policy.access_rule.description: One or more `access_rule` blocks (up to three) as defined above.

directory_active_directory.cache_netbios.description: The NetBIOS name to assign to the HPC Cache when it joins the Active Directory domain as a server.

directory_active_directory.dns_primary_i.description: The primary DNS IP address used to resolve the Active Directory domain controller's FQDN.

directory_active_directory.dns_secondary.description: The secondary DNS IP address used to resolve the Active Directory domain controller's FQDN.

directory_active_directory.domain_name.description: The fully qualified domain name of the Active Directory domain controller.

directory_active_directory.domain_netbio.description: The Active Directory domain's NetBIOS name.

directory_active_directory.password.description: The password of the Active Directory domain administrator.

directory_active_directory.username.description: The username of the Active Directory domain administrator.

directory_flat_file.group_file_uri.description: The URI of the file containing group information (`/etc/group` file format in Unix-like OS).

directory_flat_file.password_file_uri.description: The URI of the file containing user information (`/etc/passwd` file format in Unix-like OS).

directory_ldap.base_dn.description: The base distinguished name (DN) for the LDAP domain.

directory_ldap.certificate_validation_ur.description: The URI of the CA certificate to validate the LDAP secure connection.

directory_ldap.download_certificate_auto.description: Whether the certificate should be automatically downloaded. This can be set to `true` only when `certificate_validation_uri` is provided.

directory_ldap.encrypted.description: Whether the LDAP connection should be encrypted?

directory_ldap.server.description: The FQDN or IP address of the LDAP server.

dns.search_domain.description: The DNS search domain for the HPC Cache.

dns.servers.description: A list of DNS servers for the HPC Cache. At most three IP(s) are allowed to set.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this HPC Cache

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this HPC Cache. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both)

key_vault_key_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault Key which should be used to encrypt the data in this HPC Cache.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure Region where the HPC Cache should be created

mtu.description: The IPv4 maximum transmission unit configured for the subnet of the HPC Cache. Possible values range from 576 - 1500. Defaults to `1500`.

name.description: The name of the HPC Cache

ntp_server.description: The NTP server IP Address or FQDN for the HPC Cache. Defaults to `time.windows.com`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group in which to create the HPC Cache

sku_name.description: The SKU of HPC Cache to use. Possible values are (ReadWrite) - `Standard_2G`, `Standard_4G` `Standard_8G` or (ReadOnly) - `Standard_L4_5G`, `Standard_L9G`, and `Standard_L16G`

subnet_id.description: The ID of the Subnet for the HPC Cache

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the HPC Cache.

access_rule.description: One or more `access_rule` blocks (up to three) as defined below.

access_rule.access.description: The access level for this rule. Possible values are: `rw`, `ro`, `no`.

access_rule.anonymous_gid.description: The anonymous GID used when `root_squash_enabled` is `true`.

access_rule.anonymous_uid.description: The anonymous UID used when `root_squash_enabled` is `true`.

access_rule.filter.description: The filter applied to the `scope` for this rule. The filter's format depends on its scope: `default` scope matches all clients and has no filter value; `network` scope takes a CIDR format; `host` takes an IP address or fully qualified domain name. If a client does not match any filter rule and there is no default rule, access is denied.

access_rule.scope.description: The scope of this rule. The `scope` and (potentially) the `filter` determine which clients match the rule. Possible values are: `default`, `network`, `host`.

access_rule.submount_access_enabled.description: Whether allow access to subdirectories under the root export?

hpc_cache_id.description: The ID of the HPC Cache that this HPC Cache Access Policy resides in. Changing this forces a new HPC Cache Access Policy to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this HPC Cache Access Policy. Changing this forces a new HPC Cache Access Policy to be created.

access_policy_name.description: The name of the access policy applied to this target. Defaults to `default`.

cache_name.description: The name of the HPC Cache, which the HPC Cache Blob NFS Target will be added to. Changing this forces a new HPC Cache Blob NFS Target to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this HPC Cache Blob NFS Target. Changing this forces a new HPC Cache Blob NFS Target to be created.

namespace_path.description: The client-facing file path of the HPC Cache Blob NFS Target.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the HPC Cache Blob NFS Target should exist. Changing this forces a new HPC Cache Blob NFS Target to be created.

storage_container_id.description: The Resource Manager ID of the Storage Container used as the HPC Cache Blob NFS Target

usage_model.description: The type of usage of the HPC Cache Blob NFS Target. Possible values are: `READ_HEAVY_INFREQ`, `READ_HEAVY_CHECK_180`, `READ_ONLY`, `READ_WRITE`, `WRITE_WORKLOAD_15`, `WRITE_AROUND`, `WRITE_WORKLOAD_CHECK_30`, `WRITE_WORKLOAD_CHECK_60` and `WRITE_WORKLOAD_CLOUDWS`.

verification_timer_in_seconds.description: The amount of time the cache waits before it checks the back-end storage for file updates. Possible values are between `1` and `31536000`.

write_back_timer_in_seconds.description: The amount of time the cache waits after the last file change before it copies the changed file to back-end storage. Possible values are between `1` and `31536000`.

access_policy_name.description: The name of the access policy applied to this target. Defaults to `default`.

cache_name.description: The name HPC Cache, which the HPC Cache Blob Target will be added to

name.description: The name of the HPC Cache Blob Target

namespace_path.description: The client-facing file path of the HPC Cache Blob Target.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group in which to create the HPC Cache Blob Target

storage_container_id.description: The Resource Manager ID of the Storage Container used as the HPC Cache Blob Target

cache_name.description: The name HPC Cache, which the HPC Cache NFS Target will be added to

name.description: The name of the HPC Cache NFS Target

namespace_junction.description: Can be specified multiple times to define multiple `namespace_junction`. Each `namespace_junction` block supports fields documented below.

namespace_junction.access_policy_name.description: The name of the access policy applied to this target. Defaults to `default`.

namespace_junction.namespace_path.description: The client-facing file path of this NFS target within the HPC Cache NFS Target.

namespace_junction.nfs_export.description: The NFS export of this NFS target within the HPC Cache NFS Target.

namespace_junction.target_path.description: The relative subdirectory path from the `nfs_export` to map to the `namespace_path`. Defaults to `""`, in which case the whole `nfs_export` is exported.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group in which to create the HPC Cache NFS Target

target_host_name.description: The IP address or fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of the HPC Cache NFS target

usage_model.description: The type of usage of the HPC Cache NFS Target. Possible values are: `READ_HEAVY_INFREQ`, `READ_HEAVY_CHECK_180`, `READ_ONLY`, `READ_WRITE`, `WRITE_WORKLOAD_15`, `WRITE_AROUND`, `WRITE_WORKLOAD_CHECK_30`, `WRITE_WORKLOAD_CHECK_60` and `WRITE_WORKLOAD_CLOUDWS`.

verification_timer_in_seconds.description: The amount of time the cache waits before it checks the back-end storage for file updates. Possible values are between `1` and `31536000`.

write_back_timer_in_seconds.description: The amount of time the cache waits after the last file change before it copies the changed file to back-end storage. Possible values are between `1` and `31536000`.

data_disk.description: One or more `data_disk` blocks as defined below.

data_disk.blob_uri.description: Specifies the URI in Azure storage of the blob that you want to use to create the image.

data_disk.caching.description: Specifies the caching mode as `ReadWrite`, `ReadOnly`, or `None`. Defaults to `None`.

data_disk.disk_encryption_set_id.description: The ID of the Disk Encryption Set which should be used to encrypt this disk

data_disk.lun.description: Specifies the logical unit number of the data disk.

data_disk.managed_disk_id.description: Specifies the ID of the managed disk resource that you want to use to create the image

data_disk.size_gb.description: Specifies the size of the image to be created. The target size can't be smaller than the source size.

data_disk.storage_type.description: The type of Storage Disk to use. Possible values are `Premium_LRS`, `PremiumV2_LRS`, `Premium_ZRS`, `Standard_LRS`, `StandardSSD_LRS`, `StandardSSD_ZRS` and `UltraSSD_LRS`

hyper_v_generation.description: The Hyper-V Generation Type of the Virtual Machine created from the image as `V1`, `V2`. Defaults to `V1`

location.description: Specified the supported Azure location where the resource exists

name.description: Specifies the name of the image

os_disk.description: One or more `os_disk` blocks as defined below

os_disk.blob_uri.description: Specifies the URI in Azure storage of the blob that you want to use to create the image

os_disk.caching.description: Specifies the caching mode as `ReadWrite`, `ReadOnly`, or `None`. The default is `None`.

os_disk.disk_encryption_set_id.description: The ID of the Disk Encryption Set which should be used to encrypt this disk

os_disk.managed_disk_id.description: Specifies the ID of the managed disk resource that you want to use to create the image.

os_disk.os_state.description: Specifies the state of the operating system contained in the blob. Currently, the only value is Generalized. Possible values are `Generalized` and `Specialized`.

os_disk.os_type.description: Specifies the type of operating system contained in the virtual machine image. Possible values are: `Windows` or `Linux`.

os_disk.size_gb.description: Specifies the size of the image to be created

os_disk.storage_type.description: The type of Storage Disk to use. Possible values are `Premium_LRS`, `PremiumV2_LRS`, `Premium_ZRS`, `Standard_LRS`, `StandardSSD_LRS`, `StandardSSD_ZRS` and `UltraSSD_LRS`

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the image

source_virtual_machine_id.description: The Virtual Machine ID from which to create the image.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

zone_resilient.description: Is zone resiliency enabled? Defaults to `false`

allow_rule.connection_from_ips_not_allow.description: Specifies which IP is not allowed to be connected to in current device group for inbound connection.

allow_rule.connection_to_ips_not_allowed.description: Specifies which IP is not allowed to be connected to in current device group for outbound connection.

allow_rule.local_users_not_allowed.description: Specifies which local user is not allowed to login in current device group.

allow_rule.processes_not_allowed.description: Specifies which process is not allowed to be executed in current device group.

iothub_id.description: The ID of the IoT Hub which to link the Security Device Group to

name.description: Specifies the name of the Device Security Group

range_rule.description: One or more `range_rule` blocks as defined below.

range_rule.duration.description: Specifies the time range. represented in ISO 8601 duration format.

range_rule.max.description: The maximum threshold in the given time window.

range_rule.min.description: The minimum threshold in the given time window.

range_rule.type.description: The type of supported rule type. Possible Values are `ActiveConnectionsNotInAllowedRange`, `AmqpC2DMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `MqttC2DMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `HttpC2DMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `AmqpC2DRejectedMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `MqttC2DRejectedMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `HttpC2DRejectedMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `AmqpD2CMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `MqttD2CMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `HttpD2CMessagesNotInAllowedRange`, `DirectMethodInvokesNotInAllowedRange`, `FailedLocalLoginsNotInAllowedRange`, `FileUploadsNotInAllowedRange`, `QueuePurgesNotInAllowedRange`, `TwinUpdatesNotInAllowedRange` and `UnauthorizedOperationsNotInAllowedRange`.

additional_workspace.data_types.description: A list of data types which sent to workspace. Possible values are `Alerts` and `RawEvents`.

additional_workspace.workspace_id.description: The resource ID of the Log Analytics Workspace.

disabled_data_sources.description: A list of disabled data sources for the Iot Security Solution. Possible value is `TwinData`.

display_name.description: Specifies the Display Name for this Iot Security Solution.

enabled.description: Is the Iot Security Solution enabled? Defaults to `true`.

events_to_export.description: A list of data which is to exported to analytic workspace. Valid values include `RawEvents`.

iothub_ids.description: Specifies the IoT Hub resource IDs to which this Iot Security Solution is applied.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

log_analytics_workspace_id.description: Specifies the Log Analytics Workspace ID to which the security data will be sent.

log_unmasked_ips_enabled.description: Should IP addressed be unmasked in the log? Defaults to `false`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Iot Security Solution

query_for_resources.description: An Azure Resource Graph query used to set the resources monitored.

query_subscription_ids.description: A list of subscription Ids on which the user defined resources query should be executed.

recommendations_enabled.description: A `recommendations_enabled` block of options to enable or disable as defined below.

recommendations_enabled.acr_authenticati.description: Is Principal Authentication enabled for the ACR repository? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.agent_send_unuti.description: Is Agent send underutilized messages enabled? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.baseline.description: Is Security related system configuration issues identified? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.edge_hub_mem_opt.description: Is IoT Edge Hub memory optimized? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.edge_logging_opt.description: Is logging configured for IoT Edge module? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.inconsistent_mod.description: Is inconsistent module settings enabled for SecurityGroup? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.install_agent.description: is Azure IoT Security agent installed? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.ip_filter_deny_a.description: Is Default IP filter policy denied? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.ip_filter_permis.description: Is IP filter rule source allowable IP range too large? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.open_ports.description: Is any ports open on the device? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.permissive_firew.description: Does firewall policy exist which allow necessary communication to/from the device? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.permissive_input.description: Is only necessary addresses or ports are permitted in? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.permissive_outpu.description: Is only necessary addresses or ports are permitted out? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.privileged_docke.description: Is high level permissions are needed for the module? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.shared_credentia.description: Is any credentials shared among devices? Defaults to `true`.

recommendations_enabled.vulnerable_tls_c.description: Does TLS cipher suite need to be updated? Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the resource group in which to create the Iot Security Solution

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

display_name.description: A `display_name` name. Custom display name for the IoT Central application. Default is resource name.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this IoT Central Application. The only possible value is `SystemAssigned`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource has to be create

name.description: Specifies the name of the IotHub resource

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether public network access is allowed for the IoT Central Application. Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the IotHub resource has to be created

sku.description: A `sku` name. Possible values is `ST0`, `ST1`, `ST2`, Default value is `ST1`

sub_domain.description: A `sub_domain` name. Subdomain for the IoT Central URL. Each application must have a unique subdomain.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

template.description: A `template` name. IoT Central application template name. Defaults to `iotc-pnp-preview@1.0.0`

apply_to_device.description: Whether these IP Rules apply for device connectivity to IoT Hub and Device Provisioning Service associated with this IoT Central Application. Possible values are `true`, `false`. Defaults to `true`

default_action.description: Specifies the default action for the IoT Central Application Network Rule Set. Possible values are `Allow` and `Deny`. Defaults to `Deny`.

iotcentral_application_id.description: The ID of the IoT Central Application

ip_rule.description: One or more `ip_rule` blocks as defined below.

ip_rule.ip_mask.description: The IP address range in CIDR notation for the IP Rule.

ip_rule.name.description: The name of the IP Rule

display_name.description: Custom `display_name` for the organization.

cloud_to_device.default_ttl.description: The default time to live for cloud-to-device messages, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 1 minute and 48 hours. Defaults to `PT1H`.

cloud_to_device.max_delivery_count.description: The maximum delivery count for cloud-to-device per-device queues. This value must be between `1` and `100`. Defaults to `10`.

endpoint.authentication_type.description: The type used to authenticate against the endpoint. Possible values are `keyBased` and `identityBased`. Defaults to `keyBased`.

endpoint.batch_frequency_in_seconds.description: Time interval at which blobs are written to storage. Value should be between 60 and 720 seconds. Default value is 300 seconds. This attribute is applicable for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`.

endpoint.connection_string.description: The connection string for the endpoint. This attribute is mandatory and can only be specified when `authentication_type` is `keyBased`.

endpoint.container_name.description: The name of storage container in the storage account. This attribute is mandatory for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`.

endpoint.encoding.description: Encoding that is used to serialize messages to blobs. Supported values are `Avro`, `AvroDeflate` and `JSON`. Default value is `Avro`. This attribute is applicable for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`

endpoint.endpoint_uri.description: URI of the Service Bus or Event Hubs Namespace endpoint. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `identityBased` for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.ServiceBusQueue`, `AzureIotHub.ServiceBusTopic` or `AzureIotHub.EventHub`.

endpoint.entity_path.description: Name of the Service Bus Queue/Topic or Event Hub. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `identityBased` for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.ServiceBusQueue`, `AzureIotHub.ServiceBusTopic` or `AzureIotHub.EventHub`.

endpoint.file_name_format.description: File name format for the blob. All parameters are mandatory but can be reordered. This attribute is applicable for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`. Defaults to `{iothub}/{partition}/{YYYY}/{MM}/{DD}/{HH}/{mm}`.

endpoint.identity_id.description: The ID of the User Managed Identity used to authenticate against the endpoint.

endpoint.max_chunk_size_in_bytes.description: Maximum number of bytes for each blob written to storage. Value should be between 10485760(10MB) and 524288000(500MB). Default value is 314572800(300MB). This attribute is applicable for endpoint type `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`.

endpoint.name.description: The name of the endpoint. The name must be unique across endpoint types. The following names are reserved: `events`, `operationsMonitoringEvents`, `fileNotifications` and `$default`.

endpoint.resource_group_name.description: The resource group in which the endpoint will be created.

endpoint.subscription_id.description: The subscription ID for the endpoint.

endpoint.type.description: The type of the endpoint. Possible values are `AzureIotHub.StorageContainer`, `AzureIotHub.ServiceBusQueue`, `AzureIotHub.ServiceBusTopic` or `AzureIotHub.EventHub`.

enrichment.endpoint_names.description: The list of endpoints which will be enriched.

enrichment.value.description: The value of the enrichment. Value can be any static string, the name of the IoT Hub sending the message (use `$iothubname`) or information from the device twin (ex: `$twin.tags.latitude`)

event_hub_partition_count.description: The number of device-to-cloud partitions used by backing event hubs. Must be between `2` and `128`. Defaults to `4`.

event_hub_retention_in_days.description: The event hub retention to use in days. Must be between `1` and `7`. Defaults to `1`.

fallback_route.description: A `fallback_route` block as defined below. If the fallback route is enabled, messages that don't match any of the supplied routes are automatically sent to this route. Defaults to messages/events.

fallback_route.condition.description: The condition that is evaluated to apply the routing rule. Defaults to `true`. For grammar, see: <https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/iot-hub/iot-hub-devguide-query-language>.

fallback_route.enabled.description: Used to specify whether the fallback route is enabled. Defaults to `true`.

fallback_route.endpoint_names.description: The endpoints to which messages that satisfy the condition are routed. Currently only 1 endpoint is allowed.

fallback_route.source.description: The source that the routing rule is to be applied to, such as `DeviceMessages`. Possible values include: `Invalid`, `DeviceMessages`, `TwinChangeEvents`, `DeviceLifecycleEvents`, `DeviceConnectionStateEvents`, `DeviceJobLifecycleEvents` and `DigitalTwinChangeEvents`. Defaults to `DeviceMessages`.

feedback.lock_duration.description: The lock duration for the feedback queue, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 5 and 300 seconds. Defaults to `PT60S`.

feedback.max_delivery_count.description: The maximum delivery count for the feedback queue. This value must be between `1` and `100`. Defaults to `10`.

feedback.time_to_live.description: The retention time for service-bound feedback messages, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 1 minute and 48 hours. Defaults to `PT1H`.

file_upload.authentication_type.description: The type used to authenticate against the storage account. Possible values are `keyBased` and `identityBased`. Defaults to `keyBased`.

file_upload.connection_string.description: The connection string for the Azure Storage account to which files are uploaded.

file_upload.container_name.description: The name of the root container where the files should be uploaded to. The container need not exist but should be creatable using the connection_string specified.

file_upload.default_ttl.description: The period of time for which a file upload notification message is available to consume before it expires, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 1 minute and 48 hours. Defaults to `PT1H`.

file_upload.identity_id.description: The ID of the User Managed Identity used to authenticate against the storage account.

file_upload.lock_duration.description: The lock duration for the file upload notifications queue, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 5 and 300 seconds. Defaults to `PT1M`.

file_upload.max_delivery_count.description: The number of times the IoT Hub attempts to deliver a file upload notification message. Defaults to `10`.

file_upload.notifications.description: Used to specify whether file notifications are sent to IoT Hub on upload. Defaults to `false`.

file_upload.sas_ttl.description: The period of time for which the SAS URI generated by IoT Hub for file upload is valid, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 1 minute and 24 hours. Defaults to `PT1H`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this IoT Hub.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this IoT Hub. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

ip_rule.action.description: The desired action for requests captured by this rule. Possible values are `Allow`. Defaults to `Allow`.

ip_rule.ip_mask.description: The IP address range in CIDR notation for the IP rule.

ip_rule.name.description: The name of the IP rule.

local_authentication_enabled.description: If false, SAS tokens with Iot hub scoped SAS keys cannot be used for authentication. Defaults to `true`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource has to be created

min_tls_version.description: Specifies the minimum TLS version to support for this hub. The only valid value is `1.2`

name.description: Specifies the name of the IotHub resource

network_rule_set.apply_to_builtin_eventh.description: Determines if Network Rule Set is also applied to the BuiltIn EventHub EndPoint of the IotHub. Defaults to `false`.

network_rule_set.default_action.description: Default Action for Network Rule Set. Possible values are `Deny`, `Allow`. Defaults to `Deny`.

network_rule_set.ip_rule.description: One or more `ip_rule` blocks as defined below.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Is the IotHub resource accessible from a public network?

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the IotHub resource has to be created

route.condition.description: The condition that is evaluated to apply the routing rule. Defaults to `true`. For grammar, see: <https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/iot-hub/iot-hub-devguide-query-language>.

route.enabled.description: Used to specify whether a route is enabled.

route.endpoint_names.description: The list of endpoints to which messages that satisfy the condition are routed.

route.source.description: The source that the routing rule is to be applied to, such as `DeviceMessages`. Possible values include: `Invalid`, `DeviceMessages`, `TwinChangeEvents`, `DeviceLifecycleEvents`, `DeviceConnectionStateEvents`, `DeviceJobLifecycleEvents` and `DigitalTwinChangeEvents`.

sku.description: A `sku` block as defined below.

sku.capacity.description: The number of provisioned IoT Hub units.

sku.name.description: The name of the sku. Possible values are `B1`, `B2`, `B3`, `F1`, `S1`, `S2`, and `S3`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

certificate_content.description: The Base-64 representation of the X509 leaf certificate .cer file or just a .pem file content.

iothub_name.description: The name of the IoTHub that this certificate will be attached to

is_verified.description: Is the certificate verified? Defaults to `false`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the IotHub Certificate resource

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the IotHub Certificate resource has to be created

eventhub_endpoint_name.description: The name of the Event Hub-compatible endpoint in the IoT hub

iothub_name.description: The name of the IoT Hub

name.description: The name of this Consumer Group

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group that contains the IoT hub

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this IoT Hub Device Update Account.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this IoT Hub Device Update Account. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned` and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the IoT Hub Device Update Account should exist

name.description: Specifies the name which should be used for this IoT Hub Device Update Account

public_network_access_enabled.description: Specifies whether the public network access is enabled for the IoT Hub Device Update Account. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the Resource Group where the IoT Hub Device Update Account should exist

sku.description: Sku of the IoT Hub Device Update Account. Possible values are `Free` and `Standard`. Defaults to `Standard`

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the IoT Hub Device Update Account.

device_update_account_id.description: Specifies the ID of the IoT Hub Device Update Account where the IoT Hub Device Update Instance exists

diagnostic_enabled.description: Whether the diagnostic log collection is enabled. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `false`.

diagnostic_storage_account.connection_st.description: Connection String of the Diagnostic Storage Account.

diagnostic_storage_account.id.description: Resource ID of the Diagnostic Storage Account.

iothub_id.description: Specifies the ID of the IoT Hub associated with the IoT Hub Device Update Instance

name.description: Specifies the name which should be used for this IoT Hub Device Update Instance

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the IoT Hub Device Update Instance.

allocation_policy.description: The allocation policy of the IoT Device Provisioning Service (`Hashed`, `GeoLatency` or `Static`). Defaults to `Hashed`.

data_residency_enabled.description: Specifies if the IoT Device Provisioning Service has data residency enabled, removing the cross geo-pair disaster recovery. Defaults to `false`

ip_filter_rule.action.description: The desired action for requests captured by this rule. Possible values are `Accept`, `Reject`

ip_filter_rule.ip_mask.description: The IP address range in CIDR notation for the rule.

ip_filter_rule.target.description: Target for requests captured by this rule. Possible values are `all`, `deviceApi` and `serviceApi`.

linked_hub.allocation_weight.description: The weight applied to the IoT Hub. Defaults to `1`.

linked_hub.apply_allocation_policy.description: Determines whether to apply allocation policies to the IoT Hub. Defaults to `true`.

linked_hub.connection_string.description: The connection string to connect to the IoT Hub.

linked_hub.location.description: The location of the IoT hub.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource has to be created

name.description: Specifies the name of the Iot Device Provisioning Service resource

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether requests from Public Network are allowed. Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the Iot Device Provisioning Service resource has to be created

sku.description: A `sku` block as defined below.

sku.capacity.description: The number of provisioned IoT Device Provisioning Service units.

sku.name.description: The name of the sku. Currently can only be set to `S1`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

certificate_content.description: The Base-64 representation of the X509 leaf certificate .cer file or just a .pem file content.

iot_dps_name.description: The name of the IoT Device Provisioning Service that this certificate will be attached to

is_verified.description: Specifies if the certificate is created in verified state. Defaults to `false`

name.description: Specifies the name of the Iot Device Provisioning Service Certificate resource

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the Iot Device Provisioning Service Certificate resource has to be created

enrollment_read.description: Adds `EnrollmentRead` permission to this Shared Access Account. It allows read access to enrollment data.

enrollment_write.description: Adds `EnrollmentWrite` permission to this Shared Access Account. It allows write access to enrollment data.

iothub_dps_name.description: The name of the IoT Hub Device Provisioning service to which this Shared Access Policy belongs

name.description: Specifies the name of the IotHub Shared Access Policy resource

registration_read.description: Adds `RegistrationStatusRead` permission to this Shared Access Account. It allows read access to device registrations.

registration_write.description: Adds `RegistrationStatusWrite` permission to this Shared Access Account. It allows write access to device registrations.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the IotHub Shared Access Policy resource has to be created

service_config.description: Adds `ServiceConfig` permission to this Shared Access Account. It allows configuration of the Device Provisioning Service.

authentication_type.description: The type used to authenticate against the Cosmos DB Account endpoint. Possible values are `keyBased` and `identityBased`. Defaults to `keyBased`.

container_name.description: The name of the Cosmos DB Container in the Cosmos DB Database

database_name.description: The name of the Cosmos DB Database in the Cosmos DB Account

endpoint_uri.description: The URI of the Cosmos DB Account

identity_id.description: The ID of the User Managed Identity used to authenticate against the Cosmos DB Account endpoint.

name.description: The name of the endpoint. The name must be unique across endpoint types. The following names are reserved: `events`, `operationsMonitoringEvents`, `fileNotifications` and `$default`

partition_key_name.description: The name of the partition key associated with the Cosmos DB Container.

partition_key_template.description: The template for generating a synthetic partition key value for use within the Cosmos DB Container.

primary_key.description: The primary key of the Cosmos DB Account.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the Cosmos DB Account has been created

secondary_key.description: The secondary key of the Cosmos DB Account.

subscription_id.description: The subscription ID for the endpoint.

connection_string.description: The connection string for the endpoint. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `keyBased`.

endpoint_uri.description: URI of the Event Hubs Namespace endpoint. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `identityBased`.

entity_path.description: Name of the Event Hub. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `identityBased`.

identity_id.description: ID of the User Managed Identity used to authenticate against the Event Hub endpoint.

authentication_type.description: Type used to authenticate against the Service Bus Queue endpoint. Possible values are `keyBased` and `identityBased`. Defaults to `keyBased`.

endpoint_uri.description: URI of the Service Bus endpoint. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `identityBased`.

entity_path.description: Name of the Service Bus Queue. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `identityBased`.

subscription_id.description: The subscription ID for the endpoint.

authentication_type.description: Type used to authenticate against the Service Bus Topic endpoint. Possible values are `keyBased` and `identityBased`. Defaults to `keyBased`.

connection_string.description: The connection string for the endpoint. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `keyBased`.

endpoint_uri.description: URI of the Service Bus endpoint. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `identityBased`.

entity_path.description: Name of the Service Bus Topic. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `identityBased`.

identity_id.description: ID of the User Managed Identity used to authenticate against the Service Bus Topic endpoint.

iothub_id.description: The IoTHub ID for the endpoint

name.description: The name of the endpoint. The name must be unique across endpoint types. The following names are reserved: `events`, `operationsMonitoringEvents`, `fileNotifications` and `$default`

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the Service Bus Topic has been created

subscription_id.description: The subscription ID for the endpoint.

authentication_type.description: Type used to authenticate against the storage endpoint. Possible values are `keyBased` and `identityBased`. Defaults to `keyBased`.

batch_frequency_in_seconds.description: Time interval at which blobs are written to storage. Value should be between 60 and 720 seconds. Default value is 300 seconds.

connection_string.description: The connection string for the endpoint. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `keyBased`.

container_name.description: The name of storage container in the storage account.

encoding.description: Encoding that is used to serialize messages to blobs. Supported values are `Avro`, `AvroDeflate` and `JSON`. Default value is `Avro`

endpoint_uri.description: URI of the Storage Container endpoint. This corresponds to the `primary_blob_endpoint` of the parent storage account. This attribute can only be specified and is mandatory when `authentication_type` is `identityBased`.

file_name_format.description: File name format for the blob. All parameters are mandatory but can be reordered. Defaults to `{iothub}/{partition}/{YYYY}/{MM}/{DD}/{HH}/{mm}`.

identity_id.description: ID of the User Managed Identity used to authenticate against the storage endpoint.

iothub_id.description: The IoTHub ID for the endpoint

max_chunk_size_in_bytes.description: Maximum number of bytes for each blob written to storage. Value should be between 10485760(10MB) and 524288000(500MB). Default value is 314572800(300MB).

name.description: The name of the endpoint. The name must be unique across endpoint types. The following names are reserved: `events`, `operationsMonitoringEvents`, `fileNotifications` and `$default`

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the Storage Container has been created

subscription_id.description: The subscription ID for the endpoint.

endpoint_names.description: The list of endpoints which will be enriched.

iothub_name.description: The IoTHub name of the enrichment

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the IoTHub resource is created

value.description: The value of the enrichment. Value can be any static string, the name of the IoT hub sending the message (use `$iothubname`) or information from the device twin (ex: `$twin.tags.latitude`)

condition.description: The condition that is evaluated to apply the routing rule. For grammar, see: <https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/iot-hub/iot-hub-devguide-query-language>. Defaults to `true`.

enabled.description: Used to specify whether the fallback route is enabled.

endpoint_names.description: The endpoints to which messages that satisfy the condition are routed. Currently only 1 endpoint is allowed.

iothub_name.description: The name of the IoTHub to which this Fallback Route belongs

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the IotHub Storage Container Endpoint resource has to be created

source.description: The source that the routing rule is to be applied to. Possible values include: `DeviceConnectionStateEvents`, `DeviceJobLifecycleEvents`, `DeviceLifecycleEvents`, `DeviceMessages`, `DigitalTwinChangeEvents`, `Invalid`, `TwinChangeEvents`. Defaults to `DeviceMessages`.

authentication_type.description: The type used to authenticate against the storage account. Possible values are `keyBased` and `identityBased`. Defaults to `keyBased`.

connection_string.description: The connection string for the Azure Storage account to which files are uploaded.

container_name.description: The name of the root container where the files should be uploaded to. The container need not exist but should be creatable using the `connection_string` specified.

default_ttl.description: The period of time for which a file upload notification message is available to consume before it expires, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 1 minute and 48 hours. Defaults to `PT1H`.

identity_id.description: The ID of the User Managed Identity used to authenticate against the storage account.

iothub_id.description: The ID of the IoT Hub. Changing this forces a new IoT Hub to be created.

lock_duration.description: The lock duration for the file upload notifications queue, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 5 and 300 seconds. Defaults to `PT1M`.

max_delivery_count.description: The number of times the IoT Hub attempts to deliver a file upload notification message. Defaults to `10`.

notifications_enabled.description: Used to specify whether file notifications are sent to IoT Hub on upload. Defaults to `false`.

sas_ttl.description: The period of time for which the SAS URI generated by IoT Hub for file upload is valid, specified as an [ISO 8601 timespan duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This value must be between 1 minute and 24 hours. Defaults to `PT1H`.

condition.description: The condition that is evaluated to apply the routing rule. For grammar, see: <https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/iot-hub/iot-hub-devguide-query-language>. Defaults to `true`.

enabled.description: Specifies whether a route is enabled.

endpoint_names.description: The list of endpoints to which messages that satisfy the condition are routed. Currently only one endpoint is allowed.

name.description: The name of the route

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group under which the IotHub Route resource has to be created

device_connect.description: Adds `DeviceConnect` permission to this Shared Access Account. It allows sending and receiving on the device-side endpoints.

iothub_name.description: The name of the IoTHub to which this Shared Access Policy belongs

name.description: Specifies the name of the IotHub Shared Access Policy resource

registry_read.description: Adds `RegistryRead` permission to this Shared Access Account. It allows read access to the identity registry.

registry_write.description: Adds `RegistryWrite` permission to this Shared Access Account. It allows write access to the identity registry.

service_connect.description: Adds `ServiceConnect` permission to this Shared Access Account. It allows sending and receiving on the cloud-side endpoints.

cidrs.description: A list of CIDRs or IP addresses.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

name.description: Specifies the name of the IP group

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the IP group

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

cidr.description: The `CIDR` that should be added to the IP Group.

ip_group_id.description: The ID of the destination IP Group.

access_policy.description: [A list](/docs/configuration/attr-as-blocks.html) of `access_policy` objects (up to 1024) describing access policies, as described below.

access_policy.application_id.description: The object ID of an Application in Azure Active Directory.

access_policy.certificate_permissions.description: List of certificate permissions, must be one or more from the following: `Backup`, `Create`, `Delete`, `DeleteIssuers`, `Get`, `GetIssuers`, `Import`, `List`, `ListIssuers`, `ManageContacts`, `ManageIssuers`, `Purge`, `Recover`, `Restore`, `SetIssuers` and `Update`.

access_policy.key_permissions.description: List of key permissions. Possible values are `Backup`, `Create`, `Decrypt`, `Delete`, `Encrypt`, `Get`, `Import`, `List`, `Purge`, `Recover`, `Restore`, `Sign`, `UnwrapKey`, `Update`, `Verify`, `WrapKey`, `Release`, `Rotate`, `GetRotationPolicy` and `SetRotationPolicy`.

access_policy.object_id.description: The object ID of a user, service principal or security group in the Azure Active Directory tenant for the vault. The object ID must be unique for the list of access policies.

access_policy.secret_permissions.description: List of secret permissions, must be one or more from the following: `Backup`, `Delete`, `Get`, `List`, `Purge`, `Recover`, `Restore` and `Set`.

access_policy.storage_permissions.description: List of storage permissions, must be one or more from the following: `Backup`, `Delete`, `DeleteSAS`, `Get`, `GetSAS`, `List`, `ListSAS`, `Purge`, `Recover`, `RegenerateKey`, `Restore`, `Set`, `SetSAS` and `Update`.

access_policy.tenant_id.description: The Azure Active Directory tenant ID that should be used for authenticating requests to the key vault. Must match the `tenant_id` used above.

enabled_for_deployment.description: Boolean flag to specify whether Azure Virtual Machines are permitted to retrieve certificates stored as secrets from the key vault.

enabled_for_disk_encryption.description: Boolean flag to specify whether Azure Disk Encryption is permitted to retrieve secrets from the vault and unwrap keys.

enabled_for_template_deployment.description: Boolean flag to specify whether Azure Resource Manager is permitted to retrieve secrets from the key vault.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

name.description: Specifies the name of the Key Vault

network_acls.bypass.description: Specifies which traffic can bypass the network rules. Possible values are `AzureServices` and `None`.

network_acls.default_action.description: The Default Action to use when no rules match from `ip_rules` / `virtual_network_subnet_ids`. Possible values are `Allow` and `Deny`.

network_acls.ip_rules.description: One or more IP Addresses, or CIDR Blocks which should be able to access the Key Vault.

network_acls.virtual_network_subnet_ids.description: One or more Subnet IDs which should be able to access this Key Vault.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether public network access is allowed for this Key Vault. Defaults to `true`.

purge_protection_enabled.description: Is Purge Protection enabled for this Key Vault?

rbac_authorization_enabled.description: Boolean flag to specify whether Azure Key Vault uses Role Based Access Control (RBAC) for authorization of data actions.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Key Vault

sku_name.description: The Name of the SKU used for this Key Vault. Possible values are `standard` and `premium`.

soft_delete_retention_days.description: The number of days that items should be retained for once soft-deleted. This value can be between `7` and `90` (the default) days.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

tenant_id.description: The Azure Active Directory tenant ID that should be used for authenticating requests to the key vault.

application_id.description: The object ID of an Application in Azure Active Directory

certificate_permissions.description: List of certificate permissions, must be one or more from the following: `Backup`, `Create`, `Delete`, `DeleteIssuers`, `Get`, `GetIssuers`, `Import`, `List`, `ListIssuers`, `ManageContacts`, `ManageIssuers`, `Purge`, `Recover`, `Restore`, `SetIssuers` and `Update`.

key_permissions.description: List of key permissions, must be one or more from the following: `Backup`, `Create`, `Decrypt`, `Delete`, `Encrypt`, `Get`, `Import`, `List`, `Purge`, `Recover`, `Restore`, `Sign`, `UnwrapKey`, `Update`, `Verify`, `WrapKey`, `Release`, `Rotate`, `GetRotationPolicy` and `SetRotationPolicy`.

key_vault_id.description: Specifies the id of the Key Vault resource

object_id.description: The object ID of a user, service principal or security group in the Azure Active Directory tenant for the vault. The object ID of a service principal can be fetched from `azuread_service_principal.object_id`. The object ID must be unique for the list of access policies

secret_permissions.description: List of secret permissions, must be one or more from the following: `Backup`, `Delete`, `Get`, `List`, `Purge`, `Recover`, `Restore` and `Set`.

storage_permissions.description: List of storage permissions, must be one or more from the following: `Backup`, `Delete`, `DeleteSAS`, `Get`, `GetSAS`, `List`, `ListSAS`, `Purge`, `Recover`, `RegenerateKey`, `Restore`, `Set`, `SetSAS` and `Update`.

tenant_id.description: The Azure Active Directory tenant ID that should be used for authenticating requests to the key vault

action.action_type.description: The Type of action to be performed when the lifetime trigger is triggerec. Possible values include `AutoRenew` and `EmailContacts`.

certificate.description: A `certificate` block as defined below, used to Import an existing certificate. Changing this will create a new version of the Key Vault Certificate.

certificate.password.description: The password associated with the certificate.

certificate_policy.description: A `certificate_policy` block as defined below. Changing this (except the `lifetime_action` field) will create a new version of the Key Vault Certificate.

certificate_policy.x509_certificate_prop.description: A `x509_certificate_properties` block as defined below. Required when `certificate` block is not specified.

issuer_parameters.name.description: The name of the Certificate Issuer. Possible values include `Self` (for self-signed certificate), or `Unknown` (for a certificate issuing authority like `Let's Encrypt` and Azure direct supported ones).

key_properties.curve.description: Specifies the curve to use when creating an `EC` key. Possible values are `P-256`, `P-256K`, `P-384`, and `P-521`. This field will be required in a future release if `key_type` is `EC` or `EC-HSM`.

key_properties.key_size.description: The size of the key used in the certificate. Possible values include `2048`, `3072`, and `4096` for `RSA` keys, or `256`, `384`, and `521` for `EC` keys. This property is required when using RSA keys.

key_properties.key_type.description: Specifies the type of key. Possible values are `EC`, `EC-HSM`, `RSA`, `RSA-HSM` and `oct`.

key_vault_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault where the Certificate should be created

name.description: Specifies the name of the Key Vault Certificate

secret_properties.content_type.description: The Content-Type of the Certificate, such as `application/x-pkcs12` for a PFX or `application/x-pem-file` for a PEM.

subject_alternative_names.dns_names.description: A list of alternative DNS names (FQDNs) identified by the Certificate.

subject_alternative_names.emails.description: A list of email addresses identified by this Certificate.

subject_alternative_names.upns.description: A list of User Principal Names identified by the Certificate.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

trigger.days_before_expiry.description: The number of days before the Certificate expires that the action associated with this Trigger should run. Conflicts with `lifetime_percentage`.

trigger.lifetime_percentage.description: The percentage at which during the Certificates Lifetime the action associated with this Trigger should run. Conflicts with `days_before_expiry`.

x509_certificate_properties.extended_key.description: A list of Extended/Enhanced Key Usages.

x509_certificate_properties.key_usage.description: A list of uses associated with this Key. Possible values include `cRLSign`, `dataEncipherment`, `decipherOnly`, `digitalSignature`, `encipherOnly`, `keyAgreement`, `keyCertSign`, `keyEncipherment` and `nonRepudiation` and are case-sensitive.

x509_certificate_properties.validity_in_.description: The Certificates Validity Period in Months.

contact.description: One or more `contact` blocks as defined below.

key_vault_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault

account_id.description: The account number with the third-party Certificate Issuer.

admin.description: One or more `admin` blocks as defined below.

key_vault_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault in which to create the Certificate Issuer

name.description: The name which should be used for this Key Vault Certificate Issuer. Changing this forces a new Key Vault Certificate Issuer to be created.

org_id.description: The ID of the organization as provided to the issuer.

password.description: The password associated with the account and organization ID at the third-party Certificate Issuer. If not specified, will not overwrite any previous value.

provider_name.description: The name of the third-party Certificate Issuer. Possible values are: `DigiCert`, `GlobalSign`, `OneCertV2-PrivateCA`, `OneCertV2-PublicCA` and `SslAdminV2`.

automatic.time_after_creation.description: Rotate automatically at a duration after create as an [ISO 8601 duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations).

automatic.time_before_expiry.description: Rotate automatically at a duration before expiry as an [ISO 8601 duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations).

curve.description: Specifies the curve to use when creating an `EC` key. Possible values are `P-256`, `P-256K`, `P-384`, and `P-521`. This field will be required in a future release if `key_type` is `EC` or `EC-HSM`. The API will default to `P-256` if nothing is specified

key_opts.description: A list of JSON web key operations. Possible values include: `decrypt`, `encrypt`, `sign`, `unwrapKey`, `verify` and `wrapKey`. Please note these values are case sensitive.

key_size.description: Specifies the Size of the RSA key to create in bytes. For example, 1024 or 2048. *Note*: This field is required if `key_type` is `RSA` or `RSA-HSM`

key_type.description: Specifies the Key Type to use for this Key Vault Key. Possible values are `EC` (Elliptic Curve), `EC-HSM`, `RSA` and `RSA-HSM`

key_vault_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault where the Key should be created

name.description: Specifies the name of the Key Vault Key

not_before_date.description: Key not usable before the provided UTC datetime (Y-m-d'T'H:M:S'Z').

rotation_policy.expire_after.description: Expire a Key Vault Key after given duration as an [ISO 8601 duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations).

rotation_policy.notify_before_expiry.description: Notify at a given duration before expiry as an [ISO 8601 duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations).

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

admin_object_ids.description: Specifies a list of administrators object IDs for the key vault Managed Hardware Security Module

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

name.description: Specifies the name of the Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module

network_acls.bypass.description: Specifies which traffic can bypass the network rules. Possible values are `AzureServices` and `None`.

network_acls.default_action.description: The Default Action to use. Possible values are `Allow` and `Deny`.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether traffic from public networks is permitted. Defaults to `true`.

purge_protection_enabled.description: Is Purge Protection enabled for this Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module?

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module

security_domain_key_vault_certificate_id.description: A list of KeyVault certificates resource IDs (minimum of three and up to a maximum of 10) to activate this Managed HSM. More information see [activate-your-managed-hsm](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/key-vault/managed-hsm/quick-create-cli#activate-your-managed-hsm)

security_domain_quorum.description: Specifies the minimum number of shares required to decrypt the security domain for recovery. This is required when `security_domain_key_vault_certificate_ids` is specified. Valid values are between 2 and 10.

sku_name.description: The Name of the SKU used for this Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module. Possible value is `Standard_B1`

soft_delete_retention_days.description: The number of days that items should be retained for once soft-deleted. This value can be between `7` and `90` days. Defaults to `90`

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

tenant_id.description: The Azure Active Directory Tenant ID that should be used for authenticating requests to the key vault Managed Hardware Security Module

curve.description: Specifies the curve to use when creating an `EC-HSM` key. Possible values are `P-256`, `P-256K`, `P-384`, and `P-521`. This field is required if `key_type` is `EC-HSM`

expiration_date.description: Expiration UTC datetime (Y-m-d'T'H:M:S'Z'). When this parameter gets changed on reruns, if newer date is ahead of current date, an update is performed. If the newer date is before the current date, resource will be force created.

key_opts.description: A list of JSON web key operations. Possible values include: `decrypt`, `encrypt`, `sign`, `unwrapKey`, `verify`, `wrapKey` and `import`. Please note these values are case-sensitive.

key_size.description: Specifies the Size of the RSA key to create in bytes. For example, 1024 or 2048. *Note*: This field is required if `key_type` is `RSA-HSM` or `oct-HSM`

key_type.description: Specifies the Key Type to use for this Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module Key. Possible values are `EC-HSM`, `oct-HSM` and `RSA-HSM`. More details see [HSM-protected keys](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/keys/about-keys#hsm-protected-keys)

managed_hsm_id.description: Specifies the ID of the Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module that they key will be owned by

name.description: Specifies the name of the Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module Key

not_before_date.description: Key not usable before the provided UTC datetime (Y-m-d'T'H:M:S'Z').

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

expire_after.description: Specify the expiration duration on a newly rotated key as an [ISO 8601 duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). The minimum duration is `P28D`.

managed_hsm_key_id.description: The ID of the Managed HSM Key. Changing this forces a new Managed HSM Key rotation policy to be created.

time_after_creation.description: Rotate automatically at a duration after key creation as an [ISO 8601 duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). Exactly one of `time_after_creation` or `time_before_expiry` should be specified.

time_before_expiry.description: Rotate automatically at a duration before key expiry as an [ISO 8601 duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). Exactly one of `time_after_creation` or `time_before_expiry` should be specified.

managed_hsm_id.description: The ID of a Managed Hardware Security Module resource. Changing this forces a new Managed Hardware Security Module to be created.

name.description: The name in GUID notation which should be used for this Managed Hardware Security Module Role Assignment. Changing this forces a new Managed Hardware Security Module to be created.

principal_id.description: The principal ID to be assigned to this role. It can point to a user, service principal, or security group. Changing this forces a new Managed Hardware Security Module to be created.

role_definition_id.description: The resource ID of the role definition to assign. Changing this forces a new Managed Hardware Security Module to be created.

scope.description: Specifies the scope to create the role assignment. Changing this forces a new Managed Hardware Security Module to be created.

description.description: Specifies a text description about this Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module Role Definition.

managed_hsm_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module. Changing this forces a new Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module Role Definition to be created.

name.description: The name in UUID notation of this Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module Role Definition. Changing this forces a new Key Vault Managed Hardware Security Module Role Definition to be created.

permission.description: One or more `permission` blocks as defined below.

Possible values are `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/read/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/write/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/deletedKeys/read/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/deletedKeys/recover/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/backup/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/restore/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/roleAssignments/delete/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/roleAssignments/read/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/roleAssignments/write/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/roleDefinitions/read/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/roleDefinitions/write/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/roleDefinitions/delete/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/encrypt/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/decrypt/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/wrap/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/unwrap/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/sign/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/verify/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/create`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/delete`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/export/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/release/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/import/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/keys/deletedKeys/delete`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/securitydomain/download/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/securitydomain/download/read`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/securitydomain/upload/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/securitydomain/upload/read`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/securitydomain/transferkey/read`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/backup/start/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/restore/start/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/backup/status/action`, `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/restore/status/action` and `Microsoft.KeyVault/managedHsm/rng/action`.

See ['Azure Resource Manager resource provider operations'](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/role-based-access-control/resource-provider-operations) for details.
