do_not_run_extensions_on_overprovisioned.description: Should Virtual Machine Extensions be run on Overprovisioned Virtual Machines in the Scale Set? Defaults to `false`.

edge_zone.description: Specifies the Edge Zone within the Azure Region where this Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set should exist. Changing this forces a new Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set to be created.

encryption_at_host_enabled.description: Should all of the disks (including the temp disk) attached to this Virtual Machine be encrypted by enabling Encryption at Host?

eviction_policy.description: Specifies the eviction policy for Virtual Machines in this Scale Set. Possible values are `Deallocate` and `Delete`

extension.description: One or more `extension` blocks as defined below

extension.auto_upgrade_minor_version.description: Should the latest version of the Extension be used at Deployment Time, if one is available? This won't auto-update the extension on existing installation. Defaults to `true`.

extension.automatic_upgrade_enabled.description: Should the Extension be automatically updated whenever the Publisher releases a new version of this VM Extension?

extension.force_update_tag.description: A value which, when different to the previous value can be used to force-run the Extension even if the Extension Configuration hasn't changed.

extension.name.description: The name for the Virtual Machine Scale Set Extension.

extension.protected_settings.description: A JSON String which specifies Sensitive Settings (such as Passwords) for the Extension.

extension.provision_after_extensions.description: An ordered list of Extension names which this should be provisioned after.

extension.publisher.description: Specifies the Publisher of the Extension.

extension.settings.description: A JSON String which specifies Settings for the Extension.

extension.type.description: Specifies the Type of the Extension.

extension.type_handler_version.description: Specifies the version of the extension to use, available versions can be found using the Azure CLI.

extension_operations_enabled.description: Should extension operations be allowed on the Virtual Machine Scale Set? Possible values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set to be created.

extensions_time_budget.description: Specifies the duration allocated for all extensions to start. The time duration should be between `15` minutes and `120` minutes (inclusive) and should be specified in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to `PT1H30M`.

gallery_application.description: One or more `gallery_application` blocks as defined below.

gallery_application.configuration_blob_u.description: Specifies the URI to an Azure Blob that will replace the default configuration for the package if provided

gallery_application.order.description: Specifies the order in which the packages have to be installed. Possible values are between `0` and `2147483647`. Defaults to `0`

gallery_application.tag.description: Specifies a passthrough value for more generic context. This field can be any valid `string` value

gallery_application.version_id.description: Specifies the Gallery Application Version resource ID

health_probe_id.description: The ID of a Load Balancer Probe which should be used to determine the health of an instance. This is Required and can only be specified when `upgrade_mode` is set to `Automatic` or `Rolling`.

host_group_id.description: Specifies the ID of the dedicated host group that the virtual machine scale set resides in

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

instances.description: The number of Virtual Machines in the Scale Set. Defaults to `0`.

ip_configuration.application_gateway_bac.description: A list of Backend Address Pools ID's from a Application Gateway which this Virtual Machine Scale Set should be connected to.

ip_configuration.application_security_gr.description: A list of Application Security Group ID's which this Virtual Machine Scale Set should be connected to.

ip_configuration.load_balancer_backend_a.description: A list of Backend Address Pools ID's from a Load Balancer which this Virtual Machine Scale Set should be connected to.

ip_configuration.load_balancer_inbound_n.description: A list of NAT Rule ID's from a Load Balancer which this Virtual Machine Scale Set should be connected to.

ip_configuration.name.description: The Name which should be used for this IP Configuration.

ip_configuration.primary.description: Is this the Primary IP Configuration for this Network Interface? Defaults to `false`.

ip_configuration.subnet_id.description: The ID of the Subnet which this IP Configuration should be connected to.

ip_configuration.version.description: The Internet Protocol Version which should be used for this IP Configuration. Possible values are `IPv4` and `IPv6`. Defaults to `IPv4`.

ip_tag.tag.description: The IP Tag associated with the Public IP, such as `SQL` or `Storage`

ip_tag.type.description: The Type of IP Tag, such as `FirstPartyUsage`

location.description: The Azure location where the Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set should exist

max_bid_price.description: The maximum price you're willing to pay for each Virtual Machine in this Scale Set, in US Dollars; which must be greater than the current spot price. If this bid price falls below the current spot price the Virtual Machines in the Scale Set will be evicted using the `eviction_policy`. Defaults to `-1`, which means that each Virtual Machine in this Scale Set should not be evicted for price reasons.

name.description: The name of the Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set

network_interface.description: One or more `network_interface` blocks as defined below.

network_interface.auxiliary_mode.description: Specifies the auxiliary mode used to enable network high-performance feature on Network Virtual Appliances (NVAs). This feature offers competitive performance in Connections Per Second (CPS) optimization, along with improvements to handling large amounts of simultaneous connections. Possible values are `AcceleratedConnections` and `Floating`.

network_interface.auxiliary_sku.description: Specifies the SKU used for the network high-performance feature on Network Virtual Appliances (NVAs). Possible values are `A1`, `A2`, `A4` and `A8`.

network_interface.dns_servers.description: A list of IP Addresses of DNS Servers which should be assigned to the Network Interface.

network_interface.enable_accelerated_net.description: Does this Network Interface support Accelerated Networking? Defaults to `false`.

network_interface.enable_ip_forwarding.description: Does this Network Interface support IP Forwarding? Defaults to `false`.

network_interface.ip_configuration.description: One or more `ip_configuration` blocks as defined above.

network_interface.name.description: The Name which should be used for this Network Interface

network_interface.network_security_group.description: The ID of a Network Security Group which should be assigned to this Network Interface.

network_interface.primary.description: Is this the Primary IP Configuration?

os_disk.caching.description: The Type of Caching which should be used for the Internal OS Disk. Possible values are `None`, `ReadOnly` and `ReadWrite`.

os_disk.disk_encryption_set_id.description: The ID of the Disk Encryption Set which should be used to encrypt this OS Disk. Conflicts with `secure_vm_disk_encryption_set_id`

os_disk.disk_size_gb.description: The Size of the Internal OS Disk in GB, if you wish to vary from the size used in the image this Virtual Machine Scale Set is sourced from.

os_disk.secure_vm_disk_encryption_set_id.description: The ID of the Disk Encryption Set which should be used to Encrypt the OS Disk when the Virtual Machine Scale Set is Confidential VMSS. Conflicts with `disk_encryption_set_id`

os_disk.security_encryption_type.description: Encryption Type when the Virtual Machine Scale Set is Confidential VMSS. Possible values are `VMGuestStateOnly` and `DiskWithVMGuestState`

os_disk.storage_account_type.description: The Type of Storage Account which should back this the Internal OS Disk. Possible values include `Standard_LRS`, `StandardSSD_LRS`, `StandardSSD_ZRS`, `Premium_LRS` and `Premium_ZRS`

os_disk.write_accelerator_enabled.description: Should Write Accelerator be Enabled for this OS Disk? Defaults to `false`.

overprovision.description: Should Azure over-provision Virtual Machines in this Scale Set? This means that multiple Virtual Machines will be provisioned and Azure will keep the instances which become available first - which improves provisioning success rates and improves deployment time. You're not billed for these over-provisioned VM's and they don't count towards the Subscription Quota. Defaults to `true`.

plan.name.description: Specifies the name of the image from the marketplace

plan.product.description: Specifies the product of the image from the marketplace

plan.publisher.description: Specifies the publisher of the image

platform_fault_domain_count.description: Specifies the number of fault domains that are used by this Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set

priority.description: The Priority of this Virtual Machine Scale Set. Possible values are `Regular` and `Spot`. Defaults to `Regular`. Changing this value forces a new resource.

protected_settings_from_key_vault.secret.description: The URL to the Key Vault Secret which stores the protected settings.

protected_settings_from_key_vault.source.description: The ID of the source Key Vault.

provision_vm_agent.description: Should the Azure VM Agent be provisioned on each Virtual Machine in the Scale Set? Defaults to `true`. Changing this value

proximity_placement_group_id.description: The ID of the Proximity Placement Group in which the Virtual Machine Scale Set should be assigned to

public_ip_address.domain_name_label.description: The Prefix which should be used for the Domain Name Label for each Virtual Machine Instance. Azure concatenates the Domain Name Label and Virtual Machine Index to create a unique Domain Name Label for each Virtual Machine.

public_ip_address.idle_timeout_in_minute.description: The Idle Timeout in Minutes for the Public IP Address. Possible values are in the range `4` to `32`.

public_ip_address.ip_tag.description: One or more `ip_tag` blocks as defined above

public_ip_address.name.description: The Name of the Public IP Address Configuration.

public_ip_address.public_ip_prefix_id.description: The ID of the Public IP Address Prefix from where Public IP Addresses should be allocated

public_ip_address.version.description: The Internet Protocol Version which should be used for this public IP address. Possible values are `IPv4` and `IPv6`. Defaults to `IPv4`

resilient_vm_creation_enabled.description: Should resilient VM creation be enabled? When enabled, the service will attempt to create VMs in alternative fault domains or zones if the primary location fails during creation. Defaults to `false`.

resilient_vm_deletion_enabled.description: Should resilient VM deletion be enabled? When enabled, the service will use a more resilient deletion process that attempts to gracefully handle failures during VM termination. Defaults to `false`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group in which the Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set should be exist

rolling_upgrade_policy.description: A `rolling_upgrade_policy` block as defined below. This is Required and can only be specified when `upgrade_mode` is set to `Automatic` or `Rolling`

rolling_upgrade_policy.cross_zone_upgrad.description: Should the Virtual Machine Scale Set ignore the Azure Zone boundaries when constructing upgrade batches? Possible values are `true` or `false`.

rolling_upgrade_policy.max_batch_instanc.description: The maximum percent of total virtual machine instances that will be upgraded simultaneously by the rolling upgrade in one batch. As this is a maximum, unhealthy instances in previous or future batches can cause the percentage of instances in a batch to decrease to ensure higher reliability.

rolling_upgrade_policy.max_unhealthy_ins.description: The maximum percentage of the total virtual machine instances in the scale set that can be simultaneously unhealthy, either as a result of being upgraded, or by being found in an unhealthy state by the virtual machine health checks before the rolling upgrade aborts. This constraint will be checked prior to starting any batch.

rolling_upgrade_policy.max_unhealthy_upg.description: The maximum percentage of upgraded virtual machine instances that can be found to be in an unhealthy state. This check will happen after each batch is upgraded. If this percentage is ever exceeded, the rolling update aborts.

rolling_upgrade_policy.maximum_surge_ins.description: Create new virtual machines to upgrade the scale set, rather than updating the existing virtual machines. Existing virtual machines will be deleted once the new virtual machines are created for each batch. Possible values are `true` or `false`.

rolling_upgrade_policy.pause_time_betwee.description: The wait time between completing the update for all virtual machines in one batch and starting the next batch. The time duration should be specified in ISO 8601 format.

rolling_upgrade_policy.prioritize_unheal.description: Upgrade all unhealthy instances in a scale set before any healthy instances. Possible values are `true` or `false`.

scale_in.force_deletion_enabled.description: Should the virtual machines chosen for removal be force deleted when the virtual machine scale set is being scaled-in? Possible values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`.

scale_in.rule.description: The scale-in policy rule that decides which virtual machines are chosen for removal when a Virtual Machine Scale Set is scaled in. Possible values for the scale-in policy rules are `Default`, `NewestVM` and `OldestVM`, defaults to `Default`. For more information about scale in policy, please [refer to this doc](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machine-scale-sets/virtual-machine-scale-sets-scale-in-policy).

secret.description: One or more `secret` blocks as defined below.

secret.certificate.description: One or more `certificate` blocks as defined above.

secret.key_vault_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault from which all Secrets should be sourced.

secure_boot_enabled.description: Specifies whether secure boot should be enabled on the virtual machine

single_placement_group.description: Should this Virtual Machine Scale Set be limited to a Single Placement Group, which means the number of instances will be capped at 100 Virtual Machines. Defaults to `true`.

sku.description: The Virtual Machine SKU for the Scale Set, such as `Standard_F2`.

source_image_id.description: The ID of an Image which each Virtual Machine in this Scale Set should be based on. Possible Image ID types include `Image ID`, `Shared Image ID`, `Shared Image Version ID`, `Community Gallery Image ID`, `Community Gallery Image Version ID`, `Shared Gallery Image ID` and `Shared Gallery Image Version ID`.

source_image_reference.offer.description: Specifies the offer of the image used to create the virtual machines

source_image_reference.publisher.description: Specifies the publisher of the image used to create the virtual machines

source_image_reference.sku.description: Specifies the SKU of the image used to create the virtual machines.

source_image_reference.version.description: Specifies the version of the image used to create the virtual machines.

spot_restore.enabled.description: Should the Spot-Try-Restore feature be enabled? The Spot-Try-Restore feature will attempt to automatically restore the evicted Spot Virtual Machine Scale Set VM instances opportunistically based on capacity availability and pricing constraints. Possible values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`

spot_restore.timeout.description: The length of time that the Virtual Machine Scale Set should attempt to restore the Spot VM instances which have been evicted. The time duration should be between `15` minutes and `120` minutes (inclusive). The time duration should be specified in the ISO 8601 format. Defaults to `PT1H`

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to this Virtual Machine Scale Set.

termination_notification.enabled.description: Should the termination notification be enabled on this Virtual Machine Scale Set?

termination_notification.timeout.description: Length of time (in minutes, between 5 and 15) a notification to be sent to the VM on the instance metadata server till the VM gets deleted. The time duration should be specified in ISO 8601 format. Defaults to `PT5M`.

upgrade_mode.description: Specifies how Upgrades (e.g. changing the Image/SKU) should be performed to Virtual Machine Instances. Possible values are `Automatic`, `Manual` and `Rolling`. Defaults to `Manual`

user_data.description: The Base64-Encoded User Data which should be used for this Virtual Machine Scale Set.

vtpm_enabled.description: Specifies whether vTPM should be enabled on the virtual machine

zone_balance.description: Should the Virtual Machines in this Scale Set be strictly evenly distributed across Availability Zones? Defaults to `false`

zones.description: Specifies a list of Availability Zones in which this Linux Virtual Machine Scale Set should be located.

action.action_type.description: Predefined action to be taken to an Auto Heal trigger. Possible values include: `Recycle`.

action.minimum_process_execution_time.description: The minimum amount of time in `hh:mm:ss` the Linux Web App must have been running before the defined action will be run in the event of a trigger.

active_directory.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed audience values to consider when validating JWTs issued by Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_secret.description: The Client Secret for the Client ID. Cannot be used with `client_secret_setting_name`.

active_directory.client_secret_setting_n.description: The App Setting name that contains the client secret of the Client. Cannot be used with `client_secret`.

active_directory_v2.allowed_applications.description: The list of allowed Applications for the Default Authorisation Policy.

active_directory_v2.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed audience values to consider when validating JWTs issued by Azure Active Directory.

active_directory_v2.allowed_groups.description: The list of allowed Group Names for the Default Authorisation Policy.

active_directory_v2.allowed_identities.description: The list of allowed Identities for the Default Authorisation Policy.

active_directory_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with Azure Active Directory.

active_directory_v2.client_secret_certif.description: The thumbprint of the certificate used for signing purposes.

active_directory_v2.client_secret_settin.description: The App Setting name that contains the client secret of the Client.

active_directory_v2.jwt_allowed_client_a.description: A list of Allowed Client Applications in the JWT Claim.

active_directory_v2.jwt_allowed_groups.description: A list of Allowed Groups in the JWT Claim.

active_directory_v2.login_parameters.description: A map of key-value pairs to send to the Authorisation Endpoint when a user logs in.

active_directory_v2.tenant_auth_endpoint.description: The Azure Tenant Endpoint for the Authenticating Tenant. e.g. `https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant-guid}/v2.0/`

active_directory_v2.www_authentication_d.description: Should the www-authenticate provider should be omitted from the request? Defaults to `false`.

app_settings.description: A map of key-value pairs of App Settings.

apple_v2.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Apple web application.

apple_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for Apple Login.

application_logs.file_system_level.description: Log level. Possible values include: `Off`, `Verbose`, `Information`, `Warning`, and `Error`.

application_stack.docker_image_name.description: The docker image, including tag, to be used. e.g. `appsvc/staticsite:latest`.

application_stack.docker_registry_passwo.description: The User Name to use for authentication against the registry to pull the image.

application_stack.docker_registry_url.description: The URL of the container registry where the `docker_image_name` is located. e.g. `https://index.docker.io` or `https://mcr.microsoft.com`. This value is required with `docker_image_name`.

application_stack.docker_registry_userna.description: The User Name to use for authentication against the registry to pull the image.

application_stack.dotnet_version.description: The version of .NET to use. Possible values include `3.1`, `5.0`, `6.0`, `7.0`, `8.0`, `9.0`and `10.0`.

application_stack.go_version.description: The version of Go to use. Possible values include `1.18`, and `1.19`.

application_stack.java_server.description: The Java server type. Possible values include `JAVA`, `TOMCAT`, and `JBOSSEAP`.

application_stack.java_server_version.description: The Version of the `java_server` to use.

application_stack.java_version.description: The Version of Java to use. Possible values include `8`, `11`, `17`, `21` and `25`.

application_stack.node_version.description: The version of Node to run. Possible values include `12-lts`, `14-lts`, `16-lts`, `18-lts`, `20-lts`, `22-lts` and `24-lts`. This property conflicts with `java_version`.

application_stack.php_version.description: The version of PHP to run. Possible values are `7.4`, `8.0`, `8.1`, `8.2`, `8.3` and `8.4`.

application_stack.python_version.description: The version of Python to run. Possible values include `3.14`, `3.13`, `3.12`, `3.11`, `3.10`, `3.9`, `3.8` and `3.7`.

application_stack.ruby_version.description: The version of Ruby to run. Possible values include `2.6` and `2.7`.

auth_settings.additional_login_parameter.description: Specifies a map of login Parameters to send to the OpenID Connect authorization endpoint when a user logs in.

auth_settings.allowed_external_redirect_.description: Specifies a list of External URLs that can be redirected to as part of logging in or logging out of the Linux Web App.

auth_settings.default_provider.description: The default authentication provider to use when multiple providers are configured. Possible values include: `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderAzureActiveDirectory`, `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderFacebook`, `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderGoogle`, `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderMicrosoftAccount`, `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderTwitter`, `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderGithub`

auth_settings.enabled.description: Should the Authentication / Authorization feature be enabled for the Linux Web App?

auth_settings.issuer.description: The OpenID Connect Issuer URI that represents the entity that issues access tokens for this Linux Web App.

auth_settings.runtime_version.description: The RuntimeVersion of the Authentication / Authorization feature in use for the Linux Web App.

auth_settings.token_refresh_extension_ho.description: The number of hours after session token expiration that a session token can be used to call the token refresh API. Defaults to `72` hours.

auth_settings.token_store_enabled.description: Should the Linux Web App durably store platform-specific security tokens that are obtained during login flows? Defaults to `false`.

auth_settings.unauthenticated_client_act.description: The action to take when an unauthenticated client attempts to access the app. Possible values include: `RedirectToLoginPage`, `AllowAnonymous`.

auth_settings_v2.auth_enabled.description: Should the AuthV2 Settings be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

auth_settings_v2.config_file_path.description: The path to the App Auth settings.

auth_settings_v2.custom_oidc_v2.description: Zero or more `custom_oidc_v2` blocks as defined below.

auth_settings_v2.default_provider.description: The Default Authentication Provider to use when the `unauthenticated_action` is set to `RedirectToLoginPage`. Possible values include: `apple`, `azureactivedirectory`, `facebook`, `github`, `google`, `twitter` and the `name` of your `custom_oidc_v2` provider.

auth_settings_v2.excluded_paths.description: The paths which should be excluded from the `unauthenticated_action` when it is set to `RedirectToLoginPage`.

auth_settings_v2.forward_proxy_conventio.description: The convention used to determine the url of the request made. Possible values include `NoProxy`, `Standard`, `Custom`. Defaults to `NoProxy`.

auth_settings_v2.forward_proxy_custom_ho.description: The name of the custom header containing the host of the request.

auth_settings_v2.forward_proxy_custom_sc.description: The name of the custom header containing the scheme of the request.

auth_settings_v2.http_route_api_prefix.description: The prefix that should precede all the authentication and authorisation paths. Defaults to `/.auth`.

auth_settings_v2.require_authentication.description: Should the authentication flow be used for all requests.

auth_settings_v2.require_https.description: Should HTTPS be required on connections? Defaults to `true`.

auth_settings_v2.runtime_version.description: The Runtime Version of the Authentication and Authorisation feature of this App. Defaults to `~1`.

auth_settings_v2.unauthenticated_action.description: The action to take for requests made without authentication. Possible values include `RedirectToLoginPage`, `AllowAnonymous`, `Return401`, and `Return403`. Defaults to `RedirectToLoginPage`.

azure_blob_storage.level.description: The level at which to log. Possible values include `Error`, `Warning`, `Information`, `Verbose` and `Off`. **NOTE:** this field is not available for `http_logs`

azure_blob_storage.retention_in_days.description: The time in days after which to remove blobs. A value of `0` means no retention.

azure_blob_storage.sas_url.description: SAS url to an Azure blob container with read/write/list/delete permissions.

azure_blob_storage_http.retention_in_day.description: The time in days after which to remove blobs. A value of `0` means no retention.

azure_blob_storage_http.sas_url.description: SAS url to an Azure blob container with read/write/list/delete permissions.

azure_static_web_app_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with Azure Static Web App Authentication.

backup.enabled.description: Should this backup job be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

backup.name.description: The name which should be used for this Backup.

backup.storage_account_url.description: The SAS URL to the container.

client_certificate_exclusion_paths.description: Paths to exclude when using client certificates, separated by ;

client_certificate_mode.description: The Client Certificate mode. Possible values are `Required`, `Optional`, and `OptionalInteractiveUser`. This property has no effect when `client_certificate_enabled` is `false`. Defaults to `Required`.

connection_string.description: One or more `connection_string` blocks as defined below.

connection_string.name.description: The name of the Connection String.

connection_string.type.description: Type of database. Possible values include: `MySQL`, `SQLServer`, `SQLAzure`, `Custom`, `NotificationHub`, `ServiceBus`, `EventHub`, `APIHub`, `DocDb`, `RedisCache`, and `PostgreSQL`.

cors.allowed_origins.description: Specifies a list of origins that should be allowed to make cross-origin calls.

cors.support_credentials.description: Whether CORS requests with credentials are allowed. Defaults to `false`

custom_oidc_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with the Custom OIDC.

custom_oidc_v2.name.description: The name of the Custom OIDC Authentication Provider.

custom_oidc_v2.name_claim_type.description: The name of the claim that contains the users name.

custom_oidc_v2.openid_configuration_endp.description: Specifies the endpoint used for OpenID Connect Discovery. For example `https://example.com/.well-known/openid-configuration`.

custom_oidc_v2.scopes.description: The list of the scopes that should be requested while authenticating.

enabled.description: Should the Linux Web App be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

facebook.app_id.description: The App ID of the Facebook app used for login.

facebook.app_secret.description: The App Secret of the Facebook app used for Facebook login. Cannot be specified with `app_secret_setting_name`.

facebook.app_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `app_secret` value used for Facebook login. Cannot be specified with `app_secret`.

facebook.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes to be requested as part of Facebook login authentication.

facebook_v2.app_id.description: The App ID of the Facebook app used for login.

facebook_v2.app_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `app_secret` value used for Facebook Login.

facebook_v2.graph_api_version.description: The version of the Facebook API to be used while logging in.

facebook_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of scopes that should be requested as part of Facebook Login authentication.

file_system.retention_in_days.description: The retention period in days. A value of `0` means no retention.

file_system.retention_in_mb.description: The maximum size in megabytes that log files can use.

ftp_publish_basic_authentication_enabled.description: Should the default FTP Basic Authentication publishing profile be enabled. Defaults to `true`.

github.client_id.description: The ID of the GitHub app used for login.

github.client_secret.description: The Client Secret of the GitHub app used for GitHub login. Cannot be specified with `client_secret_setting_name`.

github.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for GitHub login. Cannot be specified with `client_secret`.

github.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of GitHub login authentication.

github_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the GitHub app used for login..

github_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for GitHub Login.

github_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that should be requested as part of GitHub Login authentication.

google.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Google web application.

google.client_secret.description: The client secret associated with the Google web application. Cannot be specified with `client_secret_setting_name`.

google.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for Google login. Cannot be specified with `client_secret`.

google.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication. If not specified, `openid`, `profile`, and `email` are used as default scopes.

google_v2.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed Audiences that should be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication.

google_v2.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Google web application.

google_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for Google Login.

google_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that should be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication.

headers.x_azure_fdid.description: Specifies a list of Azure Front Door IDs.

headers.x_fd_health_probe.description: Specifies if a Front Door Health Probe should be expected. The only possible value is `1`.

headers.x_forwarded_for.description: Specifies a list of addresses for which matching should be applied. Omitting this value means allow any.

headers.x_forwarded_host.description: Specifies a list of Hosts for which matching should be applied.

https_only.description: Should the Linux Web App require HTTPS connections. Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Linux Web App.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Linux Web App. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

ip_restriction.action.description: The action to take. Possible values are `Allow` or `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

ip_restriction.description.description: The Description of this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The CIDR notation of the IP or IP Range to match. For example: `10.0.0.0/24` or `192.168.10.1/32`

ip_restriction.name.description: The name which should be used for this `ip_restriction`.

ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority value of this `ip_restriction`. Defaults to `65000`.

ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.virtual_network_subnet_id.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

key_vault_reference_identity_id.description: The User Assigned Identity ID used for accessing KeyVault secrets. The identity must be assigned to the application in the `identity` block. [For more information see - Access vaults with a user-assigned identity](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/app-service-key-vault-references#access-vaults-with-a-user-assigned-identity).

location.description: The Azure Region where the Linux Web App should exist. Changing this forces a new Linux Web App to be created.

login.allowed_external_redirect_urls.description: External URLs that can be redirected to as part of logging in or logging out of the app. This is an advanced setting typically only needed by Windows Store application backends.

login.cookie_expiration_convention.description: The method by which cookies expire. Possible values include: `FixedTime`, and `IdentityProviderDerived`. Defaults to `FixedTime`.

login.cookie_expiration_time.description: The time after the request is made when the session cookie should expire. Defaults to `08:00:00`.

login.logout_endpoint.description: The endpoint to which logout requests should be made.

login.nonce_expiration_time.description: The time after the request is made when the nonce should expire. Defaults to `00:05:00`.

login.preserve_url_fragments_for_logins.description: Should the fragments from the request be preserved after the login request is made. Defaults to `false`.

login.token_refresh_extension_time.description: The number of hours after session token expiration that a session token can be used to call the token refresh API. Defaults to `72` hours.

login.token_store_enabled.description: Should the Token Store configuration Enabled. Defaults to `false`

login.token_store_path.description: The directory path in the App Filesystem in which the tokens will be stored.

login.token_store_sas_setting_name.description: The name of the app setting which contains the SAS URL of the blob storage containing the tokens.

login.validate_nonce.description: Should the nonce be validated while completing the login flow. Defaults to `true`.

logs.detailed_error_messages.description: Should detailed error messages be enabled?

logs.failed_request_tracing.description: Should the failed request tracing be enabled?

microsoft.client_id.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft.client_secret.description: The OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication. Cannot be specified with `client_secret_setting_name`.

microsoft.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name containing the OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication. Cannot be specified with `client_secret`.

microsoft.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Microsoft Account authentication. If not specified, "wl.basic" is used as the default scope.

microsoft_v2.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed Audiences that will be requested as part of Microsoft Sign-In authentication.

microsoft_v2.client_id.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name containing the OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of Login scopes that should be requested as part of Microsoft Account authentication.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Linux Web App. Changing this forces a new Linux Web App to be created.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Should public network access be enabled for the Web App. Defaults to `true`.

requests.count.description: The number of requests in the specified `interval` to trigger this rule.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Linux Web App should exist. Changing this forces a new Linux Web App to be created.

schedule.frequency_interval.description: How often the backup should be executed (e.g. for weekly backup, this should be set to `7` and `frequency_unit` should be set to `Day`).

schedule.frequency_unit.description: The unit of time for how often the backup should take place. Possible values include: `Day`, `Hour`

schedule.keep_at_least_one_backup.description: Should the service keep at least one backup, regardless of the age of backup? Defaults to `false`.

schedule.retention_period_days.description: After how many days backups should be deleted. Defaults to `30`.

schedule.start_time.description: When the schedule should start working in RFC-3339 format.

scm_ip_restriction.action.description: The action to take. Possible values are `Allow` or `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

scm_ip_restriction.description.description: The Description of this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The CIDR notation of the IP or IP Range to match. For example: `10.0.0.0/24` or `192.168.10.1/32`

scm_ip_restriction.name.description: The name which should be used for this `ip_restriction`.

scm_ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority value of this `ip_restriction`. Defaults to `65000`.

scm_ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.virtual_network_subne.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

service_plan_id.description: The ID of the Service Plan that this Linux App Service will be created in.

site_config.always_on.description: If this Linux Web App is Always On enabled. Defaults to `true`.

site_config.api_definition_url.description: The URL to the API Definition for this Linux Web App.

site_config.api_management_api_id.description: The API Management API ID this Linux Web App is associated with.

site_config.app_command_line.description: The App command line to launch.

site_config.auto_heal_setting.description: A `auto_heal_setting` block as defined above. Required with `auto_heal`.

site_config.container_registry_managed_i.description: The Client ID of the Managed Service Identity to use for connections to the Azure Container Registry.

site_config.container_registry_use_manag.description: Should connections for Azure Container Registry use Managed Identity.

site_config.default_documents.description: Specifies a list of Default Documents for the Linux Web App.

site_config.ftps_state.description: The State of FTP / FTPS service. Possible values include `AllAllowed`, `FtpsOnly`, and `Disabled`. Defaults to `Disabled`.

site_config.health_check_eviction_time_i.description: The amount of time in minutes that a node can be unhealthy before being removed from the load balancer. Possible values are between `2` and `10`. Only valid in conjunction with `health_check_path`.

site_config.health_check_path.description: The path to the Health Check.

site_config.ip_restriction.description: One or more `ip_restriction` blocks as defined above.

site_config.ip_restriction_default_actio.description: The Default action for traffic that does not match any `ip_restriction` rule. possible values include `Allow` and `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

site_config.load_balancing_mode.description: The Site load balancing. Possible values include: `WeightedRoundRobin`, `LeastRequests`, `LeastResponseTime`, `WeightedTotalTraffic`, `RequestHash`, `PerSiteRoundRobin`. Defaults to `LeastRequests` if omitted.

site_config.managed_pipeline_mode.description: Managed pipeline mode. Possible values include `Integrated`, and `Classic`. Defaults to `Integrated`.

site_config.minimum_tls_version.description: The configures the minimum version of TLS required for SSL requests. Possible values include: `1.0`, `1.1`, `1.2` and `1.3`. Defaults to `1.2`.

site_config.remote_debugging_enabled.description: Should Remote Debugging be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.remote_debugging_version.description: The Remote Debugging Version. Currently only `VS2022` is supported.

site_config.scm_ip_restriction.description: One or more `scm_ip_restriction` blocks as defined above.

site_config.scm_ip_restriction_default_a.description: The Default action for traffic that does not match any `scm_ip_restriction` rule. possible values include `Allow` and `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

site_config.scm_minimum_tls_version.description: The configures the minimum version of TLS required for SSL requests to the SCM site Possible values are `1.0`, `1.1`, `1.2` and `1.3`. Defaults to `1.2`.

site_config.scm_use_main_ip_restriction.description: Should the Linux Web App `ip_restriction` configuration be used for the SCM also.

site_config.use_32_bit_worker.description: Should the Linux Web App use a 32-bit worker? Defaults to `true`.

site_config.vnet_route_all_enabled.description: Should all outbound traffic have NAT Gateways, Network Security Groups and User Defined Routes applied? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.websockets_enabled.description: Should Web Sockets be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.worker_count.description: The number of Workers for this Linux App Service.

slow_request.count.description: The number of Slow Requests in the time `interval` to trigger this rule.

slow_request.interval.description: The time interval in the form `hh:mm:ss`.

slow_request.time_taken.description: The threshold of time passed to qualify as a Slow Request in `hh:mm:ss`.

slow_request_with_path.count.description: The number of Slow Requests in the time `interval` to trigger this rule.

slow_request_with_path.interval.description: The time interval in the form `hh:mm:ss`.

slow_request_with_path.path.description: The path for which this slow request rule applies.

slow_request_with_path.time_taken.description: The threshold of time passed to qualify as a Slow Request in `hh:mm:ss`.

status_code.count.description: The number of occurrences of the defined `status_code` in the specified `interval` on which to trigger this rule.

status_code.interval.description: The time interval in the form `hh:mm:ss`.

status_code.path.description: The path to which this rule status code applies.

status_code.status_code_range.description: The status code for this rule, accepts single status codes and status code ranges. e.g. `500` or `400-499`. Possible values are integers between `101` and `599`

status_code.sub_status.description: The Request Sub Status of the Status Code.

status_code.win32_status_code.description: The Win32 Status Code of the Request.

sticky_settings.app_setting_names.description: A list of `app_setting` names that the Linux Web App will not swap between Slots when a swap operation is triggered.

sticky_settings.connection_string_names.description: A list of `connection_string` names that the Linux Web App will not swap between Slots when a swap operation is triggered.

storage_account.description: One or more `storage_account` blocks as defined below.

storage_account.access_key.description: The Access key for the storage account.

storage_account.account_name.description: The Name of the Storage Account.

storage_account.mount_path.description: The path at which to mount the storage share.

storage_account.name.description: The name which should be used for this Storage Account.

storage_account.share_name.description: The Name of the File Share or Container Name for Blob storage.

storage_account.type.description: The Azure Storage Type. Possible values include `AzureFiles` and `AzureBlob`

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Linux Web App.

trigger.slow_request_with_path.description: One or more `slow_request_with_path` blocks as defined above.

trigger.status_code.description: One or more `status_code` blocks as defined above.

twitter.consumer_key.description: The OAuth 1.0a consumer key of the Twitter application used for sign-in.

twitter.consumer_secret.description: The OAuth 1.0a consumer secret of the Twitter application used for sign-in. Cannot be specified with `consumer_secret_setting_name`.

twitter.consumer_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the OAuth 1.0a consumer secret of the Twitter application used for sign-in. Cannot be specified with `consumer_secret`.

twitter_v2.consumer_key.description: The OAuth 1.0a consumer key of the Twitter application used for sign-in.

twitter_v2.consumer_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the OAuth 1.0a consumer secret of the Twitter application used for sign-in.

virtual_network_backup_restore_enabled.description: Whether backup and restore operations over the linked virtual network are enabled. Defaults to `false`.

virtual_network_subnet_id.description: The subnet id which will be used by this Web App for [regional virtual network integration](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-vnet-integration#regional-virtual-network-integration).

vnet_image_pull_enabled.description: Should the traffic for the image pull be routed over virtual network enabled. Defaults to `false`.

webdeploy_publish_basic_authentication_e.description: Should the default WebDeploy Basic Authentication publishing credentials enabled. Defaults to `true`.

zip_deploy_file.description: The local path and filename of the Zip packaged application to deploy to this Linux Web App.

action.action_type.description: Predefined action to be taken to an Auto Heal trigger. Possible values include: `Recycle`.

action.minimum_process_execution_time.description: The minimum amount of time in `hh:mm:ss` the Linux Web App must have been running before the defined action will be run in the event of a trigger.

active_directory.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed audience values to consider when validating JWTs issued by Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with Azure Active Directory.

active_directory.client_secret.description: The Client Secret for the Client ID. Cannot be used with `client_secret_setting_name`.

active_directory.client_secret_setting_n.description: The App Setting name that contains the client secret of the Client. Cannot be used with `client_secret`.

active_directory_v2.allowed_applications.description: The list of allowed Applications for the Default Authorisation Policy.

active_directory_v2.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed audience values to consider when validating JWTs issued by Azure Active Directory.

active_directory_v2.allowed_groups.description: The list of allowed Group Names for the Default Authorisation Policy.

active_directory_v2.allowed_identities.description: The list of allowed Identities for the Default Authorisation Policy.

active_directory_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with Azure Active Directory.

active_directory_v2.client_secret_certif.description: The thumbprint of the certificate used for signing purposes.

active_directory_v2.client_secret_settin.description: The App Setting name that contains the client secret of the Client.

active_directory_v2.jwt_allowed_client_a.description: A list of Allowed Client Applications in the JWT Claim.

active_directory_v2.jwt_allowed_groups.description: A list of Allowed Groups in the JWT Claim.

active_directory_v2.login_parameters.description: A map of key-value pairs to send to the Authorisation Endpoint when a user logs in.

active_directory_v2.tenant_auth_endpoint.description: The Azure Tenant Endpoint for the Authenticating Tenant. e.g. `https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant-guid}/v2.0/`

active_directory_v2.www_authentication_d.description: Should the www-authenticate provider should be omitted from the request? Defaults to `false`.

app_service_id.description: The ID of the Linux Web App this Deployment Slot will be part of.

app_settings.description: A map of key-value pairs of App Settings.

apple_v2.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Apple web application.

apple_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for Apple Login.

application_logs.file_system_level.description: Log level. Possible values include `Off`, `Verbose`, `Information`, `Warning`, and `Error`.

application_stack.docker_image_name.description: The docker image, including tag, to be used. e.g. `appsvc/staticsite:latest`.

application_stack.docker_registry_passwo.description: The User Name to use for authentication against the registry to pull the image.

application_stack.docker_registry_url.description: The URL of the container registry where the `docker_image_name` is located. e.g. `https://index.docker.io` or `https://mcr.microsoft.com`. This value is required with `docker_image_name`.

application_stack.docker_registry_userna.description: The User Name to use for authentication against the registry to pull the image.

application_stack.dotnet_version.description: The version of .NET to use. Possible values include `3.1`, `5.0`, `6.0`, `7.0`, `8.0`, `9.0` and `10.0`.

application_stack.go_version.description: The version of Go to use. Possible values include `1.18`, and `1.19`.

application_stack.java_server.description: The Java server type. Possible values include `JAVA`, `TOMCAT`, and `JBOSSEAP`.

application_stack.java_server_version.description: The Version of the `java_server` to use.

application_stack.java_version.description: The Version of Java to use. Possible values are `8`, `11`, `17` and `21`.

application_stack.node_version.description: The version of Node to run. Possible values are `12-lts`, `14-lts`, `16-lts`, `18-lts`, `20-lts`, `22-lts` and `24-lts`. This property conflicts with `java_version`.

application_stack.php_version.description: The version of PHP to run. Possible values are `7.4`, `8.0`, `8.1`, `8.2`, `8.3` and `8.4`.

application_stack.python_version.description: The version of Python to run. Possible values include `3.14`, `3.13`, `3.12`, `3.11`, `3.10`, `3.9`, `3.8` and `3.7`.

application_stack.ruby_version.description: The version of Ruby to run. Possible values include `2.6` and `2.7`.

auth_settings.additional_login_parameter.description: Specifies a map of login Parameters to send to the OpenID Connect authorization endpoint when a user logs in.

auth_settings.allowed_external_redirect_.description: Specifies a list of External URLs that can be redirected to as part of logging in or logging out of the Linux Web App.

auth_settings.default_provider.description: The default authentication provider to use when multiple providers are configured. Possible values include: `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderAzureActiveDirectory`, `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderFacebook`, `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderGoogle`, `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderMicrosoftAccount`, `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderTwitter`, `BuiltInAuthenticationProviderGithub`

auth_settings.enabled.description: Should the Authentication / Authorization feature be enabled for the Linux Web App?

auth_settings.issuer.description: The OpenID Connect Issuer URI that represents the entity that issues access tokens for this Linux Web App.

auth_settings.runtime_version.description: The RuntimeVersion of the Authentication / Authorization feature in use for the Linux Web App.

auth_settings.token_refresh_extension_ho.description: The number of hours after session token expiration that a session token can be used to call the token refresh API. Defaults to `72` hours.

auth_settings.token_store_enabled.description: Should the Linux Web App durably store platform-specific security tokens that are obtained during login flows? Defaults to `false`.

auth_settings.unauthenticated_client_act.description: The action to take when an unauthenticated client attempts to access the app. Possible values include: `RedirectToLoginPage`, `AllowAnonymous`.

auth_settings_v2.auth_enabled.description: Should the AuthV2 Settings be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

auth_settings_v2.config_file_path.description: The path to the App Auth settings.

auth_settings_v2.custom_oidc_v2.description: Zero or more `custom_oidc_v2` blocks as defined below.

auth_settings_v2.default_provider.description: The Default Authentication Provider to use when the `unauthenticated_action` is set to `RedirectToLoginPage`. Possible values include: `apple`, `azureactivedirectory`, `facebook`, `github`, `google`, `twitter` and the `name` of your `custom_oidc_v2` provider.

auth_settings_v2.excluded_paths.description: The paths which should be excluded from the `unauthenticated_action` when it is set to `RedirectToLoginPage`.

auth_settings_v2.forward_proxy_conventio.description: The convention used to determine the url of the request made. Possible values include `NoProxy`, `Standard`, `Custom`. Defaults to `NoProxy`.

auth_settings_v2.forward_proxy_custom_ho.description: The name of the custom header containing the host of the request.

auth_settings_v2.forward_proxy_custom_sc.description: The name of the custom header containing the scheme of the request.

auth_settings_v2.http_route_api_prefix.description: The prefix that should precede all the authentication and authorisation paths. Defaults to `/.auth`.

auth_settings_v2.require_authentication.description: Should the authentication flow be used for all requests.

auth_settings_v2.require_https.description: Should HTTPS be required on connections? Defaults to `true`.

auth_settings_v2.runtime_version.description: The Runtime Version of the Authentication and Authorisation feature of this App. Defaults to `~1`.

auth_settings_v2.unauthenticated_action.description: The action to take for requests made without authentication. Possible values include `RedirectToLoginPage`, `AllowAnonymous`, `Return401`, and `Return403`. Defaults to `RedirectToLoginPage`.

azure_blob_storage.level.description: The level at which to log. Possible values include `Error`, `Warning`, `Information`, `Verbose` and `Off`. **NOTE:** this field is not available for `http_logs`

azure_blob_storage.retention_in_days.description: The time in days after which to remove blobs. A value of `0` means no retention.

azure_blob_storage.sas_url.description: SAS URL to an Azure blob container with read/write/list/delete permissions.

azure_blob_storage_http.retention_in_day.description: The time in days after which to remove blobs. A value of `0` means no retention.

azure_blob_storage_http.sas_url.description: SAS url to an Azure blob container with read/write/list/delete permissions.

azure_static_web_app_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with Azure Static Web App Authentication.

backup.enabled.description: Should this backup job be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

backup.name.description: The name which should be used for this Backup.

backup.storage_account_url.description: The SAS URL to the container.

client_certificate_exclusion_paths.description: Paths to exclude when using client certificates, separated by ;

client_certificate_mode.description: The Client Certificate mode. Possible values are `Required`, `Optional`, and `OptionalInteractiveUser`. This property has no effect when `client_cert_enabled` is `false`. Defaults to `Required`.

connection_string.description: One or more `connection_string` blocks as defined below.

connection_string.name.description: The name of the Connection String.

connection_string.type.description: Type of database. Possible values include `APIHub`, `Custom`, `DocDb`, `EventHub`, `MySQL`, `NotificationHub`, `PostgreSQL`, `RedisCache`, `ServiceBus`, `SQLAzure`, and `SQLServer`.

cors.allowed_origins.description: Specifies a list of origins that should be allowed to make cross-origin calls.

cors.support_credentials.description: Whether CORS requests with credentials are allowed. Defaults to `false`

custom_oidc_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the Client to use to authenticate with the Custom OIDC.

custom_oidc_v2.name.description: The name of the Custom OIDC Authentication Provider.

custom_oidc_v2.name_claim_type.description: The name of the claim that contains the users name.

custom_oidc_v2.scopes.description: The list of the scopes that should be requested while authenticating.

enabled.description: Should the Linux Web App be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

facebook.app_id.description: The App ID of the Facebook app used for login.

facebook.app_secret.description: The App Secret of the Facebook app used for Facebook login. Cannot be specified with `app_secret_setting_name`.

facebook.app_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `app_secret` value used for Facebook login. Cannot be specified with `app_secret`.

facebook.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes to be requested as part of Facebook login authentication.

facebook_v2.app_id.description: The App ID of the Facebook app used for login.

facebook_v2.app_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `app_secret` value used for Facebook Login.

facebook_v2.graph_api_version.description: The version of the Facebook API to be used while logging in.

facebook_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of scopes that should be requested as part of Facebook Login authentication.

file_system.retention_in_days.description: The retention period in days. A values of `0` means no retention.

file_system.retention_in_mb.description: The maximum size in megabytes that log files can use.

ftp_publish_basic_authentication_enabled.description: Should the default FTP Basic Authentication publishing profile be enabled. Defaults to `true`.

github.client_id.description: The ID of the GitHub app used for login.

github.client_secret.description: The Client Secret of the GitHub app used for GitHub login. Cannot be specified with `client_secret_setting_name`.

github.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for GitHub login. Cannot be specified with `client_secret`.

github.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of GitHub login authentication.

github_v2.client_id.description: The ID of the GitHub app used for login.

github_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for GitHub Login.

github_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that should be requested as part of GitHub Login authentication.

google.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Google web application.

google.client_secret.description: The client secret associated with the Google web application. Cannot be specified with `client_secret_setting_name`.

google.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for Google login. Cannot be specified with `client_secret`.

google.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication. If not specified, `openid`, `profile`, and `email` are used as default scopes.

google_v2.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed Audiences that should be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication.

google_v2.client_id.description: The OpenID Connect Client ID for the Google web application.

google_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the `client_secret` value used for Google Login.

google_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that should be requested as part of Google Sign-In authentication.

headers.x_azure_fdid.description: Specifies a list of Azure Front Door IDs.

headers.x_fd_health_probe.description: Specifies if a Front Door Health Probe should be expected. The only possible value is `1`.

headers.x_forwarded_for.description: Specifies a list of addresses for which matching should be applied. Omitting this value means allow any.

headers.x_forwarded_host.description: Specifies a list of Hosts for which matching should be applied.

https_only.description: Should the Linux Web App require HTTPS connections. Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Linux Web App Slot.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Linux Web App Slot. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned` and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

ip_restriction.action.description: The action to take. Possible values are `Allow` or `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

ip_restriction.description.description: The Description of this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The CIDR notation of the IP or IP Range to match. For example: `10.0.0.0/24` or `192.168.10.1/32`

ip_restriction.name.description: The name which should be used for this `ip_restriction`.

ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority value of this `ip_restriction`. Defaults to `65000`.

ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

ip_restriction.virtual_network_subnet_id.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

key_vault_reference_identity_id.description: The User Assigned Identity ID used for accessing KeyVault secrets. The identity must be assigned to the application in the `identity` block. [For more information see - Access vaults with a user-assigned identity](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/app-service-key-vault-references#access-vaults-with-a-user-assigned-identity).

login.allowed_external_redirect_urls.description: External URLs that can be redirected to as part of logging in or logging out of the app. This is an advanced setting typically only needed by Windows Store application backends.

login.cookie_expiration_convention.description: The method by which cookies expire. Possible values include: `FixedTime`, and `IdentityProviderDerived`. Defaults to `FixedTime`.

login.cookie_expiration_time.description: The time after the request is made when the session cookie should expire. Defaults to `08:00:00`.

login.logout_endpoint.description: The endpoint to which logout requests should be made.

login.nonce_expiration_time.description: The time after the request is made when the nonce should expire. Defaults to `00:05:00`.

login.preserve_url_fragments_for_logins.description: Should the fragments from the request be preserved after the login request is made. Defaults to `false`.

login.token_refresh_extension_time.description: The number of hours after session token expiration that a session token can be used to call the token refresh API. Defaults to `72` hours.

login.token_store_enabled.description: Should the Token Store configuration Enabled. Defaults to `false`

login.token_store_path.description: The directory path in the App Filesystem in which the tokens will be stored.

login.token_store_sas_setting_name.description: The name of the app setting which contains the SAS URL of the blob storage containing the tokens.

login.validate_nonce.description: Should the nonce be validated while completing the login flow. Defaults to `true`.

logs.detailed_error_messages.description: Should detailed error messages be enabled?

logs.failed_request_tracing.description: Should the failed request tracing be enabled?

microsoft.client_id.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft.client_secret.description: The OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication. Cannot be specified with `client_secret_setting_name`.

microsoft.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name containing the OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication. Cannot be specified with `client_secret`.

microsoft.oauth_scopes.description: Specifies a list of OAuth 2.0 scopes that will be requested as part of Microsoft Account authentication. If not specified, "wl.basic" is used as the default scope.

microsoft_v2.allowed_audiences.description: Specifies a list of Allowed Audiences that will be requested as part of Microsoft Sign-In authentication.

microsoft_v2.client_id.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft_v2.client_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name containing the OAuth 2.0 client secret that was created for the app used for authentication.

microsoft_v2.login_scopes.description: The list of Login scopes that should be requested as part of Microsoft Account authentication.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Linux Web App Slot. Changing this forces a new Linux Web App Slot to be created.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Should public network access be enabled for the Web App. Defaults to `true`.

requests.count.description: The number of requests in the specified `interval` to trigger this rule.

schedule.frequency_interval.description: How often the backup should be executed (e.g. for weekly backup, this should be set to `7` and `frequency_unit` should be set to `Day`).

schedule.frequency_unit.description: The unit of time for how often the backup should take place. Possible values include: `Day`, `Hour`

schedule.keep_at_least_one_backup.description: Should the service keep at least one backup, regardless of the age of backup? Defaults to `false`.

schedule.retention_period_days.description: After how many days backups should be deleted. Defaults to `30`.

schedule.start_time.description: When the schedule should start working in RFC-3339 format.

scm_ip_restriction.action.description: The action to take. Possible values are `Allow` or `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

scm_ip_restriction.description.description: The Description of this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.ip_address.description: The CIDR notation of the IP or IP Range to match. For example: `10.0.0.0/24` or `192.168.10.1/32`

scm_ip_restriction.name.description: The name which should be used for this `ip_restriction`.

scm_ip_restriction.priority.description: The priority value of this `ip_restriction`. Defaults to `65000`.

scm_ip_restriction.service_tag.description: The Service Tag used for this IP Restriction.

scm_ip_restriction.virtual_network_subne.description: The Virtual Network Subnet ID used for this IP Restriction.

service_plan_id.description: The ID of the Service Plan in which to run this slot. If not specified the same Service Plan as the Linux Web App will be used.

site_config.always_on.description: If this Linux Web App is Always On enabled. Defaults to `true`.

site_config.api_definition_url.description: The URL to the API Definition for this Linux Web App Slot.

site_config.api_management_api_id.description: The API Management API ID this Linux Web App Slot is associated with.

site_config.app_command_line.description: The App command line to launch.

site_config.auto_heal_setting.description: A `auto_heal_setting` block as defined above. Required with `auto_heal`.

site_config.auto_swap_slot_name.description: The Linux Web App Slot Name to automatically swap to when deployment to that slot is successfully completed.

site_config.container_registry_managed_i.description: The Client ID of the Managed Service Identity to use for connections to the Azure Container Registry.

site_config.container_registry_use_manag.description: Should connections for Azure Container Registry use Managed Identity.

site_config.default_documents.description: Specifies a list of Default Documents for the Linux Web App.

site_config.ftps_state.description: The State of FTP / FTPS service. Possible values include `AllAllowed`, `FtpsOnly`, and `Disabled`. Defaults to `Disabled`.

site_config.health_check_eviction_time_i.description: The amount of time in minutes that a node can be unhealthy before being removed from the load balancer. Possible values are between `2` and `10`. Only valid in conjunction with `health_check_path`.

site_config.health_check_path.description: The path to the Health Check.

site_config.ip_restriction.description: One or more `ip_restriction` blocks as defined above.

site_config.ip_restriction_default_actio.description: The Default action for traffic that does not match any `ip_restriction` rule. possible values include `Allow` and `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

site_config.load_balancing_mode.description: The Site load balancing. Possible values include: `WeightedRoundRobin`, `LeastRequests`, `LeastResponseTime`, `WeightedTotalTraffic`, `RequestHash`, `PerSiteRoundRobin`. Defaults to `LeastRequests` if omitted.

site_config.managed_pipeline_mode.description: Managed pipeline mode. Possible values include: `Integrated`, `Classic`. Defaults to `Integrated`.

site_config.minimum_tls_version.description: The configures the minimum version of TLS required for SSL requests. Possible values are `1.0`, `1.1`, `1.2` and `1.3`. Defaults to `1.2`.

site_config.remote_debugging_enabled.description: Should Remote Debugging be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.remote_debugging_version.description: The Remote Debugging Version. Currently only `VS2022` is supported.

site_config.scm_ip_restriction.description: One or more `scm_ip_restriction` blocks as defined above.

site_config.scm_ip_restriction_default_a.description: The Default action for traffic that does not match any `scm_ip_restriction` rule. possible values include `Allow` and `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

site_config.scm_minimum_tls_version.description: The configures the minimum version of TLS required for SSL requests to the SCM site Possible values are `1.0`, `1.1`, `1.2` and `1.3`. Defaults to `1.2`.

site_config.scm_use_main_ip_restriction.description: Should the Linux Web App `ip_restriction` configuration be used for the SCM also.

site_config.use_32_bit_worker.description: Should the Linux Web App use a 32-bit worker? Defaults to `true`.

site_config.vnet_route_all_enabled.description: Should all outbound traffic have NAT Gateways, Network Security Groups and User Defined Routes applied? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.websockets_enabled.description: Should Web Sockets be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.worker_count.description: The number of Workers for this Linux App Service Slot.

slow_request.count.description: The number of Slow Requests in the time `interval` to trigger this rule.

slow_request.interval.description: The time interval in the form `hh:mm:ss`.

slow_request.time_taken.description: The threshold of time passed to qualify as a Slow Request in `hh:mm:ss`.

slow_request_with_path.count.description: The number of Slow Requests in the time `interval` to trigger this rule.

slow_request_with_path.interval.description: The time interval in the form `hh:mm:ss`.

slow_request_with_path.path.description: The path for which this slow request rule applies.

slow_request_with_path.time_taken.description: The threshold of time passed to qualify as a Slow Request in `hh:mm:ss`.

status_code.count.description: The number of occurrences of the defined `status_code` in the specified `interval` on which to trigger this rule.

status_code.interval.description: The time interval in the form `hh:mm:ss`.

status_code.path.description: The path to which this rule status code applies.

status_code.status_code_range.description: The status code for this rule, accepts single status codes and status code ranges. e.g. `500` or `400-499`. Possible values are integers between `101` and `599`

status_code.sub_status.description: The Request Sub Status of the Status Code.

status_code.win32_status_code.description: The Win32 Status Code of the Request.

storage_account.description: One or more `storage_account` blocks as defined below.

storage_account.access_key.description: The Access key for the storage account.

storage_account.account_name.description: The Name of the Storage Account.

storage_account.mount_path.description: The path at which to mount the storage share.

storage_account.name.description: The name which should be used for this Storage Account.

storage_account.share_name.description: The Name of the File Share or Container Name for Blob storage.

storage_account.type.description: The Azure Storage Type. Possible values include `AzureFiles` and `AzureBlob`

tags.description: A mapping of tags that should be assigned to the Linux Web App.

trigger.slow_request_with_path.description: One or more `slow_request_with_path` blocks as defined above.

trigger.status_code.description: One or more `status_code` blocks as defined above.

twitter.consumer_key.description: The OAuth 1.0a consumer key of the Twitter application used for sign-in.

twitter.consumer_secret.description: The OAuth 1.0a consumer secret of the Twitter application used for sign-in. Cannot be specified with `consumer_secret_setting_name`.

twitter.consumer_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the OAuth 1.0a consumer secret of the Twitter application used for sign-in. Cannot be specified with `consumer_secret`.

twitter_v2.consumer_key.description: The OAuth 1.0a consumer key of the Twitter application used for sign-in.

twitter_v2.consumer_secret_setting_name.description: The app setting name that contains the OAuth 1.0a consumer secret of the Twitter application used for sign-in.

virtual_network_backup_restore_enabled.description: Whether backup and restore operations over the linked virtual network are enabled. Defaults to `false`.

virtual_network_subnet_id.description: The subnet id which will be used by this Web App Slot for [regional virtual network integration](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-vnet-integration#regional-virtual-network-integration).

vnet_image_pull_enabled.description: Should the traffic for the image pull be routed over virtual network enabled. Defaults to `false`.

webdeploy_publish_basic_authentication_e.description: Should the default WebDeploy Basic Authentication publishing credentials enabled. Defaults to `true`.

zip_deploy_file.description: The local path and filename of the Zip packaged application to deploy to this Linux Web App.

description.description: Description of the resource.

encryption.description: An `encryption` block as defined below. Changing this forces a new Load Test to be created.

encryption.identity.description: An `identity` block as defined below. Changing this forces a new Load Test to be created.

encryption.identity_id.description: The User Assigned Identity ID that should be assigned to this Load Test Encryption. Changing this forces a new Load Test to be created.

name.description: Specifies the name of this Load Test. Changing this forces a new Load Test to be created.

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the Resource Group within which this Load Test should exist. Changing this forces a new Load Test to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Load Test.

cmk_for_query_forced.description: Is Customer Managed Storage mandatory for query management?

daily_quota_gb.description: The workspace daily quota for ingestion in GB. Defaults to `-1` (unlimited) if omitted.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of user managed identity ids to be assigned. Required if `type` is `UserAssigned`.

identity.type.description: Specifies the identity type of the Log Analytics Workspace. Possible values are `SystemAssigned` (where Azure will generate a Service Principal for you) and `UserAssigned` where you can specify the Service Principal IDs in the `identity_ids` field.

immediate_data_purge_on_30_days_enabled.description: Whether to remove the data in the Log Analytics Workspace immediately after 30 days.

internet_ingestion_enabled.description: Should the Log Analytics Workspace support ingestion over the Public Internet? Defaults to `true`.

internet_query_enabled.description: Should the Log Analytics Workspace support querying over the Public Internet? Defaults to `true`.

local_authentication_enabled.description: Specifies if the log Analytics workspace should allow local authentication methods in addition to Microsoft Entra (Azure AD). Defaults to `true`.

reservation_capacity_in_gb_per_day.description: The capacity reservation level in GB for this workspace. Possible values are `100`, `200`, `300`, `400`, `500`, `1000`, `2000` and `5000`.

retention_in_days.description: The workspace data retention in days. Possible values are between `30` and `730`.
