site_config.always_on.description: Should the Logic App be loaded at all times? Defaults to `false`.

site_config.app_scale_limit.description: The number of workers this Logic App can scale out to. Only applicable to apps on the Consumption and Premium plan.

site_config.dotnet_framework_version.description: The version of the .NET framework's CLR used in this Logic App Possible values are `v4.0` (including .NET Core 2.1 and 3.1), `v5.0`, `v6.0` and `v8.0`. [For more information on which .NET Framework version to use based on the runtime version you're targeting - please see this table](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/functions-dotnet-class-library#supported-versions). Defaults to `v4.0`.

site_config.elastic_instance_minimum.description: The number of minimum instances for this Logic App Only affects apps on the Premium plan.

site_config.ftps_state.description: State of FTP / FTPS service for this Logic App. Possible values include: `AllAllowed`, `FtpsOnly` and `Disabled`. Defaults to `AllAllowed`.

site_config.health_check_path.description: Path which will be checked for this Logic App health.

site_config.http2_enabled.description: Specifies whether the HTTP2 protocol should be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.ip_restriction.description: A list of `ip_restriction` objects representing IP restrictions as defined below.

site_config.linux_fx_version.description: Linux App Framework and version for the App Service, e.g. `DOCKER|(golang:latest)`. Setting this value will also set the `kind` of application deployed to `functionapp,linux,container,workflowapp`.

site_config.min_tls_version.description: The minimum supported TLS version for the Logic App. Possible values are `1.0`, `1.1`, `1.2` and `1.3`. Defaults to `1.2` for new Logic Apps.

site_config.pre_warmed_instance_count.description: The number of pre-warmed instances for this Logic App Only affects apps on the Premium plan.

site_config.runtime_scale_monitoring_ena.description: Should Runtime Scale Monitoring be enabled?. Only applicable to apps on the Premium plan. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.scm_ip_restriction.description: A list of `scm_ip_restriction` objects representing SCM IP restrictions as defined below.

site_config.scm_min_tls_version.description: Configures the minimum version of TLS required for SSL requests to the SCM site. Possible values are `1.0`, `1.1`, `1.2` and `1.3`.

site_config.scm_type.description: The type of Source Control used by the Logic App in use by the Windows Function App. Defaults to `None`. Possible values are: `BitbucketGit`, `BitbucketHg`, `CodePlexGit`, `CodePlexHg`, `Dropbox`, `ExternalGit`, `ExternalHg`, `GitHub`, `LocalGit`, `None`, `OneDrive`, `Tfs`, `VSO`, and `VSTSRM`

site_config.scm_use_main_ip_restriction.description: Should the Logic App `ip_restriction` configuration be used for the SCM too. Defaults to `false`.

site_config.use_32_bit_worker_process.description: Should the Logic App run in 32 bit mode, rather than 64 bit mode? Defaults to `true`.

site_config.vnet_route_all_enabled.description: Should all outbound traffic to have Virtual Network Security Groups and User Defined Routes applied.

storage_account_access_key.description: The access key which will be used to access the backend storage account for the Logic App.

storage_account_name.description: The backend storage account name which will be used by this Logic App (e.g. for Stateful workflows data)

storage_account_share_name.description: The name of the share used by the logic app, if you want to use a custom name. This corresponds to the WEBSITE_CONTENTSHARE appsetting, which this resource will create for you. If you don't specify a name, then this resource will generate a dynamic name. This setting is useful if you want to provision a storage account and create a share using `azurerm_storage_share`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

use_extension_bundle.description: Should the logic app use the bundled extension package? If true, then application settings for `AzureFunctionsJobHost__extensionBundle__id` and `AzureFunctionsJobHost__extensionBundle__version` will be created. Defaults to `true`.

version.description: The runtime version associated with the Logic App. Defaults to `~4`.

virtual_network_subnet_id.description: The subnet ID which will be used by this resource for [regional virtual network integration](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-vnet-integration#regional-virtual-network-integration).

vnet_content_share_enabled.description: Specifies whether allow routing traffic between the Logic App and Storage Account content share through a virtual network. Defaults to `false`.

interval.description: Specifies interval used for the Frequency, for example a value of `4` for `interval` and `hour` for `frequency` would run the Trigger every 4 hours.

create_option.description: The method to use when creating the managed disk

disk_access_id.description: The ID of the disk access resource for using private endpoints on disks.

disk_encryption_key.secret_url.description: The URL to the Key Vault Secret used as the Disk Encryption Key. This can be found as `id` on the `azurerm_key_vault_secret` resource.

disk_encryption_key.source_vault_id.description: The ID of the source Key Vault. This can be found as `id` on the `azurerm_key_vault` resource.

disk_encryption_set_id.description: The ID of a Disk Encryption Set which should be used to encrypt this Managed Disk. Conflicts with `secure_vm_disk_encryption_set_id`.

disk_iops_read_only.description: The number of IOPS allowed across all VMs mounting the shared disk as read-only; only settable for UltraSSD disks and PremiumV2 disks with shared disk enabled. One operation can transfer between 4k and 256k bytes.

disk_iops_read_write.description: The number of IOPS allowed for this disk; only settable for UltraSSD disks and PremiumV2 disks. One operation can transfer between 4k and 256k bytes.

disk_mbps_read_only.description: The bandwidth allowed across all VMs mounting the shared disk as read-only; only settable for UltraSSD disks and PremiumV2 disks with shared disk enabled. MBps means millions of bytes per second.

disk_mbps_read_write.description: The bandwidth allowed for this disk; only settable for UltraSSD disks and PremiumV2 disks. MBps means millions of bytes per second.

disk_size_gb.description: (Optional, Required for a new managed disk) Specifies the size of the managed disk to create in gigabytes. If `create_option` is `Copy` or `FromImage`, then the value must be equal to or greater than the source's size. The size can only be increased.

edge_zone.description: Specifies the Edge Zone within the Azure Region where this Managed Disk should exist. Changing this forces a new Managed Disk to be created.

gallery_image_reference_id.description: ID of a Gallery Image Version to copy when `create_option` is `FromImage`. This field cannot be specified if image_reference_id is specified

hyper_v_generation.description: The HyperV Generation of the Disk when the source of an `Import` or `Copy` operation targets a source that contains an operating system. Possible values are `V1` and `V2`. For `ImportSecure` it must be set to `V2`

image_reference_id.description: ID of an existing platform/marketplace disk image to copy when `create_option` is `FromImage`. This field cannot be specified if gallery_image_reference_id is specified

key_encryption_key.key_url.description: The URL to the Key Vault Key used as the Key Encryption Key. This can be found as `id` on the `azurerm_key_vault_key` resource.

key_encryption_key.source_vault_id.description: The ID of the source Key Vault. This can be found as `id` on the `azurerm_key_vault` resource.

location.description: Specified the supported Azure location where the resource exists

logical_sector_size.description: Logical Sector Size. Possible values are: `512` and `4096`. Defaults to `4096`

max_shares.description: The maximum number of VMs that can attach to the disk at the same time. Value greater than one indicates a disk that can be mounted on multiple VMs at the same time.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Managed Disk

network_access_policy.description: Policy for accessing the disk via network. Allowed values are `AllowAll`, `AllowPrivate`, and `DenyAll`. Defaults to `AllowAll`.

on_demand_bursting_enabled.description: Specifies if On-Demand Bursting is enabled for the Managed Disk.

optimized_frequent_attach_enabled.description: Specifies whether this Managed Disk should be optimized for frequent disk attachments (where a disk is attached/detached more than 5 times in a day). Defaults to `false`.

os_type.description: Specify a value when the source of an `Import`, `ImportSecure` or `Copy` operation targets a source that contains an operating system. Valid values are `Linux` or `Windows`.

performance_plus_enabled.description: Specifies whether Performance Plus is enabled for this Managed Disk. Defaults to `false`

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether it is allowed to access the disk via public network. Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Managed Disk should exist

secure_vm_disk_encryption_set_id.description: The ID of the Disk Encryption Set which should be used to Encrypt this OS Disk when the Virtual Machine is a Confidential VM. Conflicts with `disk_encryption_set_id`

security_type.description: Security Type of the Managed Disk when it is used for a Confidential VM. Possible values are `ConfidentialVM_VMGuestStateOnlyEncryptedWithPlatformKey`, `ConfidentialVM_DiskEncryptedWithPlatformKey` and `ConfidentialVM_DiskEncryptedWithCustomerKey`

source_resource_id.description: The ID of an existing Managed Disk or Snapshot to copy when `create_option` is `Copy` or the recovery point to restore when `create_option` is `Restore`

source_uri.description: URI to a valid VHD file to be used when `create_option` is `Import` or `ImportSecure`

storage_account_id.description: The ID of the Storage Account where the `source_uri` is located. Required when `create_option` is set to `Import` or `ImportSecure`

storage_account_type.description: The type of storage to use for the managed disk. Possible values are `Standard_LRS`, `StandardSSD_ZRS`, `Premium_LRS`, `PremiumV2_LRS`, `Premium_ZRS`, `StandardSSD_LRS` or `UltraSSD_LRS`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

tier.description: The disk performance tier to use. Possible values are documented [here](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/disks-change-performance). This feature is currently supported only for premium SSDs.

trusted_launch_enabled.description: Specifies if Trusted Launch is enabled for the Managed Disk

upload_size_bytes.description: Specifies the size of the managed disk to create in bytes. Required when `create_option` is `Upload`. The value must be equal to the source disk to be copied in bytes. Source disk size could be calculated with `ls -l` or `wc -c`. More information can be found at [Copy a managed disk](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/linux/disks-upload-vhd-to-managed-disk-cli#copy-a-managed-disk)

zone.description: Specifies the Availability Zone in which this Managed Disk should be located. Changing this property

access_level.description: The level of access required on the disk. Supported are Read, Write

duration_in_seconds.description: The duration for which the export should be allowed. Should be between 30 & 4294967295 seconds

managed_disk_id.description: The ID of an existing Managed Disk which should be exported

encryption_key.key_url.description: The URL to the Key Vault Key used as the Encryption Key. This can be found as `id` on the `azurerm_key_vault_key` resource.

encryption_key.source_vault_id.description: The ID of the source Key Vault. This can be found as `id` on the `azurerm_key_vault` resource.

hsm_setting.container_id.description: The resource ID of the storage container that is used for hydrating the namespace and archiving from the namespace

hsm_setting.import_prefix.description: The import prefix for the Azure Managed Lustre File System. Only blobs in the non-logging container that start with this path/prefix get hydrated into the cluster namespace

hsm_setting.logging_container_id.description: The resource ID of the storage container that is used for logging events and errors

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Azure Managed Lustre File System

identity.type.description: The type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Azure Managed Lustre File System. Only possible value is `UserAssigned`

location.description: The Azure Region where the Azure Managed Lustre File System should exist

maintenance_window.day_of_week.description: The day of the week on which the maintenance window will occur. Possible values are `Sunday`, `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday` and `Saturday`.

maintenance_window.time_of_day_in_utc.description: The time of day (in UTC) to start the maintenance window.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Azure Managed Lustre File System

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Azure Managed Lustre File System should exist

root_squash.mode.description: Squash mode of the AML file system. Possible values are `RootOnly`, and `All`.

root_squash.no_squash_nids.description: NID IP Address list(s) to be added to the TrustedSystems, separated by semicolons.

root_squash.squash_gid.description: The GID to be used for the root squash. Defaults to `0`.

root_squash.squash_uid.description: The UID to be used for the root squash. Defaults to `0`.

sku_name.description: The SKU name for the Azure Managed Lustre File System. Possible values are `AMLFS-Durable-Premium-40`, `AMLFS-Durable-Premium-125`, `AMLFS-Durable-Premium-250` and `AMLFS-Durable-Premium-500`

storage_capacity_in_tb.description: The size of the Azure Managed Lustre File System in TiB. The valid values for this field are dependant on which `sku_name` has been defined in the configuration file. For more information on the valid values for this field please see the [product documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-managed-lustre/create-file-system-resource-manager#file-system-type-and-size-options)

subnet_id.description: The resource ID of the Subnet that is used for managing the Azure Managed Lustre file system and for client-facing operations. This subnet should have at least a /24 subnet mask within the Virtual Network's address space

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Azure Managed Lustre File System.

zones.description: A list of availability zones for the Azure Managed Lustre File System

customer_managed_key.key_vault_key_id.description: The ID of the key vault key used for encryption. For example: `https://example-vault-name.vault.azure.net/keys/example-key-name/a1b2c3d4`.

customer_managed_key.user_assigned_ident.description: The ID of the User Assigned Identity that has access to the Key Vault Key.

default_database.access_keys_authenticat.description: Whether access key authentication is enabled for the database. Defaults to `false`.

default_database.client_protocol.description: Specifies whether redis clients can connect using TLS-encrypted or plaintext redis protocols. Possible values are `Encrypted` and `Plaintext`. Defaults to `Encrypted`.

default_database.clustering_policy.description: Clustering policy specified at create time. Possible values are `EnterpriseCluster`, `OSSCluster` and `NoCluster`. Defaults to `OSSCluster`.

default_database.eviction_policy.description: Specifies the Redis eviction policy. Possible values are `AllKeysLFU`, `AllKeysLRU`, `AllKeysRandom`, `VolatileLRU`, `VolatileLFU`, `VolatileTTL`, `VolatileRandom` and `NoEviction`. Defaults to `VolatileLRU`.

default_database.geo_replication_group_n.description: The name of the geo-replication group. If provided, a geo-replication group will be created for this database with itself as the only member. Use [`azurerm_managed_redis_geo_replication`](managed_redis_geo_replication.html) resource to manage group membership, linking and unlinking. All databases to be linked have to have the same group name. Refer to the [Managed Redis geo-replication documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/redis/how-to-active-geo-replication) for more information.

default_database.module.description: A `module` block as defined below. Refer to [the modules documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/redis/redis-modules) to learn more.

default_database.persistence_append_only.description: The frequency of Append Only File (AOF) backups. The only possible value is `1s`. Providing this value implies AOF persistence method is enabled. Conflicts with `persistence_redis_database_backup_frequency`, only one persistence method is allowed. Conflicts with `geo_replication_group_name`, persistence can only be enabled on non-geo-replicated databases. Refer to [the persistence documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/redis/how-to-persistence) to learn more.

default_database.persistence_redis_datab.description: The frequency of Redis Database (RDB) backups. Possible values are `1h`, `6h` and `12h`. Providing this value implies RDB persistence method is enabled. Conflicts with `persistence_append_only_file_backup_frequency`, only one persistence method is allowed. Conflicts with `geo_replication_group_name`, persistence can only be enabled on non-geo-replicated databases. Refer to [the persistence documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/redis/how-to-persistence) to learn more.

high_availability_enabled.description: Whether to enable high availability for the Managed Redis instance. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Managed Redis instance to be created.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Managed Redis instance.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Managed Redis instance. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

location.description: The Azure Region where the Managed Redis instance should exist. Refer to "Redis Cache" on the [product availability documentation](https://azure.microsoft.com/explore/global-infrastructure/products-by-region/table) for supported locations. Changing this forces a new Managed Redis instance to be created.

module.args.description: Configuration options for the module (e.g. `ERROR_RATE 0.00 INITIAL_SIZE 400`).

module.name.description: The name which should be used for this module. Possible values are `RedisBloom`, `RedisTimeSeries`, `RediSearch` and `RedisJSON`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Managed Redis instance. Changing this forces a new Managed Redis instance to be created.

public_network_access.description: The public network access setting for the Managed Redis instance. Possible values are `Enabled` and `Disabled`. Defaults to `Enabled`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Managed Redis instance should exist. Changing this forces a new Managed Redis instance to be created.

sku_name.description: The features and specification of the Managed Redis instance to deploy. Possible values are `Balanced_B0`, `Balanced_B1`, `Balanced_B10`, `Balanced_B100`, `Balanced_B1000`, `Balanced_B150`, `Balanced_B20`, `Balanced_B250`, `Balanced_B3`, `Balanced_B350`, `Balanced_B5`, `Balanced_B50`, `Balanced_B500`, `Balanced_B700`, `ComputeOptimized_X10`, `ComputeOptimized_X100`, `ComputeOptimized_X150`, `ComputeOptimized_X20`, `ComputeOptimized_X250`, `ComputeOptimized_X3`, `ComputeOptimized_X350`, `ComputeOptimized_X5`, `ComputeOptimized_X50`, `ComputeOptimized_X500`, `ComputeOptimized_X700`, `FlashOptimized_A1000`, `FlashOptimized_A1500`, `FlashOptimized_A2000`, `FlashOptimized_A250`, `FlashOptimized_A4500`, `FlashOptimized_A500`, `FlashOptimized_A700`, `MemoryOptimized_M10`, `MemoryOptimized_M100`, `MemoryOptimized_M1000`, `MemoryOptimized_M150`, `MemoryOptimized_M1500`, `MemoryOptimized_M20`, `MemoryOptimized_M2000`, `MemoryOptimized_M250`, `MemoryOptimized_M350`, `MemoryOptimized_M50`, `MemoryOptimized_M500` and `MemoryOptimized_M700`. `Balanced_B3` SKU or higher is required for geo-replication.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Managed Redis instance.

managed_redis_id.description: The ID of the Managed Redis instance. Changing this forces a new Access Policy Assignment to be created.

object_id.description: The object ID of the Azure Active Directory user, group, service principal, or managed identity to assign the access policy to. Changing this forces a new Access Policy Assignment to be created.

linked_managed_redis_ids.description: A set of other Managed Redis IDs to link together in the geo-replication group. The ID of this Managed Redis is always included by default and does not need to be provided here. Can contain up to 4 Managed Redis IDs, making up a group of 5 in total. All Managed Redis must have the same `geo_replication_group_name` configured. Once linked, the geo-replication state of all Managed Redis will be updated.

managed_redis_id.description: The ID of the Managed Redis through which geo-replication group will be managed. Linking is reciprocal, if A is linked to B, both A and B will have the same linking state. There is no need to have duplicate `azurerm_managed_redis_geo_replication` resources for each

display_name.description: A friendly name for this Management Group. If not specified, this will be the same as the `name`.

name.description: The name or UUID for this Management Group, which needs to be unique across your tenant. A new UUID will be generated if not provided

parent_management_group_id.description: The ID of the Parent Management Group.

subscription_ids.description: A list of Subscription GUIDs which should be assigned to the Management Group.

description.description: A description which should be used for this Policy Assignment.

display_name.description: The Display Name for this Policy Assignment.

enforce.description: Specifies if this Policy should be enforced or not? Defaults to `true`.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Managed Identity IDs which should be assigned to the Policy Definition.

identity.type.description: The Type of Managed Identity which should be added to this Policy Definition. Possible values are `SystemAssigned` and `UserAssigned`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Policy Assignment should exist. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

management_group_id.description: The ID of the Management Group. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

metadata.description: A JSON mapping of any Metadata for this Policy.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Policy Assignment. Cannot exceed 24 characters in length. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

non_compliance_message.description: One or more `non_compliance_message` blocks as defined below.

non_compliance_message.content.description: The non-compliance message text. When assigning policy sets (initiatives), unless `policy_definition_reference_id` is specified then this message will be the default for all policies.

non_compliance_message.policy_definition.description: When assigning policy sets (initiatives), this is the ID of the policy definition that the non-compliance message applies to.

not_scopes.description: Specifies a list of Resource Scopes (for example a Subscription, or a Resource Group) within this Management Group which are excluded from this Policy.

override_selector.in.description: Specify the list of policy reference id values to filter in. Cannot be used with `not_in`.

override_selector.kind.description: Specifies which characteristic will narrow down the set of evaluated resources. Possible values are `resourceLocation`, and `policyDefinitionReferenceId`. Defaults to `policyDefinitionReferenceId`.

override_selector.not_in.description: Specify the list of policy reference id values to filter out. Cannot be used with `in`.

overrides.description: One or more `overrides` blocks as defined below. More detail about `overrides` and `resource_selectors` see [policy assignment structure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/assignment-structure)

overrides.selectors.description: One or more `override_selector` block as defined below.

overrides.value.description: Specifies the value to override the policy property. Possible values for `policyEffect` override listed [policy effects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/effects).

parameters.description: A JSON mapping of any Parameters for this Policy.

policy_definition_id.description: The ID of the Policy Definition or Policy Definition Set. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

resource_selector.in.description: The list of allowed values for the specified kind. Cannot be used with `not_in`. Can contain up to 50 values.

resource_selector.kind.description: Specifies which characteristic will narrow down the set of evaluated resources. Possible values are `resourceLocation`, `resourceType` and `resourceWithoutLocation`.

resource_selector.not_in.description: The list of not-allowed values for the specified kind. Cannot be used with `in`. Can contain up to 50 values.

resource_selectors.description: One or more `resource_selectors` blocks as defined below to filter polices by resource properties.

resource_selectors.name.description: Specifies a name for the resource selector.

resource_selectors.selectors.description: One or more `resource_selector` block as defined below.

description.description: A description to use for this Policy Exemption.

display_name.description: A friendly display name to use for this Policy Exemption.

exemption_category.description: The category of this policy exemption. Possible values are `Waiver` and `Mitigated`.

expires_on.description: The expiration date and time in UTC ISO 8601 format of this policy exemption.

management_group_id.description: The Management Group ID where the Policy Exemption should be applied

metadata.description: The metadata for this policy exemption. This is a JSON string representing additional metadata that should be stored with the policy exemption.

name.description: The name of the Policy Exemption

policy_assignment_id.description: The ID of the Policy Assignment to be exempted at the specified Scope.

policy_definition_reference_ids.description: The policy definition reference ID list when the associated policy assignment is an assignment of a policy set definition.

failure_percentage.description: The percentage failure threshold. Possible values range between `0.0` and `1.0`. The remediation will fail if the percentage of failed remediation operations (i.e. failed deployments) exceeds this threshold.

location_filters.description: A list of the resource locations that will be remediated.

management_group_id.description: The Management Group ID at which the Policy Remediation should be applied

name.description: The name of the Policy Remediation

parallel_deployments.description: Determines how many resources to remediate at any given time. Can be used to increase or reduce the pace of the remediation. If not provided, the default parallel deployments value is used.

policy_assignment_id.description: The ID of the Policy Assignment that should be remediated.

policy_definition_reference_id.description: The unique ID for the policy definition reference within the policy set definition that should be remediated. Required when the policy assignment being remediated assigns a policy set definition.

resource_count.description: Determines the max number of resources that can be remediated by the remediation job. If not provided, the default resource count is used.

description.description: The description of this Policy Set Definition.

display_name.description: The display name of this Policy Set Definition.

management_group_id.description: The ID of the Management Group where this Policy Set Definition should be created. Changing this forces a new Policy Set Definition to be created.

metadata.description: The metadata for the Policy Set Definition in JSON format.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Policy Set Definition. Changing this forces a new Policy Set Definition to be created.

parameters.description: The parameters for the Policy Set Definition in JSON format. Reducing the number of parameters

policy_definition_group.description: One or more `policy_definition_group` blocks as defined below.

policy_definition_group.additional_metad.description: The ID of a resource that contains additional metadata for this Policy Definition Group.

policy_definition_group.category.description: The category of this Policy Definition Group.

policy_definition_group.description.description: The description of this Policy Definition Group.

policy_definition_group.display_name.description: The display name of this Policy Definition Group.

policy_definition_group.name.description: The name which should be used for this Policy Definition Group.

policy_definition_reference.description: One or more `policy_definition_reference` blocks as defined below.

policy_definition_reference.parameter_va.description: Parameter values for the references Policy Definition in JSON format.

policy_definition_reference.policy_defin.description: The ID of the Policy Definition to include in this Policy Set Definition.

policy_definition_reference.policy_group.description: Specifies a list of Policy Definition Groups names that this Policy Definition Reference belongs to.

policy_definition_reference.reference_id.description: A unique ID within this Policy Set Definition for this Policy Definition Reference.

policy_definition_reference.version.description: The version of the Policy Definition to use.

policy_type.description: The Policy Set Definition type. Possible values are `BuiltIn`, `Custom`, `NotSpecified`, and `Static`. Changing this forces a new Policy Set Definition to be created.

management_group_id.description: The ID of the Management Group to associate the Subscription with. Changing this forces a new Management to be created.

subscription_id.description: The ID of the Subscription to be associated with the Management Group. Changing this forces a new Management to be created.

debug_level.description: The Debug Level which should be used for this Resource Group Template Deployment. Possible values are `none`, `requestContent`, `responseContent` and `requestContent, responseContent`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Template should exist. Changing this forces a new Template to be created.

management_group_id.description: The ID of the Management Group to apply the Deployment Template to

name.description: The name which should be used for this Template Deployment. Changing this forces a new Template Deployment to be created.

parameters_content.description: The contents of the ARM Template parameters file - containing a JSON list of parameters.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Template.

template_content.description: The contents of the ARM Template which should be deployed into this Resource Group. Cannot be specified with `template_spec_version_id`.

template_spec_version_id.description: The ID of the Template Spec Version to deploy. Cannot be specified with `template_content`.

lock_level.description: Specifies the Level to be used for this Lock. Possible values are `CanNotDelete` and `ReadOnly`

name.description: Specifies the name of the Management Lock

notes.description: Specifies some notes about the lock. Maximum of 512 characters

scope.description: Specifies the scope at which the Management Lock should be created

cors.allowed_origins.description: A list of origins that should be allowed to make cross-origin calls.

data_store.description: One or more `data_store` blocks as defined below.

data_store.storage_account_id.description: The ID of the Storage Account that should be linked to this Azure Maps Account.

data_store.unique_name.description: The name given to the linked Storage Account.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Azure Maps Account.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Azure Maps Account. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

local_authentication_enabled.description: Is local authentication enabled for this Azure Maps Account? When `false`, all authentication to the Azure Maps data-plane REST API is disabled, except Azure AD authentication. Defaults to `true`.

location.description: The Location in which the Azure Maps Account should be provisioned

name.description: The name of the Azure Maps Account

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group in which the Azure Maps Account should exist

sku_name.description: The SKU of the Azure Maps Account. Possible values are `S0`, `S1` and `G2`

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the Azure Maps Account.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Azure Maps Creator should exist

maps_account_id.description: The ID of the Azure Maps Creator

name.description: The name of the Azure Maps Creator

storage_units.description: The storage units to be allocated. Integer values from 1 to 100, inclusive.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Azure Maps Creator.

offer.description: The Offer of the Marketplace Image

plan.description: The Plan of the Marketplace Image

publisher.description: The Publisher of the Marketplace Image

condition.description: The condition that limits the resources that the role can be assigned to

condition_version.description: The version of the condition. Possible values are `1.0` or `2.0`

delegated_managed_identity_resource_id.description: The delegated Azure Resource ID which contains a Managed Identity

description.description: The description for this Role Assignment

name.description: A unique UUID/GUID for this Role Assignment - one will be generated if not specified

principal_id.description: The ID of the Principal (User, Group or Service Principal) to assign the Role Definition to

role_definition_id.description: The Scoped-ID of the Role Definition

role_definition_name.description: The name of a built-in Role

skip_service_principal_aad_check.description: If the `principal_id` is a newly provisioned `Service Principal` set this value to `true` to skip the `Azure Active Directory` check which may fail due to replication lag. This argument is only valid if the `principal_id` is a `Service Principal` identity. Defaults to `false`

administrator_password.description: The Password associated with the `administrator_username` for the MongoDB Cluster.

administrator_username.description: The administrator username of the MongoDB Cluster

authentication_methods.description: A list of allowed authentication modes for the MongoDB Cluster. Possible values are `NativeAuth` and `MicrosoftEntraID`.

compute_tier.description: The compute tier to assign to the MongoDB Cluster. Possible values are `Free`, `M10`, `M20`, `M25`, `M30`, `M40`, `M50`, `M60`, `M80`, and `M200`.

create_mode.description: The creation mode for the MongoDB Cluster. Possible values are `Default`, `GeoReplica` and `PointInTimeRestore`. Defaults to `Default`

customer_managed_key.key_vault_key_id.description: The ID of the key vault key used for encryption. For example: `https://example-vault-name.vault.azure.net/keys/example-key-name`.

customer_managed_key.user_assigned_ident.description: The ID of the User Assigned Identity that has access to the Key Vault Key.

data_api_mode_enabled.description: Is the Data API for the MongoDB Cluster enabled? Defaults to `false`.

high_availability_mode.description: The high availability mode for the MongoDB Cluster. Possibles values are `Disabled` and `ZoneRedundantPreferred`.

identity.identity_ids.description: - A list of one or more Resource IDs for User Assigned Managed identities to assign.

identity.type.description: The type of managed identity to assign. Possible value is `UserAssigned`.

location.description: The supported Azure location where the MongoDB Cluster exists

name.description: The name which should be used for the MongoDB Cluster

preview_features.description: The preview features that can be enabled on the MongoDB Cluster

public_network_access.description: The Public Network Access setting for the MongoDB Cluster. Possibles values are `Disabled` and `Enabled`. Defaults to `Enabled`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the MongoDB Cluster

restore.description: A `restore` block as defined below. Required when `create_mode` is set to `PointInTimeRestore`

restore.point_in_time_utc.description: The point in time (in UTC) to restore from, in ISO 8601 format (e.g., `2024-01-01T00:00:00Z`)

restore.source_id.description: The ID of the source MongoDB Cluster to restore from

shard_count.description: The Number of shards to provision on the MongoDB Cluster

source_location.description: The location of the source MongoDB Cluster

source_server_id.description: The ID of the replication source MongoDB Cluster

storage_size_in_gb.description: The size of the data disk space for the MongoDB Cluster.

storage_type.description: The storage type for the MongoDB Cluster. Possible values are `PremiumSSD` and `PremiumSSDv2`. Defaults to `PremiumSSD`

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the MongoDB Cluster.

version.description: The version for the MongoDB Cluster. Possibles values are `5.0`, `6.0`, `7.0` and `8.0`.

end_ip_address.description: The end IP address of the Mongo Cluster Firewall Rule.

mongo_cluster_id.description: The ID of the Mongo Cluster

name.description: The name of the Mongo Cluster Firewall Rule

start_ip_address.description: The start IP address of the Mongo Cluster Firewall Rule.

identity_provider_type.description: The identity provider type for the Mongo Cluster User. The only possible value is `MicrosoftEntraID`

mongo_cluster_id.description: The ID of the Mongo Cluster where the User should exist

object_id.description: The Object ID of the Entra ID User or Service Principal

principal_type.description: The principal type for the Mongo Cluster User. Possible values are `user` and `servicePrincipal`

role.description: One or more `role` blocks as defined below

role.database.description: The name of the database to which the role will be applied

role.name.description: The role name. The only possible value is `root`

enabled_log.description: One or more `enabled_log` blocks as defined below.

enabled_log.category.description: The log category for the Azure Active Directory Diagnostic.

eventhub_authorization_rule_id.description: Specifies the ID of an Event Hub Namespace Authorization Rule used to send Diagnostics Data

eventhub_name.description: Specifies the name of the Event Hub where Diagnostics Data should be sent. If not specified, the default Event Hub will be used

log_analytics_workspace_id.description: Specifies the ID of a Log Analytics Workspace where Diagnostics Data should be sent.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Monitor Azure Active Directory Diagnostic Setting. Changing this forces a new Monitor Azure Active Directory Diagnostic Setting to be created.

storage_account_id.description: The ID of the Storage Account where logs should be sent

aad_auth.identifier_uri.description: The identifier URI for AAD auth.

aad_auth.object_id.description: The webhook application object Id for AAD auth.

aad_auth.tenant_id.description: The tenant id for AAD auth.

arm_role_receiver.description: One or more `arm_role_receiver` blocks as defined below.

arm_role_receiver.name.description: The name of the ARM role receiver.

arm_role_receiver.use_common_alert_schem.description: Enables or disables the common alert schema.

automation_runbook_receiver.description: One or more `automation_runbook_receiver` blocks as defined below.

automation_runbook_receiver.automation_a.description: The automation account ID which holds this runbook and authenticates to Azure resources.

automation_runbook_receiver.is_global_ru.description: Indicates whether this instance is global runbook.

automation_runbook_receiver.name.description: The name of the automation runbook receiver.

automation_runbook_receiver.service_uri.description: The URI where webhooks should be sent.

automation_runbook_receiver.use_common_a.description: Enables or disables the common alert schema.

automation_runbook_receiver.webhook_reso.description: The resource id for webhook linked to this runbook.

azure_app_push_receiver.description: One or more `azure_app_push_receiver` blocks as defined below.

azure_app_push_receiver.email_address.description: The email address of the user signed into the mobile app who will receive push notifications from this receiver.

azure_app_push_receiver.name.description: The name of the Azure app push receiver.

azure_function_receiver.description: One or more `azure_function_receiver` blocks as defined below.

azure_function_receiver.function_app_res.description: The Azure resource ID of the function app.

azure_function_receiver.function_name.description: The function name in the function app.

azure_function_receiver.http_trigger_url.description: The HTTP trigger url where HTTP request sent to.

azure_function_receiver.name.description: The name of the Azure Function receiver.

azure_function_receiver.use_common_alert.description: Enables or disables the common alert schema.

email_receiver.description: One or more `email_receiver` blocks as defined below.

email_receiver.email_address.description: The email address of this receiver.

email_receiver.name.description: The name of the email receiver. Names must be unique (case-insensitive) across all receivers within an action group.

email_receiver.use_common_alert_schema.description: Enables or disables the common alert schema.

enabled.description: Whether this action group is enabled. If an action group is not enabled, then none of its receivers will receive communications. Defaults to `true`.

event_hub_receiver.description: One or more `event_hub_receiver` blocks as defined below.

event_hub_receiver.event_hub_name.description: The name of the specific Event Hub queue.

event_hub_receiver.event_hub_namespace.description: The namespace name of the Event Hub.

event_hub_receiver.name.description: The name of the EventHub Receiver, must be unique within action group.

event_hub_receiver.subscription_id.description: The ID for the subscription containing this Event Hub. Default to the subscription ID of the Action Group.

event_hub_receiver.tenant_id.description: The Tenant ID for the subscription containing this Event Hub.

event_hub_receiver.use_common_alert_sche.description: Indicates whether to use common alert schema.

itsm_receiver.description: One or more `itsm_receiver` blocks as defined below.

itsm_receiver.connection_id.description: The unique connection identifier of the ITSM connection.

itsm_receiver.name.description: The name of the ITSM receiver.

itsm_receiver.ticket_configuration.description: A JSON blob for the configurations of the ITSM action. CreateMultipleWorkItems option will be part of this blob as well.

itsm_receiver.workspace_id.description: The Azure Log Analytics workspace ID where this connection is defined. Format is `<subscription id>|<workspace id>`, for example `00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000|00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Action Group should exist. Changing this forces a new Action Group to be created. Defaults to `global`.

logic_app_receiver.description: One or more `logic_app_receiver` blocks as defined below.

logic_app_receiver.callback_url.description: The callback url where HTTP request sent to.

logic_app_receiver.name.description: The name of the logic app receiver.

logic_app_receiver.resource_id.description: The Azure resource ID of the logic app.

logic_app_receiver.use_common_alert_sche.description: Enables or disables the common alert schema.

name.description: The name of the Action Group

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Action Group instance

short_name.description: The short name of the action group. This will be used in SMS messages.

sms_receiver.description: One or more `sms_receiver` blocks as defined below.

sms_receiver.country_code.description: The country code of the SMS receiver.

sms_receiver.name.description: The name of the SMS receiver. Names must be unique (case-insensitive) across all receivers within an action group.

sms_receiver.phone_number.description: The phone number of the SMS receiver.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

voice_receiver.description: One or more `voice_receiver` blocks as defined below.

voice_receiver.country_code.description: The country code of the voice receiver.

voice_receiver.name.description: The name of the voice receiver.

voice_receiver.phone_number.description: The phone number of the voice receiver.

webhook_receiver.description: One or more `webhook_receiver` blocks as defined below.

webhook_receiver.name.description: The name of the webhook receiver. Names must be unique (case-insensitive) across all receivers within an action group.

webhook_receiver.service_uri.description: The URI where webhooks should be sent.

webhook_receiver.use_common_alert_schema.description: Enables or disables the common alert schema.

action.description: One or more `action` blocks as defined below.

action.action_group_id.description: The ID of the Action Group can be sourced from [the `azurerm_monitor_action_group` resource](./monitor_action_group.html).

action.webhook_properties.description: The map of custom string properties to include with the post operation. These data are appended to the webhook payload.

criteria.caller.description: The email address or Azure Active Directory identifier of the user who performed the operation.

criteria.category.description: The category of the operation. Possible values are `Administrative`, `Autoscale`, `Policy`, `Recommendation`, `ResourceHealth`, `Security` and `ServiceHealth`.

criteria.level.description: The severity level of the event. Possible values are `Verbose`, `Informational`, `Warning`, `Error`, and `Critical`.

criteria.levels.description: A list of severity level of the event. Possible values are `Verbose`, `Informational`, `Warning`, `Error`, and `Critical`.

criteria.operation_name.description: The Resource Manager Role-Based Access Control operation name. Supported operation should be of the form: `<resourceProvider>/<resourceType>/<operation>`.

criteria.recommendation_category.description: The recommendation category of the event. Possible values are `Cost`, `Reliability`, `OperationalExcellence`, `HighAvailability`, `Performance` and `Security`.

criteria.recommendation_impact.description: The recommendation impact of the event. Possible values are `High`, `Medium` and `Low`.

criteria.recommendation_type.description: The recommendation type of the event.

criteria.resource_group.description: The name of resource group monitored by the activity log alert.

criteria.resource_groups.description: A list of names of resource groups monitored by the activity log alert.

criteria.resource_health.description: A block to define fine grain resource health settings.

criteria.resource_id.description: The specific resource monitored by the activity log alert. It should be within one of the `scopes`.

criteria.resource_ids.description: A list of specific resources monitored by the activity log alert. It should be within one of the `scopes`.

criteria.resource_provider.description: The name of the resource provider monitored by the activity log alert.

criteria.resource_providers.description: A list of names of resource providers monitored by the activity log alert.

criteria.resource_type.description: The resource type monitored by the activity log alert.

criteria.resource_types.description: A list of resource types monitored by the activity log alert.

criteria.service_health.description: A block to define fine grain service health settings.

criteria.status.description: The status of the event. For example, `Started`, `Failed`, or `Succeeded`.

criteria.statuses.description: A list of status of the event. For example, `Started`, `Failed`, or `Succeeded`.

criteria.sub_status.description: The sub status of the event.

criteria.sub_statuses.description: A list of sub status of the event.

description.description: The description of this activity log alert.

enabled.description: Should this Activity Log Alert be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the activity log alert rule should exist. Possible values are `global`, `westeurope`, `northeurope`, and `eastus2euap`

name.description: The name of the activity log alert

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the activity log alert instance

resource_health.current.description: The current resource health statuses that will log an alert. Possible values are `Available`, `Degraded`, `Unavailable` and `Unknown`.

resource_health.previous.description: The previous resource health statuses that will log an alert. Possible values are `Available`, `Degraded`, `Unavailable` and `Unknown`.

resource_health.reason.description: The reason that will log an alert. Possible values are `PlatformInitiated` (such as a problem with the resource in an affected region of an Azure incident), `UserInitiated` (such as a shutdown request of a VM) and `Unknown`.

scopes.description: The Scope at which the Activity Log should be applied. A list of strings which could be a resource group , or a subscription, or a resource ID (such as a Storage Account).

service_health.events.description: Events this alert will monitor Possible values are `Incident`, `Maintenance`, `Informational`, `ActionRequired` and `Security`.

service_health.locations.description: Locations this alert will monitor. For example, `West Europe`.

service_health.services.description: Services this alert will monitor. For example, `Activity Logs & Alerts`, `Action Groups`. Defaults to all Services.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

add_action_group_ids.description: Specifies a list of Action Group IDs.

alert_context.operator.description: The operator for a given condition. Possible values are `Equals`, `NotEquals`, `Contains`, and `DoesNotContain`.

alert_context.values.description: Specifies a list of values to match for a given condition.

alert_rule_id.operator.description: The operator for a given condition. Possible values are `Equals`, `NotEquals`, `Contains`, and `DoesNotContain`.

alert_rule_id.values.description: Specifies a list of values to match for a given condition.

alert_rule_name.operator.description: The operator for a given condition. Possible values are `Equals`, `NotEquals`, `Contains`, and `DoesNotContain`.

alert_rule_name.values.description: Specifies a list of values to match for a given condition.

description.description: Specifies a description for the Alert Processing Rule.

description.operator.description: The operator for a given condition. Possible values are `Equals`, `NotEquals`, `Contains`, and `DoesNotContain`.

description.values.description: Specifies a list of values to match for a given condition.

enabled.description: Should the Alert Processing Rule be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

monitor_condition.operator.description: The operator for a given condition. Possible values are `Equals` and `NotEquals`.

monitor_condition.values.description: Specifies a list of values to match for a given condition. Possible values are `Fired` and `Resolved`.

monitor_service.operator.description: The operator for a given condition. Possible values are `Equals` and `NotEquals`.

monitor_service.values.description: A list of values to match for a given condition. Possible values are `ActivityLog Administrative`, `ActivityLog Autoscale`, `ActivityLog Policy`, `ActivityLog Recommendation`, `ActivityLog Security`, `Application Insights`, `Azure Backup`, `Azure Stack Edge`, `Azure Stack Hub`, `Custom`, `Data Box Gateway`, `Health Platform`, `Log Alerts V2`, `Log Analytics`, `Platform`, `Prometheus`, `Resource Health`, `Smart Detector`, and `VM Insights - Health`.

monthly.days_of_month.description: Specifies a list of dayOfMonth to recurrence. Possible values are integers between `1` - `31`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Alert Processing Rule. Changing this forces a new Alert Processing Rule to be created.

recurrence.daily.description: One or more `daily` blocks as defined above.

recurrence.monthly.description: One or more `monthly` blocks as defined above.

recurrence.weekly.description: One or more `weekly` blocks as defined below.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Alert Processing Rule should exist. Changing this forces a new Alert Processing Rule to be created.

schedule.effective_from.description: Specifies the Alert Processing Rule effective start time (Y-m-d'T'H:M:S).

schedule.effective_until.description: Specifies the Alert Processing Rule effective end time (Y-m-d'T'H:M:S).

schedule.time_zone.description: The time zone (e.g. Pacific Standard time, Eastern Standard Time). Defaults to `UTC`. [possible values are defined here](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/previous-versions/windows/embedded/ms912391(v=winembedded.11)).

scopes.description: A list of resource IDs which will be the target of alert processing rule.

severity.operator.description: The operator for a given condition. Possible values are `Equals` and `NotEquals`.

severity.values.description: Specifies list of values to match for a given condition. Possible values are `Sev0`, `Sev1`, `Sev2`, `Sev3`, and `Sev4`.

signal_type.operator.description: The operator for a given condition. Possible values are `Equals` and `NotEquals`.

signal_type.values.description: Specifies a list of values to match for a given condition. Possible values are `Metric`, `Log`, `Unknown`, and `Health`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Alert Processing Rule.

target_resource.operator.description: The operator for a given condition. Possible values are `Equals`, `NotEquals`, `Contains`, and `DoesNotContain`.

target_resource.values.description: A list of values to match for a given condition. The values should be valid resource IDs.

target_resource_group.operator.description: The operator for a given condition. Possible values are `Equals`, `NotEquals`, `Contains`, and `DoesNotContain`.

target_resource_group.values.description: A list of values to match for a given condition. The values should be valid resource group IDs.

target_resource_type.operator.description: The operator for a given condition. Possible values are `Equals`, `NotEquals`, `Contains`, and `DoesNotContain`.

target_resource_type.values.description: A list of values to match for a given condition. The values should be valid resource types. (e.g. Microsoft.Compute/VirtualMachines)

weekly.days_of_week.description: Specifies a list of dayOfWeek to recurrence. Possible values are `Sunday`, `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday`, and `Saturday`.

action.action_group_id.description: Specifies the resource id of the monitor action group.

action.action_properties.description: Specifies the properties of an action group object.

alert_resolution.auto_resolved.description: Is the alert auto-resolution? Possible values are `true` and `false`.

alert_resolution.time_to_resolve.description: Specifies the alert auto-resolution interval, represented in ISO 8601 duration format.

cluster_name.description: Specifies the name of the Managed Kubernetes Cluster.

description.description: The description of the Alert Management Prometheus Rule Group.

interval.description: Specifies the interval in which to run the Alert Management Prometheus Rule Group represented in ISO 8601 duration format. Possible values are between `PT1M` and `PT15M`.

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the Alert Management Prometheus Rule Group should exist

name.description: Specifies the name which should be used for this Alert Management Prometheus Rule Group

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the Resource Group where the Alert Management Prometheus Rule Group should exist

rule.description: One or more `rule` blocks as defined below.

rule.annotations.description: Specifies a set of informational labels that can be used to store longer additional information such as alert descriptions or runbook links.

rule.enabled.description: Is this rule enabled? Possible values are `true` and `false`.

rule.expression.description: Specifies the Prometheus Query Language expression to evaluate. For more details see [this doc](https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/querying/basics). Evaluate at the period given by `interval` and record the result as a new set of time series with the metric name given by `record`.

rule.for.description: Specifies the amount of time alert must be active before firing, represented in ISO 8601 duration format.

rule.labels.description: Specifies the labels to add or overwrite before storing the result.

rule.severity.description: Specifies the severity of the alerts fired by the rule. Possible values are between 0 and 4.

rule_group_enabled.description: Is this Alert Management Prometheus Rule Group enabled? Possible values are `true` and `false`.

scopes.description: Specifies the resource ID of the Azure Monitor Workspace.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the Alert Management Prometheus Rule Group.

capacity.default.description: The number of instances that are available for scaling if metrics are not available for evaluation. The default is only used if the current instance count is lower than the default. Valid values are between `0` and `1000`.

capacity.maximum.description: The maximum number of instances for this resource. Valid values are between `0` and `1000`.

capacity.minimum.description: The minimum number of instances for this resource. Valid values are between `0` and `1000`.

dimensions.operator.description: The dimension operator. Possible values are `Equals` and `NotEquals`. `Equals` means being equal to any of the values. `NotEquals` means being not equal to any of the values.

email.custom_emails.description: Specifies a list of custom email addresses to which the email notifications will be sent.

email.send_to_subscription_administrator.description: Should email notifications be sent to the subscription administrator? Defaults to `false`.

email.send_to_subscription_co_administra.description: Should email notifications be sent to the subscription co-administrator? Defaults to `false`.

enabled.description: Specifies whether automatic scaling is enabled for the target resource. Defaults to `true`.

fixed_date.end.description: Specifies the end date for the profile, formatted as an RFC3339 date string.

fixed_date.start.description: Specifies the start date for the profile, formatted as an RFC3339 date string.

fixed_date.timezone.description: The Time Zone of the `start` and `end` times. A list of [possible values can be found here](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/monitor/autoscale-settings/create-or-update?view=rest-monitor-2022-10-01&tabs=HTTP#recurrentschedule). Defaults to `UTC`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the AutoScale Setting should exist

metric_trigger.dimensions.description: One or more `dimensions` block as defined below.

metric_trigger.divide_by_instance_count.description: Whether to enable metric divide by instance count.

metric_trigger.metric_name.description: The name of the metric that defines what the rule monitors, such as `Percentage CPU` for `Virtual Machine Scale Sets` and `CpuPercentage` for `App Service Plan`.

metric_trigger.metric_namespace.description: The namespace of the metric that defines what the rule monitors, such as `microsoft.compute/virtualmachinescalesets` for `Virtual Machine Scale Sets`.

metric_trigger.metric_resource_id.description: The ID of the Resource which the Rule monitors.

metric_trigger.operator.description: Specifies the operator used to compare the metric data and threshold. Possible values are: `Equals`, `NotEquals`, `GreaterThan`, `GreaterThanOrEqual`, `LessThan`, `LessThanOrEqual`.

metric_trigger.statistic.description: Specifies how the metrics from multiple instances are combined. Possible values are `Average`, `Max`, `Min` and `Sum`.

metric_trigger.threshold.description: Specifies the threshold of the metric that triggers the scale action.

metric_trigger.time_aggregation.description: Specifies how the data that's collected should be combined over time. Possible values include `Average`, `Count`, `Maximum`, `Minimum`, `Last` and `Total`.

metric_trigger.time_grain.description: Specifies the granularity of metrics that the rule monitors, which must be one of the pre-defined values returned from the metric definitions for the metric. This value must be between 1 minute and 12 hours an be formatted as an ISO 8601 string.

metric_trigger.time_window.description: Specifies the time range for which data is collected, which must be greater than the delay in metric collection (which varies from resource to resource). This value must be between 5 minutes and 12 hours and be formatted as an ISO 8601 string.

name.description: The name of the AutoScale Setting

notification.description: Specifies a `notification` block as defined below.

notification.webhook.description: One or more `webhook` blocks as defined below.

predictive.look_ahead_time.description: Specifies the amount of time by which instances are launched in advance. It must be between `PT1M` and `PT1H` in ISO 8601 format.

predictive.scale_mode.description: Specifies the predictive scale mode. Possible values are `Enabled` or `ForecastOnly`.

profile.description: Specifies one or more (up to 20) `profile` blocks as defined below.

profile.fixed_date.description: A `fixed_date` block as defined below. This cannot be specified if a `recurrence` block is specified.

profile.name.description: Specifies the name of the profile.

profile.recurrence.description: A `recurrence` block as defined below. This cannot be specified if a `fixed_date` block is specified.

profile.rule.description: One or more (up to 10) `rule` blocks as defined below.

recurrence.days.description: A list of days that this profile takes effect on. Possible values include `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday`, `Saturday` and `Sunday`.

recurrence.hours.description: A list containing a single item, which specifies the Hour interval at which this recurrence should be triggered (in 24-hour time). Possible values are from `0` to `23`.

recurrence.minutes.description: A list containing a single item which specifies the Minute interval at which this recurrence should be triggered.

recurrence.timezone.description: The Time Zone used for the `hours` field. A list of [possible values can be found here]([https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/azure/dn931928.aspx](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/monitor/autoscale-settings/create-or-update?view=rest-monitor-2022-10-01&tabs=HTTP#recurrentschedule)). Defaults to `UTC`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group in the AutoScale Setting should be created

scale_action.cooldown.description: The amount of time to wait since the last scaling action before this action occurs. Must be between 1 minute and 1 week and formatted as a ISO 8601 string.

scale_action.direction.description: The scale direction. Possible values are `Increase` and `Decrease`.

scale_action.type.description: The type of action that should occur. Possible values are `ChangeCount`, `ExactCount`, `PercentChangeCount` and `ServiceAllowedNextValue`.

scale_action.value.description: The number of instances involved in the scaling action.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

target_resource_id.description: Specifies the resource ID of the resource that the autoscale setting should be added to

webhook.service_uri.description: The HTTPS URI which should receive scale notifications.

description.description: Specifies a description for the Data Collection Endpoint.

kind.description: The kind of the Data Collection Endpoint. Possible values are `Linux` and `Windows`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Data Collection Endpoint should exist. Changing this forces a new Data Collection Endpoint to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Data Collection Endpoint. Changing this forces a new Data Collection Endpoint to be created.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether network access from public internet to the Data Collection Endpoint are allowed. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Default to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Data Collection Endpoint should exist. Changing this forces a new Data Collection Endpoint to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Data Collection Endpoint.

azure_monitor_metrics.name.description: The name which should be used for this destination. This name should be unique across all destinations regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

column.type.description: The type of the column data. Possible values are `string`, `int`, `long`, `real`, `boolean`, `datetime`,and `dynamic`.

data_collection_endpoint_id.description: The resource ID of the Data Collection Endpoint that this rule can be used with.

data_flow.description: One or more `data_flow` blocks as defined below.

data_flow.built_in_transform.description: The built-in transform to transform stream data.

data_flow.destinations.description: Specifies a list of destination names. A `azure_monitor_metrics` data source only allows for stream of kind `Microsoft-InsightsMetrics`.

data_flow.output_stream.description: The output stream of the transform. Only required if the data flow changes data to a different stream.

data_flow.streams.description: Specifies a list of streams. Possible values include but not limited to `Microsoft-Event`, `Microsoft-InsightsMetrics`, `Microsoft-Perf`, `Microsoft-Syslog`, `Microsoft-WindowsEvent`, and `Microsoft-PrometheusMetrics`.

data_flow.transform_kql.description: The KQL query to transform stream data.

data_sources.description: A `data_sources` block as defined below. This property is optional and can be omitted if the rule is meant to be used via direct calls to the provisioned endpoint.

data_sources.extension.description: One or more `extension` blocks as defined below.

data_sources.iis_log.description: One or more `iis_log` blocks as defined below.

data_sources.log_file.description: One or more `log_file` blocks as defined below.

data_sources.performance_counter.description: One or more `performance_counter` blocks as defined below.

data_sources.platform_telemetry.description: One or more `platform_telemetry` blocks as defined below.

data_sources.prometheus_forwarder.description: One or more `prometheus_forwarder` blocks as defined below.

data_sources.syslog.description: One or more `syslog` blocks as defined below.

data_sources.windows_event_log.description: One or more `windows_event_log` blocks as defined below.

data_sources.windows_firewall_log.description: One or more `windows_firewall_log` blocks as defined below.

description.description: The description of the Data Collection Rule.

destinations.event_hub.description: One or more `event_hub` blocks as defined below.

destinations.event_hub_direct.description: One or more `event_hub` blocks as defined below.

destinations.log_analytics.description: One or more `log_analytics` blocks as defined below.

destinations.monitor_account.description: One or more `monitor_account` blocks as defined below.

destinations.storage_blob.description: One or more `storage_blob` blocks as defined below.

destinations.storage_blob_direct.description: One or more `storage_blob_direct` blocks as defined below.

destinations.storage_table_direct.description: One or more `storage_table_direct` blocks as defined below.

event_hub.event_hub_id.description: The resource ID of the Event Hub.

event_hub.name.description: The name which should be used for this destination. This name should be unique across all destinations regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

event_hub_data_source.consumer_group.description: The Event Hub consumer group name.

event_hub_data_source.name.description: The name which should be used for this data source. This name should be unique across all data sources regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

event_hub_data_source.stream.description: The stream to collect from Event Hub. Possible value should be a custom stream name.

event_hub_direct.event_hub_id.description: The resource ID of the Event Hub.

event_hub_direct.name.description: The name which should be used for this destination. This name should be unique across all destinations regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

extension.extension_json.description: A JSON String which specifies the extension setting.

extension.extension_name.description: The name of the VM extension.

extension.input_data_sources.description: Specifies a list of data sources this extension needs data from. An item should be a name of a supported data source which produces only one stream. Supported data sources type: `performance_counter`, `windows_event_log`,and `syslog`.

extension.name.description: The name which should be used for this data source. This name should be unique across all data sources regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

extension.streams.description: Specifies a list of streams that this data source will be sent to. A stream indicates what schema will be used for this data and usually what table in Log Analytics the data will be sent to. Possible values include but not limited to `Microsoft-Event`, `Microsoft-InsightsMetrics`, `Microsoft-Perf`, `Microsoft-Syslog`, `Microsoft-WindowsEvent`.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Data Collection Rule. Currently, up to 1 identity is supported.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Data Collection Rule. Possible values are `SystemAssigned` and `UserAssigned`.

iis_log.log_directories.description: Specifies a list of absolute paths where the log files are located.

iis_log.name.description: The name which should be used for this data source. This name should be unique across all data sources regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

iis_log.streams.description: Specifies a list of streams that this data source will be sent to. A stream indicates what schema will be used for this data and usually what table in Log Analytics the data will be sent to. Possible value is `Microsoft-W3CIISLog`.

kind.description: The kind of the Data Collection Rule. Possible values are `Linux`, `Windows`, `AgentDirectToStore` and `WorkspaceTransforms`. A rule of kind `Linux` does not allow for `windows_event_log` data sources. And a rule of kind `Windows` does not allow for `syslog` data sources. If kind is not specified, all kinds of data sources are allowed.

label_include_filter.label.description: The label of the filter. This label should be unique across all `label_include_fileter` block. Possible value is `microsoft_metrics_include_label`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Data Collection Rule should exist. Changing this forces a new Data Collection Rule to be created.

log_analytics.name.description: The name which should be used for this destination. This name should be unique across all destinations regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

log_analytics.workspace_resource_id.description: The ID of a Log Analytic Workspace resource.

log_file.file_patterns.description: Specifies a list of file patterns where the log files are located. For example, `C:\\JavaLogs\\*.log`.

log_file.format.description: The data format of the log files. Possible values are `text` and `json`.

log_file.name.description: The name which should be used for this data source. This name should be unique across all data sources regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

log_file.streams.description: Specifies a list of streams that this data source will be sent to. A stream indicates what schema will be used for this data and usually what table in Log Analytics the data will be sent to. Possible value should be custom stream names.

monitor_account.monitor_account_id.description: The resource ID of the Monitor Account.

monitor_account.name.description: The name which should be used for this destination. This name should be unique across all destinations regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Data Collection Rule. Changing this forces a new Data Collection Rule to be created.

performance_counter.counter_specifiers.description: Specifies a list of specifier names of the performance counters you want to collect. To get a list of performance counters on Windows, run the command `typeperf`. Please see [this document](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/agents/data-sources-performance-counters#configure-performance-counters) for more information.

performance_counter.name.description: The name which should be used for this data source. This name should be unique across all data sources regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

performance_counter.sampling_frequency_i.description: The number of seconds between consecutive counter measurements (samples). The value should be integer between `1` and `1800` inclusive. `sampling_frequency_in_seconds` must be equal to `60` seconds for counters collected with `Microsoft-InsightsMetrics` stream.

performance_counter.streams.description: Specifies a list of streams that this data source will be sent to. A stream indicates what schema will be used for this data and usually what table in Log Analytics the data will be sent to. Possible values include but not limited to `Microsoft-InsightsMetrics`,and `Microsoft-Perf`.

platform_telemetry.name.description: The name which should be used for this data source. This name should be unique across all data sources regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

platform_telemetry.streams.description: Specifies a list of streams that this data source will be sent to. A stream indicates what schema will be used for this data and usually what table in Log Analytics the data will be sent to. Possible values include but not limited to `Microsoft.Cache/redis:Metrics-Group-All`.

prometheus_forwarder.label_include_filte.description: One or more `label_include_filter` blocks as defined above.

prometheus_forwarder.name.description: The name which should be used for this data source. This name should be unique across all data sources regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

prometheus_forwarder.streams.description: Specifies a list of streams that this data source will be sent to. A stream indicates what schema will be used for this data and usually what table in Log Analytics the data will be sent to. Possible value is `Microsoft-PrometheusMetrics`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Data Collection Rule should exist. Changing this forces a new Data Collection Rule to be created.

storage_blob.name.description: The name which should be used for this destination. This name should be unique across all destinations regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

storage_blob.storage_account_id.description: The resource ID of the Storage Account.

storage_blob_direct.name.description: The name which should be used for this destination. This name should be unique across all destinations regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

storage_blob_direct.storage_account_id.description: The resource ID of the Storage Account.

storage_table_direct.name.description: The name which should be used for this destination. This name should be unique across all destinations regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

storage_table_direct.storage_account_id.description: The resource ID of the Storage Account.

stream_declaration.column.description: One or more `column` blocks as defined above.

stream_declaration.stream_name.description: The name of the custom stream. This name should be unique across all `stream_declaration` blocks and must begin with a prefix of `Custom-`.

syslog.facility_names.description: Specifies a list of facility names. Use a wildcard `*` to collect logs for all facility names. Possible values are `alert`, `*`, `audit`, `auth`, `authpriv`, `clock`, `cron`, `daemon`, `ftp`, `kern`, `local5`, `local4`, `local1`, `local7`, `local6`, `local3`, `local2`, `local0`, `lpr`, `mail`, `mark`, `news`, `nopri`, `ntp`, `syslog`, `user` and `uucp`.

syslog.log_levels.description: Specifies a list of log levels. Use a wildcard `*` to collect logs for all log levels. Possible values are `Debug`, `Info`, `Notice`, `Warning`, `Error`, `Critical`, `Alert`, `Emergency`,and `*`.

syslog.name.description: The name which should be used for this data source. This name should be unique across all data sources regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

syslog.record_start_timestamp_format.description: The timestamp format of the text log files. Possible values are `ISO 8601`, `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, `M/D/YYYY HH:MM:SS AM/PM`, `Mon DD, YYYY HH:MM:SS`, `yyMMdd HH:mm:ss`, `ddMMyy HH:mm:ss`, `MMM d hh:mm:ss`, `dd/MMM/yyyy:HH:mm:ss zzz`,and `yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssK`.

syslog.streams.description: Specifies a list of streams that this data source will be sent to. A stream indicates what schema will be used for this data and usually what table in Log Analytics the data will be sent to. Possible values include but not limited to `Microsoft-Syslog`,and `Microsoft-CiscoAsa`, and `Microsoft-CommonSecurityLog`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Data Collection Rule.

windows_event_log.name.description: The name which should be used for this data source. This name should be unique across all data sources regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

windows_event_log.streams.description: Specifies a list of streams that this data source will be sent to. A stream indicates what schema will be used for this data and usually what table in Log Analytics the data will be sent to.
