Possible values include but not limited to `Microsoft-Event`,and `Microsoft-WindowsEvent` and `Microsoft-SecurityEvent`.

windows_event_log.x_path_queries.description: Specifies a list of Windows Event Log queries in XPath expression. Please see [this document](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/agents/data-collection-rule-azure-monitor-agent?tabs=cli#filter-events-using-xpath-queries) for more information.

windows_firewall_log.name.description: The name which should be used for this data source. This name should be unique across all data sources regardless of type within the Data Collection Rule.

windows_firewall_log.streams.description: Specifies a list of streams that this data source will be sent to. A stream indicates what schema will be used for this data and usually what table in Log Analytics the data will be sent to.

data_collection_endpoint_id.description: The ID of the Data Collection Endpoint which will be associated to the target resource.

data_collection_rule_id.description: The ID of the Data Collection Rule which will be associated to the target resource.

description.description: The description of the Data Collection Rule Association.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Data Collection Rule Association. Changing this forces a new Data Collection Rule Association to be created. Defaults to `configurationAccessEndpoint`.

target_resource_id.description: The ID of the Azure Resource which to associate to a Data Collection Rule or a Data Collection Endpoint

enabled_log.description: One or more `enabled_log` blocks as defined below.

enabled_log.category.description: The name of a Diagnostic Log Category for this Resource.

enabled_log.category_group.description: The name of a Diagnostic Log Category Group for this Resource.

enabled_metric.description: One or more `enabled_metric` blocks as defined below.

enabled_metric.category.description: The name of a Diagnostic Metric Category for this Resource.

eventhub_authorization_rule_id.description: Specifies the ID of an Event Hub Namespace Authorization Rule used to send Diagnostics Data.

eventhub_name.description: Specifies the name of the Event Hub where Diagnostics Data should be sent.

log_analytics_destination_type.description: Possible values are `AzureDiagnostics` and `Dedicated`. When set to `Dedicated`, logs sent to a Log Analytics workspace will go into resource specific tables, instead of the legacy `AzureDiagnostics` table.

log_analytics_workspace_id.description: Specifies the ID of a Log Analytics Workspace where Diagnostics Data should be sent.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Diagnostic Setting

partner_solution_id.description: The ID of the market partner solution where Diagnostics Data should be sent. For potential partner integrations, [click to learn more about partner integration](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/partner-solutions/overview).

storage_account_id.description: The ID of the Storage Account where logs should be sent.

target_resource_id.description: The ID of an existing Resource on which to configure Diagnostic Settings

action.description: One or more `action` blocks as defined below.

action.action_group_id.description: The ID of the Action Group can be sourced from [the `azurerm_monitor_action_group` resource](./monitor_action_group.html)

action.webhook_properties.description: The map of custom string properties to include with the post operation. These data are appended to the webhook payload.

application_insights_web_test_location_a.description: The ID of the Application Insights Resource.
The ID of the Application Insights Web Test.

auto_mitigate.description: Should the alerts in this Metric Alert be auto resolved? Defaults to `true`.

criteria.description: One or more (static) `criteria` blocks as defined below.

criteria.aggregation.description: The statistic that runs over the metric values. Possible values are `Average`, `Count`, `Minimum`, `Maximum` and `Total`.

criteria.dimension.description: One or more `dimension` blocks as defined below.

criteria.metric_name.description: One of the metric names to be monitored.

criteria.metric_namespace.description: One of the metric namespaces to be monitored.

criteria.operator.description: The criteria operator. Possible values are `Equals`, `GreaterThan`, `GreaterThanOrEqual`, `LessThan` and `LessThanOrEqual`.

criteria.skip_metric_validation.description: Skip the metric validation to allow creating an alert rule on a custom metric that isn't yet emitted? Defaults to `false`.

criteria.threshold.description: The criteria threshold value that activates the alert.

description.description: The description of this Metric Alert.

dimension.operator.description: The dimension operator. Possible values are `Include`, `Exclude` and `StartsWith`.

dynamic_criteria.aggregation.description: The statistic that runs over the metric values. Possible values are `Average`, `Count`, `Minimum`, `Maximum` and `Total`.

dynamic_criteria.alert_sensitivity.description: The extent of deviation required to trigger an alert. Possible values are `Low`, `Medium` and `High`.

dynamic_criteria.dimension.description: One or more `dimension` blocks as defined below.

dynamic_criteria.evaluation_failure_coun.description: The number of violations to trigger an alert. Should be smaller or equal to `evaluation_total_count`. Defaults to `4`.

dynamic_criteria.evaluation_total_count.description: The number of aggregated lookback points. The lookback time window is calculated based on the aggregation granularity (`window_size`) and the selected number of aggregated points. Defaults to `4`.

dynamic_criteria.ignore_data_before.description: The [ISO8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) date from which to start learning the metric historical data and calculate the dynamic thresholds.

dynamic_criteria.metric_name.description: One of the metric names to be monitored.

dynamic_criteria.metric_namespace.description: One of the metric namespaces to be monitored.

dynamic_criteria.operator.description: The criteria operator. Possible values are `LessThan`, `GreaterThan` and `GreaterOrLessThan`.

dynamic_criteria.skip_metric_validation.description: Skip the metric validation to allow creating an alert rule on a custom metric that isn't yet emitted?

enabled.description: Should this Metric Alert be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

frequency.description: The evaluation frequency of this Metric Alert, represented in ISO 8601 duration format. Possible values are `PT1M`, `PT5M`, `PT15M`, `PT30M` and `PT1H`. Defaults to `PT1M`.

name.description: The name of the Metric Alert

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Metric Alert instance

scopes.description: A set of strings of resource IDs at which the metric criteria should be applied.

severity.description: The severity of this Metric Alert. Possible values are `0`, `1`, `2`, `3` and `4`. Defaults to `3`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

target_resource_location.description: The location of the target resource.

target_resource_type.description: The resource type (e.g. `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`) of the target resource.

window_size.description: The period of time that is used to monitor alert activity, represented in ISO 8601 duration format. This value must be greater than `frequency`. Possible values are `PT1M`, `PT5M`, `PT15M`, `PT30M`, `PT1H`, `PT6H`, `PT12H` and `P1D`. Defaults to `PT5M`.

ingestion_access_mode.description: The default ingestion access mode for the associated private endpoints in scope. Possible values are `Open` and `PrivateOnly`. Defaults to `Open`.

name.description: The name of the Azure Monitor Private Link Scope

query_access_mode.description: The default query access mode for hte associated private endpoints in scope. Possible values are `Open` and `PrivateOnly`. Defaults to `Open`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Azure Monitor Private Link Scope should exist

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Azure Monitor Private Link Scope.

linked_resource_id.description: The ID of the linked resource. It must be the Log Analytics workspace or the Application Insights component or the Data Collection endpoint

name.description: The name of the Azure Monitor Private Link Scoped Service

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Azure Monitor Private Link Scoped Service should exist

scope_name.description: The name of the Azure Monitor Private Link Scope

action.action_group.description: List of action group reference resource IDs.

action.custom_webhook_payload.description: Custom payload to be sent for all webhook payloads in alerting action.

action.email_subject.description: Custom subject override for all email ids in Azure action group.

authorized_resource_ids.description: List of Resource IDs referred into query.

auto_mitigation_enabled.description: Should the alerts in this Metric Alert be auto resolved? Defaults to `false`.

data_source_id.description: The resource URI over which log search query is to be run

description.description: The description of the scheduled query rule.

enabled.description: Whether this scheduled query rule is enabled. Default is `true`.

frequency.description: Frequency (in minutes) at which rule condition should be evaluated. Values must be between 5 and 1440 (inclusive).

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the resource should exist

metric_trigger.metric_column.description: Evaluation of metric on a particular column.

metric_trigger.operator.description: Evaluation operation for rule - 'Equal', 'GreaterThan', GreaterThanOrEqual', 'LessThan', or 'LessThanOrEqual'.

metric_trigger.threshold.description: The threshold of the metric trigger. Values must be between 0 and 10000 inclusive.

name.description: The name of the scheduled query rule

query_type.description: The type of query results. Possible values are `ResultCount` and `Number`. Default is `ResultCount`. If set to `ResultCount`, `query` must include an `AggregatedValue` column of a numeric type, for example, `Heartbeat | summarize AggregatedValue = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the scheduled query rule instance

severity.description: Severity of the alert. Possible values include: 0, 1, 2, 3, or 4.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

throttling.description: Time (in minutes) for which Alerts should be throttled or suppressed. Values must be between 0 and 10000 (inclusive).

time_window.description: Time window for which data needs to be fetched for query (must be greater than or equal to `frequency`). Values must be between 5 and 2880 (inclusive).

trigger.metric_trigger.description: A `metric_trigger` block as defined above. Trigger condition for metric query rule.

trigger.operator.description: Evaluation operation for rule - 'GreaterThan', GreaterThanOrEqual', 'LessThan', or 'LessThanOrEqual'.

trigger.threshold.description: Result or count threshold based on which rule should be triggered. Values must be between 0 and 10000 inclusive.

action.action_groups.description: List of Action Group resource IDs to invoke when the alert fires.

action.custom_properties.description: Specifies the properties of an alert payload.

auto_mitigation_enabled.description: Specifies the flag that indicates whether the alert should be automatically resolved or not. Value should be `true` or `false`. The default is `false`.

criteria.metric_measure_column.description: Specifies the column containing the metric measure number.

criteria.operator.description: Specifies the criteria operator. Possible values are `Equal`, `GreaterThan`, `GreaterThanOrEqual`, `LessThan`,and `LessThanOrEqual`.

criteria.query.description: The query to run on logs. The results returned by this query are used to populate the alert.

criteria.resource_id_column.description: Specifies the column containing the resource ID. The content of the column must be an uri formatted as resource ID.

criteria.threshold.description: Specifies the criteria threshold value that activates the alert.

criteria.time_aggregation_method.description: The type of aggregation to apply to the data points in aggregation granularity. Possible values are `Average`, `Count`, `Maximum`, `Minimum`,and `Total`.

description.description: Specifies the description of the scheduled query rule.

dimension.operator.description: Operator for dimension values. Possible values are `Exclude`,and `Include`.

dimension.values.description: List of dimension values. Use a wildcard `*` to collect all.

display_name.description: Specifies the display name of the alert rule.

enabled.description: Specifies the flag which indicates whether this scheduled query rule is enabled. Value should be `true` or `false`. Defaults to `true`.

evaluation_frequency.description: How often the scheduled query rule is evaluated, represented in ISO 8601 duration format. Possible values are `PT1M`, `PT5M`, `PT10M`, `PT15M`, `PT30M`, `PT45M`, `PT1H`, `PT2H`, `PT3H`, `PT4H`, `PT5H`, `PT6H`, `P1D`.

failing_periods.minimum_failing_periods_.description: Specifies the number of violations to trigger an alert. Should be smaller or equal to `number_of_evaluation_periods`. Possible value is integer between 1 and 6.

failing_periods.number_of_evaluation_per.description: Specifies the number of aggregated look-back points. The look-back time window is calculated based on the aggregation granularity `window_duration` and the selected number of aggregated points. Possible value is integer between 1 and 6.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Scheduled Query Rule.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Scheduled Query Rule. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`.

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the Monitor Scheduled Query Rule should exist

mute_actions_after_alert_duration.description: Mute actions for the chosen period of time in ISO 8601 duration format after the alert is fired. Possible values are `PT5M`, `PT10M`, `PT15M`, `PT30M`, `PT45M`, `PT1H`, `PT2H`, `PT3H`, `PT4H`, `PT5H`, `PT6H`, `P1D` and `P2D`.

name.description: Specifies the name which should be used for this Monitor Scheduled Query Rule

query_time_range_override.description: Set this if the alert evaluation period is different from the query time range. If not specified, the value is `window_duration`*`number_of_evaluation_periods`. Possible values are `PT5M`, `PT10M`, `PT15M`, `PT20M`, `PT30M`, `PT45M`, `PT1H`, `PT2H`, `PT3H`, `PT4H`, `PT5H`, `PT6H`, `P1D` and `P2D`.

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the Resource Group where the Monitor Scheduled Query Rule should exist

scopes.description: Specifies the list of resource IDs that this scheduled query rule is scoped to

severity.description: Severity of the alert. Should be an integer between 0 and 4. Value of 0 is severest.

skip_query_validation.description: Specifies the flag which indicates whether the provided query should be validated or not. The default is false.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Monitor Scheduled Query Rule.

target_resource_types.description: List of resource type of the target resource(s) on which the alert is created/updated. For example if the scope is a resource group and targetResourceTypes is `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`, then a different alert will be fired for each virtual machine in the resource group which meet the alert criteria.

window_duration.description: Specifies the period of time in ISO 8601 duration format on which the Scheduled Query Rule will be executed (bin size). If `evaluation_frequency` is `PT1M`, possible values are `PT1M`, `PT5M`, `PT10M`, `PT15M`, `PT30M`, `PT45M`, `PT1H`, `PT2H`, `PT3H`, `PT4H`, `PT5H`, and `PT6H`. Otherwise, possible values are `PT5M`, `PT10M`, `PT15M`, `PT30M`, `PT45M`, `PT1H`, `PT2H`, `PT3H`, `PT4H`, `PT5H`, `PT6H`, `P1D`, and `P2D`.

workspace_alerts_storage_enabled.description: Specifies the flag which indicates whether this scheduled query rule check if storage is configured. Value should be `true` or `false`. The default is `false`.

authorized_resource_ids.description: A list of IDs of Resources referred into query.

criteria.metric_name.description: Name of the metric. Supported metrics are listed in the Azure Monitor [Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/metrics-supported#microsoftoperationalinsightsworkspaces) metrics namespace.

data_source_id.description: The resource URI over which log search query is to be run

description.description: The description of the scheduled query rule.

dimension.operator.description: Operator for dimension values, - 'Include'. Defaults to `Include`.

enabled.description: Whether this scheduled query rule is enabled. Default is `true`.

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the resource should exist

name.description: The name of the scheduled query rule

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the scheduled query rule instance

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

action_group.email_subject.description: Specifies a custom email subject if Email Receiver is specified in Monitor Action Group resource.

action_group.webhook_payload.description: A JSON String which Specifies the custom webhook payload if Webhook Receiver is specified in Monitor Action Group resource.

description.description: Specifies a description for the Smart Detector Alert Rule.

detector_type.description: Specifies the Built-In Smart Detector type that this alert rule will use. Currently the only possible values are `FailureAnomaliesDetector`, `RequestPerformanceDegradationDetector`, `DependencyPerformanceDegradationDetector`, `ExceptionVolumeChangedDetector`, `TraceSeverityDetector`, `MemoryLeakDetector`.

enabled.description: Is the Smart Detector Alert Rule enabled? Defaults to `true`.

frequency.description: Specifies the frequency of this Smart Detector Alert Rule in ISO8601 format.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Monitor Smart Detector Alert Rule

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the resource group in which the Monitor Smart Detector Alert Rule should exist

scope_resource_ids.description: Specifies the scopes of this Smart Detector Alert Rule.

severity.description: Specifies the severity of this Smart Detector Alert Rule. Possible values are `Sev0`, `Sev1`, `Sev2`, `Sev3` or `Sev4`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

throttling_duration.description: Specifies the duration (in ISO8601 format) to wait before notifying on the alert rule again.

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the Azure Monitor Workspace should exist

name.description: Specifies the name which should be used for this Azure Monitor Workspace

public_network_access_enabled.description: Is public network access enabled? Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the Resource Group where the Azure Monitor Workspace should exist

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Azure Monitor Workspace.

administrator_login.description: Specifies the name of the SQL administrator.

administrator_login_password.description: Specifies the password of the SQL administrator.

authentication_type.description: Specifies the type of authentication used to access the server. Valid values are `SQL` or `ADPassword`.

auto_pause_delay_in_minutes.description: Time in minutes after which database is automatically paused. A value of `-1` means that automatic pause is disabled. This property is only settable for Serverless databases.

collation.description: Specifies the collation of the database

create_mode.description: The create mode of the database. Possible values are `Copy`, `Default`, `OnlineSecondary`, `PointInTimeRestore`, `Recovery`, `Restore`, `RestoreExternalBackup`, `RestoreExternalBackupSecondary`, `RestoreLongTermRetentionBackup` and `Secondary`. Mutually exclusive with `import`

creation_source_database_id.description: The ID of the source database from which to create the new database. This should only be used for databases with `create_mode` values that use another database as reference

disabled_alerts.description: Specifies a list of alerts which should be disabled. Possible values include `Access_Anomaly`, `Sql_Injection` and `Sql_Injection_Vulnerability`.

elastic_pool_id.description: Specifies the ID of the elastic pool containing this database.

email_account_admins.description: Should the account administrators be emailed when this alert is triggered? Possible values are `Enabled` or `Disabled`. Defaults to `Disabled`.

email_addresses.description: A list of email addresses which alerts should be sent to.

enclave_type.description: Specifies the type of enclave to be used by the elastic pool. When `enclave_type` is not specified (e.g., the default) enclaves are not enabled on the database. Once enabled (e.g., by specifying `Default` or `VBS`) removing the `enclave_type` field from the configuration file will force the creation of a new resource. Possible values are `Default` or `VBS`.

geo_backup_enabled.description: A boolean that specifies if the Geo Backup Policy is enabled. Defaults to `true`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this SQL Database.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this SQL Database. Possible value is `UserAssigned`.

import.description: A `import` block as documented below. Mutually exclusive with `create_mode`.

ledger_enabled.description: A boolean that specifies if this is a ledger database. Defaults to `false`

license_type.description: Specifies the license type applied to this database. Possible values are `LicenseIncluded` and `BasePrice`.

long_term_retention_policy.immutable_bac.description: Specifies if the backups are immutable. Defaults to `false`.

long_term_retention_policy.monthly_reten.description: The monthly retention policy for an LTR backup in an ISO 8601 format. Valid value is between 1 to 120 months. e.g. `P1Y`, `P1M`, `P4W` or `P30D`. Defaults to `PT0S`.

long_term_retention_policy.week_of_year.description: The week of year to take the yearly backup. Value has to be between `1` and `52`.

long_term_retention_policy.weekly_retent.description: The weekly retention policy for an LTR backup in an ISO 8601 format. Valid value is between 1 to 520 weeks. e.g. `P1Y`, `P1M`, `P1W` or `P7D`. Defaults to `PT0S`.

long_term_retention_policy.yearly_retent.description: The yearly retention policy for an LTR backup in an ISO 8601 format. Valid value is between 1 to 10 years. e.g. `P1Y`, `P12M`, `P52W` or `P365D`. Defaults to `PT0S`.

maintenance_configuration_name.description: The name of the Public Maintenance Configuration window to apply to the database. Valid values include `SQL_Default`, `SQL_EastUS_DB_1`, `SQL_EastUS2_DB_1`, `SQL_SoutheastAsia_DB_1`, `SQL_AustraliaEast_DB_1`, `SQL_NorthEurope_DB_1`, `SQL_SouthCentralUS_DB_1`, `SQL_WestUS2_DB_1`, `SQL_UKSouth_DB_1`, `SQL_WestEurope_DB_1`, `SQL_EastUS_DB_2`, `SQL_EastUS2_DB_2`, `SQL_WestUS2_DB_2`, `SQL_SoutheastAsia_DB_2`, `SQL_AustraliaEast_DB_2`, `SQL_NorthEurope_DB_2`, `SQL_SouthCentralUS_DB_2`, `SQL_UKSouth_DB_2`, `SQL_WestEurope_DB_2`, `SQL_AustraliaSoutheast_DB_1`, `SQL_BrazilSouth_DB_1`, `SQL_CanadaCentral_DB_1`, `SQL_CanadaEast_DB_1`, `SQL_CentralUS_DB_1`, `SQL_EastAsia_DB_1`, `SQL_FranceCentral_DB_1`, `SQL_GermanyWestCentral_DB_1`, `SQL_CentralIndia_DB_1`, `SQL_SouthIndia_DB_1`, `SQL_JapanEast_DB_1`, `SQL_JapanWest_DB_1`, `SQL_NorthCentralUS_DB_1`, `SQL_UKWest_DB_1`, `SQL_WestUS_DB_1`, `SQL_AustraliaSoutheast_DB_2`, `SQL_BrazilSouth_DB_2`, `SQL_CanadaCentral_DB_2`, `SQL_CanadaEast_DB_2`, `SQL_CentralUS_DB_2`, `SQL_EastAsia_DB_2`, `SQL_FranceCentral_DB_2`, `SQL_GermanyWestCentral_DB_2`, `SQL_CentralIndia_DB_2`, `SQL_SouthIndia_DB_2`, `SQL_JapanEast_DB_2`, `SQL_JapanWest_DB_2`, `SQL_NorthCentralUS_DB_2`, `SQL_UKWest_DB_2`, `SQL_WestUS_DB_2`, `SQL_WestCentralUS_DB_1`, `SQL_FranceSouth_DB_1`, `SQL_WestCentralUS_DB_2`, `SQL_FranceSouth_DB_2`, `SQL_SwitzerlandNorth_DB_1`, `SQL_SwitzerlandNorth_DB_2`, `SQL_BrazilSoutheast_DB_1`, `SQL_UAENorth_DB_1`, `SQL_BrazilSoutheast_DB_2`, `SQL_UAENorth_DB_2`, `SQL_SouthAfricaNorth_DB_1`, `SQL_SouthAfricaNorth_DB_2`, `SQL_WestUS3_DB_1`, `SQL_WestUS3_DB_2`, `SQL_SwedenCentral_DB_1`, `SQL_SwedenCentral_DB_2`. Defaults to `SQL_Default`.

max_size_gb.description: The max size of the database in gigabytes.

min_capacity.description: Minimal capacity that database will always have allocated, if not paused. This property is only settable for Serverless databases.

name.description: The name of the MS SQL Database

read_replica_count.description: The number of readonly secondary replicas associated with the database to which readonly application intent connections may be routed. This property is only settable for Hyperscale edition databases.

read_scale.description: If enabled, connections that have application intent set to readonly in their connection string may be routed to a readonly secondary replica. This property is only settable for Premium and Business Critical databases.

recover_database_id.description: The ID of the database to be recovered. This property is only applicable when the `create_mode` is `Recovery`.

recovery_point_id.description: The ID of the Recovery Services Recovery Point Id to be restored. This property is only applicable when the `create_mode` is `Recovery`.

restore_dropped_database_id.description: The ID of the database to be restored. This property is only applicable when the `create_mode` is `Restore`.

restore_long_term_retention_backup_id.description: The ID of the long term retention backup to be restored. This property is only applicable when the `create_mode` is `RestoreLongTermRetentionBackup`.

restore_point_in_time.description: Specifies the point in time (ISO8601 format) of the source database that will be restored to create the new database. This property is only settable for `create_mode`= `PointInTimeRestore` databases.

retention_days.description: Specifies the number of days to keep in the Threat Detection audit logs.

sample_name.description: Specifies the name of the sample schema to apply when creating this database. Possible value is `AdventureWorksLT`.

secondary_type.description: How do you want your replica to be made? Valid values include `Geo`, `Named` and `Standby`. Defaults to `Geo`

server_id.description: The id of the MS SQL Server on which to create the database

short_term_retention_policy.backup_inter.description: The hours between each differential backup. This is only applicable to live databases but not dropped databases. Value has to be `12` or `24`. Defaults to `12` hours.

short_term_retention_policy.retention_da.description: Point In Time Restore configuration. Value has to be between `1` and `35`.

sku_name.description: Specifies the name of the SKU used by the database. For example, `GP_S_Gen5_2`,`HS_Gen4_1`,`BC_Gen5_2`, `ElasticPool`, `Basic`,`S0`, `P2` ,`DW100c`, `DS100`. Changing this from the HyperScale service tier to another service tier will create a new resource.

state.description: The State of the Policy. Possible values are `Enabled` or `Disabled`. Defaults to `Disabled`.

storage_account_access_key.description: Specifies the identifier key of the Threat Detection audit storage account. Required if `state` is `Enabled`.

storage_account_id.description: The resource id for the storage account used to store BACPAC file. If set, private endpoint connection will be created for the storage account. Must match storage account used for storage_uri parameter.

storage_account_type.description: Specifies the storage account type used to store backups for this database. Possible values are `Geo`, `GeoZone`, `Local` and `Zone`. Defaults to `Geo`.

storage_endpoint.description: Specifies the blob storage endpoint (e.g. <https://example.blob.core.windows.net>). This blob storage will hold all Threat Detection audit logs. Required if `state` is `Enabled`.

storage_key.description: Specifies the access key for the storage account.

storage_key_type.description: Specifies the type of access key for the storage account. Valid values are `StorageAccessKey` or `SharedAccessKey`.

storage_uri.description: Specifies the blob URI of the .bacpac file.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

threat_detection_policy.description: Threat detection policy configuration. The `threat_detection_policy` block supports fields documented below.

transparent_data_encryption_enabled.description: If set to true, Transparent Data Encryption will be enabled on the database. Defaults to `true`.

transparent_data_encryption_key_automati.description: Boolean flag to specify whether TDE automatically rotates the encryption Key to latest version or not. Possible values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`.

transparent_data_encryption_key_vault_ke.description: The fully versioned `Key Vault` `Key` URL (e.g. `'https://<YourVaultName>.vault.azure.net/keys/<YourKeyName>/<YourKeyVersion>`) to be used as the `Customer Managed Key`(CMK/BYOK) for the `Transparent Data Encryption`(TDE) layer.

zone_redundant.description: Whether or not this database is zone redundant, which means the replicas of this database will be spread across multiple availability zones. This property is only settable for Premium and Business Critical databases.

database_id.description: The ID of the SQL database to set the extended auditing policy

enabled.description: Whether to enable the extended auditing policy. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `true`.

log_monitoring_enabled.description: Enable audit events to Azure Monitor? Defaults to `true`.

retention_in_days.description: The number of days to retain logs for in the storage account. Defaults to `0`.

storage_account_access_key.description: The access key to use for the auditing storage account.

storage_account_access_key_is_secondary.description: Is `storage_account_access_key` value the storage's secondary key?

storage_endpoint.description: The blob storage endpoint (e.g. <https://example.blob.core.windows.net>). This blob storage will hold all extended auditing logs.

baseline_name.description: The name of the vulnerability assessment rule baseline. Valid options are `default` and `master`. `default` implies a baseline on a database level rule and `master` for server level rule. Defaults to `default`

baseline_result.description: A `baseline_result` block as documented below. Multiple blocks can be defined.

baseline_result.result.description: A list representing a result of the baseline.

database_name.description: Specifies the name of the MS SQL Database

server_vulnerability_assessment_id.description: The Vulnerability Assessment ID of the MS SQL Server

enclave_type.description: Specifies the type of enclave to be used by the elastic pool. When `enclave_type` is not specified (e.g., the default) enclaves are not enabled on the elastic pool. Once enabled (e.g., by specifying `Default` or `VBS`) removing the `enclave_type` field from the configuration file will force the creation of a new resource. Possible values are `Default` or `VBS`.

license_type.description: Specifies the license type applied to this database. Possible values are `LicenseIncluded` and `BasePrice`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

maintenance_configuration_name.description: The name of the Public Maintenance Configuration window to apply to the elastic pool. Valid values include `SQL_Default`, `SQL_EastUS_DB_1`, `SQL_EastUS2_DB_1`, `SQL_SoutheastAsia_DB_1`, `SQL_AustraliaEast_DB_1`, `SQL_NorthEurope_DB_1`, `SQL_SouthCentralUS_DB_1`, `SQL_WestUS2_DB_1`, `SQL_UKSouth_DB_1`, `SQL_WestEurope_DB_1`, `SQL_EastUS_DB_2`, `SQL_EastUS2_DB_2`, `SQL_WestUS2_DB_2`, `SQL_SoutheastAsia_DB_2`, `SQL_AustraliaEast_DB_2`, `SQL_NorthEurope_DB_2`, `SQL_SouthCentralUS_DB_2`, `SQL_UKSouth_DB_2`, `SQL_WestEurope_DB_2`, `SQL_AustraliaSoutheast_DB_1`, `SQL_BrazilSouth_DB_1`, `SQL_CanadaCentral_DB_1`, `SQL_CanadaEast_DB_1`, `SQL_CentralUS_DB_1`, `SQL_EastAsia_DB_1`, `SQL_FranceCentral_DB_1`, `SQL_GermanyWestCentral_DB_1`, `SQL_CentralIndia_DB_1`, `SQL_SouthIndia_DB_1`, `SQL_JapanEast_DB_1`, `SQL_JapanWest_DB_1`, `SQL_NorthCentralUS_DB_1`, `SQL_UKWest_DB_1`, `SQL_WestUS_DB_1`, `SQL_AustraliaSoutheast_DB_2`, `SQL_BrazilSouth_DB_2`, `SQL_CanadaCentral_DB_2`, `SQL_CanadaEast_DB_2`, `SQL_CentralUS_DB_2`, `SQL_EastAsia_DB_2`, `SQL_FranceCentral_DB_2`, `SQL_GermanyWestCentral_DB_2`, `SQL_CentralIndia_DB_2`, `SQL_SouthIndia_DB_2`, `SQL_JapanEast_DB_2`, `SQL_JapanWest_DB_2`, `SQL_NorthCentralUS_DB_2`, `SQL_UKWest_DB_2`, `SQL_WestUS_DB_2`, `SQL_WestCentralUS_DB_1`, `SQL_FranceSouth_DB_1`, `SQL_WestCentralUS_DB_2`, `SQL_FranceSouth_DB_2`, `SQL_SwitzerlandNorth_DB_1`, `SQL_SwitzerlandNorth_DB_2`, `SQL_BrazilSoutheast_DB_1`, `SQL_UAENorth_DB_1`, `SQL_BrazilSoutheast_DB_2`, `SQL_UAENorth_DB_2`, `SQL_SouthAfricaNorth_DB_1`, `SQL_SouthAfricaNorth_DB_2`, `SQL_WestUS3_DB_1`, `SQL_WestUS3_DB_2`, `SQL_SwedenCentral_DB_1`, `SQL_SwedenCentral_DB_2`. Defaults to `SQL_Default`.

max_size_bytes.description: The max data size of the elastic pool in bytes. Conflicts with `max_size_gb`.

max_size_gb.description: The max data size of the elastic pool in gigabytes. Conflicts with `max_size_bytes`.

name.description: The name of the elastic pool. This needs to be globally unique

per_database_settings.max_capacity.description: The maximum capacity any one database can consume.

per_database_settings.min_capacity.description: The minimum capacity all databases are guaranteed.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the elastic pool. This must be the same as the resource group of the underlying SQL server

server_name.description: The name of the SQL Server on which to create the elastic pool

sku.description: A `sku` block as defined below.

sku.capacity.description: The scale up/out capacity, representing server's compute units. For more information see the documentation for your Elasticpool configuration: [vCore-based](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/sql-database/sql-database-vcore-resource-limits-elastic-pools) or [DTU-based](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/sql-database/sql-database-dtu-resource-limits-elastic-pools).

sku.name.description: Specifies the SKU Name for this Elasticpool. The name of the SKU, will be either `vCore` based or `DTU` based. Possible `DTU` based values are `BasicPool`, `StandardPool`, `PremiumPool` while possible `vCore` based values are `GP_Gen4`, `GP_Gen5`, `GP_Fsv2`, `GP_DC`, `BC_Gen4`, `BC_Gen5`, `BC_DC`, `HS_PRMS`, `HS_MOPRMS`, or `HS_Gen5`.

sku.tier.description: The tier of the particular SKU. Possible values are `GeneralPurpose`, `BusinessCritical`, `Basic`, `Standard`, `Premium`, or `HyperScale`. For more information see the documentation for your Elasticpool configuration: [vCore-based](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/sql-database/sql-database-vcore-resource-limits-elastic-pools) or [DTU-based](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/sql-database/sql-database-dtu-resource-limits-elastic-pools).

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

zone_redundant.description: Whether or not this elastic pool is zone redundant. `tier` needs to be `Premium` for `DTU` based or `BusinessCritical` for `vCore` based `sku`.

databases.description: A set of database names to include in the failover group.

name.description: The name of the Failover Group

partner_server.id.description: The ID of a partner SQL server to include in the failover group.

read_write_endpoint_failover_policy.grac.description: The grace period in minutes, before failover with data loss is attempted for the read-write endpoint. Required when `mode` is `Automatic`.

read_write_endpoint_failover_policy.mode.description: The failover policy of the read-write endpoint for the failover group. Possible values are `Automatic` or `Manual`.

readonly_endpoint_failover_policy_enable.description: Whether failover is enabled for the readonly endpoint. Defaults to `false`.

server_id.description: The ID of the primary SQL Server on which to create the failover group

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

end_ip_address.description: The ending IP address to allow through the firewall for this rule.

name.description: The name of the firewall rule

server_id.description: The resource ID of the SQL Server on which to create the Firewall Rule

start_ip_address.description: The starting IP address to allow through the firewall for this rule.

description.description: The description of the Elastic Job.

job_agent_id.description: The ID of the Elastic Job Agent. Changing this forces a new Elastic Job to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Elastic Job. Changing this forces a new Elastic Job to be created.

database_id.description: The ID of the database to store metadata for this Elastic Job Agent. Changing this forces a new Elastic Job Agent to be created.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to assign to this Elastic Job Agent.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Elastic Job Agent. Currently only `UserAssigned` is supported.

location.description: The Azure Region where this Elastic Job Agent should exist. Changing this forces a new Elastic Job Agent to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Elastic Job Agent. Changing this forces a new Elastic Job Agent to be created.

sku.description: The name of the SKU to use for this Elastic Job Agent. Possible values are `JA100`, `JA200`, `JA400`, and `JA800`. Defaults to `JA100`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to this Elastic Job Agent.

job_agent_id.description: The ID of the Elastic Job Agent. Changing this forces a new Elastic Job Credential to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Elastic Job Credential. Changing this forces a new Elastic Job Credential to be created.

password.description: The password to use for this Elastic Job credential.

password_wo_version.description: An integer value used to trigger an update for `password_wo`. This property should be incremented when updating `password_wo`.

username.description: The username to use for this Elastic Job credential.

enabled.description: Should the Elastic Job Schedule be enabled? Defaults to `false`.

end_time.description: The end time of the schedule. Must be in RFC3339 format.

interval.description: The interval between job executions. Must be in ISO8601 duration format.

job_id.description: The ID of the Elastic Job. Changing this forces a new Elastic Job Schedule to be created.

start_time.description: The start time of the schedule. Must be in RFC3339 format.

type.description: The type of schedule. Possible values are `Once` and `Recurring`.

initial_retry_interval_seconds.description: The initial retry interval in seconds. Defaults to `1`.

job_credential_id.description: The ID of the Elastic Job Credential to use when executing this Elastic Job Step. Omit this argument to run the step under the Job Agent's managed identity (user-assigned).

job_id.description: The ID of the Elastic Job. Changing this forces a new Elastic Job Step to be created.

job_step_index.description: The index at which to insert this Elastic Job Step into the Elastic Job.

job_target_group_id.description: The ID of the Elastic Job Target Group.

maximum_retry_interval_seconds.description: The maximum retry interval in seconds. Defaults to `120`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Elastic Job Step. Changing this forces a new Elastic Job Step to be created.

output_target.job_credential_id.description: The ID of the Elastic Job Credential to use when connecting to the output destination.

output_target.mssql_database_id.description: The ID of the output database.

output_target.schema_name.description: The name of the output schema. Defaults to `dbo`.

output_target.table_name.description: The name of the output table.

retry_attempts.description: The number of retry attempts. Defaults to `10`.

retry_interval_backoff_multiplier.description: The multiplier for time between retries. Defaults to `2.0`.

sql_script.description: The T-SQL script to be executed by this Elastic Job Step.

timeout_seconds.description: The execution timeout in seconds for this Elastic Job Step. Defaults to `43200`.

job_agent_id.description: The ID of the Elastic Job Agent. Changing this forces a new Job Target Group to be created.

job_target.description: One or more `job_target` blocks as defined below.

job_target.database_name.description: The name of the MS SQL Database.

job_target.elastic_pool_name.description: The name of the MS SQL Elastic Pool.

job_target.job_credential_id.description: The ID of the job credential to use during execution of jobs.

job_target.membership_type.description: The membership type for this job target. Possible values are `Include` and `Exclude`. Defaults to `Include`.

job_target.server_name.description: The name of the MS SQL Server.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Job Target Group. Changing this forces a new Job Target Group to be created.

long_term_retention_policy.immutable_bac.description: Specifies if the backups are immutable. Defaults to `false`.

long_term_retention_policy.monthly_reten.description: The monthly retention policy for an LTR backup in an ISO 8601 format. Valid value is between 1 to 120 months. e.g. `P1Y`, `P1M`, `P4W` or `P30D`. Defaults to `PT0S`.

long_term_retention_policy.week_of_year.description: The week of year to take the yearly backup. Value has to be between `1` and `52`.

long_term_retention_policy.weekly_retent.description: The weekly retention policy for an LTR backup in an ISO 8601 format. Valid value is between 1 to 520 weeks. e.g. `P1Y`, `P1M`, `P1W` or `P7D`. Defaults to `PT0S`.

long_term_retention_policy.yearly_retent.description: The yearly retention policy for an LTR backup in an ISO 8601 format. Valid value is between 1 to 10 years. e.g. `P1Y`, `P12M`, `P52W` or `P365D`. Defaults to `PT0S`.

managed_instance_id.description: The ID of the Azure SQL Managed Instance on which to create this Managed Database

name.description: The name of the Managed Database to create

point_in_time_restore.restore_point_in_t.description: The point in time for the restore from `source_database_id`

point_in_time_restore.source_database_id.description: The source database id that will be used to restore from

short_term_retention_days.description: The backup retention period in days. This is how many days Point-in-Time Restore will be supported.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

administrator_login.description: The administrator login name for the new SQL Managed Instance

administrator_login_password.description: The password associated with the `administrator_login` user. Needs to comply with Azure's [Password Policy](https://msdn.microsoft.com/library/ms161959.aspx)

azure_active_directory_administrator.azu.description: Specifies whether only Azure AD authentication can be used to log in to this SQL Managed Instance. When `true`, the `administrator_login` and `administrator_login_password` properties can be omitted. Defaults to `false`.

azure_active_directory_administrator.log.description: The login username of the Azure AD Administrator of this SQL Managed Instance.

azure_active_directory_administrator.obj.description: The object id of the Azure AD Administrator of this SQL Managed Instance.

azure_active_directory_administrator.pri.description: The principal type of the Azure AD Administrator of this SQL Managed Instance. Possible values are `Application`, `Group`, `User`.

azure_active_directory_administrator.ten.description: The tenant id of the Azure AD Administrator of this SQL Managed Instance. Should be specified if the Azure AD Administrator is homed in a different tenant to the SQL Managed Instance.

collation.description: Specifies how the SQL Managed Instance will be collated. Defaults to `SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS`

database_format.description: Specifies the internal format of the SQL Managed Instance databases specific to the SQL engine version. Possible values are `AlwaysUpToDate` and `SQLServer2022`. Defaults to `SQLServer2022`.

dns_zone_partner_id.description: The ID of the SQL Managed Instance which will share the DNS zone. This is a prerequisite for creating an `azurerm_sql_managed_instance_failover_group`. Setting this after creation

hybrid_secondary_usage.description: Specifies the hybrid secondary usage for disaster recovery of the SQL Managed Instance. Possible values are `Active` and `Passive`. Defaults to `Active`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this SQL Managed Instance. Required when `type` includes `UserAssigned`.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this SQL Managed Instance. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned`.

license_type.description: What type of license the Managed Instance will use. Possible values are `LicenseIncluded` and `BasePrice`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

maintenance_configuration_name.description: The name of the Public Maintenance Configuration window to apply to the SQL Managed Instance. Possible values are `SQL_Default` or an Azure Location in the format `SQL_{Location}_MI_{Size}`(for example `SQL_EastUS_MI_1`). Defaults to `SQL_Default`.

minimum_tls_version.description: The Minimum TLS Version. Default value is `1.2` Valid values include `1.0`, `1.1`, `1.2`.

name.description: The name of the SQL Managed Instance. This needs to be globally unique within Azure

proxy_override.description: Specifies how the SQL Managed Instance will be accessed. Defaults to `Default`. Possible values are `Default`, `Proxy`, and `Redirect`.

public_data_endpoint_enabled.description: Is the public data endpoint enabled? Defaults to `false`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the SQL Managed Instance

service_principal_type.description: The service principal type. The only possible value is `SystemAssigned`.

sku_name.description: Specifies the SKU Name for the SQL Managed Instance. Possible values are `GP_Gen4`, `GP_Gen5`, `GP_Gen8IM`, `GP_Gen8IH`, `BC_Gen4`, `BC_Gen5`, `BC_Gen8IM` or `BC_Gen8IH`.

storage_account_type.description: Specifies the storage account type used to store backups for this database. Possible values are `GRS`, `GZRS`, `LRS`, and `ZRS`. Defaults to `GRS`.

storage_size_in_gb.description: Maximum storage space for the SQL Managed instance. This should be a multiple of 32 (GB).

subnet_id.description: The subnet resource id that the SQL Managed Instance will be associated with.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

timezone_id.description: The TimeZone ID that the SQL Managed Instance will be operating in. Defaults to `UTC`

vcores.description: Number of cores that should be assigned to the SQL Managed Instance. Values can be `8`, `16`, or `24` for Gen4 SKUs, or `4`, `6`, `8`, `10`, `12`, `16`, `20`, `24`, `32`, `40`, `48`, `56`, `64`, `80`, `96` or `128` for Gen5 SKUs.

zone_redundant_enabled.description: Specifies whether the SQL Managed Instance is zone redundant. Defaults to `false`.

azuread_authentication_only.description: When `true`, only permit logins from AAD users and administrators. When `false`, also allow local database users.

login_username.description: The login name of the principal to set as the Managed Instance Administrator.

managed_instance_id.description: The ID of the Azure SQL Managed Instance for which to set the administrator

object_id.description: The Object ID of the principal to set as the Managed Instance Administrator.

tenant_id.description: The Azure Active Directory Tenant ID.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Managed Instance Failover Group should exist

managed_instance_id.description: The ID of the Azure SQL Managed Instance which will be replicated using a Managed Instance Failover Group

name.description: The name which should be used for this Managed Instance Failover Group

partner_managed_instance_id.description: The ID of the Azure SQL Managed Instance which will be replicated to

read_write_endpoint_failover_policy.grac.description: Applies only if `mode` is `Automatic`. The grace period in minutes before failover with data loss is attempted.

read_write_endpoint_failover_policy.mode.description: The failover mode. Possible values are `Automatic` or `Manual`.

readonly_endpoint_failover_policy_enable.description: Failover policy for the read-only endpoint. Defaults to `true`.

secondary_type.description: The type of the secondary Managed Instance. Possible values are `Geo`, `Standby`. Defaults to `Geo`.

disabled_alerts.description: Specifies an array of alerts that are disabled. Possible values are `Sql_Injection`, `Sql_Injection_Vulnerability`, `Access_Anomaly`, `Data_Exfiltration`, `Unsafe_Action` and `Brute_Force`.

email_account_admins_enabled.description: Boolean flag which specifies if the alert is sent to the account administrators or not. Defaults to `false`.

email_addresses.description: Specifies an array of email addresses to which the alert is sent.

enabled.description: Specifies the state of the Security Alert Policy, whether it is enabled or disabled. Possible values are `true`, `false`.

managed_instance_name.description: Specifies the name of the MS SQL Managed Instance

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group that contains the MS SQL Managed Instance

retention_days.description: Specifies the number of days to keep in the Threat Detection audit logs. Defaults to `0`.

storage_account_access_key.description: Specifies the identifier key of the Threat Detection audit storage account. This is mandatory when you use `storage_endpoint` to specify a storage account blob endpoint.

storage_endpoint.description: Specifies the blob storage endpoint (e.g. https://example.blob.core.windows.net). This blob storage will hold all Threat Detection audit logs.

description.description: Specifies the description of the schedule.

managed_instance_id.description: Specifies the ID of the Managed Instance. Changing this forces a new Sql Start Stop Managed Instance Schedule to be created.

schedule.start_day.description: Start day of the schedule. Possible values are `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday`, `Saturday`, `Sunday`.

schedule.start_time.description: Start time of the schedule in 24-hour format (e.g., `08:00`).

schedule.stop_day.description: Stop day of the schedule. Possible values are `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday`, `Saturday`, `Sunday`.

schedule.stop_time.description: Stop time of the schedule in 24-hour format (e.g., `17:00`).

timezone_id.description: Specifies the time zone of the schedule. Defaults to `UTC`.

auto_rotation_enabled.description: When enabled, the SQL Managed Instance will continuously check the key vault for any new versions of the key being used as the TDE protector. If a new version of the key is detected, the TDE protector on the SQL Managed Instance will be automatically rotated to the latest key version within 60 minutes.

key_vault_key_id.description: To use customer managed keys from Azure Key Vault, provide the AKV Key ID. To use service managed keys, omit this field.

managed_instance_id.description: Specifies the name of the MS SQL Managed Instance
The id of the MS SQL Managed Instance

recurring_scans.description: The recurring scans settings. The `recurring_scans` block supports fields documented below.

recurring_scans.email_subscription_admin.description: Boolean flag which specifies if the schedule scan notification will be sent to the subscription administrators. Defaults to `true`.

recurring_scans.emails.description: Specifies an array of e-mail addresses to which the scan notification is sent.

recurring_scans.enabled.description: Boolean flag which specifies if recurring scans is enabled or disabled. Defaults to `false`.

storage_account_access_key.description: Specifies the identifier key of the storage account for vulnerability assessment scan results. If `storage_container_sas_key` isn't specified, `storage_account_access_key` is required.

storage_container_path.description: A blob storage container path to hold the scan results (e.g. <https://myStorage.blob.core.windows.net/VaScans/>).

storage_container_sas_key.description: A shared access signature (SAS Key) that has write access to the blob container specified in `storage_container_path` parameter. If `storage_account_access_key` isn't specified, `storage_container_sas_key` is required.

name.description: The name of the outbound firewall rule. This should be a FQDN

server_id.description: The resource ID of the SQL Server on which to create the Outbound Firewall Rule

administrator_login.description: The administrator login name for the new server. Required unless `azuread_authentication_only` in the `azuread_administrator` block is `true`. When omitted, Azure will generate a default username which cannot be subsequently changed

administrator_login_password.description: The password associated with the `administrator_login` user. Needs to comply with Azure's [Password Policy](https://msdn.microsoft.com/library/ms161959.aspx).

administrator_login_password_wo_version.description: An integer value used to trigger an update for `administrator_login_password_wo`. This property should be incremented when updating `administrator_login_password_wo`.

azuread_administrator.azuread_authentica.description: Specifies whether only AD Users and administrators (e.g. `azuread_administrator[0].login_username`) can be used to login, or also local database users (e.g. `administrator_login`). When `true`, the `administrator_login` and `administrator_login_password` properties can be omitted.

azuread_administrator.login_username.description: The login username of the Azure AD Administrator of this SQL Server.

azuread_administrator.object_id.description: The object id of the Azure AD Administrator of this SQL Server.

azuread_administrator.tenant_id.description: The tenant id of the Azure AD Administrator of this SQL Server.

connection_policy.description: The connection policy the server will use. Possible values are `Default`, `Proxy`, and `Redirect`. Defaults to `Default`.

express_vulnerability_assessment_enabled.description: Whether to enable the Express Vulnerability Assessment Configuration. Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this SQL Server.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this SQL Server. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

minimum_tls_version.description: The Minimum TLS Version for all SQL Database and SQL Data Warehouse databases associated with the server. Valid values are: `1.0`, `1.1` , `1.2` and `Disabled`. Defaults to `1.2`.

name.description: The name of the Microsoft SQL Server. This needs to be globally unique within Azure

outbound_network_restriction_enabled.description: Whether outbound network traffic is restricted for this server. Defaults to `false`.

primary_user_assigned_identity_id.description: Specifies the primary user managed identity id. Required if `type` within the `identity` block is set to either `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` or `UserAssigned` and should be set at same time as setting `identity_ids`.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether public network access is allowed for this server. Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Microsoft SQL Server

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

transparent_data_encryption_key_vault_ke.description: The fully versioned `Key Vault` `Key` URL (e.g. `'https://<YourVaultName>.vault.azure.net/keys/<YourKeyName>/<YourKeyVersion>`) to be used as the `Customer Managed Key`(CMK/BYOK) for the `Transparent Data Encryption`(TDE) layer.

version.description: The version for the new server. Valid values are: 2.0 (for v11 server) and 12.0 (for v12 server)

mssql_server_id.description: The ID of the mssql server. Changing this forces a new MSSQL Server DNS Alias to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this MSSQL Server DNS Alias. Changing this forces a new MSSQL Server DNS Alias to be created.

audit_actions_and_groups.description: A list of Actions-Groups and Actions to audit.

enabled.description: Whether to enable the extended auditing policy. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `true`.

log_monitoring_enabled.description: Enable audit events to Azure Monitor? To enable server audit events to Azure Monitor, please enable its main database audit events to Azure Monitor. Defaults to `true`.

predicate_expression.description: Specifies condition of where clause when creating an audit.

retention_in_days.description: The number of days to retain logs for in the storage account. Defaults to `0`.

server_id.description: The ID of the SQL Server to set the extended auditing policy

storage_account_access_key.description: The access key to use for the auditing storage account.

storage_account_access_key_is_secondary.description: Is `storage_account_access_key` value the storage's secondary key?

storage_account_subscription_id.description: The ID of the Subscription containing the Storage Account.

storage_endpoint.description: The blob storage endpoint (e.g. <https://example.blob.core.windows.net>). This blob storage will hold all extended auditing logs.

blob_storage_endpoint.description: The blob storage endpoint (e.g. https://example.blob.core.windows.net). This blob storage will hold all Microsoft support auditing logs.

enabled.description: Whether to enable the extended auditing policy. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `true`.

log_monitoring_enabled.description: Enable audit events to Azure Monitor? To enable server audit events to Azure Monitor, please enable its main database audit events to Azure Monitor. Defaults to `true`.

storage_account_access_key.description: The access key to use for the auditing storage account.

storage_account_subscription_id.description: The ID of the Subscription containing the Storage Account.

disabled_alerts.description: Specifies an array of alerts that are disabled. Allowed values are: `Sql_Injection`, `Sql_Injection_Vulnerability`, `Access_Anomaly`, `Data_Exfiltration`, `Unsafe_Action`.

email_account_admins.description: Are the alerts sent to the account administrators? Possible values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`.

email_addresses.description: Specifies an array of email addresses to which the alert is sent.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group that contains the MS SQL Server

retention_days.description: Specifies the number of days to keep the Threat Detection audit logs. Defaults to `0`.

server_name.description: Specifies the name of the MS SQL Server

state.description: Specifies the state of the policy. Possible values are `Disabled` or `Enabled`.

storage_account_access_key.description: Specifies the primary access key of the Threat Detection audit logs blob storage endpoint.

storage_endpoint.description: Specifies the blob storage endpoint that will hold all Threat Detection audit logs (e.g., `https://example.blob.core.windows.net`).

auto_rotation_enabled.description: When enabled, the server will continuously check the key vault for any new versions of the key being used as the TDE protector. If a new version of the key is detected, the TDE protector on the server will be automatically rotated to the latest key version within 60 minutes.

key_vault_key_id.description: To use customer managed keys from Azure Key Vault, provide the AKV Key ID. To use service managed keys, omit this field.

server_id.description: Specifies the name of the MS SQL Server

recurring_scans.email_subscription_admin.description: Boolean flag which specifies if the schedule scan notification will be sent to the subscription administrators. Defaults to `false`.

recurring_scans.emails.description: Specifies an array of email addresses to which the scan notification is sent.

recurring_scans.enabled.description: Boolean flag which specifies if recurring scans is enabled or disabled. Defaults to `false`.

server_security_alert_policy_id.description: The id of the security alert policy of the MS SQL Server

storage_container_path.description: Specifies the blob storage container path that will hold the scan results (e.g., <https://example.blob.core.windows.net/example/>).

storage_container_sas_key.description: Specifies the shared access signature (SAS Key) that has write access to the blob container specified in `storage_container_path` property.

assessment.enabled.description: Should Assessment be enabled? Defaults to `true`.

assessment.run_immediately.description: Should Assessment be run immediately? Defaults to `false`.

auto_backup.description: An `auto_backup` block as defined below. This block can be added to an existing resource, but removing this block

auto_backup.encryption_password.description: Encryption password to use. Setting a password will enable encryption.

auto_backup.manual_schedule.description: A `manual_schedule` block as documented below. When this block is present, the schedule type is set to `Manual`. Without this block, the schedule type is set to `Automated`.

auto_backup.retention_period_in_days.description: Retention period of backups, in days. Valid values are from `1` to `30`.

auto_backup.storage_account_access_key.description: Access key for the storage account where backups will be kept.

auto_backup.storage_blob_endpoint.description: Blob endpoint for the storage account where backups will be kept.

auto_backup.system_databases_backup_enab.description: Include or exclude system databases from auto backup.

auto_patching.day_of_week.description: The day of week to apply the patch on. Possible values are `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday`, `Saturday` and `Sunday`.

auto_patching.maintenance_window_duratio.description: The size of the Maintenance Window in minutes.

auto_patching.maintenance_window_startin.description: The Hour, in the Virtual Machine Time-Zone when the patching maintenance window should begin.

key_vault_credential.service_principal_n.description: The service principal name to access key vault

key_vault_credential.service_principal_s.description: The service principal name secret to access key vault

manual_schedule.days_of_week.description: A list of days on which backup can take place. Possible values are `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday`, `Saturday` and `Sunday`

manual_schedule.full_backup_frequency.description: Frequency of full backups. Valid values include `Daily` or `Weekly`.

manual_schedule.full_backup_start_hour.description: Start hour of a given day during which full backups can take place. Valid values are from `0` to `23`.

manual_schedule.full_backup_window_in_ho.description: Duration of the time window of a given day during which full backups can take place, in hours. Valid values are between `1` and `23`.

manual_schedule.log_backup_frequency_in_.description: Frequency of log backups, in minutes. Valid values are from `5` to `60`.

schedule.day_of_week.description: What day of the week the assessment will be run. Possible values are `Friday`, `Monday`, `Saturday`, `Sunday`, `Thursday`, `Tuesday` and `Wednesday`.

schedule.monthly_occurrence.description: How many months between assessment runs. Valid values are between `1` and `5`.

schedule.start_time.description: What time the assessment will be run. Must be in the format `HH:mm`.

schedule.weekly_interval.description: How many weeks between assessment runs. Valid values are between `1` and `6`.

sql_connectivity_port.description: The SQL Server port. Defaults to `1433`.

sql_connectivity_type.description: The connectivity type used for this SQL Server. Possible values are `LOCAL`, `PRIVATE` and `PUBLIC`. Defaults to `PRIVATE`.

sql_connectivity_update_password.description: The SQL Server sysadmin login password.

sql_connectivity_update_username.description: The SQL Server sysadmin login to create.

sql_instance.adhoc_workloads_optimizatio.description: Specifies if the SQL Server is optimized for adhoc workloads. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `false`.

sql_instance.collation.description: Collation of the SQL Server. Defaults to `SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS`

sql_instance.instant_file_initialization.description: Specifies if Instant File Initialization is enabled for the SQL Server. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `false`

sql_instance.lock_pages_in_memory_enable.description: Specifies if Lock Pages in Memory is enabled for the SQL Server. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `false`

sql_instance.max_dop.description: Maximum Degree of Parallelism of the SQL Server. Possible values are between `0` and `32767`. Defaults to `0`.

sql_instance.max_server_memory_mb.description: Maximum amount memory that SQL Server Memory Manager can allocate to the SQL Server process. Possible values are between `128` and `2147483647` Defaults to `2147483647`.

sql_instance.min_server_memory_mb.description: Minimum amount memory that SQL Server Memory Manager can allocate to the SQL Server process. Possible values are between `0` and `2147483647` Defaults to `0`.

sql_license_type.description: The SQL Server license type. Possible values are `AHUB` (Azure Hybrid Benefit), `DR` (Disaster Recovery), and `PAYG` (Pay-As-You-Go)

sql_virtual_machine_group_id.description: The ID of the SQL Virtual Machine Group that the SQL Virtual Machine belongs to.

storage_configuration.disk_type.description: The type of disk configuration to apply to the SQL Server. Valid values include `NEW`, `EXTEND`, or `ADD`.

storage_configuration.storage_workload_t.description: The type of storage workload. Valid values include `GENERAL`, `OLTP`, or `DW`.

storage_configuration.system_db_on_data_.description: Specifies whether to set system databases (except tempDb) location to newly created data storage. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `false`.

storage_settings.luns.description: A list of Logical Unit Numbers for the disks.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

temp_db_settings.data_file_count.description: The SQL Server default file count. This value defaults to `8`

temp_db_settings.data_file_growth_in_mb.description: The SQL Server default file size - This value defaults to `512`

temp_db_settings.data_file_size_mb.description: The SQL Server default file size - This value defaults to `256`

temp_db_settings.log_file_growth_mb.description: The SQL Server default file size - This value defaults to `512`

temp_db_settings.log_file_size_mb.description: The SQL Server default file size - This value defaults to `256`

temp_db_settings.luns.description: A list of Logical Unit Numbers for the disks.

replica.description: One or more `replica` blocks as defined below

replica.commit.description: The replica commit mode for the availability group. Possible values are `Synchronous_Commit` and `Asynchronous_Commit`

replica.failover_mode.description: The replica failover mode for the availability group. Possible values are `Manual` and `Automatic`

replica.readable_secondary.description: The replica readable secondary mode for the availability group. Possible values are `No`, `Read_Only` and `All`

replica.role.description: The replica role for the availability group. Possible values are `Primary` and `Secondary`

subnet_id.description: The ID of the subnet from which the SQL server will accept communications.
