A canonical list of versions [is available from the Microsoft Container Registry API](https://mcr.microsoft.com/v2/azure-powershell/tags/list). Changing this forces a new Resource Deployment Script to be created.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Resource Group should exist. Changing this forces a new Resource Group to be created.

managed_by.description: The ID of the resource or application that manages this Resource Group.

name.description: The Name which should be used for this Resource Group. Changing this forces a new Resource Group to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Resource Group.

active.description: Is the cost management export active? Default is `true`.

export_data_options.time_frame.description: The time frame for pulling data for the query. If custom, then a specific time period must be provided. Possible values include: `WeekToDate`, `MonthToDate`, `BillingMonthToDate`, `TheLast7Days`, `TheLastMonth`, `TheLastBillingMonth`, `Custom`.

export_data_options.type.description: The type of the query. Possible values are `ActualCost`, `AmortizedCost` and `Usage`.

export_data_storage_location.container_i.description: The Resource Manager ID of the container where exports will be uploaded

export_data_storage_location.root_folder.description: The path of the directory where exports will be uploaded

file_format.description: Format for export. Valid values are `Csv` only. Default is `Csv`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Cost Management Export

recurrence_period_end_date.description: The date the export will stop capturing information.

recurrence_period_start_date.description: The date the export will start capturing information.

recurrence_type.description: How often the requested information will be exported. Valid values include `Annually`, `Daily`, `Monthly`, `Weekly`.

resource_group_id.description: The id of the resource group on which to create an export

accumulated.description: Whether the costs data in the Cost Management View are accumulated over time. Changing this forces a new Cost Management View for a Resource Group to be created.

aggregation.column_name.description: The name of the column to aggregate. Changing this forces a new Cost Management View for a Resource Group to be created.

aggregation.name.description: The name which should be used for this aggregation. Changing this forces a new Cost Management View for a Resource Group to be created.

chart_type.description: Chart type of the main view in Cost Analysis. Possible values are `Area`, `GroupedColumn`, `Line`, `StackedColumn` and `Table`.

dataset.aggregation.description: One or more `aggregation` blocks as defined above.

dataset.granularity.description: The granularity of rows in the report. Possible values are `Daily` and `Monthly`.

dataset.grouping.description: One or more `grouping` blocks as defined below.

dataset.sorting.description: One or more `sorting` blocks as defined below, containing the order by expression to be used in the report

display_name.description: User visible input name of the Cost Management View.

grouping.name.description: The name of the column to group.

grouping.type.description: The type of the column. Possible values are `Dimension` and `TagKey`.

kpi.description: One or more `kpi` blocks as defined below, to show in Cost Analysis UI.

kpi.type.description: KPI type. Possible values are `Budget` and `Forecast`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Cost Management View for a Resource Group. Changing this forces a new Cost Management View for a Resource Group to be created.

pivot.description: One or more `pivot` blocks as defined below, containing the configuration of 3 sub-views in the Cost Analysis UI. Non table views should have three pivots.

pivot.name.description: The name of the column which should be used for this sub-view in the Cost Analysis UI.

pivot.type.description: The data type to show in this sub-view. Possible values are `Dimension` and `TagKey`.

report_type.description: The type of the report. The only possible value is `Usage`.

resource_group_id.description: The ID of the Resource Group this View is scoped to. Changing this forces a new Cost Management View for a Resource Group to be created.

sorting.direction.description: Direction of sort. Possible values are `Ascending` and `Descending`.

sorting.name.description: The name of the column to sort.

timeframe.description: The time frame for pulling data for the report. Possible values are `Custom`, `MonthToDate`, `WeekToDate` and `YearToDate`.

description.description: A description which should be used for this Policy Assignment.

display_name.description: The Display Name for this Policy Assignment.

enforce.description: Specifies if this Policy should be enforced or not? Defaults to `true`.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Managed Identity IDs which should be assigned to the Policy Definition.

identity.type.description: The Type of Managed Identity which should be added to this Policy Definition. Possible values are `SystemAssigned` and `UserAssigned`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Policy Assignment should exist. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

metadata.description: A JSON mapping of any Metadata for this Policy.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Policy Assignment. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created. Cannot exceed 64 characters in length.

non_compliance_message.description: One or more `non_compliance_message` blocks as defined below.

non_compliance_message.content.description: The non-compliance message text. When assigning policy sets (initiatives), unless `policy_definition_reference_id` is specified then this message will be the default for all policies.

non_compliance_message.policy_definition.description: When assigning policy sets (initiatives), this is the ID of the policy definition that the non-compliance message applies to.

not_scopes.description: Specifies a list of Resource Scopes (for example a Subscription, or a Resource Group) within this Management Group which are excluded from this Policy.

override_selector.in.description: Specify the list of policy reference id values to filter in. Cannot be used with `not_in`.

override_selector.kind.description: Specifies which characteristic will narrow down the set of evaluated resources. Possible values are `resourceLocation`, and `policyDefinitionReferenceId`. Defaults to `policyDefinitionReferenceId`.

override_selector.not_in.description: Specify the list of policy reference id values to filter out. Cannot be used with `in`.

overrides.description: One or more `overrides` blocks as defined below. More detail about `overrides` and `resource_selectors` see [policy assignment structure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/assignment-structure)

overrides.selectors.description: One or more `override_selector` block as defined below.

overrides.value.description: Specifies the value to override the policy property. Possible values for `policyEffect` override listed [policy effects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/effects).

parameters.description: A JSON mapping of any Parameters for this Policy.

policy_definition_id.description: The ID of the Policy Definition or Policy Definition Set. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

resource_group_id.description: The ID of the Resource Group where this Policy Assignment should be created. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

resource_selector.in.description: The list of allowed values for the specified kind. Cannot be used with `not_in`. Can contain up to 50 values.

resource_selector.kind.description: Specifies which characteristic will narrow down the set of evaluated resources. Possible values are `resourceLocation`, `resourceType` and `resourceWithoutLocation`.

resource_selector.not_in.description: The list of not-allowed values for the specified kind. Cannot be used with `in`. Can contain up to 50 values.

resource_selectors.description: One or more `resource_selectors` blocks as defined below to filter polices by resource properties.

resource_selectors.name.description: Specifies a name for the resource selector.

resource_selectors.selectors.description: One or more `resource_selector` block as defined below.

description.description: A description to use for this Policy Exemption.

display_name.description: A friendly display name to use for this Policy Exemption.

exemption_category.description: The category of this policy exemption. Possible values are `Waiver` and `Mitigated`.

expires_on.description: The expiration date and time in UTC ISO 8601 format of this policy exemption.

metadata.description: The metadata for this policy exemption. This is a JSON string representing additional metadata that should be stored with the policy exemption.

name.description: The name of the Policy Exemption

policy_assignment_id.description: The ID of the Policy Assignment to be exempted at the specified Scope

policy_definition_reference_ids.description: The policy definition reference ID list when the associated policy assignment is an assignment of a policy set definition.

resource_group_id.description: The Resource Group ID where the Policy Exemption should be applied

failure_percentage.description: A number between 0.0 to 1.0 representing the percentage failure threshold. The remediation will fail if the percentage of failed remediation operations (i.e. failed deployments) exceeds this threshold.

location_filters.description: A list of the resource locations that will be remediated.

name.description: The name of the Policy Remediation

parallel_deployments.description: Determines how many resources to remediate at any given time. Can be used to increase or reduce the pace of the remediation. If not provided, the default parallel deployments value is used.

policy_assignment_id.description: The ID of the Policy Assignment that should be remediated.

policy_definition_reference_id.description: The unique ID for the policy definition reference within the policy set definition that should be remediated. Required when the policy assignment being remediated assigns a policy set definition.

resource_count.description: Determines the max number of resources that can be remediated by the remediation job. If not provided, the default resource count is used.

resource_discovery_mode.description: The way that resources to remediate are discovered. Possible values are `ExistingNonCompliant`, `ReEvaluateCompliance`. Defaults to `ExistingNonCompliant`.

resource_group_id.description: The Resource Group ID at which the Policy Remediation should be applied

debug_level.description: The Debug Level which should be used for this Resource Group Template Deployment. Possible values are `none`, `requestContent`, `responseContent` and `requestContent, responseContent`.

deployment_mode.description: The Deployment Mode for this Resource Group Template Deployment. Possible values are `Complete` (where resources in the Resource Group not specified in the ARM Template will be destroyed) and `Incremental` (where resources are additive only).

name.description: The name which should be used for this Resource Group Template Deployment. Changing this forces a new Resource Group Template Deployment to be created.

parameters_content.description: The contents of the ARM Template parameters file - containing a JSON list of parameters.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Resource Group Template Deployment should exist. Changing this forces a new Resource Group Template Deployment to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Resource Group Template Deployment.

template_content.description: The contents of the ARM Template which should be deployed into this Resource Group. Cannot be specified with `template_spec_version_id`.

template_spec_version_id.description: The ID of the Template Spec Version to deploy. Cannot be specified with `template_content`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Resource Management Private Link should exist. Changing this forces a new Resource Management Private Link to be created.

name.description: Specifies the name of this Resource Management Private Link. Changing this forces a new Resource Management Private Link to be created.

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the Resource Group within which this Resource Management Private Link should exist. Changing this forces a new Resource Management Private Link to be created.

management_group_id.description: Specifies the Management Group ID within which this Private Link Association should exist. Changing this forces a new Private Link Association to be created.

name.description: Specifies the name of this Private Link Association, which should be a UUID. If `name` is not provided, a UUID will be generated, you should use [the `ignore_changes` attribute to ignore changes to this field](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/block/resource#ignore_changes). Changing this forces a new Private Link Association to be created.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether public network access is allowed. Changing this forces a new Private Link Association to be created.

resource_management_private_link_id.description: The Resource ID of Resource Management Private Link. Changing this forces a new Private Link Association to be created.

description.description: A description which should be used for this Policy Assignment.

display_name.description: The Display Name for this Policy Assignment.

enforce.description: Specifies if this Policy should be enforced or not? Defaults to `true`.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Managed Identity IDs which should be assigned to the Policy Definition.

identity.type.description: The Type of Managed Identity which should be added to this Policy Definition. Possible values are `SystemAssigned` and `UserAssigned`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Policy Assignment should exist. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

metadata.description: A JSON mapping of any Metadata for this Policy.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Policy Assignment. Changing this forces a new Resource Policy Assignment to be created. Cannot exceed 64 characters in length.

non_compliance_message.description: One or more `non_compliance_message` blocks as defined below.

non_compliance_message.content.description: The non-compliance message text. When assigning policy sets (initiatives), unless `policy_definition_reference_id` is specified then this message will be the default for all policies.

non_compliance_message.policy_definition.description: When assigning policy sets (initiatives), this is the ID of the policy definition that the non-compliance message applies to.

not_scopes.description: Specifies a list of Resource Scopes (for example a Subscription, or a Resource Group) within this Management Group which are excluded from this Policy.

override_selector.in.description: Specify the list of policy reference id values to filter in. Cannot be used with `not_in`.

override_selector.kind.description: Specifies which characteristic will narrow down the set of evaluated resources. Possible values are `resourceLocation`, and `policyDefinitionReferenceId`. Defaults to `policyDefinitionReferenceId`.

override_selector.not_in.description: Specify the list of policy reference id values to filter out. Cannot be used with `in`.

overrides.description: One or more `overrides` blocks as defined below. More detail about `overrides` and `resource_selectors` see [policy assignment structure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/assignment-structure)

overrides.selectors.description: One or more `override_selector` block as defined below.

overrides.value.description: Specifies the value to override the policy property. Possible values for `policyEffect` override listed [policy effects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/effects).

parameters.description: A JSON mapping of any Parameters for this Policy.

policy_definition_id.description: The ID of the Policy Definition or Policy Definition Set. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

resource_id.description: The ID of the Resource (or Resource Scope) where this should be applied. Changing this forces a new Resource Policy Assignment to be created.

resource_selector.in.description: The list of allowed values for the specified kind. Cannot be used with `not_in`. Can contain up to 50 values.

resource_selector.kind.description: Specifies which characteristic will narrow down the set of evaluated resources. Possible values are `resourceLocation`, `resourceType` and `resourceWithoutLocation`.

resource_selector.not_in.description: The list of not-allowed values for the specified kind. Cannot be used with `in`. Can contain up to 50 values.

resource_selectors.description: One or more `resource_selectors` blocks as defined below to filter polices by resource properties.

resource_selectors.name.description: Specifies a name for the resource selector.

resource_selectors.selectors.description: One or more `resource_selector` block as defined below.

description.description: A description to use for this Policy Exemption.

display_name.description: A friendly display name to use for this Policy Exemption.

exemption_category.description: The category of this policy exemption. Possible values are `Waiver` and `Mitigated`.

expires_on.description: The expiration date and time in UTC ISO 8601 format of this policy exemption.

metadata.description: The metadata for this policy exemption. This is a JSON string representing additional metadata that should be stored with the policy exemption.

name.description: The name of the Policy Exemption

policy_assignment_id.description: The ID of the Policy Assignment to be exempted at the specified Scope

policy_definition_reference_ids.description: The policy definition reference ID list when the associated policy assignment is an assignment of a policy set definition.

resource_id.description: The Resource ID where the Policy Exemption should be applied

failure_percentage.description: A number between 0.0 to 1.0 representing the percentage failure threshold. The remediation will fail if the percentage of failed remediation operations (i.e. failed deployments) exceeds this threshold.

location_filters.description: A list of the resource locations that will be remediated.

name.description: The name of the Policy Remediation

parallel_deployments.description: Determines how many resources to remediate at any given time. Can be used to increase or reduce the pace of the remediation. If not provided, the default parallel deployments value is used.

policy_assignment_id.description: The ID of the Policy Assignment that should be remediated.

policy_definition_reference_id.description: The unique ID for the policy definition reference within the policy set definition that should be remediated. Required when the policy assignment being remediated assigns a policy set definition.

resource_count.description: Determines the max number of resources that can be remediated by the remediation job. If not provided, the default resource count is used.

resource_discovery_mode.description: The way that resources to remediate are discovered. Possible values are `ExistingNonCompliant`, `ReEvaluateCompliance`. Defaults to `ExistingNonCompliant`.

resource_id.description: The Resource ID at which the Policy Remediation should be applied

feature.description: A list of `feature` blocks as defined below.

feature.name.description: Specifies the name of the feature to register.

feature.registered.description: Should this feature be Registered or Unregistered?

name.description: The namespace of the Resource Provider which should be registered

condition.description: The condition that limits the resources that the role can be assigned to

condition_version.description: The version of the condition. Possible values are `1.0` or `2.0`

delegated_managed_identity_resource_id.description: The delegated Azure Resource Id which contains a Managed Identity

description.description: The description for this Role Assignment

name.description: A unique UUID/GUID for this Role Assignment - one will be generated if not specified

principal_id.description: The ID of the Principal (User, Group or Service Principal) to assign the Role Definition to

principal_type.description: The type of the `principal_id`. Possible values are `User`, `Group` and `ServicePrincipal`

role_definition_id.description: The Scoped-ID of the Role Definition

role_definition_name.description: The name of a built-in Role

scope.description: The scope at which the Role Assignment applies to, such as `/subscriptions/0b1f6471-1bf0-4dda-aec3-111122223333`, `/subscriptions/0b1f6471-1bf0-4dda-aec3-111122223333/resourceGroups/myGroup`, or `/subscriptions/0b1f6471-1bf0-4dda-aec3-111122223333/resourceGroups/myGroup/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/myVM`, or `/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/myMG`

skip_service_principal_aad_check.description: If the `principal_id` is a newly provisioned `Service Principal` set this value to `true` to skip the `Azure Active Directory` check which may fail due to replication lag. This argument is only valid if the `principal_id` is a `Service Principal` identity. Defaults to `false`.

actions.description: One or more Allowed Actions, such as `*`, `Microsoft.Resources/subscriptions/resourceGroups/read`. See ['Azure Resource Manager resource provider operations'](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/role-based-access-control/resource-provider-operations) for details.

assignable_scopes.description: One or more assignable scopes for this Role Definition, such as `/subscriptions/0b1f6471-1bf0-4dda-aec3-111122223333`, `/subscriptions/0b1f6471-1bf0-4dda-aec3-111122223333/resourceGroups/myGroup`, `/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/0b1f6471-1bf0-4dda-aec3-111122223333` , or `/subscriptions/0b1f6471-1bf0-4dda-aec3-111122223333/resourceGroups/myGroup/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/myVM`.

data_actions.description: One or more Allowed Data Actions, such as `*`, `Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers/blobs/read`. See ['Azure Resource Manager resource provider operations'](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/role-based-access-control/resource-provider-operations) for details.

description.description: A description of the Role Definition.

name.description: The name of the Role Definition.

not_actions.description: One or more Disallowed Actions, such as `*`, `Microsoft.Resources/subscriptions/resourceGroups/read`. See ['Azure Resource Manager resource provider operations'](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/role-based-access-control/resource-provider-operations) for details.

not_data_actions.description: One or more Disallowed Data Actions, such as `*`, `Microsoft.Resources/subscriptions/resourceGroups/read`. See ['Azure Resource Manager resource provider operations'](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/role-based-access-control/resource-provider-operations) for details.

role_definition_id.description: A unique UUID/GUID which identifies this role - one will be generated if not specified

scope.description: The scope at which the Role Definition applies to, such as `/subscriptions/0b1f6471-1bf0-4dda-aec3-111122223333`, `/subscriptions/0b1f6471-1bf0-4dda-aec3-111122223333/resourceGroups/myGroup`, `/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/0b1f6471-1bf0-4dda-aec3-111122223333`, or `/subscriptions/0b1f6471-1bf0-4dda-aec3-111122223333/resourceGroups/myGroup/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/myVM`. It is recommended to use the first entry of the `assignable_scopes`

activation_rules.maximum_duration.description: The maximum length of time an activated role can be valid, in an ISO8601 Duration format (e.g. `PT8H`). Valid range is `PT30M` to `PT23H30M`, in 30 minute increments, or `PT1D`. Possible values are `PT30M`, `PT1H`, `PT1H30M`, `PT2H`, `PT2H30M`, `PT3H`, `PT3H30M`, `PT4H`, `PT4H30M`, `PT5H`, `PT5H30M`, `PT6H`, `PT6H30M`, `PT7H`, `PT7H30M`, `PT8H`, `PT8H30M`, `PT9H`, `PT9H30M`, `PT10H`, `PT10H30M`, `PT11H`, `PT11H30M`, `PT12H`, `PT12H30M`, `PT13H`, `PT13H30M`, `PT14H`, `PT14H30M`, `PT15H`, `PT15H30M`, `PT16H`, `PT16H30M`, `PT17H`, `PT17H30M`, `PT18H`, `PT18H30M`, `PT19H`, `PT19H30M`, `PT20H`, `PT20H30M`, `PT21H`, `PT21H30M`, `PT22H`, `PT22H30M`, `PT23H`, `PT23H30M` and `P1D`.

activation_rules.require_approval.description: Is approval required for activation. If `true` an `approval_stage` block must be provided.

activation_rules.require_justification.description: Is a justification required during activation of the role.

activation_rules.require_multifactor_aut.description: Is multi-factor authentication required to activate the role. Conflicts with `required_conditional_access_authentication_context`.

activation_rules.require_ticket_info.description: Is ticket information requrired during activation of the role.

activation_rules.required_conditional_ac.description: The Entra ID Conditional Access context that must be present for activation. Conflicts with `require_multifactor_authentication`.

active_assignment_rules.expiration_requi.description: Must an assignment have an expiry date. `false` allows permanent assignment.

active_assignment_rules.expire_after.description: The maximum length of time an assignment can be valid, as an ISO8601 duration. Permitted values: `P15D`, `P30D`, `P90D`, `P180D`, or `P365D`.

active_assignment_rules.require_justific.description: Is a justification required to create new assignments.

active_assignment_rules.require_multifac.description: Is multi-factor authentication required to create new assignments.

active_assignment_rules.require_ticket_i.description: Is ticket information required to create new assignments.

approval_stage.primary_approver.description: One or more `primary_approver` blocks as defined below.

eligible_assignment_rules.expiration_req.description: Must an assignment have an expiry date. `false` allows permanent assignment.

eligible_assignment_rules.expire_after.description: The maximum length of time an assignment can be valid, as an ISO8601 duration. Permitted values: `P15D`, `P30D`, `P90D`, `P180D`, or `P365D`.

notification_rules.active_assignments.description: A `notification_target` block as defined below to configure notfications on active role assignments.

notification_rules.eligible_activations.description: A `notification_target` block as defined below for configuring notifications on activation of eligible role.

notification_rules.eligible_assignments.description: A `notification_target` block as defined below to configure notification on eligible role assignments.

notification_settings.additional_recipie.description: A list of additional email addresses that will receive these notifications.

notification_settings.default_recipients.description: Should the default recipients receive these notifications.

notification_settings.notification_level.description: What level of notifications should be sent. Options are `All` or `Critical`.

primary_approver.object_id.description: The ID of the object which will act as an approver.

primary_approver.type.description: The type of object acting as an approver. Possible options are `User` and `Group`.

role_definition_id.description: The scoped Role Definition ID of the role for which this policy will apply

scope.description: The scope to which this Role Management Policy will apply. Can refer to a management group, a subscription, a resource group or a resource

address_prefix.description: The destination to which the route applies. Can be CIDR (such as `10.1.0.0/16`) or [Azure Service Tag](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/service-tags-overview) (such as `ApiManagement`, `AzureBackup` or `AzureMonitor`) format.

name.description: The name of the route

next_hop_in_ip_address.description: Contains the IP address packets should be forwarded to. Next hop values are only allowed in routes where the next hop type is `VirtualAppliance`.

next_hop_type.description: The type of Azure hop the packet should be sent to. Possible values are `VirtualNetworkGateway`, `VnetLocal`, `Internet`, `VirtualAppliance` and `None`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the route

route_table_name.description: The name of the route table within which create the route

location.description: The Azure Region where the Route Filter should exist. Changing this forces a new Route Filter to be created.

name.description: The Name which should be used for this Route Filter.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Route Filter should exist. Changing this forces a new Route Filter to be created.

rule.access.description: The access type of the rule. The only possible value is `Allow`.

rule.communities.description: The collection for bgp community values to filter on. e.g. ['12076:5010','12076:5020'].

rule.name.description: The name of the route filter rule.

rule.rule_type.description: The rule type of the rule. The only possible value is `Community`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Route Filter.

action.parameter.description: A `parameter` block as defined below. Required if `type` is anything other than `Drop`.

action.type.description: The type of the action to be taken. Possible values are `Add`, `Drop`, `Remove`, `Replace` and `Unknown`.

match_criterion.as_path.description: A list of AS paths which this criterion matches.

match_criterion.community.description: A list of BGP communities which this criterion matches.

match_criterion.match_condition.description: The match condition to apply the rule of the Route Map. Possible values are `Contains`, `Equals`, `NotContains`, `NotEquals` and `Unknown`.

match_criterion.route_prefix.description: A list of route prefixes which this criterion matches.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Route Map

rule.name.description: The unique name for the rule.

rule.next_step_if_matched.description: The next step after the rule is evaluated. Possible values are `Continue`, `Terminate` and `Unknown`. Defaults to `Unknown`.

virtual_hub_id.description: The resource ID of the Virtual Hub

branch_to_branch_traffic_enabled.description: Whether to enable route exchange between Azure Route Server and the gateway(s).

hub_routing_preference.description: The hub routing preference. Valid values are `ASPath`, `ExpressRoute` or `VpnGateway`. Defaults to `ExpressRoute`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the Route Server should exist

name.description: The name of the Route Server

public_ip_address_id.description: The ID of the Public IP Address. This option is required since September 1st 2021

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the Resource Group where the Route Server should exist

sku.description: The SKU of the Route Server. The only possible value is `Standard`

subnet_id.description: The ID of the Subnet that the Route Server will reside

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Route Server Bgp Connection

peer_asn.description: The peer autonomous system number for the Route Server Bgp Connection

peer_ip.description: The peer ip address for the Route Server Bgp Connection

route_server_id.description: The ID of the Route Server within which this Bgp connection should be created

address_prefix.description: The destination to which the route applies. Can be CIDR (such as `10.1.0.0/16`) or [Azure Service Tag](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-network/service-tags-overview) (such as `ApiManagement`, `AzureBackup` or `AzureMonitor`) format.

bgp_route_propagation_enabled.description: Boolean flag which controls propagation of routes learned by BGP on that route table. Defaults to `true`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

name.description: The name of the route.

next_hop_in_ip_address.description: Contains the IP address packets should be forwarded to. Next hop values are only allowed in routes where the next hop type is `VirtualAppliance`.

next_hop_type.description: The type of Azure hop the packet should be sent to. Possible values are `VirtualNetworkGateway`, `VnetLocal`, `Internet`, `VirtualAppliance` and `None`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the route table

route.description: List of `route` objects representing routes as defined below. Each object accepts the arguments documented below.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

allowed_ips.description: Specifies a list of inbound IPv4 or CIDRs that are allowed to access the Search Service. If the incoming IP request is from an IP address which is not included in the `allowed_ips` it will be blocked by the Search Services firewall.

authentication_failure_mode.description: Specifies the response that the Search Service should return for requests that fail authentication. Possible values include `http401WithBearerChallenge` or `http403`.

customer_managed_key_enforcement_enabled.description: Specifies whether the Search Service should enforce that non-customer resources are encrypted. Defaults to `false`.

hosting_mode.description: Specifies the Hosting Mode, which allows for High Density partitions (that allow for up to 1000 indexes) should be supported. Possible values are `HighDensity` or `Default`. Defaults to `Default`. Changing this forces a new Search Service to be created.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies the list of User Assigned Managed Service Identity IDs which should be assigned to this Search Service.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Search Service. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned`.

local_authentication_enabled.description: Specifies whether the Search Service allows authenticating using API Keys? Defaults to `true`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Search Service should exist. Changing this forces a new Search Service to be created.

name.description: The Name which should be used for this Search Service. Changing this forces a new Search Service to be created.

network_rule_bypass_option.description: Whether to allow trusted Azure services to access a network restricted Search Service? Possible values are `None` and `AzureServices`. Defaults to `None`.

partition_count.description: Specifies the number of partitions which should be created. This field cannot be set when using a `free` sku ([see the Microsoft documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/search/search-sku-tier)). Possible values include `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `6`, or `12`. Defaults to `1`.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Specifies whether Public Network Access is allowed for this resource. Defaults to `true`.

replica_count.description: Specifies the number of Replica's which should be created for this Search Service. This field cannot be set when using a `free` sku ([see the Microsoft documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/search/search-sku-tier)).

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Search Service should exist. Changing this forces a new Search Service to be created.

semantic_search_sku.description: Specifies the Semantic Search SKU which should be used for this Search Service. Possible values include `free` and `standard`.

sku.description: The SKU which should be used for this Search Service. Possible values include `basic`, `free`, `standard`, `standard2`, `standard3`, `storage_optimized_l1` and `storage_optimized_l2`.

tags.description: Specifies a mapping of tags which should be assigned to this Search Service.

name.description: Specify the name of the Azure Search Shared Private Link Resource

request_message.description: Specify the request message for requesting approval of the Shared Private Link Enabled Remote Resource.

search_service_id.description: Specify the id of the Azure Search Service

subresource_name.description: Specify the sub resource name which the Azure Search Private Endpoint is able to connect to

target_resource_id.description: Specify the ID of the Shared Private Link Enabled Remote Resource which this Azure Search Private Endpoint should be connected to

additional_data.description: A map of additional data to associate with the assessment.

assessment_policy_id.description: The ID of the security Assessment policy to apply to this resource. Changing this forces a new security Assessment to be created.

status.cause.description: Specifies the cause of the assessment status.

status.code.description: Specifies the programmatic code of the assessment status. Possible values are `Healthy`, `Unhealthy` and `NotApplicable`.

status.description.description: Specifies the human readable description of the assessment status.

target_resource_id.description: The ID of the target resource. Changing this forces a new security Assessment to be created.

categories.description: A list of the categories of resource that is at risk when the Security Center Assessment is unhealthy. Possible values are `Unknown`, `Compute`, `Data`, `IdentityAndAccess`, `IoT` and `Networking`.

description.description: The description of the Security Center Assessment.

display_name.description: The user-friendly display name of the Security Center Assessment.

implementation_effort.description: The implementation effort which is used to remediate the Security Center Assessment. Possible values are `Low`, `Moderate` and `High`.

remediation_description.description: The description which is used to mitigate the security issue.

severity.description: The severity level of the Security Center Assessment. Possible values are `Low`, `Medium` and `High`. Defaults to `Medium`.

threats.description: A list of the threat impacts for the Security Center Assessment. Possible values are `AccountBreach`, `DataExfiltration`, `DataSpillage`, `DenialOfService`, `ElevationOfPrivilege`, `MaliciousInsider`, `MissingCoverage` and `ThreatResistance`.

user_impact.description: The user impact of the Security Center Assessment. Possible values are `Low`, `Moderate` and `High`.

auto_provision.description: Should the security agent be automatically provisioned on Virtual Machines in this subscription? Possible values are `On` (to install the security agent automatically, if it's missing) or `Off` (to not install the security agent automatically).

action.description: One or more `action` blocks as defined below. An `action` tells this automation where the data is to be sent to upon being evaluated by the rules in the `source`.

connection_string.description: A connection string to send data to the target Event Hub namespace, this should include a key with send permissions.

description.description: Specifies the description for the Security Center Automation.

enabled.description: Boolean to enable or disable this Security Center Automation. Defaults to `true`.

event_source.description: Type of data that will trigger this automation. Must be one of `Alerts`, `Assessments`, `AssessmentsSnapshot`, `RegulatoryComplianceAssessment`, `RegulatoryComplianceAssessmentSnapshot`, `SecureScoreControls`, `SecureScoreControlsSnapshot`, `SecureScores`, `SecureScoresSnapshot`, `SubAssessments` or `SubAssessmentsSnapshot`. Note. assessments are also referred to as recommendations

location.description: The Azure Region where the Security Center Automation should exist. Changing this forces a new Security Center Automation to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Security Center Automation. Changing this forces a new Security Center Automation to be created.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Security Center Automation should exist. Changing this forces a new Security Center Automation to be created.

resource_id.description: The resource id of the target Logic App, Event Hub namespace or Log Analytics workspace.

rule.expected_value.description: A value that will be compared with the value in `property_path`.

rule.operator.description: The comparison operator to use, must be one of: `Contains`, `EndsWith`, `Equals`, `GreaterThan`, `GreaterThanOrEqualTo`, `LesserThan`, `LesserThanOrEqualTo`, `NotEquals`, `StartsWith`

rule.property_path.description: The JPath of the entity model property that should be checked.

rule.property_type.description: The data type of the compared operands, must be one of: `Integer`, `String`, `Boolean` or `Number`.

rule_set.description: A set of rules which evaluate upon event and data interception. This is defined in one or more `rule_set` blocks as defined below.

rule_set.rule.description: One or more `rule` blocks as defined below.

scopes.description: A list of scopes on which the automation logic is applied, at least one is required. Supported scopes are a subscription (in this format `/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000`) or a resource group under that subscription (in the format `/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/example`). The automation will only apply on defined scopes.

source.description: One or more `source` blocks as defined below. A `source` defines what data types will be processed and a set of rules to filter that data.

tags.description: A mapping of tags assigned to the resource.

trigger_url.description: The callback URL to trigger the Logic App that will receive and process data sent by this automation. This can be found in the Azure Portal under "See trigger history"

type.description: Type of Azure resource to send data to. Possible values are `EventHub`, `LogicApp` and `Workspace`.

alert_notifications.description: Whether to send security alerts notifications to the security contact.

alerts_to_admins.description: Whether to send security alerts notifications to subscription admins.

email.description: The email of the Security Center Contact.

name.description: The name of the Security Center Contact. Changing this forces a new Security Center Contact to be created.

phone.description: The phone number of the Security Center Contact.

virtual_machine_id.description: The ID of the virtual machine to be monitored by vulnerability assessment

vulnerability_assessment_provider.description: The vulnerability assesment provider to use. The only possible values is `MdeTvm`.

enabled.description: Boolean flag to enable/disable data access.

setting_name.description: The setting to manage. Possible values are `MCAS` , `WDATP`, `WDATP_EXCLUDE_LINUX_PUBLIC_PREVIEW`, `WDATP_UNIFIED_SOLUTION` and `Sentinel`

malware_scanning_on_upload_cap_gb_per_mo.description: The max GB to be scanned per Month. Must be `-1` or above `0`. Omit this property or set to `-1` if no capping is needed. Defaults to `-1`.

malware_scanning_on_upload_enabled.description: Whether On Upload malware scanning should be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

override_subscription_settings_enabled.description: Whether the settings defined for this storage account should override the settings defined for the subscription. Defaults to `false`.

scan_results_event_grid_topic_id.description: The Event Grid Topic where every scan result will be sent to. When you set an Event Grid custom topic, you must set `override_subscription_settings_enabled` to `true` to override the subscription-level settings.

sensitive_data_discovery_enabled.description: Whether Sensitive Data Discovery should be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

storage_account_id.description: The ID of the storage account the defender applied to

extension.description: One or more `extension` blocks as defined below.

extension.additional_extension_propertie.description: Key/Value pairs that are required for some extensions.

resource_type.description: The resource type this setting affects. Possible values are `AI`, `Api`, `AppServices`, `ContainerRegistry`, `KeyVaults`, `KubernetesService`, `SqlServers`, `SqlServerVirtualMachines`, `StorageAccounts`, `VirtualMachines`, `Arm`, `Dns`, `OpenSourceRelationalDatabases`, `Containers`, `CosmosDbs` and `CloudPosture`. Defaults to `VirtualMachines`

subplan.description: Resource type pricing subplan. Contact your MSFT representative for possible values

tier.description: The pricing tier to use. Possible values are `Free` and `Standard`.

scope.description: The scope of VMs to send their security data to the desired workspace, unless overridden by a setting with more specific scope.

workspace_id.description: The ID of the Log Analytics Workspace to save the data in.

mode.description: mode of the Built-in Anomaly Alert Rule. Possible Values are `Production` and `Flighting`.

name.description: The Name of the built-in Anomaly Alert Rule.

content_schema_version.description: Schema version of the content. Can be used to distinguish between flow based on the schema version.

custom_version.description: The Custom version of the content.

first_publish_date.description: The first publish date of solution content item.

icon_id.description: The ID of the icon, this id can be fetched from the solution template.

kind.description: The kind of content the metadata is for. Possible values are `AnalyticsRule`, `AnalyticsRuleTemplate`, `AutomationRule`, `AzureFunction`, `DataConnector`, `DataType`, `HuntingQuery`, `InvestigationQuery`, `LogicAppsCustomConnector`, `Parser`, `Playbook`, `PlaybookTemplate`, `Solution`, `Watchlist`, `WatchlistTemplate`, `Workbook` and `WorkbookTemplate`.

last_publish_date.description: The last publish date of solution content item.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Sentinel Metadata. Changing this forces a new Sentinel Metadata to be created.

preview_images_dark.description: Specifies a list of preview image file names used for dark theme. These will be taken from solution artifacts.

source.id.description: The id of the content source, the solution ID, Log Analytics Workspace name etc.

Possible values are `Reconnaissance`, `ResourceDevelopment`, `InitialAccess`, `Execution`, `Persistence`, `PrivilegeEscalation`, `DefenseEvasion`, `CredentialAccess`, `Discovery`, `LateralMovement`, `Collection`, `CommandAndControl`, `Exfiltration`, `Impact`, `ImpairProcessControl` and `InhibitResponseFunction`.

threat_analysis_techniques.description: Specifies a list of techniques the resource covers.

language.description: The language of the Threat Intelligence Indicator.

object_marking_refs.description: Specifies a list of Threat Intelligence marking references.

revoked.description: Whether the Threat Intelligence entity revoked.

item_search_key.description: The key used to optimize query performance when using Watchlist for joins with other data. Changing this forces a new Sentinel Watchlist to be created.

labels.description: Specifies a list of labels related to this Sentinel Watchlist. Changing this forces a new Sentinel Watchlist to be created.

client_certificate_common_name.common_na.description: The common or subject name of the certificate.

common_names.certificate_common_name.description: The common or subject name of the certificate.

common_names.certificate_issuer_thumbpri.description: The Issuer Thumbprint of the Certificate.

delta_health_policy.max_delta_unhealthy_.description: Specifies the maximum tolerated percentage of delta unhealthy applications that can have aggregated health states of error. If the current unhealthy applications do not respect the percentage relative to the state at the beginning of the upgrade, the cluster is unhealthy. Defaults to `0`.

Specifies the maximum tolerated percentage of delta unhealthy nodes that can have aggregated health states of error. If the current unhealthy nodes do not respect the percentage relative to the state at the beginning of the upgrade, the cluster is unhealthy. Defaults to `0`.

delta_health_policy.max_upgrade_domain_d.description: Specifies the maximum tolerated percentage of upgrade domain delta unhealthy nodes that can have aggregated health state of error. If there is any upgrade domain where the current unhealthy nodes do not respect the percentage relative to the state at the beginning of the upgrade, the cluster is unhealthy. Defaults to `0`.

diagnostics_config.blob_endpoint.description: The Blob Endpoint of the Storage Account.

diagnostics_config.protected_account_key.description: The protected diagnostics storage key name, such as `StorageAccountKey1`.

diagnostics_config.queue_endpoint.description: The Queue Endpoint of the Storage Account.

diagnostics_config.storage_account_name.description: The name of the Storage Account where the Diagnostics should be sent to.

diagnostics_config.table_endpoint.description: The Table Endpoint of the Storage Account.

ephemeral_ports.end_port.description: The end of the Ephemeral Port Range on this Node Type.

ephemeral_ports.start_port.description: The start of the Ephemeral Port Range on this Node Type.

fabric_settings.description: One or more `fabric_settings` blocks as defined below.

fabric_settings.name.description: The name of the Fabric Setting, such as `Security` or `Federation`.

fabric_settings.parameters.description: A map containing settings for the specified Fabric Setting.

health_policy.max_unhealthy_applications.description: Specifies the maximum tolerated percentage of applications that can have aggregated health state of error. If the upgrade exceeds this percentage, the cluster is unhealthy. Defaults to `0`.

health_policy.max_unhealthy_nodes_percen.description: Specifies the maximum tolerated percentage of nodes that can have aggregated health states of error. If an upgrade exceeds this percentage, the cluster is unhealthy. Defaults to `0`.

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the Service Fabric Cluster should exist

management_endpoint.description: Specifies the Management Endpoint of the cluster such as `http://example.com`

node_type.description: One or more `node_type` blocks as defined below.

node_type.capacities.description: The capacity tags applied to the nodes in the node type, the cluster resource manager uses these tags to understand how much resource a node has.

node_type.client_endpoint_port.description: The Port used for the Client Endpoint for this Node Type.

node_type.durability_level.description: The Durability Level for this Node Type. Possible values include `Bronze`, `Gold` and `Silver`. Defaults to `Bronze`.

node_type.http_endpoint_port.description: The Port used for the HTTP Endpoint for this Node Type.

node_type.instance_count.description: The number of nodes for this Node Type.

node_type.is_primary.description: Is this the Primary Node Type?

node_type.is_stateless.description: Should this node type run only stateless services?

node_type.multiple_availability_zones.description: Does this node type span availability zones?

node_type.name.description: The name of the Node Type.

node_type.placement_properties.description: The placement tags applied to nodes in the node type, which can be used to indicate where certain services (workload) should run.

node_type.reverse_proxy_endpoint_port.description: The Port used for the Reverse Proxy Endpoint for this Node Type. Changing this will upgrade the cluster.

reliability_level.description: Specifies the Reliability Level of the Cluster. Possible values include `None`, `Bronze`, `Silver`, `Gold` and `Platinum`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group in which the Service Fabric Cluster exists

reverse_proxy_certificate.description: A `reverse_proxy_certificate` block as defined below. Conflicts with `reverse_proxy_certificate_common_names`.

reverse_proxy_certificate.thumbprint_sec.description: The Secondary Thumbprint of the Certificate.

reverse_proxy_certificate.x509_store_nam.description: The X509 Store where the Certificate Exists, such as `My`.

reverse_proxy_certificate_common_names.x.description: The X509 Store where the Certificate Exists, such as `My`.

service_fabric_zonal_upgrade_mode.description: Specifies the logical grouping of VMs in upgrade domains. Possible values are `Hierarchical` or `Parallel`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

upgrade_mode.description: Specifies the Upgrade Mode of the cluster. Possible values are `Automatic` or `Manual`.

upgrade_policy.force_restart_enabled.description: Indicates whether to restart the Service Fabric node even if only dynamic configurations have changed.

upgrade_policy.health_check_retry_timeou.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, after which Service Fabric retries the health check if the previous health check fails. Defaults to `00:45:00`.

upgrade_policy.health_check_stable_durat.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, that Service Fabric waits in order to verify that the cluster is stable before it continues to the next upgrade domain or completes the upgrade. This wait duration prevents undetected changes of health right after the health check is performed. Defaults to `00:01:00`.

upgrade_policy.health_check_wait_duratio.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, that Service Fabric waits before it performs the initial health check after it finishes the upgrade on the upgrade domain. Defaults to `00:00:30`.

upgrade_policy.upgrade_domain_timeout.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, that Service Fabric takes to upgrade a single upgrade domain. After this period, the upgrade fails. Defaults to `02:00:00`.

upgrade_policy.upgrade_replica_set_check.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, that Service Fabric waits for a replica set to reconfigure into a safe state, if it is not already in a safe state, before Service Fabric proceeds with the upgrade. Defaults to `10675199.02:48:05.4775807`.

upgrade_policy.upgrade_timeout.description: Specifies the duration, in "hh:mm:ss" string format, that Service Fabric takes for the entire upgrade. After this period, the upgrade fails. Defaults to `12:00:00`.

vm_image.description: Specifies the Image expected for the Service Fabric Cluster, such as `Windows`

vmss_zonal_upgrade_mode.description: Specifies the upgrade mode for the virtual machine scale set updates that happen in all availability zones at once. Possible values are `Hierarchical` or `Parallel`.

active_directory.client_application_id.description: The ID of the Client Application.

active_directory.cluster_application_id.description: The ID of the Cluster Application.

authentication.description: Controls how connections to the cluster are authenticated. A `authentication` block as defined below.

authentication.certificate.description: One or more `certificate` blocks as defined below.

backup_service_enabled.description: If true, backup service is enabled.

certificate.type.description: The type of the certificate. Can be `AdminClient` or `ReadOnlyClient`.

certificates.store.description: The certificate store on the Virtual Machine to which the certificate should be added.

certificates.url.description: The URL of a certificate that has been uploaded to Key Vault as a secret

client_connection_port.description: Port to use when connecting to the cluster.

custom_fabric_setting.description: One or more `custom_fabric_setting` blocks as defined below.

dns_name.description: Hostname for the cluster. If unset the cluster's name will be used..

dns_service_enabled.description: If true, DNS service is enabled.

http_gateway_port.description: Port that should be used by the Service Fabric Explorer to visualize applications and cluster status.

lb_rule.description: One or more `lb_rule` blocks as defined below.

lb_rule.probe_protocol.description: Protocol for the probe. Can be one of `tcp`, `udp`, `http`, or `https`.

lb_rule.probe_request_path.description: Path for the probe to check, when probe protocol is set to `http`.

lb_rule.protocol.description: The transport protocol used in this rule. Can be one of `tcp` or `udp`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Resource Group should exist. Changing this forces a new Resource Group to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Resource Group. Changing this forces a new Resource Group to be created.

node_type.description: One or more `node_type` blocks as defined below.

node_type.application_port_range.description: Sets the port range available for applications. Format is `<from_port>-<to_port>`, for example `10000-20000`.

node_type.capacities.description: Specifies a list of key/value pairs used to set capacity tags for this node type.

node_type.data_disk_size_gb.description: The size of the data disk in gigabytes..

node_type.data_disk_type.description: The type of the disk to use for storing data. It can be one of `Premium_LRS`, `Standard_LRS`, or `StandardSSD_LRS`. Defaults to `Standard_LRS`.

node_type.ephemeral_port_range.description: Sets the port range available for the OS. Format is `<from_port>-<to_port>`, for example `10000-20000`. There has to be at least 255 ports available and cannot overlap with `application_port_range`..

node_type.multiple_placement_groups_enab.description: If set the node type can be composed of multiple placement groups.

node_type.name.description: The name which should be used for this node type.

node_type.placement_properties.description: Specifies a list of placement tags that can be used to indicate where services should run..

node_type.primary.description: If set to true, system services will run on this node type. Only one node type should be marked as primary. Primary node type cannot be deleted or changed once they're created.

node_type.stateless.description: If set to true, only stateless workloads can run on this node type.

node_type.vm_image_offer.description: The offer type of the marketplace image cluster VMs will use.

node_type.vm_image_publisher.description: The publisher of the marketplace image cluster VMs will use.

upgrade_wave.description: Upgrade wave for the fabric runtime. Default is `Wave0`, allowed value must be one of `Wave0`, `Wave1`, or `Wave2`.

maximum_elastic_worker_count.description: The maximum number of workers to use in an Elastic SKU Plan or Premium Plan that have `premium_plan_auto_scale_enabled` set to `true`. Cannot be set unless using an Elastic or Premium SKU.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Service Plan. Changing this forces a new Service Plan to be created.

os_type.description: The O/S type for the App Services to be hosted in this plan. Possible values include `Windows`, `Linux`, and `WindowsContainer`

per_site_scaling_enabled.description: Should Per Site Scaling be enabled. Defaults to `false`.

premium_plan_auto_scale_enabled.description: Should automatic scaling be enabled for the Premium SKU Plan. Defaults to `false`. Cannot be set unless using a Premium SKU.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Service Plan should exist. Changing this forces a new Service Plan to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the AppService.

worker_count.description: The number of Workers (instances) to be allocated.

zone_balancing_enabled.description: Should the Service Plan balance across Availability Zones in the region.

capacity.description: Specifies the capacity. When `sku` is `Premium`, capacity can be `1`, `2`, `4`, `8` or `16`. When `sku` is `Basic` or `Standard`, capacity can be `0` only.

customer_managed_key.identity_id.description: The ID of the User Assigned Identity that has access to the key.

customer_managed_key.infrastructure_encr.description: Used to specify whether enable Infrastructure Encryption (Double Encryption)

customer_managed_key.key_vault_key_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault Key which should be used to Encrypt the data in this Service Bus Namespace.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Service Bus namespace.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Service Bus Namespace. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

local_auth_enabled.description: Whether or not SAS authentication is enabled for the Service Bus namespace. Defaults to `true`.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

minimum_tls_version.description: The minimum supported TLS version for this Service Bus Namespace. Valid values are: `1.0`, `1.1` and `1.2`. Defaults to `1.2`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Service Bus Namespace resource

network_rule_set.default_action.description: Specifies the default action for the Network Rule Set. Possible values are `Allow` and `Deny`. Defaults to `Allow`.

network_rule_set.ip_rules.description: One or more IP Addresses, or CIDR Blocks which should be able to access the Service Bus Namespace.

network_rule_set.network_rules.description: One or more `network_rules` blocks as defined below.

network_rule_set.public_network_access_e.description: Whether to allow traffic over public network. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `true`.

network_rule_set.trusted_services_allowe.description: Are Azure Services that are known and trusted for this resource type are allowed to bypass firewall configuration? See [Trusted Microsoft Services](https://github.com/MicrosoftDocs/azure-docs/blob/master/articles/service-bus-messaging/includes/service-bus-trusted-services.md)

network_rules.ignore_missing_vnet_servic.description: Should the Service Bus Namespace Network Rule Set ignore missing Virtual Network Service Endpoint option in the Subnet? Defaults to `false`.

network_rules.subnet_id.description: The Subnet ID which should be able to access this Service Bus Namespace.

premium_messaging_partitions.description: Specifies the number messaging partitions. Only valid when `sku` is `Premium` and the minimum number is `1`. Possible values include `0`, `1`, `2`, and `4`. Defaults to `0` for Standard, Basic namespace

public_network_access_enabled.description: Is public network access enabled for the Service Bus Namespace? Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to

sku.description: Defines which tier to use. Options are `Basic`, `Standard` or `Premium`. Please note that setting this field to `Premium` will force the creation of a new resource.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

listen.description: Grants listen access to this Authorization Rule. Defaults to `false`.

manage.description: Grants manage access to this Authorization Rule. When this property is `true` - both `listen` and `send` must be too. Defaults to `false`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the ServiceBus Namespace Authorization Rule resource

namespace_id.description: Specifies the ID of the ServiceBus Namespace

send.description: Grants send access to this Authorization Rule. Defaults to `false`.

infrastructure_encryption_enabled.description: Used to specify whether enable Infrastructure Encryption

key_vault_key_id.description: The ID of the Key Vault Key which should be used to Encrypt the data in this Service Bus Namespace.

namespace_id.description: The ID of the Service Bus namespace

alias_authorization_rule_id.description: The Shared access policies used to access the connection string for the alias.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Disaster Recovery Config. This is the alias DNS name that will be created

partner_namespace_id.description: The ID of the Service Bus Namespace to replicate to.

primary_namespace_id.description: The ID of the primary Service Bus Namespace to replicate

auto_delete_on_idle.description: The ISO 8601 timespan duration of the idle interval after which the Queue is automatically deleted, minimum of 5 minutes.

batched_operations_enabled.description: Boolean flag which controls whether server-side batched operations are enabled. Defaults to `true`.

dead_lettering_on_message_expiration.description: Boolean flag which controls whether the Queue has dead letter support when a message expires. Defaults to `false`.

default_message_ttl.description: The ISO 8601 timespan duration of the TTL of messages sent to this queue. This is the default value used when TTL is not set on message itself.

duplicate_detection_history_time_window.description: The ISO 8601 timespan duration during which duplicates can be detected. Defaults to `PT10M` (10 Minutes).

express_enabled.description: Boolean flag which controls whether Express Entities are enabled. An express queue holds a message in memory temporarily before writing it to persistent storage. Defaults to `false` for Basic and Standard. For Premium, it MUST be set to `false`.

forward_dead_lettered_messages_to.description: The name of a Queue or Topic to automatically forward dead lettered messages to.

forward_to.description: The name of a Queue or Topic to automatically forward messages to. Please [see the documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/service-bus-messaging/service-bus-auto-forwarding) for more information.

lock_duration.description: The ISO 8601 timespan duration of a peek-lock; that is, the amount of time that the message is locked for other receivers. Maximum value is 5 minutes. Defaults to `PT1M` (1 Minute).

max_delivery_count.description: Integer value which controls when a message is automatically dead lettered. Defaults to `10`.

max_message_size_in_kilobytes.description: Integer value which controls the maximum size of a message allowed on the queue for Premium SKU. For supported values see the "Large messages support" section of [this document](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/service-bus-messaging/service-bus-premium-messaging#large-messages-support-preview).

max_size_in_megabytes.description: Integer value which controls the size of memory allocated for the queue. For supported values see the "Queue or topic size" section of [Service Bus Quotas](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/service-bus-messaging/service-bus-quotas).

name.description: Specifies the name of the ServiceBus Queue resource

namespace_id.description: The ID of the ServiceBus Namespace to create this queue in

partitioning_enabled.description: Boolean flag which controls whether to enable the queue to be partitioned across multiple message brokers

requires_duplicate_detection.description: Boolean flag which controls whether the Queue requires duplicate detection

requires_session.description: Boolean flag which controls whether the Queue requires sessions. This will allow ordered handling of unbounded sequences of related messages. With sessions enabled a queue can guarantee first-in-first-out delivery of messages

status.description: The status of the Queue. Possible values are `Active`, `Creating`, `Deleting`, `Disabled`, `ReceiveDisabled`, `Renaming`, `SendDisabled`, `Unknown`. Note that `Restoring` is not accepted. Defaults to `Active`.

listen.description: Does this Authorization Rule have Listen permissions to the ServiceBus Queue? Defaults to `false`.

manage.description: Does this Authorization Rule have Manage permissions to the ServiceBus Queue? When this property is `true` - both `listen` and `send` must be too. Defaults to `false`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Authorization Rule

queue_id.description: Specifies the ID of the ServiceBus Queue

send.description: Does this Authorization Rule have Send permissions to the ServiceBus Queue? Defaults to `false`.

auto_delete_on_idle.description: The idle interval after which the topic is automatically deleted as an [ISO 8601 duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). The minimum duration is `5` minutes or `PT5M`. Defaults to `P10675199DT2H48M5.4775807S`.

batched_operations_enabled.description: Boolean flag which controls whether the Subscription supports batched operations.

client_scoped_subscription.client_id.description: Specifies the Client ID of the application that created the client-scoped subscription

client_scoped_subscription.is_client_sco.description: Whether the client scoped subscription is durable. This property can only be controlled from the application side.
Whether the client scoped subscription is shareable. Defaults to `true`

client_scoped_subscription_enabled.description: whether the subscription is scoped to a client id. Defaults to `false`.

dead_lettering_on_filter_evaluation_erro.description: Boolean flag which controls whether the Subscription has dead letter support on filter evaluation exceptions. Defaults to `true`.

dead_lettering_on_message_expiration.description: Boolean flag which controls whether the Subscription has dead letter support when a message expires.

default_message_ttl.description: The Default message timespan to live as an [ISO 8601 duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). This is the duration after which the message expires, starting from when the message is sent to Service Bus. This is the value used when TimeToLive is not set on a message itself. Defaults to `P10675199DT2H48M5.4775807S`.

forward_dead_lettered_messages_to.description: The name of a Queue or Topic to automatically forward Dead Letter messages to.

forward_to.description: The name of a Queue or Topic to automatically forward messages to.

lock_duration.description: The lock duration for the subscription as an [ISO 8601 duration](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations). Defaults to `PT1M`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the ServiceBus Subscription resource

requires_session.description: Boolean flag which controls whether this Subscription supports the concept of a session

status.description: The status of the Subscription. Possible values are `Active`,`ReceiveDisabled`, or `Disabled`. Defaults to `Active`.

topic_id.description: The ID of the ServiceBus Topic to create this Subscription in

action.description: Represents set of actions written in SQL language-based syntax that is performed against a BrokeredMessage.

correlation_filter.description: A `correlation_filter` block as documented below to be evaluated against a BrokeredMessage. Required when `filter_type` is set to `CorrelationFilter`.

correlation_filter.properties.description: A list of user defined properties to be included in the filter. Specified as a map of name/value pairs.

correlation_filter.reply_to.description: Address of the queue to reply to.

filter_type.description: Type of filter to be applied to a BrokeredMessage. Possible values are `SqlFilter` and `CorrelationFilter`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the ServiceBus Subscription Rule

sql_filter.description: Represents a filter written in SQL language-based syntax that to be evaluated against a BrokeredMessage. Required when `filter_type` is set to `SqlFilter`.

subscription_id.description: The ID of the ServiceBus Subscription in which this Rule should be created

auto_delete_on_idle.description: The ISO 8601 timespan duration of the idle interval after which the Topic is automatically deleted, minimum of 5 minutes. Defaults to `P10675199DT2H48M5.4775807S`.

batched_operations_enabled.description: Boolean flag which controls if server-side batched operations are enabled.

default_message_ttl.description: The ISO 8601 timespan duration of TTL of messages sent to this topic if no TTL value is set on the message itself. Defaults to `P10675199DT2H48M5.4775807S`.

duplicate_detection_history_time_window.description: The ISO 8601 timespan duration during which duplicates can be detected. Defaults to `PT10M` (10 Minutes).

express_enabled.description: Boolean flag which controls whether Express Entities are enabled. An express topic holds a message in memory temporarily before writing it to persistent storage.

max_message_size_in_kilobytes.description: Integer value which controls the maximum size of a message allowed on the topic for Premium SKU. For supported values see the "Large messages support" section of [this document](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/service-bus-messaging/service-bus-premium-messaging#large-messages-support-preview). Defaults to `256`.

max_size_in_megabytes.description: Integer value which controls the size of memory allocated for the topic. For supported values see the "Queue/topic size" section of [this document](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/service-bus-messaging/service-bus-quotas). Defaults to `5120`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the ServiceBus Topic resource

namespace_id.description: The ID of the ServiceBus Namespace to create this topic in

partitioning_enabled.description: Boolean flag which controls whether to enable the topic to be partitioned across multiple message brokers

requires_duplicate_detection.description: Boolean flag which controls whether the Topic requires duplicate detection. Defaults to `false`

status.description: The Status of the Service Bus Topic. Acceptable values are `Active` or `Disabled`. Defaults to `Active`.

support_ordering.description: Boolean flag which controls whether the Topic supports ordering.

listen.description: Grants listen access to this this Authorization Rule. Defaults to `false`.

manage.description: Grants manage access to this this Authorization Rule. When this property is `true` - both `listen` and `send` must be too. Defaults to `false`.

send.description: Grants send access to this this Authorization Rule. Defaults to `false`.
