name.description: The name which should be used for this Recovery Service. Changing this forces a new Site to be created.

recovery_vault_id.description: The ID of the Recovery Services Vault where the Site created. Changing this forces a new Site to be created.

appliance_name.description: The name of VMWare appliance which handles the replication

default_log_storage_account_id.description: The ID of the stroage account that should be used for logging during replication.

default_recovery_disk_type.description: The type of storage account that should be used for recovery disks when a failover is done. Possible values are `Premium_LRS`, `PremiumV2_LRS`, `Premium_ZRS`, `Standard_LRS`, `StandardSSD_LRS`, `StandardSSD_ZRS` and `UltraSSD_LRS`.

default_target_disk_encryption_set_id.description: The ID of the default Disk Encryption Set that should be used for the disks when a failover is done.

license_type.description: The license type of the VM. Possible values are `NoLicenseType`, `NotSpecified` and `WindowsServer`. Defaults to `NotSpecified`.

managed_disk.description: One or more `managed_disk` block as defined below. It's available only if mobility service is already installed on the source VM.

managed_disk.disk_id.description: The ID of the disk to be replicated.

managed_disk.log_storage_account_id.description: The ID of the storage account that should be used for logging during replication.

managed_disk.target_disk_encryption_set_.description: The ID of the Disk Encryption Set that should be used for the disks when a failover is done.

managed_disk.target_disk_type.description: The disk type of the disk to be created when a failover is done. Possible values are `Premium_LRS`, `PremiumV2_LRS`, `Premium_ZRS`, `Standard_LRS`, `StandardSSD_LRS`, `StandardSSD_ZRS` and `UltraSSD_LRS`.

multi_vm_group_name.description: Name of group in which all machines will replicate together and have shared crash consistent and app-consistent recovery points when failed over.

name.description: The name of the replicated VM

network_interface.description: One or more `network_interface` block as defined below.

network_interface.is_primary.description: Whether this `network_interface` is primary for the replicated VM.

network_interface.source_mac_address.description: Mac address of the network interface of source VM.

network_interface.target_static_ip.description: Static IP to assign when a failover is done.

network_interface.target_subnet_name.description: Name of the subnet to use when a failover is done.

network_interface.test_subnet_name.description: Name of the subnet to use when a test failover is done.

physical_server_credential_name.description: The name of the credential to access the source VM

recovery_replication_policy_id.description: The ID of the policy to use for this replicated VM.

recovery_vault_id.description: The ID of the Recovery Services Vault where the replicated VM is created.

source_vm_name.description: The name of the source VM in VMWare

target_availability_set_id.description: The ID of availability set that the new VM should belong to when a failover is done.

target_boot_diagnostics_storage_account_.description: The ID of the storage account that should be used for boot diagnostics when a failover is done.

target_network_id.description: The ID of network to use when a failover is done.

target_proximity_placement_group_id.description: The ID of Proximity Placement Group the new VM should belong to when a failover is done.

target_resource_group_id.description: The ID of resource group where the VM should be created when a failover is done.

target_vm_name.description: Name of the VM that should be created when a failover is done

target_vm_size.description: Size of the VM that should be created when a failover is done, such as `Standard_F2`. If it's not specified, it will automatically be set by detecting the source VM size.

target_zone.description: Specifies the Availability Zone where the Failover VM should exist.

test_network_id.description: The ID of network to use when a test failover is done.

application_consistent_snapshot_frequenc.description: Specifies the frequency at which to create application consistent recovery points. Must between `0` to `720`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Classic Replication Policy. Changing this forces a new Replication Policy to be created.

recovery_point_retention_in_minutes.description: Specifies the period up to which the recovery points will be retained. Must between `0` to `21600`.

recovery_vault_id.description: ID of the Recovery Services Vault. Changing this forces a new Replication Policy to be created.

name.description: The name of the replication policy association. Changing this forces a new association to be created.

policy_id.description: The ID of the VMWare replication policy which to be associated. Changing this forces a new association to be created.

recovery_vault_id.description: The ID of the Recovery Service Vault to which the policy should be associated.

create_option.description: Indicates how the snapshot is to be created. Possible values are `Copy` or `Import`.

disk_access_id.description: Specifies the ID of the Disk Access which should be used for this Snapshot. This is used in conjunction with setting `network_access_policy` to `AllowPrivate`.

disk_encryption_key.secret_url.description: The URL to the Key Vault Secret used as the Disk Encryption Key. This can be found as `id` on the `azurerm_key_vault_secret` resource.

disk_encryption_key.source_vault_id.description: The ID of the source Key Vault. This can be found as `id` on the `azurerm_key_vault` resource.

disk_size_gb.description: The size of the Snapshotted Disk in GB.

incremental_enabled.description: Specifies if the Snapshot is incremental

key_encryption_key.key_url.description: The URL to the Key Vault Key used as the Key Encryption Key. This can be found as `id` on the `azurerm_key_vault_key` resource.

key_encryption_key.source_vault_id.description: The ID of the source Key Vault. This can be found as `id` on the `azurerm_key_vault` resource.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

name.description: Specifies the name of the Snapshot resource

network_access_policy.description: Policy for accessing the disk via network. Possible values are `AllowAll`, `AllowPrivate`, or `DenyAll`. Defaults to `AllowAll`.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Policy for controlling export on the disk. Possible values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Snapshot

source_resource_id.description: Specifies a reference to an existing snapshot, when `create_option` is `Copy`

source_uri.description: Specifies the URI to a Managed or Unmanaged Disk

storage_account_id.description: Specifies the ID of an storage account. Used with `source_uri` to allow authorization during import of unmanaged blobs from a different subscription

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

type.description: The Token type. Possible values include `Bitbucket`, `Dropbox`, `Github`, and `OneDrive`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Spring Cloud Accelerator. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud Accelerator to be created. The only possible value is `default`.

spring_cloud_service_id.description: The ID of the Spring Cloud Service. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud Accelerator to be created.

deployment_name.description: Specifies the name of Spring Cloud Deployment which is going to be active.

spring_cloud_app_id.description: Specifies the id of the Spring Cloud Application

api_try_out_enabled.description: Specifies whether the API try-out feature is enabled. When enabled, users can try out the API by sending requests and viewing responses in API portal.

gateway_ids.description: Specifies a list of Spring Cloud Gateway.

instance_count.description: Specifies the required instance count of the Spring Cloud API Portal. Possible Values are between `1` and `500`. Defaults to `1` if not specified.

sso.client_id.description: The public identifier for the application.

sso.client_secret.description: The secret known only to the application and the authorization server.

sso.scope.description: It defines the specific actions applications can be allowed to do on a user's behalf.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Spring Cloud API Portal Domain. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud API Portal Domain to be created.

spring_cloud_api_portal_id.description: The ID of the Spring Cloud API Portal. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud API Portal Domain to be created.

thumbprint.description: Specifies the thumbprint of the Spring Cloud Certificate that binds to the Spring Cloud API Portal Domain.

addon_json.description: A JSON object that contains the addon configurations of the Spring Cloud Service.

custom_persistent_disk.mount_options.description: These are the mount options for a persistent disk.

custom_persistent_disk.mount_path.description: The mount path of the persistent disk.

custom_persistent_disk.read_only_enabled.description: Indicates whether the persistent disk is a readOnly one.

custom_persistent_disk.share_name.description: The share name of the Azure File share.

custom_persistent_disk.storage_name.description: The name of the Spring Cloud Storage.

https_only.description: Is only HTTPS allowed? Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Spring Cloud Application.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Spring Cloud Application. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

ingress_settings.backend_protocol.description: Specifies how ingress should communicate with this app backend service. Allowed values are `GRPC` and `Default`. Defaults to `Default`.

ingress_settings.read_timeout_in_seconds.description: Specifies the ingress read time out in seconds. Defaults to `300`.

ingress_settings.send_timeout_in_seconds.description: Specifies the ingress send time out in seconds. Defaults to `60`.

ingress_settings.session_affinity.description: Specifies the type of the affinity, set this to `Cookie` to enable session affinity. Allowed values are `Cookie` and `None`. Defaults to `None`.

ingress_settings.session_cookie_max_age.description: Specifies the time in seconds until the cookie expires.

is_public.description: Does the Spring Cloud Application have public endpoint? Defaults to `false`.

persistent_disk.size_in_gb.description: Specifies the size of the persistent disk in GB. Possible values are between `0` and `50`.

public_endpoint_enabled.description: Should the App in vnet injection instance exposes endpoint which could be accessed from Internet?

service_name.description: Specifies the name of the Spring Cloud Service resource

tls_enabled.description: Is End to End TLS Enabled? Defaults to `false`.

cosmosdb_gremlin_database_name.description: Specifies the name of the Gremlin Database which the Spring Cloud App should be associated with. Should only be set when `api_type` is `gremlin`.

binding_type.description: Specifies the Build Pack Binding Type. Allowed values are `ApacheSkyWalking`, `AppDynamics`, `ApplicationInsights`, `Dynatrace`, `ElasticAPM` and `NewRelic`.

authentication.description: The authentication info. An `authentication` block as defined below.

authentication.certificate.description: Service principal certificate for `servicePrincipal` auth. Should be specified when `type` is set to `servicePrincipalCertificate`.

authentication.client_id.description: Client ID for `userAssignedIdentity` or `servicePrincipal` auth. Should be specified when `type` is set to `servicePrincipalSecret` or `servicePrincipalCertificate`. When `type` is set to `userAssignedIdentity`, `client_id` and `subscription_id` should be either both specified or both not specified.

authentication.client_type.description: The application client type. Possible values are `none`, `dotnet`, `java`, `python`, `go`, `php`, `ruby`, `django`, `nodejs` and `springBoot`. Defaults to `none`.

authentication.name.description: Username or account name for secret auth. `name` and `secret` should be either both specified or both not specified when `type` is set to `secret`.

authentication.principal_id.description: Principal ID for `servicePrincipal` auth. Should be specified when `type` is set to `servicePrincipalSecret` or `servicePrincipalCertificate`.

authentication.secret.description: Password or account key for secret auth. `secret` and `name` should be either both specified or both not specified when `type` is set to `secret`.

authentication.secret_store.description: An option to store secret value in secure place. An `secret_store` block as defined below.

authentication.subscription_id.description: Subscription ID for `userAssignedIdentity`. `subscription_id` and `client_id` should be either both specified or both not specified.

authentication.vnet_solution.description: The type of the VNet solution. Possible values are `serviceEndpoint`, `privateLink`.

cors.allowed_methods.description: Allowed HTTP methods on cross-site requests. The special value `*` allows all methods. If not set, `GET` and `HEAD` are allowed by default. Possible values are `DELETE`, `GET`, `HEAD`, `MERGE`, `POST`, `OPTIONS` and `PUT`.

cors.allowed_origin_patterns.description: Allowed origin patterns to make cross-site requests.

cors.allowed_origins.description: Allowed origins to make cross-site requests. The special value `*` allows all domains.

cors.credentials_allowed.description: is user credentials are supported on cross-site requests?

cors.exposed_headers.description: HTTP response headers to expose for cross-site requests.

cors.max_age_seconds.description: How long, in seconds, the response from a pre-flight request can be cached by clients.

environment_variables.description: Specifies the environment variables of the Spring Cloud Gateway as a map of key-value pairs.

instance_count.description: Specifies the required instance count of the Spring Cloud Gateway. Possible Values are between `1` and `500`. Defaults to `1` if not specified.

local_response_cache_per_instance.description: A `local_response_cache_per_instance` block as defined below. Only one of `local_response_cache_per_instance` or `local_response_cache_per_route` can be specified.

local_response_cache_per_instance.size.description: Specifies the maximum size of cache (10MB, 900KB, 1GB...) to determine if the cache needs to evict some entries.

local_response_cache_per_instance.time_t.description: Specifies the time before a cached entry is expired (300s, 5m, 1h...).

local_response_cache_per_route.description: A `local_response_cache_per_route` block as defined below. Only one of `local_response_cache_per_instance` or `local_response_cache_per_route` can be specified.

local_response_cache_per_route.size.description: Specifies the maximum size of cache (10MB, 900KB, 1GB...) to determine if the cache needs to evict some entries.

local_response_cache_per_route.time_to_l.description: Specifies the time before a cached entry is expired (300s, 5m, 1h...).

name.description: The name which should be used for this Spring Cloud Gateway. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud Gateway to be created. The only possible value is `default`.

public_network_access_enabled.description: Indicates whether the Spring Cloud Gateway exposes endpoint.

quota.cpu.description: Specifies the required cpu of the Spring Cloud Deployment. Possible Values are `500m`, `1`, `2`, `3` and `4`. Defaults to `1` if not specified.

quota.memory.description: Specifies the required memory size of the Spring Cloud Deployment. Possible Values are `512Mi`, `1Gi`, `2Gi`, `3Gi`, `4Gi`, `5Gi`, `6Gi`, `7Gi`, and `8Gi`. Defaults to `2Gi` if not specified.

sensitive_environment_variables.description: Specifies the sensitive environment variables of the Spring Cloud Gateway as a map of key-value pairs.

spring_cloud_service_id.description: The ID of the Spring Cloud Service. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud Gateway to be created.

sso.client_id.description: The public identifier for the application.

sso.client_secret.description: The secret known only to the application and the authorization server.

sso.scope.description: It defines the specific actions applications can be allowed to do on a user's behalf.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Spring Cloud Gateway Custom Domain. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud Gateway Custom Domain to be created.

spring_cloud_gateway_id.description: The ID of the Spring Cloud Gateway. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud Gateway Custom Domain to be created.

thumbprint.description: Specifies the thumbprint of the Spring Cloud Certificate that binds to the Spring Cloud Gateway Custom Domain.

filters.description: Specifies a list of filters which are used to modify the request before sending it to the target endpoint, or the received response in app level.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Spring Cloud Gateway Route Config. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud Gateway Route Config to be created.

open_api.description: One or more `open_api` blocks as defined below.

predicates.description: Specifies a list of conditions to evaluate a route for each request in app level. Each predicate may be evaluated against request headers and parameter values. All of the predicates associated with a route must evaluate to true for the route to be matched to the request.

protocol.description: Specifies the protocol of routed Spring Cloud App. Allowed values are `HTTP` and `HTTPS`.

route.description: One or more `route` blocks as defined below.

route.classification_tags.description: Specifies the classification tags which will be applied to methods in the generated OpenAPI documentation.

route.description.description: Specifies the description which will be applied to methods in the generated OpenAPI documentation.

route.filters.description: Specifies a list of filters which are used to modify the request before sending it to the target endpoint, or the received response.

route.predicates.description: Specifies a list of conditions to evaluate a route for each request. Each predicate may be evaluated against request headers and parameter values. All of the predicates associated with a route must evaluate to true for the route to be matched to the request.

route.sso_validation_enabled.description: Should the sso validation be enabled?

route.title.description: Specifies the title which will be applied to methods in the generated OpenAPI documentation.

route.token_relay.description: Should pass currently-authenticated user's identity token to application service?

route.uri.description: Specifies the full uri which will override `appName`.

spring_cloud_app_id.description: The ID of the Spring Cloud App.

spring_cloud_gateway_id.description: The ID of the Spring Cloud Gateway. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud Gateway Route Config to be created.

sso_validation_enabled.description: Should the sso validation be enabled in app level?

environment_variables.description: Specifies the environment variables of the Spring Cloud Deployment as a map of key-value pairs.

instance_count.description: Specifies the required instance count of the Spring Cloud Deployment. Possible Values are between `1` and `500`. Defaults to `1` if not specified.

jvm_options.description: Specifies the jvm option of the Spring Cloud Deployment.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Spring Cloud Deployment

quota.cpu.description: Specifies the required cpu of the Spring Cloud Deployment. Possible Values are `500m`, `1`, `2`, `3` and `4`. Defaults to `1` if not specified.

quota.memory.description: Specifies the required memory size of the Spring Cloud Deployment. Possible Values are `512Mi`, `1Gi`, `2Gi`, `3Gi`, `4Gi`, `5Gi`, `6Gi`, `7Gi`, and `8Gi`. Defaults to `1Gi` if not specified.

runtime_version.description: Specifies the runtime version of the Spring Cloud Deployment. Possible Values are `Java_8`, `Java_11` and `Java_17`. Defaults to `Java_8`.

spring_cloud_app_id.description: Specifies the id of the Spring Cloud Application in which to create the Deployment

agent_enabled.description: Specifies whether enable the agent. Defaults to `true`.

app_name.description: Specifies the application name used to report data to New Relic.

app_server_port.description: Specifies the port number to differentiate JVMs for the same app on the same machine.

audit_mode_enabled.description: Specifies whether enable plain text logging of all data sent to New Relic to the agent logfile. Defaults to `false`.

auto_app_naming_enabled.description: Specifies whether enable the reporting of data separately for each web app. Defaults to `false`.

auto_transaction_naming_enabled.description: Specifies whether enable the component-based transaction naming. Defaults to `true`.

custom_tracing_enabled.description: Specifies whether enable all instrumentation using an `@Trace` annotation. Disabling this causes `@Trace` annotations to be ignored. Defaults to `true`.

globally_enabled.description: Specifies whether the Spring Cloud Application Performance Monitoring resource for Application Insights is enabled globally. Defaults to `false`.

labels.description: Specifies a mapping of labels to be added to the New Relic application.

license_key.description: Specifies the license key associated with the New Relic account. This key binds your agent's data to your account in New Relic service.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Spring Cloud Application Performance Monitoring resource for New Relic

spring_cloud_service_id.description: The ID of the Spring Cloud Service

build_agent_pool_size.description: Specifies the size for this Spring Cloud Service's default build agent pool. Possible values are `S1`, `S2`, `S3`, `S4` and `S5`. This field is applicable only for Spring Cloud Service with enterprise tier.

config_server_git_setting.description: A `config_server_git_setting` block as defined below. This field is applicable only for Spring Cloud Service with basic and standard tier.

config_server_git_setting.label.description: The default label of the Git repository, should be the branch name, tag name, or commit-id of the repository.

config_server_git_setting.repository.description: One or more `repository` blocks as defined below.

config_server_git_setting.search_paths.description: An array of strings used to search subdirectories of the Git repository.

config_server_git_setting.uri.description: The URI of the default Git repository used as the Config Server back end, should be started with `http://`, `https://`, `git@`, or `ssh://`.

container_registry.description: One or more `container_registry` block as defined below. This field is applicable only for Spring Cloud Service with enterprise tier.

container_registry.name.description: Specifies the name of the container registry.

container_registry.password.description: Specifies the password of the container registry.

container_registry.server.description: Specifies the login server of the container registry.

container_registry.username.description: Specifies the username of the container registry.

default_build_service.description: A `default_build_service` block as defined below. This field is applicable only for Spring Cloud Service with enterprise tier.

default_build_service.container_registry.description: Specifies the name of the container registry used in the default build service.

http_basic_auth.password.description: The password used to access the Git repository server, required when the Git repository server supports HTTP Basic Authentication.

http_basic_auth.username.description: The username that's used to access the Git repository server, required when the Git repository server supports HTTP Basic Authentication.

location.description: Specifies the supported Azure location where the resource exists

log_stream_public_endpoint_enabled.description: Should the log stream in vnet injection instance could be accessed from Internet?

managed_environment_id.description: The resource Id of the Managed Environment that the Spring Apps instance builds on. Can only be specified when `sku_tier` is set to `StandardGen2`.

marketplace.description: A `marketplace` block as defined below. Can only be specified when `sku` is set to `E0`.

marketplace.plan.description: Specifies the plan ID of the 3rd Party Artifact that is being procured.

marketplace.product.description: Specifies the 3rd Party artifact that is being procured.

marketplace.publisher.description: Specifies the publisher ID of the 3rd Party Artifact that is being procured.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Spring Cloud Service resource

network.app_network_resource_group.description: Specifies the Name of the resource group containing network resources of Azure Spring Cloud Apps

network.app_subnet_id.description: Specifies the ID of the Subnet which should host the Spring Boot Applications deployed in this Spring Cloud Service

network.cidr_ranges.description: A list of (at least 3) CIDR ranges (at least /16) which are used to host the Spring Cloud infrastructure, which must not overlap with any existing CIDR ranges in the Subnet

network.outbound_type.description: Specifies the egress traffic type of the Spring Cloud Service. Possible values are `loadBalancer` and `userDefinedRouting`. Defaults to `loadBalancer`

network.read_timeout_seconds.description: Ingress read time out in seconds.

network.service_runtime_network_resource.description: Specifies the Name of the resource group containing network resources of Azure Spring Cloud Service Runtime

network.service_runtime_subnet_id.description: Specifies the ID of the Subnet where the Service Runtime components of the Spring Cloud Service will exist

repository.label.description: The default label of the Git repository, should be the branch name, tag name, or commit-id of the repository.

repository.name.description: A name to identify on the Git repository, required only if repos exists.

repository.pattern.description: An array of strings used to match an application name. For each pattern, use the `{application}/{profile}` format with wildcards.

repository.search_paths.description: An array of strings used to search subdirectories of the Git repository.

repository.uri.description: The URI of the Git repository that's used as the Config Server back end should be started with `http://`, `https://`, `git@`, or `ssh://`.

resource_group_name.description: Specifies The name of the resource group in which to create the Spring Cloud Service

service_registry_enabled.description: Whether enable the default Service Registry. This field is applicable only for Spring Cloud Service with enterprise tier.

sku_name.description: Specifies the SKU Name for this Spring Cloud Service. Possible values are `B0`, `S0` and `E0`. Defaults to `S0`

sku_tier.description: Specifies the SKU Tier for this Spring Cloud Service. Possible values are `Basic`, `Enterprise`, `Standard` and `StandardGen2`. The attribute is automatically computed from API response except when `managed_environment_id` is defined

ssh_auth.host_key.description: The host key of the Git repository server, should not include the algorithm prefix as covered by `host-key-algorithm`.

ssh_auth.host_key_algorithm.description: The host key algorithm, should be `ssh-dss`, `ssh-rsa`, `ecdsa-sha2-nistp256`, `ecdsa-sha2-nistp384`, or `ecdsa-sha2-nistp521`. Required only if `host-key` exists.

ssh_auth.private_key.description: The SSH private key to access the Git repository, required when the URI starts with `git@` or `ssh://`.

ssh_auth.strict_host_key_checking_enable.description: Indicates whether the Config Server instance will fail to start if the host_key does not match. Defaults to `true`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

trace.connection_string.description: The connection string used for Application Insights.

trace.sample_rate.description: The sampling rate of Application Insights Agent. Must be between `0.0` and `100.0`. Defaults to `10.0`.

zone_redundant.description: Whether zone redundancy is enabled for this Spring Cloud Service. Defaults to `false`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Spring Cloud Storage. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud Storage to be created.

spring_cloud_service_id.description: The ID of the Spring Cloud Service where the Spring Cloud Storage should exist. Changing this forces a new Spring Cloud Storage to be created.

storage_account_key.description: The access key of the Azure Storage Account.

storage_account_name.description: The account name of the Azure Storage Account.

location.description: The Azure Region where the SSH Public Key should exist. Changing this forces a new SSH Public Key to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this SSH Public Key. Changing this forces a new SSH Public Key to be created.

public_key.description: SSH public key used to authenticate to a virtual machine through ssh. the provided public key needs to be at least 2048-bit and in ssh-rsa format.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the SSH Public Key should exist. Changing this forces a new SSH Public Key to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the SSH Public Key.

automanage_configuration_id.description: The ID of the Automanage Configuration assigned to the Azure Stack HCI Cluster.

client_id.description: The Client ID of the Azure Active Directory Application which is used by the Azure Stack HCI Cluster

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on the Azure Stack HCI Cluster. Possible value is `SystemAssigned`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Azure Stack HCI Cluster should exist

name.description: The name which should be used for this Azure Stack HCI Cluster

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Azure Stack HCI Cluster should exist

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Azure Stack HCI Cluster.

tenant_id.description: The Tenant ID of the Azure Active Directory which is used by the Azure Stack HCI Cluster

adapter_property_override.jumbo_packet.description: The jumbo frame size of the adapter. This parameter should only be modified based on your OEM guidance. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

adapter_property_override.network_direct.description: The network direct of the adapter. This parameter should only be modified based on your OEM guidance. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.
The network direct technology of the adapter. This parameter should only be modified based on your OEM guidance. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

arc_resource_ids.description: Specifies a list of IDs of Azure ARC machine resource to be part of cluster. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

cluster.azure_service_endpoint.description: Specifies the Azure blob service endpoint, for example, `core.windows.net`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

cluster.cloud_account_name.description: Specifies the Azure Storage account name of the cloud witness for the Azure Stack HCI cluster. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

cluster.name.description: Specifies the name of the cluster. It must be 3-15 characters long and contain only letters, numbers and hyphens. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

cluster.witness_path.description: Specifies the fileshare path of the local witness for the Azure Stack HCI cluster. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

cluster.witness_type.description: Specifies the type of the witness. Possible values are `Cloud`, `FileShare`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

host_network.intent.description: One or more `intent` blocks as defined below. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

host_network.storage_auto_ip_enabled.description: Whether allows users to specify IPs and Mask for Storage NICs when Network ATC is not assigning the IPs for storage automatically. Optional parameter required only for [3 nodes switchless deployments](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure-stack/hci/concepts/physical-network-requirements?tabs=overview%2C23H2reqs#using-switchless). Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

host_network.storage_connectivity_switch.description: Defines how the storage adapters between nodes are connected either switch or switch less. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `false`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

host_network.storage_network.description: One or more `storage_network` blocks as defined below. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

infrastructure_network.dhcp_enabled.description: Whether DHCP is enabled for hosts and cluster IPs. Possible values are `true` and `false`. defaults to `false`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

infrastructure_network.dns_server.description: Specifies a list of IPv4 addresses of the DNS servers in your environment. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

infrastructure_network.gateway.description: Specifies the default gateway that should be used for the provided IP address space. It should be in the format of an IPv4 IP address. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

infrastructure_network.ip_pool.description: One or more `ip_pool` blocks as defined below. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

infrastructure_network.subnet_mask.description: Specifies the subnet mask that matches the provided IP address space. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

intent.adapter.description: Specifies a list of ID of network interfaces used for the network intent. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

intent.adapter_property_override.description: A `adapter_property_override` block as defined above. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

intent.adapter_property_override_enabled.description: Whether to override adapter properties. Possible values are `true` and `false`. defaults to `false`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

intent.name.description: Specifies the name of the intent. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

intent.qos_policy_override.description: A `qos_policy_override` block as defined below. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

intent.qos_policy_override_enabled.description: Whether to override QoS policy. Possible values are `true` and `false`. defaults to `false`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

intent.traffic_type.description: Specifies a list of network traffic types. Possible values are `Compute`, `Storage`, `Management`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

intent.virtual_switch_configuration_over.description: A `virtual_switch_configuration_override` block as defined below. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.
Whether to override virtual switch configuration. Possible values are `true` and `false`. defaults to `false`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

ip_pool.ending_address.description: Specifies starting IP address for the management network. A minimum of six free, contiguous IPv4 addresses (excluding your host IPs) are needed for infrastructure services such as clustering. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

ip_pool.starting_address.description: Specifies ending IP address for the management network. A minimum of six free, contiguous IPv4 addresses (excluding your host IPs) are needed for infrastructure services such as clustering. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

observability.episodic_data_upload_enabl.description: Whether to collect log data to facilitate quicker issue resolution. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

observability.eu_location_enabled.description: Whether to store log and diagnostic data sent to Microsoft in EU or outside of EU. The log and diagnostic data is sent to the appropriate diagnostics servers depending upon where your cluster resides. Setting this to `false` results in all data sent to Microsoft to be stored outside of EU. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

observability.streaming_data_client_enab.description: Whether the telemetry data will be sent to Microsoft. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

optional_service.custom_location.description: Specifies the name of custom location. A custom location will be created after the deployment is completed. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

physical_node.ipv4_address.description: Specifies the IPv4 address assigned to each physical server on your Azure Stack HCI cluster. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

physical_node.name.description: The NETBIOS name of each physical server on your Azure Stack HCI cluster. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

qos_policy_override.bandwidth_percentage.description: Specifies the percentage of the allocated storage traffic bandwidth. This parameter should only be modified based on your OEM guidance. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

qos_policy_override.priority_value8021_a.description: Specifies the Cluster traffic priority. This parameter should only be modified based on your OEM guidance. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

Specifies the Priority Flow Control where Data Center Bridging (DCB) is used. This parameter should only be modified based on your OEM guidance. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.description: One or more `scale_unit` blocks as defined below. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.active_directory_organization.description: Specify the full name of the Active Directory Organizational Unit container object prepared for the deployment, including the domain components. For example:`OU=HCI01,DC=contoso,DC=com`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.bitlocker_boot_volume_enabled.description: Whether to enable BitLocker for boot volume. Possible values are `true` and `false`. When set to `true`, BitLocker XTS_AES 256-bit encryption is enabled for all data-at-rest on the OS volume of your Azure Stack HCI cluster. This setting is TPM-hardware dependent. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.bitlocker_data_volume_enabled.description: Whether to enable BitLocker for data volume. Possible values are `true` and `false`. When set to `true`, BitLocker XTS-AES 256-bit encryption is enabled for all data-at-rest on your Azure Stack HCI cluster shared volumes. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.cluster.description: A `cluster` block as defined above. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.credential_guard_enabled.description: Whether to enable credential guard. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `false`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.domain_fqdn.description: Specifies the FQDN for deploying cluster. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.drift_control_enabled.description: Whether to enable drift control. Possible values are `true` and `false`. When set to `true`, the security baseline is re-applied regularly. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.drtm_protection_enabled.description: Whether to enable DRTM protection. Possible values are `true` and `false`. When set to `true`, Secure Boot is enabled on your Azure HCI cluster. This setting is hardware dependent. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.episodic_data_upload_enabled.description: Whether to collect log data to facilitate quicker issue resolution. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.eu_location_enabled.description: Whether to store data sent to Microsoft in EU. The log and diagnostic data is sent to the appropriate diagnostics servers depending upon where your cluster resides. Setting this to `false` results in all data sent to Microsoft to be stored outside of the EU. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `false`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.host_network.description: A `host_network` block as defined above. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.hvci_protection_enabled.description: Whether to enable HVCI protection. Possible values are `true` and `false`. When set to `true`, Hypervisor-protected Code Integrity is enabled on your Azure HCI cluster. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.infrastructure_network.description: One or more `infrastructure_network` blocks as defined above. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.name_prefix.description: Specifies the name prefix to deploy cluster. It must be 1-8 characters long and contain only letters, numbers and hyphens Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.optional_service.description: A `optional_service` block as defined above. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.physical_node.description: One or more `physical_node` blocks as defined above. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.secrets_location.description: The URI to the Key Vault or secret store. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.side_channel_mitigation_enabl.description: Whether to enable side channel mitigation. Possible values are `true` and `false`. When set to `true`, all side channel mitigations are enabled on your Azure HCI cluster. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.smb_cluster_encryption_enable.description: Whether to enable SMB cluster encryption. Possible values are `true` and `false`. When set to `true`, cluster east-west traffic is encrypted. Defaults to `false`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.smb_signing_enabled.description: Whether to enable SMB signing. Possible values are `true` and `false`. When set to `true`, the SMB default instance requires sign in for the client and server services. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.storage.description: A `storage` block as defined below. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.streaming_data_client_enabled.description: Whether the telemetry data will be sent to Microsoft. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

scale_unit.wdac_enabled.description: Whether to enable WDAC. Possible values are `true` and `false`. When set to `true`, applications and the code that you can run on your Azure Stack HCI cluster are limited. Defaults to `true`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

stack_hci_cluster_id.description: The ID of the Azure Stack HCI cluster. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

storage.configuration_mode.description: The configuration mode of storage. If set to `Express` and your storage is configured as per best practices based on the number of nodes in the cluster. Possible values are `Express`, `InfraOnly` and `KeepStorage`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

storage_network.name.description: The name of the storage network. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

storage_network.network_adapter_name.description: The name of the network adapter. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

storage_network.vlan_id.description: Specifies the ID for the VLAN storage network. This setting is applied to the network interfaces that route the storage and VM migration traffic. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

version.description: The deployment template version. The format must be a set of numbers separated by dots such as `10.0.0.0`. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

virtual_switch_configuration_override.en.description: Specifies the IoV enable status for Virtual Switch. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

virtual_switch_configuration_override.lo.description: Specifies the load balancing algorithm for Virtual Switch. Changing this forces a new Stack HCI Deployment Setting to be created.

arc_setting_id.description: The ID of the Azure Stack HCI Cluster Arc Setting

auto_upgrade_minor_version_enabled.description: Indicates whether the extension should use a newer minor version if one is available at deployment time. Once deployed, however, the extension will not upgrade minor versions unless redeployed, even with this property set to true

automatic_upgrade_enabled.description: Indicates whether the extension should be automatically upgraded by the platform if there is a newer version available. Possible values are `true` and `false`. Defaults to `true`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Azure Stack HCI Extension

protected_settings.description: The json formatted protected settings for the extension.

publisher.description: The name of the extension handler publisher, such as `Microsoft.Azure.Monitor`

settings.description: The json formatted public settings for the extension.

type.description: Specifies the type of the extension. For example `CustomScriptExtension` or `AzureMonitorLinuxAgent`

type_handler_version.description: Specifies the version of the script handler.

dns_servers.description: A list of IPv4 addresses of DNS servers available to VMs deployed in the Logical Networks

ip_pool.end.description: The IPv4 address of the end of the IP address pool

ip_pool.start.description: The IPv4 address of the start of the IP address pool

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Azure Stack HCI Logical Network should exist

route.next_hop_ip_address.description: The IPv4 address of the next hop

subnet.address_prefix.description: The address prefix in CIDR notation

subnet.ip_allocation_method.description: The IP address allocation method for the subnet. Possible values are `Dynamic` and `Static`

subnet.ip_pool.description: One or more `ip_pool` block as defined above

subnet.vlan_id.description: The VLAN ID for the Logical Network

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Azure Stack HCI Logical Network.

virtual_switch_name.description: The name of the virtual switch on the cluster used to associate with the Azure Stack HCI Logical Network. Possible switch names can be retrieved by following this [Azure guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure-stack/hci/manage/create-logical-networks?tabs=azurecli#prerequisites)

custom_location_id.description: The ID of the Custom Location where the Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image should exist

hyperv_generation.description: The hypervisor generation of the Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image. Possible values are `V1` and `V2`. Changing this forces a new Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image to be created.

identifier.description: An `identifier` block as defined below. Changing this forces a new Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image to be created.

identifier.offer.description: The offer of the Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image. Changing this forces a new Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image to be created.

identifier.publisher.description: The publisher of the Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image. Changing this forces a new Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image to be created.

identifier.sku.description: The sku of the Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image. Changing this forces a new Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image to be created.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image should exist. Changing this forces a new Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image to be created.

os_type.description: The Operating System type of the Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image. Possible values are `Windows` and `Linux`. Changing this forces a new Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image to be created.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image should exist. Changing this forces a new Azure Stack HCI Marketplace Gallery Image to be created.
