address_prefixes.description: The address prefixes to use for the subnet.

default_outbound_access_enabled.description: Enable default outbound access to the internet for the subnet. Defaults to `true`.

delegation.description: One or more `delegation` blocks as defined below.

ip_address_pool.id.description: The ID of the Network Manager IP Address Management (IPAM) Pool.

ip_address_pool.number_of_ip_addresses.description: The number of IP addresses to allocated to the subnet. The value must be a string that represents a positive number, e.g., `"100"`.

name.description: The name of the subnet

private_endpoint_network_policies.description: Enable or Disable network policies for the private endpoint on the subnet. Possible values are `Disabled`, `Enabled`, `NetworkSecurityGroupEnabled` and `RouteTableEnabled`. Defaults to `Disabled`.

private_link_service_network_policies_en.description: Enable or Disable network policies for the private link service on the subnet. Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the subnet

service_delegation.actions.description: A list of Actions which should be delegated.

service_delegation.name.description: The name of service to delegate to.

service_endpoint_policy_ids.description: The list of IDs of Service Endpoint Policies to associate with the subnet.

service_endpoints.description: The list of Service endpoints to associate with the subnet.

sharing_scope.description: The sharing scope of the subnet. Possible value is `Tenant`.

virtual_network_name.description: The name of the virtual network to which to attach the subnet

nat_gateway_id.description: The ID of the NAT Gateway which should be associated with the Subnet

subnet_id.description: The ID of the Subnet

network_security_group_id.description: The ID of the Network Security Group which should be associated with the Subnet

subnet_id.description: The ID of the Subnet

route_table_id.description: The ID of the Route Table which should be associated with the Subnet

subnet_id.description: The ID of the Subnet

definition.description.description: The description of this Subnet Service Endpoint Storage Policy Definition.

definition.name.description: The name which should be used for this Subnet Service Endpoint Storage Policy Definition.

definition.service.description: The type of service resources. Valid values are `Microsoft.Storage` or `Global`. When the `service_resources` property contains resource IDs, this property must be `Microsoft.Storage`. When the `service_resources` property contains Aliases, this property must be `Global`. Defaults to `Microsoft.Storage`.

definition.service_resources.description: Specifies a list of resources or aliases that this Subnet Service Endpoint Storage Policy Definition applies to.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Subnet Service Endpoint Storage Policy should exist. Changing this forces a new Subnet Service Endpoint Storage Policy to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Subnet Service Endpoint Storage Policy. Changing this forces a new Subnet Service Endpoint Storage Policy to be created.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Subnet Service Endpoint Storage Policy should exist. Changing this forces a new Subnet Service Endpoint Storage Policy to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Subnet Service Endpoint Storage Policy.

alias.description: The Alias name for the subscription. Terraform will generate a new GUID if this is not supplied. Changing this forces a new Subscription to be created.

billing_scope_id.description: The Azure Billing Scope ID. Can be a Microsoft Customer Account Billing Scope ID, a Microsoft Partner Account Billing Scope ID or an Enrollment Billing Scope ID.

subscription_id.description: The ID of the Subscription. Changing this forces a new Subscription to be created.

subscription_name.description: The Name of the Subscription. This is the Display Name in the portal.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the Subscription.

workload.description: The workload type of the Subscription. Possible values are `Production` (default) and `DevTest`. Changing this forces a new Subscription to be created.

active.description: Is the cost management export active? Default is `true`.

export_data_options.time_frame.description: The time frame for pulling data for the query. If custom, then a specific time period must be provided. Possible values include: `WeekToDate`, `MonthToDate`, `BillingMonthToDate`, `TheLast7Days`, `TheLastMonth`, `TheLastBillingMonth`, `Custom`.

export_data_options.type.description: The type of the query. Possible values are `ActualCost`, `AmortizedCost` and `Usage`.

export_data_storage_location.container_i.description: The Resource Manager ID of the container where exports will be uploaded

export_data_storage_location.root_folder.description: The path of the directory where exports will be uploaded

file_format.description: Format for export. Valid values are `Csv` only. Default is `Csv`.

name.description: Specifies the name of the Cost Management Export

recurrence_period_end_date.description: The date the export will stop capturing information.

recurrence_period_start_date.description: The date the export will start capturing information.

recurrence_type.description: How often the requested information will be exported. Valid values include `Annually`, `Daily`, `Monthly`, `Weekly`.

subscription_id.description: The id of the subscription on which to create an export

accumulated.description: Whether the costs data in the Cost Management View are accumulated over time. Changing this forces a new Cost Management View for a Subscription to be created.

aggregation.column_name.description: The name of the column to aggregate. Changing this forces a new Cost Management View for a Subscription to be created.

aggregation.name.description: The name which should be used for this aggregation. Changing this forces a new Cost Management View for a Subscription to be created.

chart_type.description: Chart type of the main view in Cost Analysis. Possible values are `Area`, `GroupedColumn`, `Line`, `StackedColumn` and `Table`.

dataset.aggregation.description: One or more `aggregation` blocks as defined above.

dataset.granularity.description: The granularity of rows in the report. Possible values are `Daily` and `Monthly`.

dataset.grouping.description: One or more `grouping` blocks as defined below.

dataset.sorting.description: One or more `sorting` blocks as defined below, containing the order by expression to be used in the report

display_name.description: User visible input name of the Cost Management View.

grouping.name.description: The name of the column to group.

grouping.type.description: The type of the column. Possible values are `Dimension` and `TagKey`.

kpi.description: One or more `kpi` blocks as defined below, to show in Cost Analysis UI.

kpi.type.description: KPI type. Possible values are `Budget` and `Forecast`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Cost Management View for a Subscription. Changing this forces a new Cost Management View for a Subscription to be created.

pivot.description: One or more `pivot` blocks as defined below, containing the configuration of 3 sub-views in the Cost Analysis UI. Non table views should have three pivots.

pivot.name.description: The name of the column which should be used for this sub-view in the Cost Analysis UI.

pivot.type.description: The data type to show in this sub-view. Possible values are `Dimension` and `TagKey`.

report_type.description: The type of the report. The only possible value is `Usage`.

sorting.direction.description: Direction of sort. Possible values are `Ascending` and `Descending`.

sorting.name.description: The name of the column to sort.

subscription_id.description: The ID of the Subscription this View is scoped to. Changing this forces a new Cost Management View for a Subscription to be created.

timeframe.description: The time frame for pulling data for the report. Possible values are `Custom`, `MonthToDate`, `WeekToDate` and `YearToDate`.

description.description: A description which should be used for this Policy Assignment.

display_name.description: The Display Name for this Policy Assignment.

enforce.description: Specifies if this Policy should be enforced or not? Defaults to `true`.

identity.identity_ids.description: A list of User Managed Identity IDs which should be assigned to the Policy Definition.

identity.type.description: The Type of Managed Identity which should be added to this Policy Definition. Possible values are `SystemAssigned` or `UserAssigned`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Policy Assignment should exist. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

metadata.description: A JSON mapping of any Metadata for this Policy.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Policy Assignment. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created. Cannot exceed 64 characters in length.

non_compliance_message.description: One or more `non_compliance_message` blocks as defined below.

non_compliance_message.content.description: The non-compliance message text. When assigning policy sets (initiatives), unless `policy_definition_reference_id` is specified then this message will be the default for all policies.

non_compliance_message.policy_definition.description: When assigning policy sets (initiatives), this is the ID of the policy definition that the non-compliance message applies to.

not_scopes.description: Specifies a list of Resource Scopes (for example a Subscription, or a Resource Group) within this Management Group which are excluded from this Policy.

override_selector.in.description: Specify the list of policy reference id values to filter in. Cannot be used with `not_in`.

override_selector.kind.description: Specifies which characteristic will narrow down the set of evaluated resources. Possible values are `resourceLocation`, and `policyDefinitionReferenceId`. Defaults to `policyDefinitionReferenceId`.

override_selector.not_in.description: Specify the list of policy reference id values to filter out. Cannot be used with `in`.

overrides.description: One or more `overrides` blocks as defined below. More detail about `overrides` and `resource_selectors` see [policy assignment structure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/assignment-structure)

overrides.selectors.description: One or more `override_selector` block as defined below.

overrides.value.description: Specifies the value to override the policy property. Possible values for `policyEffect` override listed [policy effects](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/governance/policy/concepts/effects).

parameters.description: A JSON mapping of any Parameters for this Policy.

policy_definition_id.description: The ID of the Policy Definition or Policy Definition Set. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

resource_selector.in.description: The list of allowed values for the specified kind. Cannot be used with `not_in`. Can contain up to 50 values.

resource_selector.kind.description: Specifies which characteristic will narrow down the set of evaluated resources. Possible values are `resourceLocation`, `resourceType` and `resourceWithoutLocation`.

resource_selector.not_in.description: The list of not-allowed values for the specified kind. Cannot be used with `in`. Can contain up to 50 values.

resource_selectors.description: One or more `resource_selectors` blocks as defined below to filter polices by resource properties.

resource_selectors.name.description: Specifies a name for the resource selector.

resource_selectors.selectors.description: One or more `resource_selector` block as defined below.

subscription_id.description: The ID of the Subscription where this Policy Assignment should be created. Changing this forces a new Policy Assignment to be created.

description.description: A description to use for this Policy Exemption.

display_name.description: A friendly display name to use for this Policy Exemption.

exemption_category.description: The category of this policy exemption. Possible values are `Waiver` and `Mitigated`.

expires_on.description: The expiration date and time in UTC ISO 8601 format of this policy exemption.

metadata.description: The metadata for this policy exemption. This is a JSON string representing additional metadata that should be stored with the policy exemption.

name.description: The name of the Policy Exemption

policy_assignment_id.description: The ID of the Policy Assignment to be exempted at the specified Scope

policy_definition_reference_ids.description: The policy definition reference ID list when the associated policy assignment is an assignment of a policy set definition.

subscription_id.description: The Subscription ID where the Policy Exemption should be applied

failure_percentage.description: A number between 0.0 to 1.0 representing the percentage failure threshold. The remediation will fail if the percentage of failed remediation operations (i.e. failed deployments) exceeds this threshold.

location_filters.description: A list of the resource locations that will be remediated.

name.description: The name of the Policy Remediation

parallel_deployments.description: Determines how many resources to remediate at any given time. Can be used to increase or reduce the pace of the remediation. If not provided, the default parallel deployments value is used.

policy_assignment_id.description: The ID of the Policy Assignment that should be remediated.

policy_definition_reference_id.description: The unique ID for the policy definition reference within the policy set definition that should be remediated. Required when the policy assignment being remediated assigns a policy set definition.

resource_count.description: Determines the max number of resources that can be remediated by the remediation job. If not provided, the default resource count is used.

resource_discovery_mode.description: The way that resources to remediate are discovered. Possible values are `ExistingNonCompliant`, `ReEvaluateCompliance`. Defaults to `ExistingNonCompliant`.

subscription_id.description: The Subscription ID at which the Policy Remediation should be applied

debug_level.description: The Debug Level which should be used for this Subscription Template Deployment. Possible values are `none`, `requestContent`, `responseContent` and `requestContent, responseContent`.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Subscription Template Deployment should exist. Changing this forces a new Subscription Template Deployment to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Subscription Template Deployment. Changing this forces a new Subscription Template Deployment to be created.

parameters_content.description: The contents of the ARM Template parameters file - containing a JSON list of parameters.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Subscription Template Deployment.

template_content.description: The contents of the ARM Template which should be deployed into this Subscription.

template_spec_version_id.description: The ID of the Template Spec Version to deploy into the Subscription. Cannot be specified with `template_content`.

end_ip_address.description: The ending IP address to allow through the firewall for this rule.

name.description: The Name of the firewall rule

start_ip_address.description: The starting IP address to allow through the firewall for this rule.

synapse_workspace_id.description: The ID of the Synapse Workspace on which to create the Firewall Rule

compute_type.description: Compute type of the cluster which will execute data flow job. Valid values are `General`, `ComputeOptimized` and `MemoryOptimized`. Defaults to `General`.

core_count.description: Core count of the cluster which will execute data flow job. Valid values are `8`, `16`, `32`, `48`, `80`, `144` and `272`. Defaults to `8`.

description.description: Integration runtime description.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Synapse Azure Integration Runtime should exist. Use `AutoResolve` to create an auto-resolve integration runtime. Changing this forces a new Synapse Azure Integration Runtime to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Synapse Azure Integration Runtime. Changing this forces a new Synapse Azure Integration Runtime to be created.

synapse_workspace_id.description: The Synapse Workspace ID in which to associate the Integration Runtime with. Changing this forces a new Synapse Azure Integration Runtime to be created.

time_to_live_min.description: Time to live (in minutes) setting of the cluster which will execute data flow job. Defaults to `0`.

description.description: Integration runtime description.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Synapse Self-hosted Integration Runtime. Changing this forces a new Synapse Self-hosted Integration Runtime to be created.

synapse_workspace_id.description: The Synapse Workspace ID in which to associate the Integration Runtime with. Changing this forces a new Synapse Self-hosted Integration Runtime to be created.

additional_properties.description: A map of additional properties to associate with the Synapse Linked Service.

annotations.description: List of tags that can be used for describing the Synapse Linked Service.

description.description: The description for the Synapse Linked Service.

integration_runtime.name.description: The integration runtime reference to associate with the Synapse Linked Service.

integration_runtime.parameters.description: A map of parameters to associate with the integration runtime.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Synapse Linked Service. Changing this forces a new Synapse Linked Service to be created.

parameters.description: A map of parameters to associate with the Synapse Linked Service.

synapse_workspace_id.description: The Synapse Workspace ID in which to associate the Linked Service with. Changing this forces a new Synapse Linked Service to be created.

type.description: The type of data stores that will be connected to Synapse. Valid Values include `AmazonMWS`, `AmazonRdsForOracle`, `AmazonRdsForSqlServer`, `AmazonRedshift`, `AmazonS3`, `AzureBatch`

type_properties_json.description: A JSON object that contains the properties of the Synapse Linked Service.

name.description: Specifies the name which should be used for this Managed Private Endpoint

subresource_name.description: Specifies the sub resource name which the Synapse Private Endpoint is able to connect to

synapse_workspace_id.description: The ID of the Synapse Workspace on which to create the Managed Private Endpoint

target_resource_id.description: The ID of the Private Link Enabled Remote Resource which this Synapse Private Endpoint should be connected to

location.description: Specifies the Azure location where the Synapse Private Link Hub exists

name.description: The name which should be used for this Synapse Private Link Hub. Changing this forces a new Synapse Private Link Hub to be created.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Synapse Private Link Hub

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Synapse Private Link Hub.

principal_id.description: The ID of the Principal (User, Group or Service Principal) to assign the Synapse Role Definition to

principal_type.description: The Type of the Principal. One of `User`, `Group` or `ServicePrincipal`

role_name.description: The Role Name of the Synapse Built-In Role. Possible values are `Apache Spark Administrator`, `Synapse Administrator`, `Synapse Artifact Publisher`, `Synapse Artifact User`, `Synapse Compute Operator`, `Synapse Contributor`, `Synapse Credential User`, `Synapse Linked Data Manager`, `Synapse Monitoring Operator`, `Synapse SQL Administrator` and `Synapse User`

synapse_spark_pool_id.description: The Synapse Spark Pool which the Synapse Role Assignment applies to

synapse_workspace_id.description: The Synapse Workspace which the Synapse Role Assignment applies to

auto_pause.delay_in_minutes.description: Number of minutes of idle time before the Spark Pool is automatically paused. Must be between `5` and `10080`.

auto_scale.description: An `auto_scale` block as defined below. Exactly one of `node_count` or `auto_scale` must be specified.

auto_scale.max_node_count.description: The maximum number of nodes the Spark Pool can support. Must be between `3` and `200`.

auto_scale.min_node_count.description: The minimum number of nodes the Spark Pool can support. Must be between `3` and `200`.

cache_size.description: The cache size in the Spark Pool.

compute_isolation_enabled.description: Indicates whether compute isolation is enabled or not. Defaults to `false`.

dynamic_executor_allocation_enabled.description: Indicates whether Dynamic Executor Allocation is enabled or not. Defaults to `false`.

library_requirement.filename.description: The name of the library requirements file.

max_executors.description: The maximum number of executors allocated only when `dynamic_executor_allocation_enabled` set to `true`.

min_executors.description: The minimum number of executors allocated only when `dynamic_executor_allocation_enabled` set to `true`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Synapse Spark Pool. Changing this forces a new Synapse Spark Pool to be created.

node_count.description: The number of nodes in the Spark Pool. Exactly one of `node_count` or `auto_scale` must be specified.

node_size.description: The level of node in the Spark Pool. Possible values are `Small`, `Medium`, `Large`, `None`, `XLarge`, `XXLarge` and `XXXLarge`.

node_size_family.description: The kind of nodes that the Spark Pool provides. Possible values are `HardwareAcceleratedFPGA`, `HardwareAcceleratedGPU`, `MemoryOptimized`, and `None`.

session_level_packages_enabled.description: Indicates whether session level packages are enabled or not. Defaults to `false`.

spark_config.content.description: The contents of a spark configuration.

spark_config.filename.description: The name of the file where the spark configuration `content` will be stored.

spark_events_folder.description: The Spark events folder. Defaults to `/events`.

spark_log_folder.description: The default folder where Spark logs will be written. Defaults to `/logs`.

spark_version.description: The Apache Spark version. Possible values are `3.4` and `3.5`.

synapse_workspace_id.description: The ID of the Synapse Workspace where the Synapse Spark Pool should exist. Changing this forces a new Synapse Spark Pool to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Synapse Spark Pool.

collation.description: The name of the collation to use with this pool, only applicable when `create_mode` is set to `Default`. Azure default is `SQL_LATIN1_GENERAL_CP1_CI_AS`. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool to be created.

create_mode.description: Specifies how to create the SQL Pool. Valid values are: `Default`, `Recovery` or `PointInTimeRestore`. Must be `Default` to create a new database. Defaults to `Default`. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool to be created.

geo_backup_policy_enabled.description: Is geo-backup policy enabled? Defaults to `true`.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Synapse SQL Pool. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool to be created.

recovery_database_id.description: The ID of the Synapse SQL Pool or SQL Database which is to back up, only applicable when `create_mode` is set to `Recovery`. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool to be created.

restore.description: A `restore` block as defined below. Only applicable when `create_mode` is set to `PointInTimeRestore`. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool to be created.

restore.point_in_time.description: Specifies the Snapshot time to restore formatted as an RFC3339 date string. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool to be created.

restore.source_database_id.description: The ID of the Synapse SQL Pool or SQL Database which is to restore. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool to be created.

sku_name.description: Specifies the SKU Name for this Synapse SQL Pool. Possible values are `DW100c`, `DW200c`, `DW300c`, `DW400c`, `DW500c`, `DW1000c`, `DW1500c`, `DW2000c`, `DW2500c`, `DW3000c`, `DW5000c`, `DW6000c`, `DW7500c`, `DW10000c`, `DW15000c` or `DW30000c`.

storage_account_type.description: The storage account type that will be used to store backups for this Synapse SQL Pool. Possible values are `LRS` or `GRS`. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool to be created.

synapse_workspace_id.description: The ID of Synapse Workspace within which this SQL Pool should be created. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Synapse SQL Pool.

log_monitoring_enabled.description: Enable audit events to Azure Monitor? To enable server audit events to Azure Monitor, please enable its master database audit events to Azure Monitor. Defaults to `true`.

retention_in_days.description: The number of days to retain logs for in the storage account. Defaults to `0`.

sql_pool_id.description: The ID of the Synapse SQL pool to set the extended auditing policy

storage_account_access_key.description: The access key to use for the auditing storage account.

storage_account_access_key_is_secondary.description: Is `storage_account_access_key` value the storage's secondary key?

storage_endpoint.description: The blob storage endpoint (e.g. <https://example.blob.core.windows.net>). This blob storage will hold all extended auditing logs.

disabled_alerts.description: Specifies an array of alerts that are disabled. Allowed values are: `Sql_Injection`, `Sql_Injection_Vulnerability`, `Access_Anomaly`, `Data_Exfiltration`, `Unsafe_Action`.

email_account_admins_enabled.description: Boolean flag which specifies if the alert is sent to the account administrators or not. Defaults to `false`.

email_addresses.description: Specifies an array of email addresses to which the alert is sent.

policy_state.description: Specifies the state of the policy, whether it is enabled or disabled or a policy has not been applied yet on the specific SQL pool. Possible values are `Disabled`, `Enabled` and `New`.

retention_days.description: Specifies the number of days to keep in the Threat Detection audit logs. Defaults to `0`.

sql_pool_id.description: Specifies the ID of the Synapse SQL Pool

storage_account_access_key.description: Specifies the identifier key of the Threat Detection audit storage account.

storage_endpoint.description: Specifies the blob storage endpoint (e.g. <https://example.blob.core.windows.net>). This blob storage will hold all Threat Detection audit logs.

recurring_scans.description: The recurring scans settings. The `recurring_scans` block supports fields documented below.

recurring_scans.email_subscription_admin.description: Boolean flag which specifies if the schedule scan notification will be sent to the subscription administrators. Defaults to `true`.

recurring_scans.emails.description: Specifies an array of email addresses to which the scan notification is sent.

recurring_scans.enabled.description: Boolean flag which specifies if recurring scans is enabled or disabled. Defaults to `false`.

sql_pool_security_alert_policy_id.description: The ID of the security alert policy of the Synapse SQL Pool

storage_account_access_key.description: Specifies the identifier key of the storage account for vulnerability assessment scan results. If `storage_container_sas_key` isn't specified, `storage_account_access_key` is required.

storage_container_path.description: A blob storage container path to hold the scan results (e.g. <https://example.blob.core.windows.net/VaScans/>).

storage_container_sas_key.description: A shared access signature (SAS Key) that has write access to the blob container specified in `storage_container_path` parameter. If `storage_account_access_key` isn't specified, `storage_container_sas_key` is required.

baseline.description: One or more `baseline` blocks as defined below.

baseline.result.description: Specifies a list of rule baseline result.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Synapse SQL Pool Vulnerability Assessment Rule Baseline.

rule_name.description: The ID of the vulnerability assessment rule.

sql_pool_vulnerability_assessment_id.description: The ID of the Synapse SQL Pool Vulnerability Assessment. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool Vulnerability Assessment Rule Baseline to be created.

context.description: Specifies the session context value that a request can be classified against.

end_time.description: The workload classifier end time for classification. It's of the `HH:MM` format in UTC time zone.

importance.description: The workload classifier importance. The allowed values are `low`, `below_normal`, `normal`, `above_normal` and `high`.

label.description: Specifies the label value that a request can be classified against.

member_name.description: The workload classifier member name used to classified against.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Synapse SQL Pool Workload Classifier. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool Workload Classifier to be created.

start_time.description: The workload classifier start time for classification. It's of the `HH:MM` format in UTC time zone.

workload_group_id.description: The ID of the Synapse SQL Pool Workload Group. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool Workload Classifier to be created.

importance.description: The workload group importance level. Defaults to `normal`.

max_resource_percent.description: The workload group cap percentage resource.

max_resource_percent_per_request.description: The workload group request maximum grant percentage. Defaults to `3`.

min_resource_percent.description: The workload group minimum percentage resource.

min_resource_percent_per_request.description: The workload group request minimum grant percentage.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Synapse SQL Pool Workload Group. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool Workload Group to be created.

query_execution_timeout_in_seconds.description: The workload group query execution timeout.

sql_pool_id.description: The ID of the Synapse SQL Pool. Changing this forces a new Synapse SQL Pool Workload Group to be created.

azure_devops_repo.account_name.description: Specifies the Azure DevOps account name.

azure_devops_repo.branch_name.description: Specifies the collaboration branch of the repository to get code from.

azure_devops_repo.project_name.description: Specifies the name of the Azure DevOps project.

azure_devops_repo.repository_name.description: Specifies the name of the git repository.

azure_devops_repo.root_folder.description: Specifies the root folder within the repository. Set to `/` for the top level.

azure_devops_repo.tenant_id.description: the ID of the tenant for the Azure DevOps account.

azuread_authentication_only.description: Is Azure Active Directory Authentication the only way to authenticate with resources inside this synapse Workspace. Defaults to `false`.

compute_subnet_id.description: Subnet ID used for computes in workspace

customer_managed_key.key_name.description: An identifier for the key. Name needs to match the name of the key used with the `azurerm_synapse_workspace_key` resource. Defaults to "cmk" if not specified.

customer_managed_key.key_versionless_id.description: The Azure Key Vault Key Versionless ID to be used as the Customer Managed Key (CMK) for double encryption (e.g. `https://example-keyvault.vault.azure.net/type/cmk/`).

customer_managed_key.user_assigned_ident.description: The User Assigned Identity ID to be used for accessing the Customer Managed Key for encryption.

data_exfiltration_protection_enabled.description: Is data exfiltration protection enabled in this workspace? If set to `true`, `managed_virtual_network_enabled` must also be set to `true`

github_repo.branch_name.description: Specifies the collaboration branch of the repository to get code from.

github_repo.git_url.description: Specifies the GitHub Enterprise host name. For example: <https://github.mydomain.com>.

github_repo.repository_name.description: Specifies the name of the git repository.

github_repo.root_folder.description: Specifies the root folder within the repository. Set to `/` for the top level.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Synapse Workspace.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be associated with this Synapse Workspace. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned` and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

linking_allowed_for_aad_tenant_ids.description: Allowed AAD Tenant Ids For Linking.

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the synapse Workspace should exist

managed_virtual_network_enabled.description: Is Virtual Network enabled for all computes in this workspace?

name.description: Specifies the name which should be used for this synapse Workspace

public_network_access_enabled.description: Whether public network access is allowed for the Cognitive Account. Defaults to `true`.

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the Resource Group where the synapse Workspace should exist

sql_administrator_login.description: Specifies The login name of the SQL administrator

sql_administrator_login_password.description: The Password associated with the `sql_administrator_login` for the SQL administrator. If this is not provided `customer_managed_key` must be provided.

sql_identity_control_enabled.description: Are pipelines (running as workspace's system assigned identity) allowed to access SQL pools?

storage_data_lake_gen2_filesystem_id.description: Specifies the ID of storage data lake gen2 filesystem resource

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Synapse Workspace.

login.description: The login name of the Azure AD Administrator of this Synapse Workspace.

object_id.description: The object id of the Azure AD Administrator of this Synapse Workspace.

synapse_workspace_id.description: The ID of the Synapse Workspace where the Azure AD Administrator should be configured.

tenant_id.description: The tenant id of the Azure AD Administrator of this Synapse Workspace.

log_monitoring_enabled.description: Enable audit events to Azure Monitor? To enable server audit events to Azure Monitor, please enable its master database audit events to Azure Monitor. Defaults to `true`.

retention_in_days.description: The number of days to retain logs for in the storage account. Defaults to `0`.

storage_account_access_key.description: The access key to use for the auditing storage account.

storage_account_access_key_is_secondary.description: Is `storage_account_access_key` value the storage's secondary key?

storage_endpoint.description: The blob storage endpoint (e.g. <https://example.blob.core.windows.net>). This blob storage will hold all extended auditing logs.

synapse_workspace_id.description: The ID of the Synapse workspace to set the extended auditing policy

active.description: Specifies if the workspace should be encrypted with this key.

customer_managed_key_name.description: Specifies the name of the workspace key. Should match the name of the key in the synapse workspace.

customer_managed_key_versionless_id.description: The Azure Key Vault Key Versionless ID to be used as the Customer Managed Key (CMK) for double encryption

synapse_workspace_id.description: The ID of the Synapse Workspace where the encryption key should be configured.

disabled_alerts.description: Specifies an array of alerts that are disabled. Allowed values are: `Sql_Injection`, `Sql_Injection_Vulnerability`, `Access_Anomaly`, `Data_Exfiltration`, `Unsafe_Action`.

email_account_admins_enabled.description: Boolean flag which specifies if the alert is sent to the account administrators or not. Defaults to `false`.

email_addresses.description: Specifies an array of email addresses to which the alert is sent.

policy_state.description: Specifies the state of the policy, whether it is enabled or disabled or a policy has not been applied yet on the specific workspace. Possible values are `Disabled`, `Enabled` and `New`.

retention_days.description: Specifies the number of days to keep in the Threat Detection audit logs. Defaults to `0`.

storage_account_access_key.description: Specifies the identifier key of the Threat Detection audit storage account.

storage_endpoint.description: Specifies the blob storage endpoint (e.g. <https://example.blob.core.windows.net>). This blob storage will hold all Threat Detection audit logs.

synapse_workspace_id.description: Specifies the ID of the Synapse Workspace

login.description: The login name of the Azure AD SQL Administrator of this Synapse Workspace.

object_id.description: The object id of the Azure AD SQL Administrator of this Synapse Workspace.

synapse_workspace_id.description: The ID of the Synapse Workspace where the Azure AD SQL Administrator should be configured.

tenant_id.description: The tenant id of the Azure AD SQL Administrator of this Synapse Workspace.

recurring_scans.description: The recurring scans settings. The `recurring_scans` block supports fields documented below.

recurring_scans.email_subscription_admin.description: Boolean flag which specifies if the schedule scan notification will be sent to the subscription administrators. Defaults to `true`.

recurring_scans.emails.description: Specifies an array of email addresses to which the scan notification is sent.

recurring_scans.enabled.description: Boolean flag which specifies if recurring scans is enabled or disabled. Defaults to `false`.

storage_account_access_key.description: Specifies the identifier key of the storage account for vulnerability assessment scan results. If `storage_container_sas_key` isn't specified, `storage_account_access_key` is required.

storage_container_path.description: A blob storage container path to hold the scan results (e.g. <https://example.blob.core.windows.net/VaScans/>).

storage_container_sas_key.description: A shared access signature (SAS Key) that has write access to the blob container specified in `storage_container_path` parameter. If `storage_account_access_key` isn't specified, `storage_container_sas_key` is required.

workspace_security_alert_policy_id.description: The ID of the security alert policy of the Synapse Workspace

custom_location_id.description: The ID of the Custom Location for the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Availability Set

location.description: The Azure Region where the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Availability Set should exist

name.description: The name of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Availability Set

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the System Center Virtual Machine Availability Set should exist

system_center_virtual_machine_manager_se.description: The ID of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Server

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Availability Set.

custom_location_id.description: The ID of the Custom Location for the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Cloud

location.description: The Azure Region where the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Cloud should exist

name.description: The name of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Cloud

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the System Center Virtual Machine Cloud should exist

system_center_virtual_machine_manager_se.description: The ID of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Server Inventory Item

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Cloud.

custom_location_id.description: The ID of the Custom Location for the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Server

fqdn.description: The FQDN of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Server

location.description: The Azure Region where the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Server should exist

name.description: The name of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Server

password.description: The password that is used to connect to the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Server

port.description: The port on which the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Server is listening. Possible values are between `1` and `65535`

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the System Center Virtual Machine Manager should exist

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Server.

username.description: The username that is used to connect to the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Server

custom_location_id.description: The ID of the Custom Location for the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Instance

hardware.cpu_count.description: The number of vCPUs for the Virtual Machine. Possible values are between `1` and `64`.

hardware.dynamic_memory_max_in_mb.description: The max dynamic memory for the Virtual Machine. Possible values are between `32` and `1048576`.

hardware.dynamic_memory_min_in_mb.description: The min dynamic memory for the Virtual Machine. Possible values are between `32` and `1048576`.

hardware.limit_cpu_for_migration_enabled.description: Whether processor compatibility mode for live migration of Virtual Machines is enabled.

hardware.memory_in_mb.description: The size of a Virtual Machine's memory. Possible values are between `32` and `1048576`.

infrastructure.checkpoint_type.description: The type of checkpoint supported for the Virtual Machine. Possible values are `Disabled`, `Production`, `ProductionOnly` and `Standard`.

infrastructure.system_center_virtual_mac.description: The ID of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Cloud resource to use for deploying the Virtual Machine
The ID of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Inventory Item for System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Instance
The ID of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Template to use for deploying the Virtual Machine
The ID of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine

network_interface.ipv4_address_type.description: The IPv4 address type. Possible values are `Dynamic` and `Static`.

network_interface.ipv6_address_type.description: The IPv6 address type. Possible values are `Dynamic` and `Static`.

network_interface.mac_address_type.description: The MAC address type. Possible values are `Dynamic` and `Static`.

network_interface.name.description: The name of the Virtual Network in System Center Virtual Machine Manager Server that the Network Interface is connected to.

network_interface.virtual_network_id.description: The ID of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Network to connect the Network Interface.

operating_system.admin_password.description: The admin password of the Virtual Machine

operating_system.computer_name.description: The computer name of the Virtual Machine

scoped_resource_id.description: The ID of the Hybrid Compute Machine where this System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Instance is stored

storage_disk.bus.description: The disk bus. Possible values are between `0` and `3`.

storage_disk.bus_type.description: The disk bus type. Possible values are `IDE` and `SCSI`.

storage_disk.lun.description: The disk lun. Possible values are between `0` and `63`.

storage_disk.storage_qos_policy_name.description: The name of the Storage QoS policy.

storage_disk.template_disk_id.description: The disk ID in the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Template

storage_disk.vhd_type.description: The disk vhd type. Possible values are `Dynamic` and `Fixed`.

system_center_virtual_machine_manager_av.description: A list of IDs of System Center Virtual Machine Manager Availability Set.

password.description: The password that is used to connect to the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Instance Guest Agent

provisioning_action.description: The provisioning action that is used to define the different types of operations for the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Instance Guest Agent. Possible values are `install`, `repair` and `uninstall`. Defaults to `install`

scoped_resource_id.description: The ID of the Hybrid Compute Machine where this System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Instance Guest Agent is stored

username.description: The username that is used to connect to the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Instance Guest Agent

custom_location_id.description: The ID of the Custom Location for the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Template

location.description: The Azure Region where the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Template should exist

name.description: The name of the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Template

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the System Center Virtual Machine Manager Virtual Machine Template should exist

always_serve_enabled.description: If Always Serve is enabled, probing for endpoint health will be disabled and endpoints will be included in the traffic routing method. Defaults to `false`.

minimum_child_endpoints.description: This argument specifies the minimum number of endpoints that must be ‘online’ in the child profile in order for the parent profile to direct traffic to any of the endpoints in that child profile. This value must be larger than `0`.

minimum_required_child_endpoints_ipv4.description: This argument specifies the minimum number of IPv4 (DNS record type A) endpoints that must be ‘online’ in the child profile in order for the parent profile to direct traffic to any of the endpoints in that child profile. This argument only applies to Endpoints of type `nestedEndpoints` and

minimum_required_child_endpoints_ipv6.description: This argument specifies the minimum number of IPv6 (DNS record type AAAA) endpoints that must be ‘online’ in the child profile in order for the parent profile to direct traffic to any of the endpoints in that child profile. This argument only applies to Endpoints of type `nestedEndpoints` and

name.description: The name of the External Endpoint

priority.description: Specifies the priority of this Endpoint, this must be specified for Profiles using the `Priority` traffic routing method. Supports values between 1 and 1000, with no Endpoints sharing the same value. If omitted the value will be computed in order of creation.

profile_id.description: The ID of the Traffic Manager Profile that this External Endpoint should be created within

subnet.description: One or more `subnet` blocks as defined below

subnet.first.description: The first IP Address in this subnet.

subnet.last.description: The last IP Address in this subnet.

subnet.scope.description: The block size (number of leading bits in the subnet mask).

target_resource_id.description: The resource id of an Azure resource to target.

weight.description: Specifies how much traffic should be distributed to this endpoint, this must be specified for Profiles using the Weighted traffic routing method. Valid values are between `1` and `1000`. Defaults to `1`.

custom_header.name.description: The name of the custom header.

custom_header.value.description: The value of custom header. Applicable for HTTP and HTTPS protocol.

dns_config.description: This block specifies the DNS configuration of the Profile. One `dns_config` block as defined below.

dns_config.relative_name.description: The relative domain name, this is combined with the domain name used by Traffic Manager to form the FQDN which is exported as documented below

dns_config.ttl.description: The TTL value of the Profile used by Local DNS resolvers and clients.

max_return.description: The amount of endpoints to return for DNS queries to this Profile. Possible values range from `1` to `8`.

monitor_config.description: This block specifies the Endpoint monitoring configuration for the Profile. One `monitor_config` block as defined below.

monitor_config.custom_header.description: One or more `custom_header` blocks as defined below.

monitor_config.expected_status_code_rang.description: A list of status code ranges in the format of `100-101`.

monitor_config.interval_in_seconds.description: The interval used to check the endpoint health from a Traffic Manager probing agent. You can specify two values here: `30` (normal probing) and `10` (fast probing). The default value is `30`.

monitor_config.path.description: The path used by the monitoring checks. Required when `protocol` is set to `HTTP` or `HTTPS` - cannot be set when `protocol` is set to `TCP`.

monitor_config.port.description: The port number used by the monitoring checks.

monitor_config.protocol.description: The protocol used by the monitoring checks, supported values are `HTTP`, `HTTPS` and `TCP`.

monitor_config.timeout_in_seconds.description: The amount of time the Traffic Manager probing agent should wait before considering that check a failure when a health check probe is sent to the endpoint. If `interval_in_seconds` is set to `30`, then `timeout_in_seconds` can be between `5` and `10`. The default value is `10`. If `interval_in_seconds` is set to `10`, then valid values are between `5` and `9` and `timeout_in_seconds` is required.

monitor_config.tolerated_number_of_failu.description: The number of failures a Traffic Manager probing agent tolerates before marking that endpoint as unhealthy. Valid values are between `0` and `9`. The default value is `3`

name.description: The name of the Traffic Manager profile

profile_status.description: The status of the profile, can be set to either `Enabled` or `Disabled`. Defaults to `Enabled`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Traffic Manager profile

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

traffic_routing_method.description: Specifies the algorithm used to route traffic. Possible values are `Geographic`, `Weighted`, `Performance`, `Priority`, `Subnet` and `MultiValue`.

traffic_view_enabled.description: Indicates whether Traffic View is enabled for the Traffic Manager profile.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Trusted Signing Account. Changing this forces a new Trusted Signing Account to be created.

sku_name.description: The sku name of this Trusted Signing Account. Possible values are `Basic` and `Premium`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Trusted Signing Account.

isolation_scope.description: The isolation scope for the User Assigned Identity. The only possible value is `Regional`.

name.description: Specifies the name of this User Assigned Identity. Changing this forces a new User Assigned Identity to be created.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the User Assigned Identity.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of user managed identity ids to be assigned. Required if `type` is `UserAssigned`.

identity.type.description: Specifies the identity type of the Video Indexer Account. Possible values are `SystemAssigned` (where Azure will generate a Service Principal for you), `UserAssigned` where you can specify the Service Principal IDs in the `identity_ids` field, and `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` which assigns both a system managed identity as well as the specified user assigned identities.

location.description: The Azure location where the Video Indexer Account exists

name.description: The name of the Video Indexer Account. Changing the name

public_network_access.description: The public network access for the Video Indexer Account. Possible values are `Enabled` and `Disabled`. Defaults to `Enabled`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group that the Video Indexer Account will be associated with. Changing the name

storage.storage_account_id.description: The ID of the storage account to be associated with the Video Indexer Account. Changing this forces a new Video Indexer Account to be created.

storage.user_assigned_identity_id.description: The reference to the user assigned identity to use to access the Storage Account.

application_group_id.description: Resource ID for a Virtual Desktop Application Group to associate with the Virtual Desktop Application

command_line_argument_policy.description: Specifies whether this published application can be launched with command line arguments provided by the client, command line arguments specified at publish time, or no command line arguments at all. Possible values include: `DoNotAllow`, `Allow`, `Require`.

command_line_arguments.description: Command Line Arguments for Virtual Desktop Application.

description.description: Option to set a description for the Virtual Desktop Application.

friendly_name.description: Option to set a friendly name for the Virtual Desktop Application.

icon_index.description: The index of the icon you wish to use.

icon_path.description: Specifies the path for an icon which will be used for this Virtual Desktop Application.

name.description: The name of the Virtual Desktop Application. Changing the name

path.description: The file path location of the app on the Virtual Desktop OS.

show_in_portal.description: Specifies whether to show the RemoteApp program in the RD Web Access server.

default_desktop_display_name.description: Option to set the display name for the default sessionDesktop desktop when `type` is set to `Desktop`. A value here is mandatory for connections to the desktop using the Windows 365 portal. Without it the connection will hang at 'Loading Client'.

description.description: Option to set a description for the Virtual Desktop Application Group.

friendly_name.description: Option to set a friendly name for the Virtual Desktop Application Group.

host_pool_id.description: Resource ID for a Virtual Desktop Host Pool to associate with the Virtual Desktop Application Group. Changing the name

location.description: The location/region where the Virtual Desktop Application Group is located

name.description: The name of the Virtual Desktop Application Group. Changing the name

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Virtual Desktop Application Group

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

type.description: Type of Virtual Desktop Application Group. Valid options are `RemoteApp` or `Desktop` application groups

custom_rdp_properties.description: A valid custom RDP properties string for the Virtual Desktop Host Pool, available properties can be [found in this article](https://docs.microsoft.com/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/clients/rdp-files).

description.description: A description for the Virtual Desktop Host Pool.

friendly_name.description: A friendly name for the Virtual Desktop Host Pool.

load_balancer_type.description: `BreadthFirst` load balancing distributes new user sessions across all available session hosts in the host pool. Possible values are `BreadthFirst`, `DepthFirst` and `Persistent`.

location.description: The location/region where the Virtual Desktop Host Pool is located

maximum_sessions_allowed.description: A valid integer value from 0 to 999999 for the maximum number of users that have concurrent sessions on a session host.

name.description: The name of the Virtual Desktop Host Pool

personal_desktop_assignment_type.description: `Automatic` assignment – The service will select an available host and assign it to an user. Possible values are `Automatic` and `Direct`. `Direct` Assignment – Admin selects a specific host to assign to an user

preferred_app_group_type.description: Option to specify the preferred Application Group type for the Virtual Desktop Host Pool. Valid options are `None`, `Desktop` or `RailApplications`. Default is `Desktop`.

public_network_access.description: Whether public network access is allowed for the Virtual Desktop Host Pool. Possible values are `Enabled`, `Disabled`, `EnabledForClientsOnly` and `EnabledForSessionHostsOnly`. Defaults to `Enabled`.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the resource group in which to create the Virtual Desktop Host Pool

schedule.day_of_week.description: The day of the week on which agent updates should be performed. Possible values are `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday`, `Saturday`, and `Sunday`

schedule.hour_of_day.description: The hour of day the update window should start. The update is a 2 hour period following the hour provided. The value should be provided as a number between 0 and 23, with 0 being midnight and 23 being 11pm. A leading zero should not be used.

scheduled_agent_updates.description: A `scheduled_agent_updates` block as defined below. This enables control of when Agent Updates will be applied to Session Hosts.

scheduled_agent_updates.enabled.description: Enables or disables scheduled updates of the AVD agent components (RDAgent, Geneva Monitoring agent, and side-by-side stack) on session hosts. If this is enabled then up to two `schedule` blocks must be defined. Default is `false`.

scheduled_agent_updates.schedule.description: A `schedule` block as defined below. A maximum of two blocks can be added.

scheduled_agent_updates.timezone.description: Specifies the time zone in which the agent update schedule will apply, [the possible values are defined here](https://jackstromberg.com/2017/01/list-of-time-zones-consumed-by-azure/). If `use_session_host_timezone` is enabled then it will override this setting. Default is `UTC`

scheduled_agent_updates.use_session_host.description: Specifies whether scheduled agent updates should be applied based on the timezone of the affected session host. If configured then this setting overrides `timezone`. Default is `false`.

start_vm_on_connect.description: Enables or disables the Start VM on Connection Feature. Defaults to `false`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

type.description: The type of the Virtual Desktop Host Pool. Valid options are `Personal` or `Pooled`. Changing the type

validate_environment.description: Allows you to test service changes before they are deployed to production. Defaults to `false`.

vm_template.description: A VM template for session hosts configuration within hostpool. This is a JSON string.

expiration_date.description: A valid `RFC3339Time` for the expiration of the token..

hostpool_id.description: The ID of the Virtual Desktop Host Pool to link the Registration Info to. Changing this forces a new Registration Info resource to be created. Only a single virtual_desktop_host_pool_registration_info resource should be associated with a given hostpool. Assigning multiple resources will produce inconsistent results.

description.description: A description of the Scaling Plan.

exclusion_tag.description: The name of the tag associated with the VMs you want to exclude from autoscaling.

friendly_name.description: Friendly name of the Scaling Plan.

host_pool.description: One or more `host_pool` blocks as defined below.

host_pool.hostpool_id.description: The ID of the HostPool to assign the Scaling Plan to.

host_pool.scaling_plan_enabled.description: Specifies if the scaling plan is enabled or disabled for the HostPool.

location.description: The Azure Region where the Virtual Desktop Scaling Plan should exist. Changing this forces a new Virtual Desktop Scaling Plan to be created.

name.description: The name which should be used for this Virtual Desktop Scaling Plan . Changing this forces a new Virtual Desktop Scaling Plan to be created.

resource_group_name.description: The name of the Resource Group where the Virtual Desktop Scaling Plan should exist. Changing this forces a new Virtual Desktop Scaling Plan to be created.

schedule.description: One or more `schedule` blocks as defined below.

schedule.days_of_week.description: A list of Days of the Week on which this schedule will be used. Possible values are `Monday`, `Tuesday`, `Wednesday`, `Thursday`, `Friday`, `Saturday`, and `Sunday`

schedule.off_peak_load_balancing_algorit.description: The load Balancing Algorithm to use during Off-Peak Hours. Possible values are `DepthFirst` and `BreadthFirst`.

schedule.off_peak_start_time.description: The time at which Off-Peak scaling will begin. This is also the end-time for the Ramp-Down period. The time must be specified in "HH:MM" format.

schedule.peak_load_balancing_algorithm.description: The load Balancing Algorithm to use during Peak Hours. Possible values are `DepthFirst` and `BreadthFirst`.

schedule.peak_start_time.description: The time at which Peak scaling will begin. This is also the end-time for the Ramp-Up period. The time must be specified in "HH:MM" format.

schedule.ramp_down_capacity_threshold_pe.description: This is the value in percentage of used host pool capacity that will be considered to evaluate whether to turn on/off virtual machines during the ramp-down and off-peak hours. For example, if capacity threshold is specified as 60% and your total host pool capacity is 100 sessions, autoscale will turn on additional session hosts once the host pool exceeds a load of 60 sessions.

schedule.ramp_down_force_logoff_users.description: Whether users will be forced to log-off session hosts once the `ramp_down_wait_time_minutes` value has been exceeded during the Ramp-Down period. Possible values are `true` and `false`.

schedule.ramp_down_load_balancing_algori.description: The load Balancing Algorithm to use during the Ramp-Down period. Possible values are `DepthFirst` and `BreadthFirst`.

schedule.ramp_down_minimum_hosts_percent.description: The minimum percentage of session host virtual machines that you would like to get to for ramp-down and off-peak hours. For example, if Minimum percentage of hosts is specified as 10% and total number of session hosts in your host pool is 10, autoscale will ensure a minimum of 1 session host is available to take user connections.

schedule.ramp_down_notification_message.description: The notification message to send to users during Ramp-Down period when they are required to log-off.

schedule.ramp_down_start_time.description: The time at which Ramp-Down scaling will begin. This is also the end-time for the Ramp-Up period. The time must be specified in "HH:MM" format.

schedule.ramp_down_stop_hosts_when.description: Controls Session Host shutdown behaviour during Ramp-Down period. Session Hosts can either be shutdown when all sessions on the Session Host have ended, or when there are no Active sessions left on the Session Host. Possible values are `ZeroSessions` and `ZeroActiveSessions`.

schedule.ramp_down_wait_time_minutes.description: The number of minutes during Ramp-Down period that autoscale will wait after setting the session host VMs to drain mode, notifying any currently signed in users to save their work before forcing the users to logoff. Once all user sessions on the session host VM have been logged off, Autoscale will shut down the VM.

schedule.ramp_up_capacity_threshold_perc.description: This is the value of percentage of used host pool capacity that will be considered to evaluate whether to turn on/off virtual machines during the ramp-up and peak hours. For example, if capacity threshold is specified as `60%` and your total host pool capacity is `100` sessions, autoscale will turn on additional session hosts once the host pool exceeds a load of `60` sessions.

schedule.ramp_up_load_balancing_algorith.description: The load Balancing Algorithm to use during the Ramp-Up period. Possible values are `DepthFirst` and `BreadthFirst`.

schedule.ramp_up_minimum_hosts_percent.description: Specifies the minimum percentage of session host virtual machines to start during ramp-up for peak hours. For example, if Minimum percentage of hosts is specified as `10%` and total number of session hosts in your host pool is `10`, autoscale will ensure a minimum of `1` session host is available to take user connections.

schedule.ramp_up_start_time.description: The time at which Ramp-Up scaling will begin. This is also the end-time for the Ramp-Up period. The time must be specified in "HH:MM" format.

tags.description: A mapping of tags which should be assigned to the Virtual Desktop Scaling Plan .

time_zone.description: Specifies the Time Zone which should be used by the Scaling Plan for time based events, [the possible values are defined here](https://jackstromberg.com/2017/01/list-of-time-zones-consumed-by-azure/).

enabled.description: Should the Scaling Plan be enabled on this Host Pool.

hub_routing_preference.description: The hub routing preference. Possible values are `ExpressRoute`, `ASPath` and `VpnGateway`. Defaults to `ExpressRoute`.

virtual_router_auto_scale_min_capacity.description: Minimum instance capacity for the scaling configuration of the Virtual Hub Router. Defaults to `2`.

propagated_route_table.labels.description: The list of labels to assign to this route table.

routing.associated_route_table_id.description: The ID of the route table associated with this Virtual Hub connection.

routing.outbound_route_map_id.description: The resource ID of the Route Map associated with this Routing Configuration for outbound advertised routes.

routing.static_vnet_local_route_override.description: The static VNet local route override criteria that is used to determine whether NVA in spoke VNet is bypassed for traffic with destination in spoke VNet. Possible values are `Contains` and `Equal`. Defaults to `Contains`

routing.static_vnet_propagate_static_rou.description: Whether the static routes should be propagated to the Virtual Hub. Defaults to `true`.

static_vnet_route.address_prefixes.description: A list of CIDR Ranges which should be used as Address Prefixes.

virtual_hub_id.description: The ID of the Virtual Hub within which this Security Partner Provider should be created

additional_capabilities.ultra_ssd_enable.description: Should Ultra SSD disk be enabled for this Virtual Machine?

additional_unattend_config.component.description: Specifies the name of the component to configure with the added content. The only allowable value is `Microsoft-Windows-Shell-Setup`.

additional_unattend_config.content.description: Specifies the base-64 encoded XML formatted content that is added to the unattend.xml file for the specified path and component.

additional_unattend_config.pass.description: Specifies the name of the pass that the content applies to. The only allowable value is `oobeSystem`.

additional_unattend_config.setting_name.description: Specifies the name of the setting to which the content applies. Possible values are: `FirstLogonCommands` and `AutoLogon`.

availability_set_id.description: The ID of the Availability Set in which the Virtual Machine should exist

boot_diagnostics.enabled.description: Should Boot Diagnostics be enabled for this Virtual Machine?

boot_diagnostics.storage_uri.description: The Storage Account's Blob Endpoint which should hold the virtual machine's diagnostic files.

delete_data_disks_on_termination.description: Should the Data Disks (either the Managed Disks / VHD Blobs) be deleted when the Virtual Machine is destroyed? Defaults to `false`.

delete_os_disk_on_termination.description: Should the OS Disk (either the Managed Disk / VHD Blob) be deleted when the Virtual Machine is destroyed? Defaults to `false`.

identity.identity_ids.description: Specifies a list of User Assigned Managed Identity IDs to be assigned to this Virtual Machine.

identity.type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Service Identity that should be configured on this Virtual Machine. Possible values are `SystemAssigned`, `UserAssigned`, `SystemAssigned, UserAssigned` (to enable both).

license_type.description: Specifies the BYOL Type for this Virtual Machine. This is only applicable to Windows Virtual Machines. Possible values are `Windows_Client` and `Windows_Server`.

location.description: Specifies the Azure Region where the Virtual Machine exists

name.description: Specifies the name of the Virtual Machine

network_interface_ids.description: A list of Network Interface IDs which should be associated with the Virtual Machine.

os_profile.description: An `os_profile` block as defined below. Required when `create_option` in the `storage_os_disk` block is set to `FromImage`.

os_profile.admin_password.description: (Optional for Windows, Optional for Linux) The password associated with the local administrator account.

os_profile.admin_username.description: Specifies the name of the local administrator account.

os_profile.computer_name.description: Specifies the name of the Virtual Machine

os_profile.custom_data.description: Specifies custom data to supply to the machine. On Linux-based systems, this can be used as a cloud-init script. On other systems, this will be copied as a file on disk. Internally, Terraform will base64 encode this value before sending it to the API. The maximum length of the binary array is 65535 bytes

os_profile_linux_config.description: (Required, when a Linux machine) An `os_profile_linux_config` block as defined below.

os_profile_linux_config.disable_password.description: Specifies whether password authentication should be disabled. If set to `false`, an `admin_password` must be specified.

os_profile_linux_config.ssh_keys.description: One or more `ssh_keys` blocks as defined below. This field is required if `disable_password_authentication` is set to `true`.

os_profile_secrets.description: One or more `os_profile_secrets` blocks as defined below.

os_profile_secrets.source_vault_id.description: Specifies the ID of the Key Vault to use.

os_profile_secrets.vault_certificates.description: One or more `vault_certificates` blocks as defined below.

os_profile_windows_config.description: (Required, when a Windows machine) An `os_profile_windows_config` block as defined below.

os_profile_windows_config.enable_automat.description: Are automatic updates enabled on this Virtual Machine? Defaults to `false`.

os_profile_windows_config.provision_vm_a.description: Should the Azure Virtual Machine Guest Agent be installed on this Virtual Machine? Defaults to `false`.

os_profile_windows_config.timezone.description: Specifies the time zone of the virtual machine, [the possible values are defined here](https://jackstromberg.com/2017/01/list-of-time-zones-consumed-by-azure/)

os_profile_windows_config.winrm.description: One or more `winrm` blocks as defined below.

plan.name.description: Specifies the name of the image from the marketplace.

plan.product.description: Specifies the product of the image from the marketplace.

plan.publisher.description: Specifies the publisher of the image.

primary_network_interface_id.description: The ID of the Network Interface (which must be attached to the Virtual Machine) which should be the Primary Network Interface for this Virtual Machine.

proximity_placement_group_id.description: The ID of the Proximity Placement Group to which this Virtual Machine should be assigned

resource_group_name.description: Specifies the name of the Resource Group in which the Virtual Machine should exist

ssh_keys.key_data.description: The Public SSH Key which should be written to the `path` defined above.

ssh_keys.path.description: The path of the destination file on the virtual machine

storage_data_disk.description: One or more `storage_data_disk` blocks as defined below.

storage_data_disk.caching.description: Specifies the caching requirements for the Data Disk. Possible values include `None`, `ReadOnly` and `ReadWrite`.

storage_data_disk.create_option.description: Specifies how the data disk should be created. Possible values are `Attach`, `FromImage` and `Empty`.

storage_data_disk.disk_size_gb.description: Specifies the size of the data disk in gigabytes.

storage_data_disk.lun.description: Specifies the logical unit number of the data disk. This needs to be unique within all the Data Disks on the Virtual Machine.

storage_data_disk.managed_disk_id.description: Specifies the ID of an Existing Managed Disk which should be attached to this Virtual Machine. When this field is set `create_option` must be set to `Attach`.

storage_data_disk.managed_disk_type.description: Specifies the type of managed disk to create. Possible values are either `Standard_LRS`, `StandardSSD_LRS`, `Premium_LRS` or `UltraSSD_LRS`.

storage_data_disk.name.description: The name of the Data Disk.

storage_data_disk.vhd_uri.description: Specifies the URI of the VHD file backing this Unmanaged Data Disk.

storage_data_disk.write_accelerator_enab.description: Specifies if Write Accelerator is enabled on the disk. This can only be enabled on `Premium_LRS` managed disks with no caching and [M-Series VMs](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/workloads/sap/how-to-enable-write-accelerator). Defaults to `false`.

storage_image_reference.id.description: Specifies the ID of the Custom Image which the Virtual Machine should be created from

storage_image_reference.offer.description: Specifies the offer of the image used to create the virtual machine

storage_image_reference.publisher.description: Specifies the publisher of the image used to create the virtual machine

storage_image_reference.sku.description: Specifies the SKU of the image used to create the virtual machine

storage_image_reference.version.description: Specifies the version of the image used to create the virtual machine

storage_os_disk.caching.description: Specifies the caching requirements for the OS Disk. Possible values include `None`, `ReadOnly` and `ReadWrite`.

storage_os_disk.create_option.description: Specifies how the OS Disk should be created. Possible values are `Attach` (managed disks only) and `FromImage`.

storage_os_disk.disk_size_gb.description: Specifies the size of the OS Disk in gigabytes.

storage_os_disk.image_uri.description: Specifies the Image URI in the format `publisherName:offer:skus:version`. This field can also specify the [VHD URI](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/linux/tutorial-custom-images) of a custom VM image to clone. When cloning a Custom (Unmanaged) Disk Image the `os_type` field must be set.

storage_os_disk.managed_disk_id.description: Specifies the ID of an existing Managed Disk which should be attached as the OS Disk of this Virtual Machine. If this is set then the `create_option` must be set to `Attach`

storage_os_disk.managed_disk_type.description: Specifies the type of Managed Disk which should be created. Possible values are `Standard_LRS`, `StandardSSD_LRS` or `Premium_LRS`.

storage_os_disk.name.description: Specifies the name of the OS Disk.

storage_os_disk.os_type.description: Specifies the Operating System on the OS Disk. Possible values are `Linux` and `Windows`.

storage_os_disk.vhd_uri.description: Specifies the URI of the VHD file backing this Unmanaged OS Disk

storage_os_disk.write_accelerator_enable.description: Specifies if Write Accelerator is enabled on the disk. This can only be enabled on `Premium_LRS` managed disks with no caching and [M-Series VMs](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/workloads/sap/how-to-enable-write-accelerator). Defaults to `false`.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the Virtual Machine.

vault_certificates.certificate_store.description: (Required, on windows machines) Specifies the certificate store on the Virtual Machine where the certificate should be added to, such as `My`.

vault_certificates.certificate_url.description: The ID of the Key Vault Secret. Stored secret is the Base64 encoding of a JSON Object that which is encoded in UTF-8 of which the contents need to be:

vm_size.description: Specifies the [size of the Virtual Machine](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/sizes-general). See also [Azure VM Naming Conventions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/vm-naming-conventions).

winrm.certificate_url.description: The ID of the Key Vault Secret which contains the encrypted Certificate which should be installed on the Virtual Machine. This certificate must also be specified in the `vault_certificates` block within the `os_profile_secrets` block.

winrm.protocol.description: Specifies the protocol of listener. Possible values are `HTTP` or `HTTPS`.

zones.description: A list of a single item of the Availability Zone which the Virtual Machine should be allocated in

configuration_id.description: The ARM resource ID of the Automanage Configuration to assign to the Virtual Machine

virtual_machine_id.description: The ARM resource ID of the Virtual Machine to assign the Automanage Configuration to

caching.description: Specifies the caching requirements for this Data Disk. Possible values include `None`, `ReadOnly` and `ReadWrite`.

create_option.description: The Create Option of the Data Disk, such as `Empty` or `Attach`. Defaults to `Attach`

lun.description: The Logical Unit Number of the Data Disk, which needs to be unique within the Virtual Machine

managed_disk_id.description: The ID of an existing Managed Disk which should be attached

virtual_machine_id.description: The ID of the Virtual Machine to which the Data Disk should be attached

write_accelerator_enabled.description: Specifies if Write Accelerator is enabled on the disk. This can only be enabled on `Premium_LRS` managed disks with no caching and [M-Series VMs](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/workloads/sap/how-to-enable-write-accelerator). Defaults to `false`.

auto_upgrade_minor_version.description: Specifies if the platform deploys the latest minor version update to the `type_handler_version` specified.

automatic_upgrade_enabled.description: Should the Extension be automatically updated whenever the Publisher releases a new version of this VM Extension?

failure_suppression_enabled.description: Should failures from the extension be suppressed? Possible values are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`.

name.description: The name of the virtual machine extension peering

protected_settings.description: The protected_settings passed to the extension, like settings, these are specified as a JSON object in a string.

protected_settings_from_key_vault.secret.description: The URL to the Key Vault Secret which stores the protected settings.

protected_settings_from_key_vault.source.description: The ID of the source Key Vault.

provision_after_extensions.description: Specifies the collection of extension names after which this extension needs to be provisioned.

publisher.description: The publisher of the extension, available publishers can be found by using the Azure CLI

settings.description: The settings passed to the extension, these are specified as a JSON object in a string.

tags.description: A mapping of tags to assign to the resource.

type.description: The type of extension, available types for a publisher can be found using the Azure CLI.
